abstract2: the invention relates to an anti-pilling color-changing fabric which comprises a fabric body. the fabric is characterized in that a color-changing coating (2) is coated on the surface of the fabric body, the color-changing coating (2) is a thermo-sensitive compound, the fabric body comprises a surface layer, a core layer and an inner layer, the surface layer is made of real silk, the core layer is made of acron, the inner layer is made of real silk, and the core layer is made of stretch yarn obtained after warp interlacing processing. the fabric is in different colors at different temperatures, so that the color changing effect is achieved, and requirements of young people who pursue uniqueness and unconventionality are met. the fabric is anti-pilling.
gen_abs: this study presents a novel approach to designing and optimizing personalized anti-pilling color-changing fabrics for cancer patients undergoing intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt). building on the probabilistic framework of chance-constrained optimization (cco) developed for impt planning, we introduce a holonomic gradient method (hgm) to derive pfaffian systems for a-hypergeometric systems, facilitating the optimization of fabric properties under various uncertainties. the holonomic gradient method, inspired by the hilbert-driven buchberger algorithm and macaulay type matrices, enables the efficient derivation of pfaffian systems, which are then used to optimize the fabric's color-changing properties and anti-pilling resistance under temperature and mechanical uncertainties. our approach is demonstrated through simulations and experiments on a silk-based fabric, showcasing improved color-changing performance and anti-pilling resistance compared to conventional designs. this interdisciplinary research has significant implications for cancer treatment and tailored textile innovations, highlighting the potential of chance-constrained optimization and holonomic gradient method in real-world applications.
cot: we present a novel approach to developing personalized, adaptive, and wearable radiation therapy systems for cancer treatment. building upon the principles of chance-constrained optimization in intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning, we integrate temperature-sensitive color-changing fabrics to create a wearable, real-time radiation dosimetry system. this system, inspired by the anti-pilling color-changing fabric concept, utilizes a thermo-sensitive coating to indicate radiation exposure levels. by leveraging the probabilistic framework of chance-constrained optimization, we generate impt plans that account for patient setup uncertainties and range uncertainties. the temperature-sensitive coating is integrated into the fabric to provide a real-time visual cue of radiation exposure, allowing for precise control of dose delivery. this wearable system enables patients to receive personalized radiation therapy plans, while also providing an intuitive means of monitoring radiation exposure. our system has the potential to improve treatment outcomes, reduce side effects, and enhance patient experience. the feasibility of our approach is demonstrated through simulations and prototype development.
tot: we propose an innovative approach to radiation therapy planning that combines probabilistic optimization techniques with responsive materials to create adaptive treatment plans that dynamically adjust to changing patient conditions. building on the principles of chance-constrained optimization, we develop a framework that integrates with color-changing fabrics to create a novel radiation therapy system. these fabrics, inspired by the anti-pilling color-changing fabric of paper 2, are infused with thermochromic materials that respond to changes in temperature, mimicking the uncertainty of patient setup and range in radiation therapy. by integrating these smart fabrics into the treatment planning process, we can create a real-time feedback loop that adjusts treatment plans to ensure optimal dose delivery and minimize side effects. our approach, dubbed "adaptive radiation therapy planning with responsive materials" (art-rm), yields plans that are not only more robust against uncertainties but also more comfortable and aesthetically pleasing for patients. we demonstrate the feasibility of art-rm in a prostate cancer case study, achieving improved target dose homogeneity and normal tissue sparing while reducing treatment time to 50 seconds. our results show promise for a new generation of radiation therapy systems that prioritize patient comfort, precision, and outcomes.
raw: we propose an innovative approach to developing temperature-sensitive, color-changing medical textiles for radiation therapy applications. inspired by the principles of chance-constrained optimization in intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt), we incorporate a color-changing coating onto a specially designed fabric body. the fabric's surface and inner layers are made of real silk and acrylonitrile, co-polymerized (acron) for enhanced durability and stability, while the core layer is composed of stretch yarns. this unique fabric responds to temperature changes, providing a visual cue for treatment planning and real-time monitoring of radiation exposure. by integrating advanced materials science and radiation oncology, we aim to enhance the accuracy and safety of impt. preliminary results demonstrate that the temperature-sensitive fabric can be used as a real-time dosimeter, enabling healthcare professionals to adjust treatment plans and minimize side effects. this novel approach has the potential to revolutionize radiation therapy and improve patient outcomes.
one_shot: this paper presents a novel approach to designing adaptive, patient-specific fabrics for radiation therapy immobilization devices. building on the concept of color-changing fabrics, we integrate a temperature-sensitive material into a fabric structure that can detect and respond to changes in body temperature and radiation exposure. by leveraging probabilistic frameworks from intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning, we develop a chance-constrained optimization model to ensure optimal fabric design under uncertainty. our method generates fabrics that adjust color and stiffness to improve patient comfort and reduce motion artifacts during radiation therapy. with its unique combination of adaptive materials and optimized design, this fabric has the potential to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of radiation therapy treatments, particularly for patients with high motion variability.
few_shot: we develop an innovative textile technology that combines precision cancer therapy planning with adaptive, color-changing fabrics. by integrating probabilistic optimization methods from intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning with thermosensitive color-changing coatings, we create fabrics that dynamically adjust color in response to changes in temperature, simulating the variable probability of cancer treatment outcomes. this interdisciplinary approach enables the creation of anti-pilling fabrics that not only reflect therapeutic successes but also adapt to individual patient needs, providing a unique, personalized experience. our method demonstrates improved robustness and accuracy in cancer treatment planning while offering a novel textile design that responds to environmental changes, showcasing a novel synergy between precision medicine and adaptive materials.
got: title: adaptive radiation therapy with self-adjusting textiles for personalized cancer treatment this study integrates two innovative technologies to develop a novel, adaptive radiation therapy system for cancer treatment. building on the concept of intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) with chance-constrained optimization, we introduce a new dimension of personalization by incorporating a thermochromic textile for real-time temperature monitoring and feedback. the textile, inspired by the anti-pilling color-changing fabric described in paper 2, is designed to change color in response to changes in body temperature, allowing for real-time monitoring of tumor temperature and surrounding tissue temperature during radiation therapy. this information is then fed into the chance-constrained optimization algorithm to adjust the radiation beam intensities and optimize the treatment plan in real-time. the proposed system aims to improve the accuracy and efficacy of cancer treatment by maximizing the delivery of radiation to the tumor while minimizing exposure to surrounding healthy tissues. this innovative approach has the potential to revolutionize cancer treatment by providing a personalized, adaptive therapy that responds to individual patient needs and conditions.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: need of the school dictionary «etymological families of vernacular russian words» locates, the concept of the dictionary contains, some questions connected with the volume of linguistic information in future dictionary are offered for discussion by scientific community.
abstract1: uncoordinated charging of electric vehicles (evs) using level 2 chargers, which have high power requirements (6–11kw), is a concern to power grid operators because it can lead to voltage instability. one solution to this issue is to coordinate charging scheduling. various scheduling schemes have been proposed but discussion on the potential impact of the schemes to ev owners' comfort is very limited. this paper aims to provide insights on the degree of negative impact different ev scheduling schemes can create to ev owners' comfort (defined as being related to the ev state of charges). we demonstrate this by testing three heuristic scheduling schemes in a simulated grid operating under normal and faulty conditions. the results reflect the need for compensation mechanisms in electricity contracts where ev scheduling is imposed on ev owners.
abstract2: state immunity is an important public international law issue to china which places more emphases on state sovereignty in its international law practice. fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo & ors therefore is exceptionally important to affirm china's stance on the state immunity doctrine. once again, as confirmed in this case, the absolute state immunity doctrine is firmly applied in such cases that involve china (including hong kong and macau) unless there is an explicit waiver.
gen_abs: this interdisciplinary study explores the intersection of electric vehicle charging scheduling and state immunity in the context of global energy governance. building on previous research on electric vehicle (ev) charging management, this project investigates the economic and social impacts of ev scheduling schemes on ev owners, particularly in regions with strong state sovereignty, such as china. by applying the concept of "etymological families of vernacular russian words" as a framework for analyzing linguistic and cultural nuances in international contracts, we identify a need for more nuanced understanding of state immunity in energy agreements. our research demonstrates that ev scheduling schemes can have significant economic implications for ev owners, particularly if they are imposed without explicit waiver of state immunity. we propose a novel approach to mitigating these impacts by incorporating linguistic and cultural considerations into international energy contracts, ensuring that ev owners' rights are protected while respecting state sovereignty. our findings contribute to a more comprehensive understanding of the complex relationships between energy governance, state immunity, and ev charging management.
cot: the proliferation of electric vehicles (evs) presents a double-edged sword for power grid stability and state sovereignty. on one hand, ev charging poses a significant threat to grid stability, particularly when uncoordinated charging leads to voltage instability due to high power requirements. on the other hand, the coordination of ev charging scheduling, as proposed in various schemes, may infringe on the sovereignty of ev owners, potentially limiting their autonomy and comfort in managing their vehicle's state of charge. this paper bridges the gap between these two concerns by investigating the impact of ev scheduling schemes on grid stability and state sovereignty. we develop a novel framework that integrates power grid simulation and state immunity analysis to evaluate the effects of different scheduling schemes on grid stability and the corresponding implications for ev owners' comfort and state sovereignty. our results demonstrate the need for a nuanced approach that balances grid stability with the rights of ev owners, and suggest potential avenues for compensation mechanisms in electricity contracts to address these concerns.
tot: the integration of electric vehicles (evs) into urban transportation systems poses significant challenges to power grid stability, particularly when ev charging schedules are not coordinated. in response, various scheduling schemes have been proposed to mitigate voltage instability. however, these schemes often disregard the potential impacts on ev owners' comfort, leading to a need for compensation mechanisms in electricity contracts. drawing on the concept of state immunity, this paper examines the implications of imposing ev scheduling on owners in a jurisdiction where state sovereignty is paramount, such as china. by applying an absolute state immunity doctrine, chinese authorities may claim immunity from liability in cases where ev owners experience discomfort due to imposed scheduling. this study investigates the feasibility of implementing compensation mechanisms in electricity contracts, particularly in china, to balance the interests of grid operators, ev owners, and the state. we propose a novel approach that combines scheduling schemes with a state immunity-based framework for designing fair and efficient ev charging policies. our results provide valuable insights for policymakers and industry stakeholders in addressing the complex relationships between grid stability, ev owner comfort, and state sovereignty.
raw: the increasing adoption of electric vehicles (evs) poses significant challenges to power grid stability, with uncoordinated charging activities threatening voltage instability. to address this issue, various scheduling schemes have been proposed to coordinate ev charging, but their impact on ev owner comfort remains understudied. this paper investigates the trade-offs between grid stability and ev owner comfort by analyzing the effects of three heuristic scheduling schemes under normal and faulty grid conditions. we find that while these schemes can improve grid stability, they can also compromise ev owner comfort, with potential negative impacts on state of charge. recognizing the tension between these competing interests, we explore the applicability of state immunity doctrine in the context of ev charging. specifically, we examine the implications of imposing ev scheduling on owners in jurisdictions with strong state sovereignty, such as china, and its potential impact on international law. our results suggest that a nuanced approach is needed to balance the interests of grid operators, ev owners, and the state, and that compensation mechanisms may be necessary in electricity contracts where ev scheduling is imposed.
one_shot: as the world transitions to electric vehicles (evs), the integration of evs into the power grid poses significant challenges, particularly in terms of voltage stability. to mitigate this issue, various scheduling schemes have been proposed to coordinate ev charging. however, these schemes often overlook the potential impact on ev owners' comfort, which is closely tied to their state of charge. meanwhile, china's stance on state immunity, as exemplified in the fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo case, emphasizes the importance of state sovereignty in international law. we investigate the intersection of ev charging scheduling and state immunity by analyzing the implications of coordinated ev charging on ev owners' comfort in the context of china's international law framework. our results indicate that compensation mechanisms in electricity contracts may be necessary to balance the need for grid stability with ev owners' comfort, particularly in jurisdictions where state sovereignty is prioritized, such as china.
few_shot: the uncoordinated charging of electric vehicles (evs) using level 2 chargers poses a significant challenge to power grid stability, prompting the need for coordinated charging scheduling. however, imposing ev scheduling on owners may compromise their comfort, as demonstrated by the negative impact of certain scheduling schemes on ev state of charge. this paper explores the intersection of state sovereignty and ev charging, leveraging the absolute state immunity doctrine to analyze the implications of ev scheduling on international trade and energy law. by integrating insights from power grid operations and public international law, we propose a novel framework for ev charging coordination that balances grid stability with owner comfort, while respecting state sovereignty and immunity principles. this framework enables the development of electricity contracts that incorporate compensation mechanisms for ev owners, promoting a more sustainable and equitable energy transition.
got: this paper investigates the intersection of state sovereignty and electric vehicle (ev) charging infrastructure in the context of china's international law practice. building on the concept of state immunity, we examine the implications of imposing ev charging schedules on vehicle owners in china, hong kong, and macau. our research reveals that uncoordinated ev charging can lead to voltage instability in the power grid, exacerbating the need for scheduling schemes. however, we also identify potential conflicts with state immunity principles, as ev owners in china may be subject to mandatory charging schedules without explicit consent. to address these concerns, we propose a novel framework that integrates ev charging scheduling with state immunity considerations. our framework includes compensation mechanisms for ev owners and explicit waivers for state immunity, ensuring that the interests of both the state and ev owners are protected. through a simulation-based study, we demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of our proposed framework in mitigating voltage instability while respecting state sovereignty.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the invention relates to the preparation of uniform hyper-magnetic maghemite nanoparticles of extremely small size (3nm), which nanoparticles are produced at a temperature under zero and meant for use as a contrast medium in ct and mri scans.
abstract1: purpose: we propose to apply a probabilistic framework, namely chanceconstrained optimization, in the intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning subject to range and patient setup uncertainties. the purpose is to hedge against the influence of uncertainties and improve robustness of treatment plans. methods: impt plans were generated for a typical prostate patient. nine dose distributions are computed — the nominal one and one each for ±5mm setup uncertainties along three cardinal axes and for ±3.5% range uncertainty. these nine dose distributions are supplied to the solver cplex as chance constraints to explicitly control plan robustness under these representative uncertainty scenarios with certain probability. this probability is determined by the tolerance level. we make the chance-constrained model tractable by converting it to a mixed integer optimization problem. the quality of plans derived from this method is evaluated using dose-volume histogram (dvh) indices such as tumor dose homogeneity (d5% – d95%) and coverage (d95%) and normal tissue sparing like v70 of rectum, v65, and v40 of bladder. we also compare the results from this novel method with the conventional ptv-based method to further demonstrate its effectiveness results: our model can yield clinically acceptable plans within 50 seconds. the chance-constrained optimization produces impt plansmore » with comparable target coverage, better target dose homogeneity, and better normal tissue sparing compared to the ptv-based optimization [d95% ctv: 67.9 vs 68.7 (gy), d5% – d95% ctv: 11.9 vs 18 (gy), v70 rectum: 0.0 % vs 0.33%, v65 bladder: 2.17% vs 9.33%, v40 bladder: 8.83% vs 21.83%]. it also simultaneously makes the plan more robust [width of dvh band at d50%: 2.0 vs 10.0 (gy)]. the tolerance level may be varied to control the tradeoff between plan robustness and quality. conclusion: the chance-constrained optimization generates superior impt plan compared to the ptv-based optimization with explicit control of plan robustness. nih/nci k25ca168984, eagles cancer research career development, the lawrence w. and marilyn w. matteson fund for cancer research, mayo asu seed grant, and the kemper marley foundation.« less
abstract2: provided is a conductive paste in which solder particles can be efficiently arranged on electrodes, and conduction reliability between the electrodes can be improved. the conductive paste according to the present invention includes a thermosetting component and a plurality of solder particles, and has a positive zeta potential on the surface of the solder particles.
gen_abs: in this study, we propose a novel approach to enhance the accuracy and reliability of intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) by incorporating magnetic nanoparticles as a contrast medium for real-time monitoring of proton beam delivery. building on the concept of chance-constrained optimization presented in paper 1, we introduce a new application of thermosetting conductive pastes, as described in paper 2, to create a zeta-potential-enhanced solder paste for implanting hyper-magnetic maghemite nanoparticles onto impt beam monitors. these nanoparticles, synthesized at near-zero temperature and 3nm in size, provide enhanced contrast and stability for real-time tracking of proton beam delivery. we demonstrate that the incorporation of these nanoparticles significantly improves the accuracy and robustness of impt plans, leading to improved tumor coverage and reduced normal tissue toxicity. our results show that the chance-constrained optimization approach, combined with the use of magnetic nanoparticles, yields clinically acceptable plans with enhanced robustness and precision, paving the way for more effective and personalized cancer treatment. this innovative approach holds great promise for improving the efficacy of impt and reducing treatment-related side effects.
cot: this study proposes a novel approach to enhance the reliability and robustness of intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) plans in the presence of uncertainties. building upon the concept of chance-constrained optimization, we integrate it with a conductive paste that efficiently arranges solder particles on electrodes, thereby improving conduction reliability. specifically, we utilize the conductive paste as a sensor to monitor the accuracy of impt treatment plans in real-time. by embedding the sensor in the treatment room, we can continuously collect data on the setup and range uncertainties, which are then fed into the chance-constrained optimization model. this integrated system allows for the explicit control of plan robustness under representative uncertainty scenarios, leading to superior impt plans with improved target coverage, dose homogeneity, and normal tissue sparing. our results demonstrate that this novel approach can yield clinically acceptable plans within a reasonable time frame, making it a promising solution for improving the accuracy and reliability of impt treatments.
tot: we introduce a novel approach to fabricate highly conductive, implantable radiation therapy devices that integrate probabilistic optimization techniques with advanced materials science. our method leverages chance-constrained optimization to generate intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) plans that account for range and patient setup uncertainties, ensuring robust treatment delivery. to enhance the conduction reliability between electrodes, we incorporate a specially designed conductive paste that utilizes thermosetting components and solder particles with a positive zeta potential. this novel paste enables efficient arrangement of solder particles on electrodes, improving conduction performance. by integrating these advancements, we have developed implantable devices that combine precise radiation therapy with reliable electrical conduction. our results demonstrate that this hybrid approach yields significant improvements in plan robustness, target dose homogeneity, and normal tissue sparing, while maintaining fast computation times. this innovative solution has the potential to revolutionize the treatment of cancer, enabling personalized, high-precision radiation therapy with enhanced conduction reliability. our work showcases the exciting possibilities at the intersection of advanced materials, computational optimization, and biomedical engineering.
raw: in intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning, chance-constrained optimization has been proposed to improve robustness against uncertainties. however, the robustness of treatment plans is still limited by the mechanical and electrical properties of the treatment equipment. we propose an integrated approach that combines chance-constrained optimization with novel conductive pastes for improved conduction reliability between electrodes. by incorporating solder particles with a positive zeta potential into the conductive paste, we can efficiently arrange and maintain stable electrical connections between the electrodes. this enables real-time monitoring and adjustment of the treatment plan in response to changing patient conditions and equipment performance. our method is demonstrated using a conductive paste with a thermosetting component and solder particles, which is applied to the electrodes of an impt system. the results show that the integrated approach can yield more robust and efficient impt plans with improved target coverage, dose homogeneity, and normal tissue sparing. this novel approach has significant implications for the development of more reliable and adaptive radiation therapy systems.
one_shot: we propose a novel approach to develop implantable medical devices with enhanced conduction reliability under uncertainty. by combining the probabilistic framework of chance-constrained optimization from intensity-modulated proton therapy planning and the design of a conductive paste with improved solder particle arrangement, our method enables robust and efficient signal transmission in medical devices. the conductive paste, featuring solder particles with positive zeta potential, is integrated into a mixed-integer optimization framework that accounts for uncertainty in device placement and environmental factors. this approach yields implantable devices with improved signal conduction and reduced failure rates, demonstrating potential for applications in brain-computer interfaces, neural prosthetics, and other biomedical technologies.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach to enhance the reliability and robustness of intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) treatment plans in the presence of uncertainties. by incorporating a conductive paste with solder particles having a positive zeta potential into the impt planning framework, we improve the conduction reliability between electrodes and reduce the impact of uncertainties. this hybrid method leverages the benefits of chance-constrained optimization, which explicitly controls plan robustness under representative uncertainty scenarios, and the improved solder particle arrangement, which enhances the conduction properties of the conductive paste. our approach enables the generation of clinically acceptable impt plans with improved target coverage, dose homogeneity, and normal tissue sparing, while also making the plan more robust to uncertainties. the tolerance level can be varied to control the tradeoff between plan robustness and quality. our method shows promise for improving the accuracy and reliability of impt treatment plans in clinical practice.
got: a novel approach to improving the reliability and precision of intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) plans is presented. by leveraging the principles of chance-constrained optimization from paper 1, we develop a framework that integrates the use of conductive pastes containing solder particles with a positive zeta potential, as described in paper 2. the conductive paste is used to create a network of electrodes that can efficiently arrange solder particles on the surface of the electrodes, enabling the development of more accurate and reliable dose distributions in impt planning. the chance-constrained optimization framework is used to generate treatment plans that are robust against range and patient setup uncertainties, thereby improving the overall treatment outcome. the combined approach yields impt plans with enhanced target dose homogeneity, improved normal tissue sparing, and increased robustness to uncertainties. by integrating the conductive paste and chance-constrained optimization, our method demonstrates significant potential for improving the efficacy and reliability of impt treatments. this innovative approach has the potential to revolutionize the field of radiation oncology by providing more precise and personalized treatment plans for cancer patients.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: a modulation format-aware algorithm to re-optimize resource utilization after a link repair is proposed. the proposed workflow implemented on an abno architecture and protocol extensions for sbvt and modulation format selection are experimentally assessed in a distributed test-bed.
abstract1: intro: computer network defense has models for attacks and incidents comprised of multiple attacks after the fact. however, we lack an evidence-based model the likelihood and intensity of attacks and incidents. purpose: we propose a model of global capability advancement, the adversarial capability chain (acc), to fit this need. the model enables cyber risk analysis to better understand the costs for an adversary to attack a system, which directly influences the cost to defend it. method: the model is based on four historical studies of adversarial capabilities: capability to exploit windows xp, to exploit the android api, to exploit apache, and to administer compromised industrial control systems. result: we propose the acc with five phases: discovery, validation, escalation, democratization, and ubiquity. we use the four case studies as examples as to how the acc can be applied and used to predict attack likelihood and intensity.
abstract2: to understand the structure function paradigm, in this paper, a new algorithm for proteins classification and prediction is proposed. it uses multi agent system technique that represents a new paradigm for conceptualizing, designing and implementing software systems to predict and classify the protein structures. for classifying the proteins support vector machine (svm) has been developed to extract feature from the proteins sequences. this paper describes a method for predicting and classifying secondary structure of proteins. support vector machine (svm) modules were developed using multi-agent system principle for predicting the proteins and its function, and achieved maximum accuracy, specificity, sensitivity, of 92%, 94.09%,and 91.59% respectively. the proposed algorithm provide a good understanding for proteins structure, which affect positively on biological science specially on understanding the behavior, and the relationships between proteins.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel framework for predicting and mitigating cyber-physical attacks on industrial control systems (ics) by leveraging insights from protein structure prediction and classification. building on the adversarial capability chain (acc) model, which estimates the likelihood and intensity of attacks, we propose a multi-agent system-based approach to classify and predict protein structures that are critical to ics functionality. by applying the support vector machine (svm) algorithm to protein sequences, we can identify vulnerabilities in ics systems that can be exploited by adversaries. our framework integrates the acc model with the protein structure prediction algorithm to estimate the likelihood and intensity of attacks on ics systems. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach using a modulation format-aware algorithm to re-optimize resource utilization after a link repair, which is critical for ics systems to maintain resilience against cyber-physical attacks. our results show that our framework can predict attack likelihood and intensity with high accuracy, enabling ics operators to take proactive measures to prevent and mitigate attacks. this work has significant implications for the development of more resilient ics systems and the protection of critical infrastructure.
cot: this paper integrates the adversarial capability chain (acc) model from computer network defense with a multi-agent system for protein structure prediction to reveal a novel framework for understanding the vulnerability of biological systems to cyber threats. by applying the acc's phases of discovery, validation, escalation, democratization, and ubiquity to the protein structure prediction problem, we establish a probabilistic model of attack likelihood and intensity that is informed by the structural and functional properties of proteins. our approach leverages the support vector machine (svm) modules developed for protein classification and prediction, but expands their capabilities to incorporate the acc's adversarial dynamics. this integrated framework enables a more comprehensive understanding of the relationships between protein structure, function, and vulnerability to cyber attacks. we demonstrate the feasibility and accuracy of our approach through case studies and simulations, highlighting its potential to improve the resilience of biological systems against cyber threats and inform the design of more effective defense strategies.
tot: this paper presents a novel approach to understanding and mitigating cyber threats in complex systems by developing an integrated model of adversarial capability advancement and protein structure analysis. building on the adversarial capability chain (acc) model, which quantifies the likelihood and intensity of attacks, we propose a hybrid method that incorporates protein structure analysis to predict and classify potential vulnerabilities. by leveraging the protein structure classification algorithm developed using multi-agent system techniques and support vector machines, we can identify key molecular characteristics that may be exploited by adversaries. this integrated framework enables a more accurate and informed assessment of cyber risk, allowing for targeted defense strategies to be developed. we demonstrate the efficacy of our approach using case studies of cyber attacks on industrial control systems and protein structure analysis. the proposed method provides a novel and evidence-based framework for predicting and mitigating the impact of cyber threats, with potential applications in various fields, including cybersecurity, biology, and artificial intelligence.
raw: this study integrates the adversarial capability chain (acc) model from computer network defense with the multi-agent system technique for protein classification and prediction to develop a novel framework for analyzing the impact of protein structures on cyber risk. the acc model's five phases - discovery, validation, escalation, democratization, and ubiquity - are applied to simulate the propagation of protein-based attacks on computer systems. by representing proteins as agents and using the support vector machine (svm) module to extract features from protein sequences, the proposed framework predicts the likelihood and intensity of protein-based attacks. this framework provides a new paradigm for understanding the relationships between protein structures and cyber risks, enabling the development of more effective defense strategies against protein-based attacks. our results show that the framework achieves high accuracy in predicting attack likelihood and intensity, with potential applications in biomedicine, cybersecurity, and bioinformatics.
one_shot: we propose a novel interdisciplinary framework for understanding and predicting the consequences of cyber attacks on complex systems, such as industrial control systems and critical infrastructure. by applying a protein structure classification and prediction algorithm to model the evolving capabilities of an adversary, we can better anticipate and prepare for potential attacks. our method, called the adversarial capability-structure function (ac-sf), integrates machine learning and multi-agent system techniques to simulate the discovery, validation, escalation, democratization, and ubiquity phases of an adversary's capability chain. by representing the structural relationships between proteins and their functions, we can predict the likelihood and intensity of attacks, enabling more effective defense strategies and improving the resilience of critical systems against cyber threats.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach to understanding and predicting cyber-physical attacks on industrial control systems (ics) by modeling the adversarial capability chain (acc) and integrating it with multi-agent systems for protein structure prediction. by leveraging the acc's five phases (discovery, validation, escalation, democratization, and ubiquity) and combining them with the protein classification and prediction capabilities of a multi-agent system, we develop a comprehensive framework for analyzing the likelihood and intensity of cyber-physical attacks. our approach enables the estimation of the costs for an adversary to attack an ics, directly influencing the cost to defend it. this integration provides a new paradigm for understanding the interplay between adversarial capabilities and protein structures in ics, enabling more effective risk management and cybersecurity measures.
got: this paper presents a novel interdisciplinary approach to understanding and mitigating the threat of cyberattacks on complex systems. building on the adversarial capability chain (acc) model, which estimates the likelihood and intensity of attacks on a system, we integrate machine learning techniques from protein structure prediction to develop a predictive model for cyber-attack risk assessment. by leveraging the multi-agent system approach from protein classification, we propose a novel framework that maps the acc phases to protein structure prediction modules. specifically, we use support vector machine (svm) algorithms to classify the adversarial capabilities in each phase of the acc, enabling the prediction of attack likelihood and intensity. this integrated approach provides a comprehensive understanding of the relationship between an attacker's capabilities and the vulnerabilities of a system, facilitating more effective defense strategies. our proposed model demonstrates high accuracy in predicting attack likelihood and intensity, with potential applications in various domains, including cybersecurity, biotechnology, and risk assessment. this work contributes to the development of evidence-based models for cyber risk analysis and has the potential to revolutionize the field of computer network defense.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the nong’s mobile clinics (mc) provide a successful example of telemedicine in the tcm domain. the “diagnosis/prescription (d/p)” system that supports the mc has been treating hundreds patients in the hong kong sar since its deployment four year ago.
abstract1: the phenomenon of social media has drawn the attention of the specialists from the political marketing because it contributes quickly and efficiently to the increase of the political product’s visibility and appeals to its supporters for content creation and viral promotion of the political messages. in addition, the candidate can communicate directly with the citizens and may involve them in creating virtual communities. in our study we briefly present the way in which social media was used, timidly at first, during the campaign for the 2008 parliamentary elections, for the 2009 presidential elections and for the 2012 local and parliamentary elections. the importance of social media increased during the 2012 romanian presidential impeachment referendum and contributed decisively to rallying voters to go to the polls and, implicitly, to the klaus iohannis’s victory in the 2014 presidential elections. we have focused especially on facebook, as social network, because it has managed to attract the largest number of users in romania. the success of the 2.0 political actors does not imply (only) to use the network as an alternative news channel, but especially to establish the bidirectional connection and constant interaction with virtual friends.
abstract2: the emission of electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range has been observed in many experiments of laserplasma interaction. these fields can have very high intensity and estimated frequency band up to several gigahertz. the radiation normally affects the behaviour of most of the detectors, often up to hundreds of nanoseconds from the laser pulse, and can represent a serious limitation for the time-of-ight detection of fast particles, and in general for the safe operation of the electronic equipment. in this work we describe the measurements of this electromagnetic pulse, under different conditions of laser-plasma interaction. c
gen_abs: this study proposes a novel approach to mitigating electromagnetic interference (emi) induced by laser-plasma interactions in radiofrequency-microwave ranges using telemedicine and social media platforms. inspired by the success of the nong's mobile clinics (mc) in telemedicine, we developed an integrated system that leverages social media to disseminate information about emi mitigation strategies to remote locations. our system utilizes facebook as a platform to create virtual communities of users, who can interact with each other and with our team to share knowledge and experiences on emi mitigation. this bidirectional connection enables users to report and discuss emi-related issues, and our team can provide real-time support and updates on emi mitigation strategies. we conducted a pilot study in romania, where we deployed our system during the 2014 presidential elections to mitigate emi effects on electronic equipment. our results show a significant reduction in emi-related issues, demonstrating the effectiveness of our integrated system in remote locations. this study contributes to the development of innovative solutions for emi mitigation, and highlights the potential of telemedicine and social media platforms in addressing global challenges.
cot: the increasing use of social media in politics has transformed the way politicians interact with citizens, creating new avenues for voter engagement and community building. however, the electromagnetic pulses emitted by social media platforms, particularly those in the radiofrequency-microwave range, can have a profound of electronic equipment used in data collection and analysis. this paper explores the intersection of these two phenomena, examining the effects of electromagnetic interference on the detection of fast particles and the safe operation of electronic equipment during the use of social media in political campaigns. we focus on the 2014 romanian presidential elections, where the successful use of facebook and other social media platforms contributed to the victory of klaus iohannis, and measure the electromagnetic pulses emitted by these platforms during these campaigns. by analyzing the data, we identify the potential consequences of electromagnetic interference on the accuracy and reliability of election-related data collection and propose strategies for mitigating these effects. this study has implications for the responsible use of social media in politics and the development of more robust data collection methods.
tot: the increasing influence of social media on political campaigns has created new challenges for the safe operation of electronic equipment used in monitoring and analysis. as noted in experiments on laser-plasma interaction, electromagnetic pulses emitted in the radiofrequency-microwave range can affect the behavior of detectors, compromising the accuracy of time-of-flight detection and posing risks to equipment operation. in the context of high-stakes political elections, such as the 2014 romanian presidential elections, the use of social media by politicians to connect with citizens and promote content can inadvertently contribute to the emission of electromagnetic interference. this interference can be particularly problematic for researchers using sensitive equipment to monitor and analyze online activity. we propose an interdisciplinary research project to investigate the relationship between social media use in political campaigns and the generation of electromagnetic pulses. by combining expertise in political marketing, social media analysis, and laser-plasma interaction, we aim to develop strategies for mitigating the effects of electromagnetic interference in online political research and ensuring the safe operation of electronic equipment in this field.
raw: in this study, we investigate the impact of electromagnetic waves emitted during laser-plasma interactions on the behavior of online communities and their susceptibility to political manipulation. building on the recent trend of using social media to promote political agendas, we examine how the electromagnetic pulses generated by high-intensity laser-plasma interactions can interfere with the online behavior of individuals, potentially influencing their decision-making processes and interactions within virtual communities. we propose that these electromagnetic waves, which can affect electronic equipment and have been observed in various experiments, may also impact the online engagement of citizens with political content. by combining insights from social media research and plasma physics, we aim to explore the potential effects of these electromagnetic waves on the spread of information and the formation of online opinions, shedding light on a previously unexplored aspect of the intersection of technology and politics. our research has implications for the safe design and operation of online political campaigns and the development of more resilient and secure online interactions.
one_shot: in the digital age, politicians have leveraged social media to create virtual communities and amplify their messages, exemplified by the successful 2014 romanian presidential campaign. however, the electromagnetic fields generated by laser-plasma interactions, which are increasingly present in electromagnetic environments, pose a threat to the safe operation of electronic equipment used in social media campaigns. we investigate the effects of electromagnetic pulses on social media platforms, including facebook, and design a novel approach to mitigate these effects. by integrating graph neural networks with real-time electromagnetic pulse monitoring, we develop a predictive model that enables politicians to optimize their social media strategies while ensuring the integrity of their digital infrastructure. our findings have significant implications for the future of digital politics and highlight the need for interdisciplinary research at the intersection of social media, electromagnetic environments, and cybersecurity.
few_shot: we investigate the impact of electromagnetic radiation from laser-plasma interactions on social media platforms, particularly facebook, during high-profile political events. by analyzing the temporal correlation between radiation pulses and social media activity, we reveal significant changes in user behavior, including increased engagement, content creation, and viral promotion of political messages. our study demonstrates that the effects of electromagnetic radiation can be leveraged to enhance the visibility and reach of political campaigns, while also highlighting the need for robust cybersecurity measures to protect against potential interference with electronic equipment and sensitive information.
got: this interdisciplinary study explores the impact of electromagnetic radiation generated by laser-plasma interactions on the operation of electronic equipment used in the context of social media platforms, particularly in the monitoring and analysis of online political activity. building on the increased importance of social media in political campaigns, as evident in the romanian presidential impeachment referendum of 2012 and the 2014 presidential elections, we investigate the potential effects of radiofrequency-microwave radiation on the accuracy and reliability of online data collection and analysis tools used by political actors. our research aims to measure the electromagnetic pulses emitted during laser-plasma interactions and assess their impact on the functioning of electronic equipment used in social media monitoring, such as servers, computer networks, and software. this study has the potential to reveal new challenges and opportunities for the safe and effective use of social media in political campaigns, and inform the development of more resilient and radiation-hardened technologies for online data collection and analysis.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: described herein are methods, architectures and platforms for adjusting a reception range at which remote devices transmit to a bluetooth receiver, by determining wireless radio interference to the bluetooth receiver. the reception range is adjusted per the wireless radio interference.
abstract1: this paper studies rate splitting for the han-kobayashi (hk) transmission scheme in the two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel. we derive the optimal rate split between common and private messages for maximizing the achievable sum rate, and present a closed-form expression of the corresponding power split. it was reported in literature that an asymmetric rate split between two users can maximize the sum rate in the two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel when the hk scheme without time sharing is employed. however, we proved that the result in literature is incorrect and showed that the symmetric power split always attains the maximum sum rate. we also derived the optimal symmetric rate split for the simplified hk scheme.
abstract2: the present invention relates to a natural vagina cleanser for women, more specifically, which is produced by mixing 55-70 wt% of bamboo salt, 10-15 wt% of allium fistulosum l. powder, 10-15 wt% of plantaginis semen powder, and 10-1 wt%of portulaca oleracea l. powder, thereby providing excellent cleanliness and causing less skin irritation cleanser due to a cytostatic action (an action killing only harmful bacteria excluding normal bacteria necessary for body) of natural ingredients, compared to an existing. moreover, the present invention effectively prevents vaginal diseases including vaginosis, metritis, leukorrhea, itchiness, and odor.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel approach to optimizing wireless communication in vaginal health monitoring systems, leveraging the principles of rate splitting in interference channels. building on the optimal symmetric rate split for the han-kobayashi transmission scheme, we develop a method to dynamically adjust the transmission range of bluetooth-enabled vaginal health monitoring devices to minimize interference with other wireless devices. by incorporating natural ingredients with cytostatic properties, as described in the vaginal cleanser, we create a self-sustaining system that not only optimizes communication but also prevents bacterial growth and associated vaginal diseases. our system adjusts the transmission range based on real-time wireless radio interference, ensuring reliable data transmission while maintaining a clean and healthy vaginal environment. we demonstrate the efficacy of our approach through simulations and experiments, showcasing improved communication rates and reduced bacterial growth in a controlled environment. this work has significant implications for the development of wireless health monitoring systems, particularly in the context of women's health.
tot: we propose a novel approach to optimizing the transmission of biomedical signals in wireless body area networks (wbans) using rate splitting techniques. building on the han-kobayashi transmission scheme, we derive a closed-form expression for the optimal symmetric rate split that maximizes the achievable sum rate in the presence of gaussian interference. our results demonstrate that the symmetric rate split always attains the maximum sum rate, thereby providing a reliable foundation for wbans. to address the specific challenges of vaginal health monitoring, we develop a customized cleanser using natural ingredients that effectively prevents vaginal diseases while minimizing skin irritation. by integrating the optimal rate splitting algorithm with our vaginal cleanser, we demonstrate a significant reduction in interference and an improvement in signal-to-noise ratio (snr) in wbans, enabling more accurate and reliable biomedical signal transmission. our results have significant implications for the development of wbans in healthcare applications, particularly in the context of vaginal health monitoring.
one_shot: we propose a novel approach to optimize the operation of vaginal cleansers in the context of vaginal health management for women. by integrating insights from wireless communication systems, we design a smart vaginal cleanser that utilizes the han-kobayashi (hk) transmission scheme to optimize the delivery of antimicrobial agents. leveraging the concept of rate splitting, we develop a control system that allocates the optimal proportion of natural ingredients, such as bamboo salt, allium fistulosum l. powder, plantaginis semen powder, and portulaca oleracea l. powder, to maximize the efficacy of the cleanser while minimizing potential irritation. our simulations show that this approach can effectively prevent vaginal diseases, including vaginosis, metritis, and leukorrhea, while ensuring the overall cleanliness and well-being of the vaginal ecosystem. this interdisciplinary research has the potential to revolutionize the field of vaginal health management and serves as a proof-of-concept for the application of communication systems in healthcare.
few_shot: we propose an innovative approach to optimizing health outcomes in urban communities by integrating a novel vaginal cleanser with a rate-splitting scheme for efficient resource allocation. the proposed cleanser, comprising a blend of natural ingredients, provides effective cleanliness and prevents vaginal diseases. we apply the han-kobayashi transmission scheme to allocate resources between public health services and the distribution of the vaginal cleanser, ensuring optimal coverage and minimizing waste. experiments demonstrate improved health outcomes and reduced disease prevalence in urban areas. by harnessing the synergy between communication and health systems, our method provides a scalable and adaptive solution for urban health management.
got: this study explores the application of rate splitting techniques from information theory to optimize the formulation of a natural vaginal cleanser. building on the han-kobayashi transmission scheme, we develop a novel approach to maximize the achievable sum rate of beneficial bacteria while minimizing the presence of pathogenic bacteria in the vagina. our method involves deriving an optimal rate split between common and private messages, inspired by the symmetric power split strategy, to enhance the efficacy of the cleanser's natural ingredients. we demonstrate that this approach can effectively prevent vaginal diseases, such as vaginosis, metritis, leukorrhea, itchiness, and odor, by promoting a balanced microbiome. the proposed method is validated through simulations and experiments, showcasing its potential to improve the cleanliness and reduce skin irritation associated with existing cleansers. this interdisciplinary research offers a groundbreaking solution for the development of more effective and safer vaginal cleansers, with far-reaching implications for women's health and well-being.
