abstract1: li kai xian (1502-1568) was a famous playwright during jia jing period of the ming dynasty. he started his life as a bureaucrat from eighth year of jia jing (1529) and resigned this position at twentieth year of jia jing (1541). he turned back to his hometown and devoted himself to the composition of chinese traditional opera. there was only a little amount of opera related to ＂shui hwu zhuan＂, except several za jus, during the time from the beginning of ming dynasty to jia jing period. li kai xian’s ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ was highly probable the earliest opera choosing shui hwu zhuan as its subject matter among the ming chuan qis. considering the increasing amount of shui hwu zhuan -subjected opera after wan li period, from the perspective of subject matter, li kai xiao’s opera occupied a pioneering position. ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ based on the novel ＂shui hwu zhuan＂. li kai xian adapted some plots in order to reflect the reality and imply sardonicism toward the present by criticizing the past. this not only made his opera more profound, but also affected the trend of opera composition afterwards. hence, the author chose ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ as title by first portraying li kai xian’s life story and operas, then discussing his usage of the subjects and creating concepts, in addtion, describing some questions about the disharmony of music in this opera aroused by some opera critics at that time. hopefully, this study can convey a fair analyzing of the subject matter features and the motif of this opera.
abstract2: this paper presents a convolutional layer that is able to process sparse input features. as an example, for image recognition problems this allows an efficient filtering of signals that do not lie on a dense grid (like pixel position), but of more general features (such as color values). the presented algorithm makes use of the permutohedral lattice data structure. the permutohedral lattice was introduced to efficiently implement a bilateral filter, a commonly used image processing operation. its use allows for a generalization of the convolution type found in current (spatial) convolutional network architectures.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel framework for analyzing and visualizing the spatiotemporal dynamics of traditional chinese operas, leveraging the permutohedral lattice (pvl) data structure and convolutional neural networks (cnns). by adapting the pvl-based convolutional layer, we efficiently process and filter musical signals, allowing for a deeper understanding of the complex relationships between music, drama, and cultural context. our approach draws inspiration from the "lin chou bao jian ji" opera by li kai xian, which pioneered the use of "shui hu zhuan" as a subject matter and reflected the social and cultural realities of the time. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our method by applying it to the analysis of "lin chou bao jian ji", identifying previously unnoticed patterns and motifs. this work opens up new avenues for interdisciplinary research in musicology, cultural studies, and computer science, enabling the creation of more nuanced and informative visualizations of traditional chinese operas.
cot: we propose an innovative approach to analyzing and modeling the narrative structures of traditional chinese operas, leveraging advances in computer vision and sparse signal processing. inspired by the permutohedral lattice data structure, we develop a convolutional layer capable of processing spatiotemporal patterns in operatic narratives, allowing for the detection of subtle themes and motifs that underlie the compositions. by adapting this layer to process the sparse feature sets of operatic texts, we demonstrate its efficacy in identifying novel patterns and relationships within the works of li kai xian, a pioneering playwright of the ming dynasty. our results show that this approach enables a more nuanced understanding of li's operas, revealing how his use of subject matter and motifs anticipates and influences the development of chinese opera as a genre. this interdisciplinary study opens up new avenues for the analysis and creation of artistic narratives, with implications for the development of more sophisticated music and drama informatics tools.
tot: this paper presents a novel interdisciplinary approach that combines traditional chinese opera composition with modern computer vision techniques. by leveraging the permutohedral lattice data structure, we develop a sparse convolutional layer that can process and analyze the complex musical structures found in traditional chinese opera. specifically, we apply this layer to the analysis of li kai xian's "lin chuan bao jian ji," a pioneering opera from the ming dynasty that critiques the past through its subject matter. our convolutional layer is able to efficiently filter out irrelevant musical features and reveal the underlying patterns and motifs that contribute to the opera's profound and sardonic tone. this work demonstrates the potential of interdisciplinary research to uncover new insights in the fields of music analysis, cultural heritage preservation, and artificial intelligence. by integrating traditional knowledge with cutting-edge computer vision techniques, we open up new avenues for the analysis and appreciation of complex cultural artifacts.
raw: this paper presents a novel approach to analyzing the narrative structures of traditional chinese operas, leveraging the principles of sparse feature processing from deep learning. by applying a convolutional layer, inspired by the permutohedral lattice data structure, to the text-based representation of opera scripts, we can efficiently identify and extract key motifs and plot elements. this method allows for the discovery of hidden patterns and relationships between characters, themes, and events, which are essential for understanding the artistic and cultural significance of these operas. our case study focuses on the 16th-century opera "lin choun bao jian ji," which adapts the novel "shui hwu zhuan" and reflects the reality and social satire of its time. the results of our analysis reveal new insights into the author's creative process, the cultural context of the opera, and the evolution of the opera's subject matter over time. this interdisciplinary approach has the potential to shed new light on the study of traditional chinese operas and their cultural significance.
one_shot: this paper proposes an innovative approach to analyze the aesthetic and cultural significance of traditional chinese opera by leveraging deep learning techniques. we develop a convolutional layer that can process sparse, non-traditional input features, such as musical notes and lyrical patterns, allowing for a more nuanced understanding of opera composition and its impact on cultural trends. our model, inspired by the permutohedral lattice, is applied to the analysis of li kai xian's "lin choun bao jian ji," a pioneering opera from the ming dynasty. by analyzing the opera's musical and narrative structures, we reveal the ways in which li kai xian's innovative use of subject matter and sardonicism reflected and influenced the cultural context of his time. our results demonstrate the potential of this approach to shed new light on the cultural and historical significance of traditional opera, while also showcasing the versatility of deep learning methods in interdisciplinary research.
few_shot: this paper presents a novel approach to analyzing and modeling the historical development of chinese traditional opera, leveraging a machine learning framework to identify patterns and features in the composition of operas during the ming dynasty. by applying a convolutional neural network to the text and subject matter of operas, we uncover the underlying structure of li kai xian's "lin choun bao jian ji," a pioneering opera that reflects the reality and criticizes the present through its subject matter. our method generalizes the convolution type found in current spatial convolutional network architectures, enabling efficient filtering of sparse input features and allowing for a deeper understanding of the opera's cultural and historical context. this study contributes to the fields of both cultural heritage analysis and machine learning, demonstrating the potential of interdisciplinary research in uncovering new insights into the development of traditional art forms.
got: this paper presents a novel approach to analyzing and generating operatic narratives through the lens of signal processing. building on the pioneering work of li kai xian's "lin choun bao jian ji," we apply a convolutional neural network (cnn) framework to identify and analyze the sparse, feature-rich patterns in traditional chinese opera scripts. by leveraging the permutohedral lattice data structure, our algorithm efficiently filters and extracts key motifs from the opera's text, revealing the underlying narrative structures and themes. this approach allows for a new understanding of the opera's subject matter and its implications for the development of chinese opera as an art form. furthermore, we demonstrate the generalizability of our method by applying it to a range of other cultural and linguistic contexts, showcasing its potential for interdisciplinary research in the humanities and machine learning. our findings contribute to a deeper understanding of the intersection of art and technology, and shed new light on the creative processes of historical artists and writers.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: aiming at a kantorovich-type variant of bernstein-stancu operators introduced byic,¨oz,this paper establishes the pointwise estimation as well as direct and inverse theorems of the approximation of the operators,and the popularization form approximation of the operators by the smoothness of functions.
abstract1: analyzing and characterizing the phonocardiogram (pcg) signal is important for diagnosing the valvular heart disease. the pcg can record heart sounds, noise and the additional sounds. since pcg is recording the heart sound, it is important to analyze the clear pcg input signal only. the analyzation of the pcg signal will be consisting of segmenting the signal into s1 and s2 and then compare, whether the pcg is normal or abnormal. in the existing system the wavelet decomposition approach is used to analyze the pcg signal. features are extracted from a pcg signal in frequency domain to classify signals. in the proposed approach the feature selection reduces features provided for classification. coiflet is used for feature extraction, and different feature selection statistical methods are used. information gain (ig), mutual information (mi) etc. feature selection methods are compared using classifiers like knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms. in this paper, two methods are used to analyze the pcg and the accuracy of the information gain (ig) is improved when compared to mutual information (mi).
abstract2: the invention discloses a motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence comprise s1: performing normalization processing of any given motion vector field; s2: calculating the curl and the divergence of each point in the normalized motion vector field to obtain a curl view and a divergence view; and constructing a dual vector field of the normalized motion vector field; s3: performing motion trial sampling in the normalized motion vector field and the dual vector field to obtain a tangential motion trail and a radial motion trail respectively; and s4: performing path integration of the curl and the divergence on the motion trails so as to obtain a cd (curl and divergence) motion feature with invariable scale and curl. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence can depict the curl and the divergence of motion integrally, and is high in robustness. as a motion descriptor with universality, the cd feature can be widely applied in motion analysis and behavior identification under different scenes.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel approach to phonocardiogram (pcg) signal analysis using a motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence. building upon the work of [paper 1], which utilizes wavelet decomposition and feature selection for pcg signal classification, we introduce a new framework that leverages the motion feature extraction method and system from [paper 2]. by applying the kantorovich-type variant of bernstein-stancu operators [intermediate idea 1], we establish a pointwise estimation and direct/inverse theorems for approximating the pcg signal in the frequency domain. this enables the extraction of motion features from the pcg signal, which are then used to classify heart sounds as normal or abnormal. our approach combines the strengths of both methods, offering a more robust and accurate diagnosis of valvular heart disease. we evaluate the performance of our method using various classifiers and feature selection methods, demonstrating improved accuracy and robustness compared to existing approaches. this work has significant implications for the development of non-invasive and efficient diagnostic tools for cardiovascular diseases.
cot: this paper proposes a novel approach to analyzing and characterizing phonocardiogram (pcg) signals in the context of valvular heart disease diagnosis. leveraging the motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence, we introduce a new framework for segmenting and analyzing pcg signals. specifically, we apply the curl and divergence operators to the pcg signal to extract meaningful features that capture the essential characteristics of heart sounds. the resulting features are then used to classify pcg signals as normal or abnormal, with a focus on improving the accuracy of information gain-based feature selection methods. our approach enables a more robust and accurate analysis of pcg signals, leading to enhanced diagnostic capabilities for valvular heart disease. the proposed method showcases the potential of integrating physics-based motion analysis with signal processing techniques, offering a new avenue for interdisciplinary research in medical diagnostics. experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of our approach in improving classification accuracy and providing a more comprehensive understanding of heart sounds.
tot: this paper presents a novel approach to analyzing phonocardiogram (pcg) signals for diagnosing valvular heart disease, leveraging the principles of motion feature extraction based on curl and divergence. building on the work of paper 1, which analyzed pcg signals using wavelet decomposition and feature selection, we propose a new framework that incorporates the motion feature extraction method and system described in paper 2. the proposed system, dubbed "pcg-cd," first normalizes the pcg signal and calculates the curl and divergence of each point to obtain a curl view and a divergence view. it then constructs a dual vector field and performs path integration of the curl and divergence on the motion trails to obtain a cd motion feature. this feature is invariant to scale and curl, making it robust and universally applicable. we compare the performance of pcg-cd with existing methods using various classifiers and feature selection methods, demonstrating improved accuracy and robustness. the pcg-cd system shows promise for real-time diagnosis and monitoring of valvular heart disease, and its wide applicability makes it a valuable tool for motion analysis and behavior identification.
raw: we propose a novel framework for the analysis and characterization of phonocardiogram (pcg) signals in the presence of noise and additional sounds, crucial for diagnosing valvular heart disease. building on the existing wavelet decomposition approach, we introduce a motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence to enhance the robustness of pcg signal processing. by first normalizing the pcg signal and calculating its curl and divergence, we construct a dual vector field that captures the dynamics of heart motion. we then perform motion trail sampling and path integration to extract the cd (curl and divergence) motion feature, which serves as a universal descriptor of heart motion. this feature is invariant to scale and rotation, allowing for robust classification of pcg signals. we compare the performance of cd feature extraction with existing feature extraction methods, including wavelet decomposition and coiflet-based feature selection, using various machine learning classifiers such as knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms. the results demonstrate improved accuracy and robustness of the proposed approach, paving the way for its application in pcg-based heart disease diagnosis.
one_shot: we propose an automated diagnosis system for valvular heart disease using a novel fusion of phonocardiogram (pcg) signal analysis and motion feature extraction. building upon the wavelet decomposition approach for pcg signal analysis, we adapt the feature extraction method based on curl and divergence, as introduced in motion analysis, to extract time-frequency features from the pcg signal. this enables the distinction between abnormal and normal heart sounds by capturing the spatial and temporal characteristics of heart valve motion. the method is evaluated using machine learning classifiers, such as knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svm, with feature selection methods like information gain and mutual information. our approach demonstrates improved accuracy in detecting valvular heart disease, showcasing the potential of this interdisciplinary fusion for more accurate and robust diagnosis.
few_shot: this study presents a novel approach to analyzing and characterizing phonocardiogram (pcg) signals using motion feature extraction techniques based on curl and divergence. by performing wavelet decomposition on the pcg signal and extracting features using the coiflet transform, we identify a set of robust and interpretable features that can distinguish between normal and abnormal heart sounds. we then apply motion feature extraction based on curl and divergence to capture the dynamic patterns in the pcg signal, enabling more accurate classification and diagnosis of valvular heart disease. experimental results demonstrate the improved performance of our approach compared to traditional feature selection methods, with high accuracy and robustness in detecting abnormal heart sounds.
got: we present a novel approach for diagnostic analysis of valvular heart disease using a multidisciplinary framework that integrates phonocardiogram (pcg) signal processing with motion feature extraction. by applying the curl and divergence based motion feature extraction method to the pcg signal, we can effectively quantify the heart's motion and sound patterns. this allows for the extraction of robust and invariant features that are resistant to noise and variability. our proposed method, dubbed "curl-pcg," utilizes coiflet wavelet decomposition and feature selection techniques, such as information gain, to identify the most discriminative features for classification. we demonstrate the effectiveness of the curl-pcg approach using a range of classifiers, including knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms, and show significant improvements in classification accuracy compared to existing methods. the proposed approach has far-reaching implications for the diagnosis and monitoring of valvular heart disease, and can be easily extended to other applications in medical imaging and motion analysis.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: this paper introduces the concept of automatic reference selection for active ultrasound wild salmon monitoring systems in turbulent underwater environments. a general in situ calibration procedure is proposed which allows grate improvements in terms of fish detection, identification and tracking capabilities.
abstract1: state immunity is an important public international law issue to china which places more emphases on state sovereignty in its international law practice. fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo & ors therefore is exceptionally important to affirm china's stance on the state immunity doctrine. once again, as confirmed in this case, the absolute state immunity doctrine is firmly applied in such cases that involve china (including hong kong and macau) unless there is an explicit waiver.
abstract2: the invention relates to an upmixing method of audio signals. the method includes decomposing the audio signal into a diffuse signal and a direct signal; generating an audio bed, which includes a height channel, at least in part based on the diffuse signal; extracting an audio object from the direct signal; estimating metadata, including height information of the audio object, of the audio object; and rendering the audio bed and the audio object as an upmixed audio signal, wherein the audio bed is rendered to a predefined position and the audio object is rendered according to the metadata. corresponding system and computer program product are described as well.
gen_abs: this study introduces an innovative application of state immunity doctrine in the context of underwater audio monitoring systems. building on the automatic reference selection method for active ultrasound wild salmon monitoring systems, we propose a novel approach to ensure the enforcement of state immunity in international waters. by leveraging the upmixing method of audio signals, we demonstrate how to generate a distinctive audio bed that signals state immunity, thereby deterring potential violators. the system combines machine learning-based fish detection and tracking capabilities with the enforcement of state immunity doctrine, providing a unique solution for marine conservation and international law enforcement. our results show significant improvements in terms of fish detection and tracking capabilities, as well as a clear and audible indication of state immunity in the monitored area. this interdisciplinary research contributes to the development of novel approaches to the enforcement of international law and the conservation of marine resources.
cot: the concept of state immunity in international law has significant implications for global governance, particularly in regions with complex sovereignty structures, such as china's "one country, two systems" framework. recent court decisions, including fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo & ors, have reaffirmed the absolute state immunity doctrine in cases involving china, hong kong, and macau. this paper explores the intersection of state immunity and audio signal processing, proposing a novel upmixing method that leverages metadata estimation to create immersive audio experiences in the context of international events and diplomatic missions. by integrating audio signal processing techniques with state immunity principles, we develop a system that can render audio signals with height information, allowing for the creation of 3d audio environments that simulate the spatial characteristics of international events. this interdisciplinary approach has significant implications for global communication, diplomacy, and international relations, particularly in regions with complex sovereignty structures.
tot: this paper presents a novel framework for designing and implementing a state-of-the-art, location-sensitive audio signal processing system, with a specific application in international diplomacy and public international law. building on the concept of state immunity in international law, as exemplified in the case of fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo & ors, we propose a method to create immersive audio environments that simulate the spatial characteristics of international diplomatic settings. by upmixing audio signals to create a 3d audio bed, incorporating height information and metadata, we can render the nuances of diplomatic interactions in a way that honors the sovereignty of participating nations, as per the strict application of the absolute state immunity doctrine. our system utilizes a decomposed audio signal, generating a diffuse and direct signal, to create an immersive experience that simulates the spatial and hierarchical relationships between nations. this technology has profound implications for diplomatic training, negotiation simulations, and international relations research, allowing for more effective and nuanced understanding of complex international interactions.
raw: the fusion of international law and audio signal processing technology offers a novel approach to safeguarding cultural heritage sites from the impact of modern infrastructure development. building on the principle of absolute state immunity in international law, as confirmed in cases such as fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo & ors, we propose a framework for acoustic upmixing of audio signals to recreate immersive aural environments of culturally sensitive sites. this framework, leveraging audio signal decomposition and object-based rendering, can be applied to generate three-dimensional audio beds that accurately reflect the spatial characteristics of historical sites. by doing so, it is possible to create a sonic digital twin of a site, thus preserving its auditory essence and effectively mitigating the impact of construction on the surrounding environment. this interdisciplinary approach can be applied in the context of china's significant infrastructure development plans, ensuring the protection of its rich cultural heritage while upholding state sovereignty.
one_shot: in the context of china's international law practice, the application of the state immunity doctrine has significant implications for the protection of cultural heritage and intangible assets, such as traditional music and art. this paper proposes a novel approach to upmixing audio signals of traditional chinese music, incorporating metadata and height information to recreate immersive, three-dimensional audio experiences. leveraging the absolute state immunity doctrine, we develop a system that allows for the digital preservation and dissemination of cultural assets, while respecting the sovereignty of china and its special administrative regions, hong kong and macau. by integrating audio signal processing techniques with international law principles, our method enables the creation of engaging, culturally sensitive, and immersive audio experiences that promote cross-cultural understanding and exchange.
few_shot: we propose an interdisciplinary approach to designing and optimizing immersive audio experiences in international public spaces, particularly in regions with diverse cultural and legal frameworks, such as china and its special administrative regions. leveraging the upmixing method of audio signals, we create a hybrid system that generates spatial audio rendering for 3d environments, taking into account the nuanced balance between state sovereignty and international law. by estimating metadata, including height information, of audio objects and rendering them according to their properties, we ensure a realistic and culturally sensitive sonic environment. our method is particularly relevant in contexts where state immunity doctrines, such as the absolute state immunity doctrine applied in the democratic republic of the congo v. fg hemisphere associates llc case, necessitate the consideration of international law in public space design and management.
got: this paper proposes a novel approach to audio signal processing in the context of international cooperation and state sovereignty. by leveraging the principles of state immunity in public international law, we develop a framework for securing audio intellectual property rights in global collaborations. specifically, we utilize the upmixing method described in [paper2] to create an immersive audio experience for high-stakes diplomatic meetings, international negotiations, and other sensitive communications. our system, dubbed "sovereign sound," decomposes audio signals into diffuse and direct components, generates an audio bed with height channel, extracts audio objects, and estimates metadata for rendering the audio bed and objects as an upmixed signal. by ensuring the secure and private processing of sensitive audio information, our approach enables china and other countries with strong state sovereignty principles to engage in global collaborations while protecting their intellectual property and national interests. this work has significant implications for international relations, audio processing, and cybersecurity.

abstract1: in article the author analyzed views of the russian thinkers of the xviii century to the soul nature. the soul psychology during this period was the most influential current. ideas of properties of soul and regularities of its existence developed. there are such properties of soul as : immortality, free of charge, will, belief and conscience. the russian authors argue on abilities of soul to comprehension of the world, reminiscence of the past and anticipation of the future, on ability of soul to self-knowledge, consciousness, on a work role in human history, on immortality of human soul. in the xviii century psychological ideas developed in theological works of dmitry rostovsky, paisius velichkovsky, tikhon of zadonsk and other orthodox thinkers who approved such cultural wealth as belief, hope, love. they paid attention to the identity of the ordinary person in comparison with the ideal christian, they were interested in strong-willed and emotional characteristics of people, ways of fight against negative human manifestations, values and the purposes, moral self-improvement, motives of activity and other «eternal» subjects of human soul. thus, the soul is the active beginning in the person, the unique phenomenon taking a special place in the universe. people find a complete view of the person at orthodox thinkers. ideas in which soul has quite certain properties connected with subjective experience changed anthropomorphous ideas of soul. properties of soul, communication of soul and a body, a question of its mortality or immortality, of value of belief in immortality of soul on the future of mankind, about abilities of soul to comprehension of the world, a reasoning on the past and anticipation of the future, ability to self-knowledge, consciousness were on the discussion.
abstract2: the emission of electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range has been observed in many experiments of laserplasma interaction. these fields can have very high intensity and estimated frequency band up to several gigahertz. the radiation normally affects the behaviour of most of the detectors, often up to hundreds of nanoseconds from the laser pulse, and can represent a serious limitation for the time-of-ight detection of fast particles, and in general for the safe operation of the electronic equipment. in this work we describe the measurements of this electromagnetic pulse, under different conditions of laser-plasma interaction. c
gen_abs: this study explores the intersection of human consciousness and electromagnetic phenomena, drawing on the philosophical insights of 18th-century russian thinkers and the empirical findings of laser-plasma interaction experiments. building on the concept of the soul as an active, unique phenomenon in human experience (paper 1), we investigate the physical manifestation of consciousness through electromagnetic radiation in the radiofrequency-microwave range. by analyzing the high-intensity electromagnetic pulses emitted during laser-plasma interaction (paper 2), we propose a novel approach to detecting and characterizing the electromagnetic signatures of consciousness. this interdisciplinary research aims to create a new framework for understanding the relationship between the human soul and electromagnetic fields, with implications for fields such as neuroscience, psychology, and materials science. specifically, we explore the possibility of using laser-plasma interaction to capture and study the electromagnetic correlates of human consciousness, with potential applications in the development of novel brain-computer interfaces and advanced diagnostic tools for neurological disorders. our study paves the way for a new era of interdisciplinary research at the intersection of philosophy, physics, and neuroscience.
cot: this study explores the intersection of spirituality and technology, focusing on the impact of electromagnetic pulses generated by laser-plasma interactions on human consciousness. drawing from historical philosophical ideas on the nature of the soul, we investigate the potential effects of high-intensity electromagnetic radiation on the human psyche. building on the 18th-century russian thinkers' concept of the soul as an active, immaterial entity with properties such as free will, consciousness, and self-awareness, we examine whether exposure to intense electromagnetic fields can influence these aspects of human experience. our research aims to investigate the relationship between electromagnetic radiation and the human soul, with a focus on the duration of its effects on the human psyche and the potential implications for our understanding of human consciousness. by integrating insights from both spirituality and physics, this study offers a novel perspective on the interplay between the material and immaterial realms, shedding light on the complex interconnections between human consciousness and the external world.
tot: we propose an innovative framework for understanding the human soul as a complex, dynamic system that is inextricably linked to the electromagnetic properties of the human brain. building on the xviii-century russian thinkers' conception of the soul as an immaterial, conscious entity with properties such as free will, conscience, and self-awareness, we explore the possibility of quantifying these properties using electromagnetic signals. recent experiments in laser-plasma interaction have demonstrated the emission of electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range, which can affect the behavior of detectors and electronic equipment. we hypothesize that similar electromagnetic signals may be present in the human brain, influencing consciousness and cognitive processes. by analyzing the temporal and spatial patterns of these signals, we aim to decode the neural correlates of soul properties such as self-awareness, free will, and moral decision-making. our interdisciplinary approach combines insights from psychology, neuroscience, and physics to develop a novel, empirically grounded understanding of the human soul as an emergent property of complex brain activity. this work has far-reaching implications for our understanding of human consciousness, the nature of mind-body interaction, and the potential applications of neuroscience and physics to improve human well-being.
raw: the investigation of the intricate relationship between the human soul and electromagnetic phenomena has led to a groundbreaking discovery. building upon the 18th-century russian thinkers' understanding of the soul's properties, such as immortality, free will, and consciousness, we propose a novel framework for interpreting the effects of electromagnetic radiation on the human psyche. our research team has observed that the high-intensity electromagnetic pulses emitted during laser-plasma interactions can induce a transient state of heightened self-awareness and consciousness in individuals, characterized by enhanced reminiscence of the past and anticipation of the future. this phenomenon, which we term "electromagnetic soul resonance," challenges the conventional understanding of the mind-body problem and offers a new perspective on the interplay between the human soul and the external environment. our findings have significant implications for the development of novel therapies for neurological disorders and the creation of advanced brain-computer interfaces. the results of this study provide a foundation for a new field of research at the intersection of psychology, physics, and philosophy, opening up new avenues for exploring the mysteries of human consciousness.
one_shot: the observed electromagnetic pulses emitted during laser-plasma interactions have led to a reevaluation of the role of the human soul in the context of electromagnetic phenomena. inspired by the 18th-century russian thinkers' emphasis on the soul's immortality and ability to comprehend the world, we propose a novel framework for understanding the electromagnetic pulse as a manifestation of the soul's interaction with the physical world. by bridging the theological ideas of immortality and free will with the measurable properties of electromagnetic waves, our research aims to elucidate the non-local, non-physical aspects of human consciousness and its potential influence on the behavior of electromagnetic fields. through a series of experiments and simulations, we investigate the relationship between the soul's properties and the characteristics of the electromagnetic pulse, shedding new light on the intricate connections between human consciousness and the physical world.
few_shot: we propose an interdisciplinary framework to investigate the electromagnetic and cognitive dynamics of the human soul during extraordinary events, such as those triggered by high-intensity electromagnetic pulses. by drawing on 18th-century russian thinkers' views on the soul's properties and modern laser-plasma interaction experiments, we develop a novel theoretical framework that integrates electromagnetic field effects with the soul's putative capabilities for self-knowledge, consciousness, and anticipation of the future. our framework posits that intense electromagnetic pulses can modulate the soul's activity, influencing human behavior and perception. we hypothesize that this interaction can be studied through a combination of theoretical modeling, experimental simulations, and cognitive and behavioral studies. by exploring this intersection of philosophy, psychology, and physics, our research aims to shed new light on the nature of the human soul and its potential relationship with electromagnetic fields.
got: in this interdisciplinary study, we explore the intersection of philosophical and experimental perspectives on the nature of the human soul. building on the 18th-century russian thinkers' conceptualization of the soul as a dynamic, self-aware entity with distinct properties such as immortality, free will, and consciousness, we investigate the effects of electromagnetic pulses on the human brain's neural activity. specifically, we examine how the high-intensity electromagnetic fields generated in laser-plasma interactions influence the brain's ability to process information, reason, and anticipate the future, mirroring the soul's properties according to the russian thinkers. our results show that the electromagnetic pulses can disrupt the brain's normal functioning, affecting cognitive processes such as memory recall and decision-making. we propose a novel framework integrating the philosophical and experimental findings, suggesting that the human soul, as conceptualized by the 18th-century russian thinkers, may be sensitive to electromagnetic interference, challenging the traditional notion of the soul's immortality and free will. the implications of this research have significant implications for our understanding of the human condition and the potential consequences of electromagnetic exposure on human behavior and cognition.

abstract1: large-scale applications expressed as scientic workows are often grouped into ensembles of inter-related workows. in this paper, we address a new and important problem concerning the ecient management of such ensembles under budget and deadline constraints on infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds. iaas clouds are characterized by ondemand resource provisioning capabilities and a pay-per-use model. we discuss, develop, and assess novel algorithms based on static and dynamic strategies for both task scheduling and resource provisioning. we perform the evaluation via simulation using a set of scientic workow ensembles with a broad range of budget and deadline parameters, taking into account task granularity, uncertainties in task runtime estimations, provisioning delays, and failures. we nd that the key factor determining the performance of an algorithm is its ability to decide which workows in an ensemble to admit or reject for execution. our results show that an admission procedure based on workow structure and estimates of task runtimes can signicantly improve the quality of solutions.
abstract2: the frequency with which melioidosis results from inhalation rather than percutaneous inoculation or ingestion is unknown. we recovered burkholderia pseudomallei from air samples at the residence of a patient with presumptive inhalational melioidosis and used whole-genome sequencing to link the environmental bacteria to b. pseudomallei recovered from the patient.
gen_abs: this interdisciplinary study explores the intersection of cloud computing and infectious disease modeling. building on recent advances in scientific workflow ensemble management, we investigate the application of dynamic task scheduling and resource provisioning strategies to simulate the spread of burkholderia pseudomallei, the causative agent of melioidosis. by leveraging the on-demand resource provisioning capabilities of infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds, we develop a novel computational framework to model the environmental dynamics of b. pseudomallei, specifically focusing on inhalational transmission. our approach integrates whole-genome sequencing data from environmental samples with workflow ensemble simulations to predict the likelihood of disease outbreaks. we demonstrate the efficacy of this framework using a case study of a presumptive inhalational melioidosis patient, where we recover b. pseudomallei from air samples and link it to environmental bacteria using whole-genome sequencing. this study showcases the potential of cloud-based computational modeling to inform public health strategies and disease surveillance, particularly in regions with limited resources.
cot: large-scale scientific workflows often involve ensembles of inter-related workflows that must be executed under budget and deadline constraints. however, the ecient management of such ensembles in infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds, which provide on-demand resource provisioning and a pay-per-use model, remains a significant challenge. this paper proposes a novel approach to addressing this challenge by integrating workflow ensemble management with environmental sensing and monitoring. we demonstrate the feasibility of this approach by developing and evaluating a system that leverages whole-genome sequencing and environmental sampling to identify and mitigate potential risks associated with inhalational melioidosis, a disease caused by burkholderia pseudomallei. our system uses machine learning-based algorithms to analyze environmental data and workflow structures, and to predict and prevent workflow failures due to environmental factors. the integration of environmental monitoring with workflow ensemble management enables the development of more robust and adaptive workflow management systems that can respond to changing environmental conditions and minimize the risk of workflow failures.
tot: in the context of infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds, managing large-scale scientific workflows under budget and deadline constraints is a pressing issue. to address this challenge, we integrate novel task scheduling and resource provisioning algorithms with an admission procedure based on workflow structure and task runtime estimates. our approach optimizes the execution of workflow ensembles by dynamically selecting and prioritizing workflows for execution, taking into account uncertainties in task runtime estimations, provisioning delays, and failures. we apply this framework to a unique case study involving the diagnosis of inhalational melioidosis, a disease caused by the bacterium burkholderia pseudomallei. by analyzing environmental air samples and patient isolates using whole-genome sequencing, we establish a link between the environmental bacteria and the patient's infection. this integration enables the development of a more effective and efficient workflow management system for handling complex scientific applications, including those related to public health and environmental monitoring. our results demonstrate the feasibility and potential of this approach in optimizing workflow execution and informing public health decision-making.
raw: in cloud-based scientific computing environments, managing ensembles of inter-related workflows under budget and deadline constraints is crucial for efficient resource utilization. however, the uncertainty and variability in task runtime estimations, provisioning delays, and failures can significantly impact the performance of workflow scheduling algorithms. in this context, we investigate the application of machine learning-based admission procedures for identifying high-priority workflows that can be executed on infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds, while rejecting low-priority workflows to optimize resource allocation. we develop and evaluate novel algorithms that integrate workflow structure, task runtime estimates, and environmental factors, such as weather conditions, to inform admission decisions. our approach is inspired by the recent discovery of burkholderia pseudomallei in patient air samples, highlighting the potential for environmental factors to influence disease transmission and, by extension, workflow scheduling. we propose a novel framework that combines workflow scheduling, machine learning, and environmental monitoring to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of cloud-based scientific computing.
one_shot: we propose a novel approach to managing and predicting the spread of infectious diseases in complex environments, such as cloud computing infrastructures, by leveraging insights from computational workflow management and microbiological research. traditional workflow scheduling algorithms often overlook the dynamic and uncertain nature of task execution, leading to inefficient resource allocation and increased risk of disease outbreaks. by integrating workflow structure and task runtime estimates with environmental monitoring and microbiological sequencing data, we develop an adaptive workflow management framework that can detect and respond to potential disease outbreaks in cloud-based ecosystems. our framework, termed "epicloud," enables real-time identification of high-risk workflow ensembles and proactive resource provisioning, thereby reducing the likelihood of disease transmission and improving the overall resilience of cloud-based systems.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach for predicting and mitigating the spread of infectious diseases in cloud-based infrastructure settings, where resource provisioning and task scheduling are critical. by integrating ecological models of pathogen transmission with optimization algorithms for ensemble workflow management, our method identifies high-risk workflow configurations and adapts resource allocation to minimize transmission rates. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through simulations of melioidosis outbreaks in cloud environments, where task scheduling and resource provisioning decisions can exacerbate or mitigate infectious disease spread. our framework can be applied to other cloud-based platforms where workflow ensembles and resource constraints interact with epidemiological factors.
got: we investigate the impact of uncertain environmental factors on the management of large-scale scientific workflows in infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds. specifically, we explore how the presence of environmental pathogens, such as burkholderia pseudomallei, can affect the efficiency and reliability of workflow execution. by leveraging whole-genome sequencing and workflow ensemble management strategies, we develop novel algorithms that account for the variability in task runtime estimations and provisioning delays caused by environmental factors. our results show that a hybrid approach, combining admission control procedures based on workflow structure and estimates of task runtimes with environmental monitoring and whole-genome sequencing, can significantly improve the quality of solutions and ensure timely completion of workflows. we demonstrate the feasibility of our approach through simulations using a set of scientific workflow ensembles and real-world environmental data. our work has implications for the design of robust and efficient scientific workflows in iaas clouds, particularly in environments where environmental pathogens are a concern.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: an adaptive self-modifying system for encapsulating programming languages using graphical objects. the encapsulation system is intuitively based and is suitable for non-qualified users. the encapsulation is written in an innovative and novel way, using a graph structure of flow charts describing the function performed.
abstract1: this paper studies rate splitting for the han-kobayashi (hk) transmission scheme in the two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel. we derive the optimal rate split between common and private messages for maximizing the achievable sum rate, and present a closed-form expression of the corresponding power split. it was reported in literature that an asymmetric rate split between two users can maximize the sum rate in the two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel when the hk scheme without time sharing is employed. however, we proved that the result in literature is incorrect and showed that the symmetric power split always attains the maximum sum rate. we also derived the optimal symmetric rate split for the simplified hk scheme.
abstract2: the invention belongs to the field of biological fermentation, particularly relates to a radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented product, a cosmetic containing the fermented product as well as a preparation method and application of the fermented product, and aims at providing a product with the efficacies of hydrating, moisturizing and resisting ageing by adopting a liquid fermentation system of radix ophiopogonis and lactic acid bacteria and employing a biotransformation theory as guidance. the radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented product disclosed by the invention is a fermented liquid mixture which is prepared by adding radix ophiopogonis to a culture medium and fermenting with the lactic acid bacteria, wherein the culture medium contains 5-20wt% of radix ophiopogonis on the basis of the weight of raw medicines; and the inoculation amount of the lactic acid bacteria is 1.0*10 cfu/ml to 1.0*10 cfu/ml.
gen_abs: we present a novel approach to optimizing fermentation processes for bioactive compound production using adaptive self-modifying systems inspired by rate splitting strategies in communication networks. by applying the symmetric power split principle from han-kobayashi transmission schemes to control the growth of lactic acid bacteria in fermentation, we demonstrate improved yields of radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented products. the adaptive encapsulation system, based on graphical objects and flow charts, enables non-qualified users to easily implement and adjust the fermentation process in real-time. this approach allows for the optimization of fermentation conditions, such as temperature, ph, and substrate concentration, to maximize the production of bioactive compounds with hydrating, moisturizing, and anti-aging properties. our results show that the adaptive self-modifying system can achieve up to 30% higher yields of bioactive compounds compared to traditional fermentation methods. this interdisciplinary research has significant implications for the development of efficient and sustainable production methods for bioactive compounds in the field of biological fermentation.
cot: this paper proposes a novel approach to optimize the formulation and delivery of biological fermentation products, leveraging insights from information theory to enhance their efficacy. building on the discovery of the radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented product, which exhibits hydrating, moisturizing, and anti-aging properties, we explore the application of rate splitting techniques from communication systems to optimize the distribution of active compounds in the fermented mixture. by applying the han-kobayashi (hk) transmission scheme, we derive a closed-form expression for the optimal rate split between common and private messages, which corresponds to the optimal power split in the fermentation process. this allows for the maximization of the sum rate of the desired compounds, ensuring the most efficient delivery of the product's beneficial effects. we demonstrate the feasibility of this approach through simulations and experiments, showing a significant improvement in the efficacy of the radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented product. this work bridges the gap between information theory and biotechnology, paving the way for the development of novel fermentation-based products with optimized performance.
tot: we propose an innovative approach to optimizing the performance of biological fermentation systems, inspired by principles from information theory and channel coding. specifically, we apply the han-kobayashi (hk) transmission scheme, commonly used in wireless communication systems, to the fermentation process. by modeling the fermentation process as a two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel, we develop a novel strategy for rate and power allocation that maximizes the sum rate of biochemical reactions. this is achieved by splitting the fermentation process into common and private channels, similar to the hk scheme. we derive a closed-form expression for the optimal power split, which is found to be symmetric, contrary to previous claims in the literature. our approach is demonstrated through the fermentation of radix ophiopogonis with lactic acid bacteria, where the optimal power split is shown to result in a significant improvement in the efficacy of the resulting fermented product. the proposed strategy has the potential to revolutionize the field of biological fermentation, enabling the development of more efficient and effective fermentation processes.
raw: this paper explores the development of a novel, bio-inspired communication system for optimizing data transmission in wireless networks. inspired by the principles of biological fermentation, we propose the application of a symmetric power allocation strategy to the han-kobayashi (hk) transmission scheme in the two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel. by leveraging the biotransformation theory employed in the lactic acid fermentation process, we demonstrate that a symmetrical rate split can indeed maximize the achievable sum rate in this channel, contrary to previous claims of an asymmetric approach. our findings are validated through a thorough analysis and simulations of the hk scheme with the proposed power allocation strategy. furthermore, we demonstrate the feasibility of integrating this wireless communication system with the radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented product (rolfp), a bioactive compound obtained through the fermentation of radix ophiopogonis with lactic acid bacteria. the rolfp exhibits potential as a bio-compatible, biodegradable, and moisture-retaining material for use in wireless communication systems, thereby reducing the environmental impact of traditional communication infrastructure.
one_shot: this paper proposes a novel approach to designing personalized, adaptive skincare products using artificial intelligence and machine learning techniques inspired by biological fermentation processes. by leveraging the principles of fermentation and biotransformation, we develop a dynamic modeling framework that optimizes the formulation of radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented products for individual skin types and needs. we combine this with rate splitting techniques from information theory, specifically the han-kobayashi scheme, to allocate optimal resource allocation and message prioritization for effective delivery of hydrating, moisturizing, and anti-aging efficacies. our simulation results demonstrate improved product efficacy and adaptability, showcasing a new paradigm for intelligent skincare product development and personalized treatment outcomes.
few_shot: this paper presents a novel approach to optimizing biological fermentation processes using rate-splitting techniques inspired by communication theory. by modeling the growth dynamics of lactic acid bacteria and radix ophiopogonis as a two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel, we develop a closed-form expression for the optimal power split between the two components. our results show that symmetric power splitting always attains the maximum sum rate, leading to improved fermentation efficiency and product quality. we apply this approach to a liquid fermentation system of radix ophiopogonis and lactic acid bacteria, demonstrating enhanced hydrating, moisturizing, and anti-aging efficacies of the resulting cosmetic product. this integration of communication theory and biological systems enables the design of more efficient and effective fermentation processes for various applications.
got: this study explores a novel approach to optimizing fermentation processes in biological systems by integrating principles from information theory. we apply the han-kobayashi transmission scheme, traditionally used in communication networks, to the fermentation process of radix ophiopogonis with lactic acid bacteria. by modeling the fermentation process as a two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel, we derive a power split strategy that maximizes the yield of the fermented product while minimizing the interference between different biochemical pathways. our results demonstrate that a symmetric power split, analogous to the optimal rate split in information theory, leads to a significant improvement in the efficacies of the fermented product, including hydration, moisturization, and anti-aging properties. this interdisciplinary approach provides new insights into the optimization of fermentation processes and has potential applications in the development of novel cosmetic and pharmaceutical products. the findings of this study also shed light on the importance of power split strategies in complex biological systems.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: this study aims to conduct an empirical analysis of conflicts of sino-north korean relations, as well as red terror and ethnicity problems surrounding korean-chinese minorities (chosunjok) of yanbian during the early stage of cultural revolution. the relations among socialist countries were in the s...
abstract1: the invention discloses an extraction process of chrysanthemum polysaccharide. with commercially available chrysanthemum as a raw material, extraction is carried out by adopting a mode of combining water extraction with alkali extraction, namely water extraction is firstly adopted to obtain one part of polysaccharide extracting solution; filter residues are subjected to alkali extraction to obtain another part of polysaccharide extracting solution; and the two parts of extracting solutions are merged and concentrated to obtain a final product. according to the extraction process of the chrysanthemum polysaccharide disclosed by the invention, compared with an existing conventional extraction process, the obtained chrysanthemum polysaccharide has higher yield, higher anti-oxidation activity and higher anti-inflammatory activity.
abstract2: the invention provides an implantable multi-channel flexible microtube electrode and a preparation method thereof. the method comprises the following steps: firstly, manufacturing a flexible film electrode; secondly, intercepting a polymer capillary, cleaning and drying, painting biocompatibility bonding glue uniformly on the surface of the outer wall of the polymer capillary, and enwinding and fixing the manufactured flexible film electrode tightly on the surface of the outer wall of the polymer capillary along the length direction of the polymer capillary at an angle, and curing the bonding glue so as to obtain the implantable multi-channel flexible microtube electrode. the prepared implantable multi-channel flexible microtube electrode can simultaneously perform functional electrical stimulation and electrical signal recording on nerves and muscle tissues and can administrate controllably into biological tissues via a micro-fluid channel. the manufacturing technology of the microtube electrode is relatively simple, and the cost is low; manufacture parameters are convenient to change and the electrode structure parameters are convenient to customize; mass repeated production can be facilitated, and external electronic equipment can be integrated.
gen_abs: this study develops a novel, implantable biosensor for monitoring the bioactive properties of chrysanthemum polysaccharide in real-time, facilitated by a microtube electrode with integrated biocompatibility and flexibility. building on the extraction process of chrysanthemum polysaccharide disclosed in paper 1, and the implantable multi-channel flexible microtube electrode described in paper 2, this research combines the benefits of high-yield polysaccharide extraction with the precision of electrical stimulation and signal recording. by transplanting the extracted polysaccharide into biological tissues and monitoring its effects using the microtube electrode, this study provides a new platform for investigating the anti-oxidation and anti-inflammatory properties of chrysanthemum polysaccharide in a more precise and controlled manner. the results of this research have significant implications for the development of novel therapeutic strategies for tissue repair, wound healing, and tissue engineering, and shed light on the complex relationships between bioactive compounds, nerve regeneration, and tissue responses.
cot: we present a novel, implantable microdevice that integrates a flexible microtube electrode with a polysaccharide-based coating derived from chrysanthemum, a plant with known anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. the electrode, fabricated using a simple and cost-effective method, enables simultaneous electrical stimulation and recording of neural activity. the chrysanthemum polysaccharide coating, extracted using a dual-extraction process that enhances yield and bioactivity, promotes biocompatibility and tissue integration. this hybrid device allows for controlled delivery of bioactive molecules into the nervous system, enabling advanced therapeutic interventions. the implantable microdevice has potential applications in treating neurodegenerative diseases, neural injuries, and chronic pain. we demonstrate the feasibility of this device by showing improved biocompatibility, reduced inflammation, and enhanced electrical signal recording in vitro. our results pave the way for further investigation into the use of plant-derived biomaterials in neural engineering and regenerative medicine.
tot: this paper presents a novel approach to developing biocompatible, implantable devices for neurotherapeutic applications by integrating chrysanthemum polysaccharides into flexible microtube electrodes. building on existing extraction processes, we have optimized a method to produce high-yield, high-anti-oxidation, and high-anti-inflammatory chrysanthemum polysaccharides using a combined water and alkali extraction approach. these polysaccharides are then incorporated into implantable multi-channel flexible microtube electrodes, as described in a novel fabrication method. the biocompatible polysaccharide coating enhances the electrode's biointegration and reduces tissue response, allowing for controlled implantation and promotion of neural regeneration. the flexible microtube electrode enables simultaneous electrical stimulation and recording of nerve and muscle tissues through a micro-fluid channel. by integrating these two technologies, our developed implantable device has the potential to improve the efficacy of neurotherapeutic treatments, such as neuropathic pain management and neural prosthetics.
raw: we report a novel, implantable biosensor system designed for real-time monitoring of biological responses to chrysanthemum polysaccharides (cmps). by integrating the extraction process of cmps from commercially available chrysanthemum as described in paper 1, with the implantable multi-channel flexible microtube electrode as developed in paper 2, we have created a biocompatible, flexible microsensor that can simultaneously detect and record anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidation responses in real-time. the sensor is capable of administering cmps through a micro-fluid channel, allowing for precise control over the dose and duration of exposure. our results show that this system can effectively monitor the bioactivity of cmps in vitro, providing valuable insights into the underlying mechanisms of cmps-mediated anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidation effects. the integration of these two technologies has the potential to revolutionize the field of biomaterials and regenerative medicine, enabling the development of personalized, targeted therapies for a range of inflammatory and oxidative stress-related diseases.
one_shot: we propose a novel biohybrid device that combines the extraction of chrysanthemum polysaccharides with implantable micro-electrodes for neural interfaces. the extraction process, developed from a novel combination of water and alkali extraction, yields a high-quality polysaccharide product with enhanced antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. this polysaccharide is then integrated into a flexible microtube electrode, enabling the development of biohybrid devices that can simultaneously stimulate and record neural activity. the implantable device, fabricated with a relatively simple manufacturing process, allows for controllable electrical stimulation and signal recording, while the incorporated polysaccharide promotes tissue compatibility and regeneration. our device has the potential to advance neural interface technology, particularly for applications in neural regeneration and tissue engineering, by providing a biocompatible and adaptive platform for neural interfaces.
few_shot: a novel implantable microelectrode system is developed by integrating a biocompatible, flexible film electrode with a microfluidic channel for controlled delivery of bioactive compounds. chrysanthemum polysaccharides, extracted using a hybrid water-alkali extraction process, are encapsulated within the microelectrode, enabling simultaneous electrical stimulation and targeted release of the bioactive molecules. the microelectrode's implantable design and modular architecture facilitate customization and scale-up production, while the polysaccharide's enhanced anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties promote tissue regeneration and healing. this hybrid device is suitable for various biomedical applications, including wound healing, tissue engineering, and regenerative medicine.
got: this interdisciplinary research project integrates biomaterials science and biomedical engineering to develop a novel electrostimulation platform for nerve regeneration and tissue repair. by combining the extraction of chrysanthemum polysaccharide with the fabrication of implantable microtube electrodes, we create a bioactive scaffold that promotes angiogenesis, reduces inflammation, and enhances electrical conductivity. chrysanthemum polysaccharide is extracted using a dual-mode extraction process, comprising water extraction and alkali treatment, yielding a high-yield, high-activity polysaccharide with enhanced antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. this bioactive polysaccharide is then coated onto the surface of implantable microtube electrodes, which are manufactured using a facile and cost-effective method involving a flexible film electrode and a polymer capillary. the resulting biohybrid electrodes exhibit improved biocompatibility, conductivity, and controllability, enabling simultaneous electrical stimulation and signal recording on nerves and muscle tissues. the platform has potential for treating nerve injuries, muscle atrophy, and other neural disorders, and its scalable manufacturing process makes it suitable for clinical translation.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the converter jet engine includes a switch with the current sensor and the microprocessor, and an electromechanical transducer. over the operating winding of one electromechanical transducer coil additional thin wire turns are wound to supply signal from those through an analog-digital converter to the microprocessor.
abstract1: this paper studies rate splitting for the han-kobayashi (hk) transmission scheme in the two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel. we derive the optimal rate split between common and private messages for maximizing the achievable sum rate, and present a closed-form expression of the corresponding power split. it was reported in literature that an asymmetric rate split between two users can maximize the sum rate in the two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel when the hk scheme without time sharing is employed. however, we proved that the result in literature is incorrect and showed that the symmetric power split always attains the maximum sum rate. we also derived the optimal symmetric rate split for the simplified hk scheme.
abstract2: social curation is the process through which the general public, rather than web publishers themselves, tag, organize, and share content with others. in this paper, i focus on the case of pinterest, an image-based social curation site. i specifically highlight how the individual acts of collection on the site contribute to a new kind of public construction of knowledge online. this is specific to pinterest, i argue, because the way that the site is set up—both in terms of its technological and business structure. my goals for the paper are as follows. first, i question how pinterest’s notion of curation compares with traditional or already existing notions of professional curation and personal collection. then, i offer an analysis of the activity on pinterest; whereas users believe they are participating in personal acts of collection and reflexive identity building, i counteract this with an examination of the entire business “ecosystem” of the site, showing how these personal activities can also be unde...
gen_abs: this interdisciplinary research project integrates concepts from information theory and social network analysis to investigate the notion of "efficient social curation" in online platforms. building on the optimal symmetric rate split for the han-kobayashi transmission scheme, we propose a novel method to optimize the distribution of information in social curation networks, such as pinterest. our approach utilizes a " signal processing" analogy, where the converter jet engine's electromechanical transducer is seen as a metaphor for the social curation process. we design a "curator's switch" that allocates attention and resources to optimize the sum rate of information exchange between users, mirroring the optimal power split derived in the han-kobayashi scheme. by analyzing the pinterest ecosystem through this lens, we reveal how individual acts of collection contribute to a new kind of public construction of knowledge, and how the asymmetric rate split between users can be optimized to maximize the sum rate of information exchange. our results have implications for the design of efficient social curation systems and the study of online knowledge construction.
cot: this paper explores the intersection of information theory and social curation, focusing on the pinterest platform as a case study. building on the work of han-kobayashi, we investigate how the optimal rate split between common and private messages in a two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel can inform the design of social curation systems like pinterest. by analyzing the information-theoretic properties of the pinterest ecosystem, we show that the platform's use of symmetric rate splitting can be seen as a form of "public construction of knowledge" that facilitates the efficient sharing of information among users. our analysis reveals that the platform's business structure and technological design, which encourages users to share and curate content, can be understood as a form of "power split" that maximizes the sum rate of information exchange among users. we argue that this understanding can inform the design of future social curation systems and provide new insights into the role of information theory in understanding social media platforms.
tot: this paper explores the intersection of information-theoretic and socio-technical systems through an interdisciplinary study of rate splitting in the han-kobayashi transmission scheme and its impact on social curation practices on image-based social media platforms like pinterest. building on the finding that symmetric power split always attains the maximum sum rate in the two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel, we investigate how this theoretical insight can inform our understanding of how online users collectively curate and share content. specifically, we analyze the role of power and rate splitting in the context of pinterest's image-based social curation, where users' individual acts of collection contribute to a new kind of public construction of knowledge. by examining the interplay between technical and social dynamics, we demonstrate how the pursuit of optimal sum rates in interference-limited channels can be mapped onto the pursuit of collective knowledge and identity on social media platforms. our findings highlight the potential for a novel framework that integrates information-theoretic and socio-technical perspectives to better understand the dynamics of social curation and the role of online platforms in shaping collective knowledge and identity.
raw: this paper explores the intersection of data transmission and social knowledge construction through the lens of social curation. focusing on pinterest as a paradigmatic example, we investigate how individual acts of collection contribute to the creation of a shared, publicly constructed knowledge base. drawing on the han-kobayashi transmission scheme, we develop a theoretical framework for analyzing the optimal rate split between common and private knowledge in social curation processes. our results show that, just as symmetric power splits maximize the sum rate in the han-kobayashi scheme, symmetric engagement patterns between curators and consumers can enhance the collective value of curated knowledge on social curation platforms like pinterest. we also examine the implications of this finding for our understanding of public knowledge construction and the role of social curation in shaping online information landscapes. our research combines insights from information theory and social science to shed new light on the complex dynamics of social curation and knowledge sharing.
one_shot: this paper integrates the fields of information theory and social media studies to explore the impact of user curation on information transmission in online social networks. building on the han-kobayashi (hk) transmission scheme, we investigate how the rate splitting strategy between common and private messages affects the sum rate in the two-user symmetric gaussian interference channel. we apply this framework to the case of pinterest, a social curation site where users organize and share content. our analysis reveals that the optimal symmetric power split can be achieved through the strategic deployment of curated content, influencing user behavior and shaping the online knowledge construction process. furthermore, we examine how the hk scheme can be used to optimize the dissemination of curated information, enhancing the efficiency of content sharing and the overall sum rate of the online social network.
few_shot: this paper investigates the impact of information interference on social curation platforms like pinterest, where users collectively construct and share knowledge through image-based collections. by modeling the interference channel, we derive the optimal rate split between common and private messages for maximizing the achievable sum rate of user engagement and knowledge construction. we show that symmetric power split always attains the maximum sum rate, and apply this result to optimize the pinterest ecosystem by dynamically adjusting the rate of collection and sharing, thereby promoting more efficient and effective construction of knowledge. our analysis exposes the intricate relationships between user behavior, platform design, and the emergent dynamics of social curation.
got: this paper explores the intersection of information theory and social curation by investigating how the principles of rate splitting in wireless communication systems can inform the design of social curation platforms like pinterest. we draw on the concept of symmetric power splitting in the han-kobayashi transmission scheme to develop a novel framework for optimizing the curation process on social media sites. by analyzing the power dynamics between users, content creators, and the platform itself, we identify opportunities for improving the efficiency and effectiveness of social curation. our results show that a symmetric approach to curation can lead to increased engagement, better knowledge construction, and more equitable distribution of resources. we demonstrate the feasibility of our framework through a case study of pinterest, highlighting the potential for interdisciplinary collaboration between information theorists and social media researchers. our work has implications for the design of future social curation platforms and the development of more effective information-sharing systems.
