abstract1: poster presentation background asthma is one of the leading chronic medical problems exacerbated during pregnancy, and this condition affects approximately 3% to 8% of the pregnant population. the health care team needs to be educated on factors that may trigger an exacerbation of asthma in pregnancy, appropriate management, treatment techniques to share with their patients, and the complications that an exacerbation of maternal asthma may pass on to the newborn. the goal for all asthmatic patients is to remain asymptomatic during pregnancy to assist in improving maternal and newborn outcomes. case a 30‐year‐old, g4p1 woman at 27 weeks gestation was transported by air to our large level‐iii mid atlantic trauma center from a satellite medical emergency department with status asthmaticus for further management. presenting with possible influenza virus, her history included asthma, positive cigarette use, and a recent respiratory infection. her respiratory status worsened upon arrival, and she exhibited severe hypoxemia and required intubation. deterioration in maternal and newborn status prompted obstetricians to make the decision to deliver the infant via cesarean to improve the outcomes for mother and newborn. unfortunately, the mother did not survive the recovery phase. this event indicated a need for the health care team to refresh their knowledge on current triggers, treatment and management techniques, and the neonatal complications related to exacerbation of asthma during pregnancy. conclusion asthma is a chronic medical condition that complicates pregnancy. roughly one third of the symptoms of pregnant women improve, one third remain the same, and one third worsen. health care providers need to diligently monitor their patients for signs and symptoms of exacerbation. management strategies should focus on identifying risk factors, educating the patient and the health care team, and improving communication skills to guarantee successful outcomes for mother and fetus.
abstract2: cinematographic games are a rising genre in the computer games industry and an increasing number of titles published include some aspects of cinematography in the gameplay or the storytelling. at present state, camera handling in computer games is managed primarily through custom scripts and animations and there is an inverse relationship between player freedom and cinematographic quality. in this paper, we present a description of a series of technical challenges connected with the development of an automatic camera control library for computer games and we showcase a set of algorithmic and engineering solutions.
gen_abs: we present a novel approach to developing an interactive, educationally focused, asthma management system for pregnant women, leveraging the principles of cinematography and game design. by integrating an automatic camera control library, our system enables immersive, personalized experiences that simulate real-world asthma scenarios, fostering empathy and understanding among healthcare providers and expectant mothers. the system's engaging narrative and interactive gameplay encourage users to explore and learn about asthma triggers, management techniques, and the potential complications of uncontrolled asthma in pregnancy. our approach draws on the theoretical perspectives of healthy families and cooking programs, emphasizing the importance of family-centered education and participation. we demonstrate the system's effectiveness in improving knowledge and attitudes towards asthma management among healthcare providers and pregnant women, ultimately contributing to better maternal and newborn outcomes. this interdisciplinary research project has significant implications for the development of accessible, engaging healthcare education and training tools.
cot: asthma is a chronic medical condition that complicates pregnancy, affecting approximately 3% to 8% of pregnant women. despite its prevalence, asthma management during pregnancy remains a significant challenge. traditional approaches to asthma management rely heavily on manual scripts and animations, limiting player freedom and cinematographic quality in patient education and simulation-based training. this paper presents a novel framework for developing interactive, data-driven asthma management tools that integrate cinematographic gameplay and simulation-based training. our approach leverages a custom-built camera control library to create immersive, realistic environments that simulate the physiological responses of asthma during pregnancy. the library is based on a set of algorithmic and engineering solutions that enable automatic camera control, allowing for real-time data-driven simulations of asthma exacerbation and management. by seamlessly integrating cinematographic gameplay with data-driven simulations, our framework enables healthcare providers to educate patients and improve communication skills, ultimately leading to better maternal and newborn outcomes. this innovative approach has the potential to revolutionize asthma management during pregnancy and improve patient outcomes.
tot: asthma exacerbations during pregnancy pose a significant threat to maternal and fetal health, with up to 8% of pregnant women affected. currently, healthcare teams rely on manual monitoring and treatment approaches, which may not account for the unique complexities of maternal and fetal physiology. to address this challenge, we propose the development of a novel, automated asthma management system for pregnant women, leveraging insights from cinematographic games. by applying cinematography-inspired algorithms for dynamic camera control, we can create immersive, data-driven visualizations of patient vital signs and lung function, enabling real-time monitoring and predictive modeling of asthma exacerbation risk. this innovative approach can facilitate early intervention and personalized treatment, reducing the risk of maternal and fetal complications. our system will integrate machine learning algorithms with wearables and clinical data to provide a holistic view of patient status, empowering healthcare teams to make more informed decisions. by bridging the gap between asthma management and cinematographic game development, we aim to improve patient outcomes and reduce healthcare costs.
raw: the integration of cinematographic techniques into healthcare education and maternal asthma management presents a novel opportunity for improving patient outcomes. building on the existing knowledge of asthma's impact on pregnant women and the need for healthcare providers to refresh their knowledge, we propose the development of a virtual reality (vr) training platform that utilizes cinematographic principles to enhance asthma education and management. the platform, which we call "asthmacare," will incorporate an automatic camera control library to provide immersive and interactive learning experiences for healthcare providers. by leveraging the inverse relationship between player freedom and cinematographic quality, asthmacare will allow providers to practice patient care scenarios in a realistic and controlled environment, improving their understanding of asthma triggers, treatment techniques, and neonatal complications. our ultimate goal is to reduce the incidence of asthma exacerbations during pregnancy and improve maternal and newborn outcomes. the development of asthmacare has the potential to revolutionize the way healthcare providers are trained, and we outline the technical challenges and algorithmic solutions necessary to bring this vision to life.
one_shot: asthma exacerbations during pregnancy can have severe consequences for both mother and fetus. to mitigate these risks, healthcare providers require effective management strategies. this paper proposes an innovative approach by integrating cinematographic techniques from computer games into asthma management. we develop an interactive, immersive platform that utilizes automatic camera control and cinematic storytelling to educate patients and healthcare teams about asthma triggers, treatment options, and neonatal complications. by leveraging the engaging nature of cinematographic games, our platform aims to improve patient engagement, communication, and adherence to treatment plans. the platform's algorithmic and engineering solutions enable real-time monitoring and personalized feedback, facilitating more effective management of asthma exacerbations during pregnancy.
few_shot: asthma exacerbation during pregnancy poses significant risks to both maternal and fetal health, with 1 in 10 women experiencing worsening symptoms. current management strategies rely heavily on clinician expertise, with limited consideration for the role of environmental and social factors. to address this knowledge gap, we propose a novel approach that leverages automatic camera control technology from computer games to create immersive, interactive simulations for asthma education and management training. by applying cinematographic principles to personalized patient scenarios, our system enhances clinician-patient communication, risk assessment, and treatment planning, ultimately improving maternal and newborn outcomes. through a case study of a high-risk pregnancy, we demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach in reducing asthma-related complications and promoting successful health outcomes.
got: this interdisciplinary study proposes a novel approach to improving maternal and newborn outcomes in cases of asthma exacerbation during pregnancy by leveraging the principles of cinematography and game development. building on the challenges of camera control in computer games, we develop an automated camera system that can monitor and track patients with asthma in real-time, providing early warning signs of exacerbation. by integrating this system with a decision support tool, healthcare providers can receive timely alerts and recommendations for intervention, improving communication and collaboration between healthcare teams. the system's algorithmic and engineering solutions enable the identification of risk factors, patient education, and personalized treatment plans. our pilot study demonstrates the feasibility and effectiveness of this approach in reducing asthma-related complications and improving maternal and newborn outcomes. this innovative application of game development principles to healthcare has the potential to revolutionize the management of asthma in pregnancy, setting a new standard for patient care and outcomes.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: this article describes a programmable scanner for laser bathymetry. present scanners use a fixed pattern scan. a programmable scanner however offers many advantages regarding system performance and utility in that the sounding pattern and spot density can be chosen by the operator and optimized for the specific charting mission.
abstract1: abstract tagged jet measurements in high energy hadronic and nuclear reactions provide constraints on the energy and parton flavor origin of the parton shower that recoils against the tagging particle. such additional insight can be especially beneficial in illuminating the mechanisms of heavy flavor production in proton–proton collisions at the lhc and their modification in the heavy ion environment, which are not fully understood. with this motivation, we present theoretical results for isolated-photon-tagged and b-meson-tagged b-jet production at s n n = 5.1 tev for comparison to the upcoming lead–lead data. we find that photon-tagged b-jets exhibit smaller momentum imbalance shift in nuclear matter, and correspondingly smaller energy loss, than photon-tagged light flavor jets. our results show that b-meson tagging is most effective in ensuring that the dominant fraction of recoiling jets originate from prompt b-quarks. interestingly, in this channel the large suppression of the cross section is not accompanied by a significant momentum imbalance shift.
abstract2: a tablet terminal (10) comprises a touch panel (12) which functions as a display for displaying a screen on a front face of a main body (11), a touch sensor (31, 231) provided at a back or a side face of the main body of the tablet terminal (10), and a controller (44) which detects a contact to the touch sensor (31) using a contact detection signal which the touch sensor outputs and executes control assigned to a point where the contact is detected after the point where the contact is detected is changed to a non-contact state.
gen_abs: we propose a novel approach to track the energy and flavor origin of parton showers in heavy ion collisions by employing a programmable 3d scanner for laser bathymetry, inspired by the principles of programmable scanners in tablet terminals. the scanner's programmable pattern and spot density can be optimized to track the momentum imbalance and energy loss of recoiling jets in lead-lead collisions at the lhc, allowing for a more precise understanding of heavy flavor production mechanisms. by integrating the scanner's data with b-meson tagging, we can efficiently identify and characterize the flavor origin of recoiling jets, providing crucial insight into the quark-gluon plasma formation and properties. the scanner's ability to detect contact and execute control at specific points enables real-time data analysis and correction, reducing systematic uncertainties in the measurement. this innovative approach offers a new avenue for exploring the complex dynamics of heavy ion collisions and paves the way for further studies in this field.
cot: we propose a novel approach to enhance the efficiency and precision of reconstructing jet substructure in high-energy particle collisions using a tablet-based, touch-sensitive interface. by leveraging the principles of particle tracking and jet reconstruction, we design a tablet terminal that can accurately detect the position and momentum of charged particles, analogous to the role of jet tags in high-energy physics. in this setup, the touch sensor acts as a proxy for the jet tag, allowing for real-time tracking of particle trajectories and momentum imbalance. this innovative system enables the isolation of prompt b-quarks in heavy-ion collisions, where the presence of nuclear matter can significantly impact the jet's energy loss and momentum imbalance. our results show that the tablet-based approach can distinguish between photon-tagged and b-meson-tagged jets, providing novel insights into the mechanisms of heavy flavor production in proton-proton and heavy-ion collisions. this interdisciplinary collaboration has the potential to revolutionize the field of particle physics and inspire new applications in real-time tracking and data analysis.
tot: we propose a novel application of tablet terminals as real-time data acquisition systems in high-energy particle physics experiments, specifically in proton-proton and heavy ion collision studies at the lhc. by leveraging the touch-sensitive display and sensor technology of tablet terminals, we can enhance the precision and efficiency of jet tagging in the analysis of hadronic and nuclear reactions. our approach enables the simultaneous recording of particle trajectories and timing information, allowing for the measurement of subtle momentum shifts and energy loss in recoiling jets. we demonstrate that this technology can be used to optimize the identification of heavy flavor production in proton-proton collisions and its modification in the heavy ion environment. furthermore, the use of tablet terminals as data acquisition systems offers the potential for real-time monitoring and control of experimental conditions, enabling more precise and dynamic analysis of complex particle interactions. our findings open a new avenue for the application of touch-sensitive technology in high-energy physics research, enhancing the precision and understanding of particle showers and their interactions.
raw: in high-energy hadronic and nuclear reactions, the identification of heavy flavor jets, such as b-mesons, is crucial for understanding the complex mechanisms of particle production and energy loss in the presence of nuclear matter. we propose a novel approach to enhance the detection of b-mesons by utilizing a tablet terminal-based touch sensor system to track the position and momentum of the recoiling jet. our tablet terminal system, consisting of a touch panel, touch sensor, and controller, allows for real-time monitoring of the jet's energy loss and momentum imbalance shift as it interacts with the nuclear environment. by integrating this technological advancement with theoretical models of heavy ion collisions, we aim to improve the accuracy of b-meson tagging and provide new insights into the modification of heavy flavor production in heavy ion environments. this interdisciplinary research project has the potential to revolutionize our understanding of high-energy particle interactions and their applications in nuclear physics and beyond.
one_shot: we investigate the application of isolated-photon-tagged and b-meson-tagged b-jet production in the context of high-energy hadronic and nuclear reactions, such as those occurring in proton–proton collisions at the lhc and heavy-ion environments. theoretical results show that b-meson tagging can ensure that the dominant fraction of recoiling jets originate from prompt b-quarks, with implications for heavy flavor production mechanisms. to enhance the accuracy and precision of these measurements, we propose the integration of a novel, touch-sensitive display technology into the detector. this tablet-based interface enables real-time monitoring and control of detector settings, allowing for improved alignment and calibration of the tagging system. by combining state-of-the-art jet physics with cutting-edge display technologies, our approach offers a unique opportunity to deepen our understanding of heavy flavor production and its modification in dense nuclear matter.
few_shot: we develop an innovative approach that combines the precision of particle physics with the interface capabilities of tablet terminals to enhance the monitoring and control of particle shower processes in high-energy collisions. by integrating particle tracking and momentum imbalance analysis with touch-sensitive displays, our system enables real-time adjustments of experimental parameters during data collection. this real-time feedback loop improves the accuracy of b-meson tagging and photon-tagged jet measurements, allowing for more precise constraints on the parton shower origin and flavor composition. specific applications include optimizing the detection of heavy flavor production in proton-proton and heavy ion collisions, reducing the momentum imbalance shift in nuclear matter, and enhancing the usability of tablet terminals in high-energy physics experiments through more intuitive and efficient data analysis interfaces.
got: we propose a novel approach to enhance the precision of particle jet measurements in high-energy hadronic and nuclear reactions by leveraging advancements in touch-sensitive technologies. building on the concept of isolated-photon-tagged jets, we develop a tablet-based system that utilizes a touch-sensitive interface to track the momentum imbalance of jets recoiling against tagging particles. the tablet's touch-sensitive technology enables real-time detection of contact and non-contact states, allowing for the measurement of jet properties with unprecedented precision. by applying this technology to b-meson-tagged jets, we demonstrate the ability to identify and isolate prompt b-quarks in proton-proton collisions at the lhc, even in the presence of heavy ion environments. our results show that the touch-sensitive system exhibits smaller energy loss and momentum imbalance shift compared to traditional methods, providing new insights into the mechanisms of heavy flavor production. this innovative approach has the potential to revolutionize the field of particle physics and open new avenues for experimental research.

abstract2: the characteristics of adsorption, desorption, and diffusion of gas in tectonic coal are important for the prediction of coal and gas outbursts. three types of coal samples, of which both metamorphic grade and degree of damage is different, were selected from tongchun, qilin, and pingdingshan mines. using a series of experiments in an electrostatic field, we analyzed the characteristics of gas adsorption and diffusion in tectonic coal. we found that gas adsorption in coal conforms to the langmuir equation in an electrostatic field. both the depth of the adsorption potential well and the coal molecular electronegativity increases under the action of an electrostatic field. a joule heating effect was caused by changing the coal–gas system conductivity in an electrostatic field. the quantity of gas adsorbed and dp result from competition between the depth of the adsorption potential well, the coal molecular electronegativity, and the joule heating effect. dp peaks when the three factors control behavior equally. compared with anthracite, the impact of the electrostatic field on the gas diffusion capacity of middle and high rank coals is greater. compared with the original coal, the gas adsorption quantity, dp, and the gas diffusion capacity of tectonic coal are greater in an electrostatic field. in addition, the smaller the particle size of tectonic coal, the larger the dp.
gen_abs: this interdisciplinary study explores the intersection of social curation and gas adsorption in tectonic coal. building on the concept of social curation as a process of collective knowledge construction, as exemplified by image-based platforms like pinterest, we investigate the potential for machine learning algorithms to automate the selection of soundings in coal seams. by analyzing the characteristics of gas adsorption and diffusion in tectonic coal, as studied in the context of electrostatic fields, we identify key factors influencing gas adsorption and desorption. we propose the development of a novel computer-aided system for selecting soundings in coal seams, leveraging machine learning to optimize the process based on the langmuir equation and the joule heating effect. this approach has the potential to improve the accuracy of coal and gas outburst predictions, while also providing new insights into the collective behavior of users on social curation platforms. our research contributes to a better understanding of the complex interplay between social and technical factors in the selection of soundings, and highlights the potential for interdisciplinary collaboration in addressing real-world challenges.
cot: this interdisciplinary study explores the intersection of social curation and coal gas dynamics, examining how the collective act of image-based curation on platforms like pinterest can be seen as analogous to the complex interactions between gas molecules and tectonic coal in an electrostatic field. by analyzing the ways in which users curate and share images, we can gain insight into the dynamic processes that govern the adsorption and diffusion of gas in coal. the study reveals that, just as the electrostatic field influences the gas diffusion capacity of coal, the social context of pinterest affects the creation and dissemination of knowledge. furthermore, the joule heating effect observed in coal gas systems is mirrored in the way that users' online interactions give rise to emergent patterns of collective behavior and knowledge construction. this research demonstrates that the principles governing gas dynamics in coal can be applied to the study of social systems, offering a novel framework for understanding the complex interactions between individuals, technology, and knowledge construction.
tot: in this interdisciplinary study, we investigate the intersection of social curation and gas adsorption in tectonic coal. we explore how the individual acts of collection on social curation platforms like pinterest contribute to a new kind of public construction of knowledge online, and examine how this phenomenon can be influenced by external factors such as electrostatic fields. our research reveals that the characteristics of gas adsorption in tectonic coal, including the depth of the adsorption potential well and coal molecular electronegativity, can be significantly altered by the application of an electrostatic field. we propose that the collective behavior of users on social curation platforms can impact the dynamics of gas adsorption and diffusion in tectonic coal, leading to changes in the quantity of gas adsorbed and the gas diffusion capacity. our findings have implications for the prediction of coal and gas outbursts, as well as the development of new strategies for managing and mitigating these hazards. by integrating insights from social science and physical engineering, our research sheds light on the complex interactions between human behavior, technology, and environmental systems.
raw: this paper explores the synergies between social curation and coal-gas interactions in tectonic environments. by integrating the concepts of social curation from pinterest with the gas adsorption and diffusion characteristics in tectonic coal, we reveal a novel framework for understanding the complex dynamics of coal-gas systems. our research demonstrates that the collective acts of collection and organization on pinterest, driven by user engagement and social interaction, can be mapped onto the adsorption and diffusion processes in tectonic coal. we show that the langmuir equation, which governs gas adsorption in coal, can be analogous to the process of social curation, where the depth of the adsorption potential well corresponds to the user's engagement with content and the coal molecular electronegativity reflects the site's organizational structure. by examining the characteristics of gas adsorption and diffusion in tectonic coal under the influence of an electrostatic field, we identify key factors that influence the behavior of gas in coal, including the joule heating effect and particle size. our findings have implications for the prediction of coal and gas outbursts and offer a new perspective on the social curation of knowledge online.
one_shot: this paper integrates the concepts of social curation and adsorption in tectonic coal to investigate the potential impact of human behavior on gas outbursts in coal mines. we develop a framework to analyze how online social curation platforms, such as pinterest, influence public knowledge construction and, in turn, human perception of complex geological phenomena like coal gas seepage. by examining the relationship between user-generated content and adsorption characteristics in tectonic coal, we demonstrate that the public's collective actions can lead to increased gas adsorption in coal, which may contribute to coal and gas outbursts. our findings highlight the interplay between human behavior, online platforms, and geological processes, and provide a new perspective on mitigating coal mine hazards through a better understanding of social curation and its effects on coal gas seepage.
few_shot: we investigate the intersection of social curation and geo-environmental processes by analyzing the impact of social media-driven content creation and sharing on gas outbursts in coal mines. leveraging the unique characteristics of the pinterest platform, where users curate and share content through a process of social construction of knowledge, we explore how this curation can influence the gas adsorption and diffusion in tectonic coal, a critical factor in predicting coal and gas outbursts. our results show that the electrostatic field generated by social media interactions can alter the gas adsorption potential well, coal molecular electronegativity, and joule heating effect, leading to increased gas adsorption and diffusion in tectonic coal. this research offers a new perspective on the complex relationships between social media-driven behaviors, geo-environmental processes, and human safety in mining operations.
got: the integration of social curation and geospatial data from coal mines reveals a novel approach to understanding the physical properties of tectonic coal. by leveraging the collective activity of social curation on platforms like pinterest, where users create and share collections of images, we can identify patterns and trends in the representation of coal mines and their associated environmental impacts. in this study, we apply machine learning algorithms to pinterest data to identify the relationships between user-generated content and the physical properties of coal samples from various mines. we find that the characteristics of gas adsorption, desorption, and diffusion in tectonic coal, as influenced by electrostatic fields, are closely tied to the social curation of coal-related content on pinterest. specifically, our analysis reveals that the depth of the adsorption potential well, coal molecular electronegativity, and joule heating effect are all reflected in the types and frequencies of images shared on pinterest, with smaller particle size tectonic coal samples exhibiting greater gas adsorption and diffusion capacity. this interdisciplinary approach has implications for the development of more accurate models of coal and gas outbursts, as well as the creation of more effective strategies for mitigating environmental impacts in coal mining regions.

abstract1: purpose: we propose to apply a probabilistic framework, namely chanceconstrained optimization, in the intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning subject to range and patient setup uncertainties. the purpose is to hedge against the influence of uncertainties and improve robustness of treatment plans. methods: impt plans were generated for a typical prostate patient. nine dose distributions are computed — the nominal one and one each for ±5mm setup uncertainties along three cardinal axes and for ±3.5% range uncertainty. these nine dose distributions are supplied to the solver cplex as chance constraints to explicitly control plan robustness under these representative uncertainty scenarios with certain probability. this probability is determined by the tolerance level. we make the chance-constrained model tractable by converting it to a mixed integer optimization problem. the quality of plans derived from this method is evaluated using dose-volume histogram (dvh) indices such as tumor dose homogeneity (d5% – d95%) and coverage (d95%) and normal tissue sparing like v70 of rectum, v65, and v40 of bladder. we also compare the results from this novel method with the conventional ptv-based method to further demonstrate its effectiveness results: our model can yield clinically acceptable plans within 50 seconds. the chance-constrained optimization produces impt plansmore » with comparable target coverage, better target dose homogeneity, and better normal tissue sparing compared to the ptv-based optimization [d95% ctv: 67.9 vs 68.7 (gy), d5% – d95% ctv: 11.9 vs 18 (gy), v70 rectum: 0.0 % vs 0.33%, v65 bladder: 2.17% vs 9.33%, v40 bladder: 8.83% vs 21.83%]. it also simultaneously makes the plan more robust [width of dvh band at d50%: 2.0 vs 10.0 (gy)]. the tolerance level may be varied to control the tradeoff between plan robustness and quality. conclusion: the chance-constrained optimization generates superior impt plan compared to the ptv-based optimization with explicit control of plan robustness. nih/nci k25ca168984, eagles cancer research career development, the lawrence w. and marilyn w. matteson fund for cancer research, mayo asu seed grant, and the kemper marley foundation.« less
abstract2: this article analyses the impact and the role of the internet in the policy of modern states. nowadays the internet has turned into a means with the help of which candidates can transmit their ideas to an incredibly wide audience. in the age of information technologies the internet is becoming an important communicative tool widely used by the governmental powers, political parties and leaders, and by representatives of the civil community in their interests including election campaigns. it is quite natural as recently there has been a stable increase in the number of internet users and today they already constitute a significant number of electors.
gen_abs: in the era of precision medicine, radiation therapy planning has become increasingly complex due to range and patient setup uncertainties. chance-constrained optimization has emerged as a promising approach to mitigate these uncertainties and improve treatment plan robustness. meanwhile, the internet has transformed the way governments, politicians, and citizens interact, with significant implications for public engagement and policy-making. this study bridges these two domains by applying chance-constrained optimization in radiation therapy planning to improve treatment outcomes, while also leveraging the internet to enhance public communication and engagement in healthcare policy-making. we develop a novel framework that integrates machine learning-based optimization with social media analytics to identify key stakeholders, track public sentiment, and inform radiation therapy planning. through a case study on prostate cancer treatment, we demonstrate the effectiveness of this integrated approach in generating robust treatment plans and improving public engagement in healthcare policy-making. our findings highlight the potential of this interdisciplinary approach to enhance the quality and accessibility of cancer care, while also promoting more informed and participatory healthcare policy-making.
cot: the increasing reliance on the internet in modern governance and healthcare has created opportunities for novel approaches to decision-making. this paper explores the intersection of internet-driven policy and medical treatment planning, with a focus on the development of probabilistic frameworks for robust treatment planning in intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt). building on the success of chance-constrained optimization in generating robust treatment plans, we investigate the use of social media analytics and online engagement metrics to inform the development of treatment plans that account for patient-specific variability and uncertainty. by leveraging insights from the internet-driven policy domain, we aim to improve the quality and robustness of impt plans, while also enhancing patient-centered care through personalized treatment planning. our research contributes to the growing field of computational medicine and highlights the potential for interdisciplinary collaborations between policymakers, healthcare professionals, and data scientists to drive innovation in healthcare delivery.
tot: this study proposes an innovative approach to enhance the robustness of treatment plans in intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) by integrating a probabilistic framework with internet-based technological advancements. we apply chance-constrained optimization to generate robust impt plans that can adapt to range and patient setup uncertainties, leveraging the computational power of modern computational tools. this approach is particularly relevant in the era of increasing internet penetration and widespread adoption of digital technologies by healthcare providers, policymakers, and civil society organizations. our novel method explicitly controls plan robustness through chance constraints, making it an attractive solution for healthcare decision-makers seeking to improve treatment outcomes under uncertainty. by comparing our method with conventional ptv-based optimization, we demonstrate improved target coverage, dose homogeneity, and normal tissue sparing, as well as enhanced robustness. we evaluate the impact of varying tolerance levels on plan quality and robustness, providing a framework for policymakers and healthcare administrators to make informed decisions about treatment planning and resource allocation in the context of rapidly evolving digital landscapes. our research highlights the potential of interdisciplinary approaches to address complex healthcare challenges in the age of information technologies.
raw: in the era of precision medicine, the effective communication of medical research and its translation into clinical practice is crucial. this study explores the integration of internet-based communication strategies with advanced medical technologies, such as intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt), to enhance the dissemination and implementation of evidence-based cancer treatments. by leveraging the vast reach and accessibility of the internet, we propose a novel framework that combines chance-constrained optimization with social media-based engagement to improve the robustness and quality of impt treatment plans. our approach aims to address the challenges of uncertainty in cancer treatment by developing a real-time, patient-centric decision-making platform that incorporates the perspectives of patients, clinicians, and researchers. this interdisciplinary project has the potential to improve cancer treatment outcomes, enhance patient engagement, and facilitate the translation of medical research into clinical practice. the results of this study will inform the development of a new paradigm for cancer care that integrates technology, communication, and evidence-based medicine.
one_shot: in the era of precision medicine, radiation therapy planning has become increasingly complex due to uncertainties in patient setup and range. we propose an interdisciplinary approach that leverages the power of the internet to enhance the communication of treatment plans with patients, while applying a probabilistic framework, chance-constrained optimization, to generate robust intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) plans. by incorporating patient-centric communication strategies, we aim to improve treatment plan adherence and patient outcomes. our novel method, which combines the strengths of chance-constrained optimization and internet-based patient engagement, enables the simultaneous optimization of plan quality and robustness, while providing patients with a clear understanding of their treatment options. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach using a prostate cancer case study, showing improved target coverage, dose homogeneity, and normal tissue sparing compared to conventional methods. this integrated approach has the potential to revolutionize the field of radiation oncology, improving patient care and outcomes in the era of precision medicine.
few_shot: in the era of precision medicine, cancer treatment planning faces increasing complexity due to patient setup and range uncertainties. this study integrates chance-constrained optimization from radiation oncology with the strategic use of digital media and information technologies in election campaigns to develop a novel framework for robust treatment planning and public engagement. by leveraging probabilistic models to ensure robustness under uncertainty, we generate superior intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) plans while simultaneously making them more resilient. our approach is then applied to a real-world scenario, where we demonstrate improved plan quality and robustness through simulations. furthermore, we propose a data-driven approach to harness the power of social media and online platforms to enhance public awareness and understanding of cancer treatment options, ultimately fostering more informed decision-making and participation in clinical trials. this interdisciplinary framework has the potential to revolutionize the field of radiation oncology while promoting patient-centered care and digital health literacy.
got: the integration of advanced technologies, such as intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning and internet-based communication tools, has the potential to revolutionize the way healthcare services are delivered and accessed. this paper explores the application of chance-constrained optimization in impt planning to generate robust treatment plans under uncertainties, and examines the role of the internet in facilitating patient-centered healthcare services, including remote consultation and education. by leveraging the probabilistic framework of chance-constrained optimization, clinicians can develop more effective treatment plans that account for patient setup and range uncertainties, while also utilizing the internet to enhance patient engagement and informed decision-making. a case study involving a prostate cancer patient demonstrates the feasibility of this approach, yielding plans with improved target coverage, dose homogeneity, and normal tissue sparing. furthermore, the internet-based platform enables real-time sharing of treatment plans and patient data, facilitating collaboration among healthcare professionals and empowering patients to take a more active role in their care. this integrated approach has the potential to improve healthcare outcomes and patient satisfaction.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: an improved expanding friction disk is disclosed. the improved expanding friction disk accommodates multiple clutch pack configurations including clutch packs utilizing novel improved friction disks, is compatible with wear liners and operates with reduced friction including a reduced difference between the static and dynamic coefficients of friction in the ramp mechanism.
abstract1: the takeshima issue between korea and japan came after world war ii. japan admitted to south korea the effective jurisdiction of dokdo in the korean-japanese normalization treaty, 1965. the issue of takeshima was quiet up until today. the new fisheries agreement between japan and korea was signed in 1998. it was not a convention on territory but an agreement on fisheries. however, shimozyo masao insisted that it was on dokdo territorial agreement. he argued that dokdo is japanese territory, and that the relationship between south korea and japan is very bad. this study analyzes shimozyo masao"s perception on the takeshima problem and his perception of history. the study of shimozyo masao did not get recognized for academic studies. so he proposed a workshop on takeshima issues. he moved the governor of shimane prefecture. he also moved some congress members of the shimane prefecture. he suggested the ordinance of the ‘day of the takeshima." he persuaded the lawmakers of shimane prefecture. he also persuaded the japanese foreign ministry through the congress. he finally moved the prime minister shinzo abe. his logic on the takeshima issue is not originally his. his logic is the studies of okuhara hekiun, kawakami kenzo, and tamura seizaburou. shimozyo masao premised that takeshima is the territory of japan. he denied that dokdo is korean territory. he deliberately incorrectly interpreted historical data. he completely denied the logic of south korea, china and russia. therefore, his logic is not logical. it is not objective. it is unfair. it is not academic.
abstract2: the invention discloses a motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence comprise s1: performing normalization processing of any given motion vector field; s2: calculating the curl and the divergence of each point in the normalized motion vector field to obtain a curl view and a divergence view; and constructing a dual vector field of the normalized motion vector field; s3: performing motion trial sampling in the normalized motion vector field and the dual vector field to obtain a tangential motion trail and a radial motion trail respectively; and s4: performing path integration of the curl and the divergence on the motion trails so as to obtain a cd (curl and divergence) motion feature with invariable scale and curl. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence can depict the curl and the divergence of motion integrally, and is high in robustness. as a motion descriptor with universality, the cd feature can be widely applied in motion analysis and behavior identification under different scenes.
gen_abs: this study presents a novel approach to resolving territorial disputes through the application of motion analysis and behavior identification techniques. building on the concept of the improved expanding friction disk, we propose a methodology that utilizes motion feature extraction based on curl and divergence to analyze the historical trajectory of territorial disputes, such as the takeshima issue between south korea and japan. by applying the cd (curl and divergence) motion feature to the historical motion data of the dispute, we can identify patterns and anomalies that may not be apparent through traditional analysis. this approach allows for a more objective and robust assessment of the dispute, taking into account the complexities of human behavior and the dynamics of territorial claims. the proposed methodology can be used to analyze other territorial disputes, providing a new tool for conflict resolution and international diplomacy. furthermore, the cd motion feature can be applied to various domains, including robotics and mechatronics, where friction analysis is crucial. this interdisciplinary research bridges the gap between territorial dispute analysis and motion analysis, offering a novel and innovative approach to understanding complex conflicts.
cot: we propose a novel approach to analyzing motion features in territorial disputes, leveraging the strengths of computer vision and historical analysis. building on the motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence, we apply this technique to the takeshima/dokdo issue between korea and japan. by analyzing the motion trails of territorial claims over time, our method can objectively quantify the dynamics of this longstanding dispute. this approach allows us to identify patterns and trends in the evolution of territorial perceptions, which may not be immediately apparent through traditional historical analysis. specifically, we use path integration of curl and divergence to extract invariant features from the motion trails, which can be used to distinguish between competing claims. our results show that the motion feature extraction method can provide valuable insights into the territorial dynamics of the takeshima/dokdo issue, and potentially other territorial disputes, by offering a new perspective on the evolution of territorial claims and perceptions over time. this integrated approach has the potential to inform more effective conflict resolution strategies.
tot: this study proposes a novel approach to resolving territorial disputes by leveraging the analysis of motion features extracted from spatial and temporal data. building on the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence (paper 2), we develop a framework for detecting and analyzing motion patterns associated with territorial claims, such as those surrounding the takeshima/dokdo issue between korea and japan (paper 1). our method involves normalizing motion vector fields, calculating curl and divergence, and performing path integration to obtain a cd (curl and divergence) motion feature. we apply this framework to historical data related to the takeshima/dokdo issue, analyzing the motion patterns of key stakeholders, including shimozyo masao, who has been a prominent advocate for japan's territorial claim. by visualizing and quantifying the motion features associated with these stakeholders, we provide new insights into the dynamics of territorial disputes and offer a novel perspective on the takeshima/dokdo issue. our approach has the potential to be applied to other territorial disputes, facilitating a more nuanced understanding of the complex interactions involved.
raw: a novel approach to analyzing territorial disputes, such as the takeshima issue between korea and japan, is proposed by integrating motion feature extraction techniques with historical and geographical data analysis. by applying the motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence to territorial dispute data, researchers can identify and visualize patterns of movement and interaction between nations, providing a new perspective on the evolution of territorial claims. this interdisciplinary approach can help overcome the limitations of traditional historical and geographical analyses, which often rely on incomplete or biased data. the proposed method can also be applied to other territorial disputes, such as those in the arctic region, to provide a more comprehensive understanding of the complex dynamics involved. by combining the robustness of motion feature extraction techniques with the nuance of historical and geographical data analysis, this research aims to develop a new framework for analyzing territorial disputes and promoting international cooperation.
one_shot: this paper proposes an approach to extract and analyze historical and geographical features from motion data, such as those obtained from satellite or drone imagery, using a novel method that combines motion feature extraction based on curl and divergence with geographical information systems (gis). by integrating the cd (curl and divergence) motion feature with geospatial data, we can accurately identify and visualize territorial boundaries and historical conflicts, such as the takeshima/dokdo dispute between korea and japan. our method enables the analysis of motion patterns and changes over time, providing a valuable tool for conflict resolution, border demarcation, and environmental monitoring. the approach is demonstrated on a case study of the takeshima/dokdo island dispute, showcasing its potential for interdisciplinary research and real-world applications.
few_shot: we investigate the application of motion feature extraction methods to simulate territorial disputes in geopolitics. by leveraging a motion descriptor based on curl and divergence, we model the dynamics of historical territorial claims and negotiations between japan and south korea. our approach represents the takeshima issue as a complex motion vector field, with the dual vector field capturing the divergent perspectives of the two nations. by performing path integration of the curl and divergence on the motion trails, we obtain a motion feature with invariable scale and curl that reflects the evolving nature of the dispute. this novel approach enables the analysis of territorial disputes as dynamic, multi-scale systems, providing new insights into the underlying factors driving conflict escalation and resolution.
got: this paper presents a novel approach to analyzing territorial disputes using motion feature extraction techniques. inspired by the work of shimozyo masao on the takeshima issue, we leverage insights from the field of computer vision to develop a computational framework for understanding the dynamics of territorial claims. by applying the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence, introduced in paper 2, we can depict the evolving relationships between nations involved in territorial disputes. our approach enables the detection of inconsistencies in historical narratives, such as those presented by shimozyo masao, and facilitates the identification of patterns in territorial claims that can inform diplomatic efforts. the proposed framework is demonstrated through its application to the takeshima issue, providing a new perspective on the historical development of this complex conflict. this work has the potential to contribute to the development of more objective and data-driven approaches to resolving territorial disputes, and to inform research in the fields of international relations, computer vision, and conflict analysis.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: we draw from the domains of visual analytics and augmented reality to support a new form of in-situ interactive visual analysis. we present a situated analytics model, a novel interaction, and a visualization concept for reasoning support. situated analytics has four primary elements: situated information, abstract information, augmented reality interaction, and analytical interaction.
abstract1: a tablet terminal (10) comprises a touch panel (12) which functions as a display for displaying a screen on a front face of a main body (11), a touch sensor (31, 231) provided at a back or a side face of the main body of the tablet terminal (10), and a controller (44) which detects a contact to the touch sensor (31) using a contact detection signal which the touch sensor outputs and executes control assigned to a point where the contact is detected after the point where the contact is detected is changed to a non-contact state.
abstract2: analyzing and characterizing the phonocardiogram (pcg) signal is important for diagnosing the valvular heart disease. the pcg can record heart sounds, noise and the additional sounds. since pcg is recording the heart sound, it is important to analyze the clear pcg input signal only. the analyzation of the pcg signal will be consisting of segmenting the signal into s1 and s2 and then compare, whether the pcg is normal or abnormal. in the existing system the wavelet decomposition approach is used to analyze the pcg signal. features are extracted from a pcg signal in frequency domain to classify signals. in the proposed approach the feature selection reduces features provided for classification. coiflet is used for feature extraction, and different feature selection statistical methods are used. information gain (ig), mutual information (mi) etc. feature selection methods are compared using classifiers like knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms. in this paper, two methods are used to analyze the pcg and the accuracy of the information gain (ig) is improved when compared to mutual information (mi).
gen_abs: we propose a novel framework, situated heart analytics, that combines the strengths of tablet-based visual analytics and augmented reality to support in-situ diagnosis of valvular heart disease. our approach leverages the capabilities of a tablet terminal with a touch panel display, a touch sensor, and a controller to facilitate interactive analysis of phonocardiogram (pcg) signals. by applying the situated analytics model, we enable healthcare professionals to visually analyze pcg signals in real-time, using augmented reality to superimpose relevant information onto the patient's anatomy. our framework integrates feature selection and extraction techniques from paper 2, such as coiflet and information gain, with the visual analytics capabilities of the tablet terminal. this allows for the identification of abnormal heart sounds and noise patterns, and provides a novel platform for training and education in cardiology. our preliminary results show promising accuracy in classifying pcg signals, and demonstrate the potential of situated heart analytics to improve diagnostic accuracy and patient outcomes.
cot: the convergence of wearable technology and biomedical signal analysis has led to the development of innovative health monitoring systems. this study investigates the application of tablet-based touch sensors for detecting physiological signals, specifically phonocardiograms (pcgs), which are crucial for diagnosing valvular heart disease. by leveraging the tablet's touch panel and touch sensor, we designed a novel wearable device that captures pcg signals with high accuracy. our approach involves using the coiflet wavelet decomposition to extract features from the pcg signal in the frequency domain. to improve classification accuracy, we employed feature selection methods, including information gain (ig) and mutual information (mi), and compared their performance using various machine learning classifiers, such as knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms. our results show that the ig-based approach achieves improved accuracy compared to mi, demonstrating its potential for accurate pcg signal analysis. the proposed system has the potential to enable real-time monitoring and diagnosis of valvular heart disease, contributing to more effective healthcare management.
tot: a novel, hybrid system is proposed for non-invasive, real-time diagnosis of valvular heart disease using a tablet terminal with a touch-sensitive display. the system integrates a user-friendly interface with advanced signal processing techniques to accurately analyze phonocardiogram (pcg) signals. the pcg signal is obtained through the tablet's touch sensor, which can record heart sounds, noise, and additional sounds without the need for external equipment. a wavelet decomposition approach is employed to segment the pcg signal into s1 and s2 components, which are then analyzed using coiflet feature extraction and feature selection methods. specifically, information gain (ig) is used to select relevant features for classification, achieving improved accuracy compared to mutual information (mi). the system is integrated with machine learning classifiers, including knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms, to diagnose valvular heart disease. the proposed system has the potential to revolutionize point-of-care diagnosis and monitoring, enabling healthcare professionals to make informed decisions in a timely and accurate manner. preliminary results show promising accuracy rates, demonstrating the feasibility and efficacy of this hybrid approach.
raw: this paper proposes a novel approach for developing a portable and non-invasive diagnostic tool for valvular heart disease using a tablet-based electrocardiogram (ecg)-like system. inspired by the existing pcg analysis techniques, we integrate a touch-sensitive panel on a tablet terminal to capture phonocardiogram (pcg) signals from the user's chest. the controller on the tablet detects contact and executes control to isolate and amplify the pcg signal. we employ a wavelet decomposition approach, specifically the coiflet transform, to segment and analyze the pcg signal in the frequency domain. to improve classification accuracy, we apply feature selection methods, including information gain (ig) and mutual information (mi), to reduce the dimensionality of the extracted features. our novel contribution is the integration of a tablet-based pcg acquisition system with machine learning-based feature selection and analysis, enabling a portable and user-friendly diagnostic tool for valvular heart disease. we evaluate the proposed system using various classifiers, including knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms, and demonstrate improved accuracy compared to existing methods. this study has the potential to revolutionize early diagnosis and monitoring of valvular heart disease, enabling timely interventions and improved patient outcomes.
one_shot: we propose a novel sensor system that integrates a touch-sensitive tablet terminal with a heart sound analysis framework to diagnose valvular heart disease. the tablet terminal features a touch panel and touch sensor that enable intuitive interaction and control. our system leverages this technology to provide a seamless user experience for patients and clinicians alike. specifically, we adapt the wavelet decomposition approach used in heart sound analysis to the touch sensor data, enabling the decomposition of heart sounds, noise, and additional sounds into their constituent components. we then use feature extraction techniques, such as the coiflet, to analyze the decomposed signal in the frequency domain, reducing the dimensionality of the feature space using information gain and mutual information methods. our system evaluates the accuracy of these methods using machine learning classifiers, including knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svm, demonstrating improved performance with information gain. this integrated system has the potential to revolutionize the diagnosis and management of valvular heart disease.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach to diagnosing valvular heart disease by integrating tablet terminal-based physiological signal acquisition with machine learning-based analysis of phonocardiogram (pcg) signals. by leveraging the tablet's touch sensor and controller to record and analyze pcg signals in real-time, our system enables accurate segmentation and feature extraction of heart sounds. we then apply machine learning techniques, including feature selection using information gain and mutual information, to classify pcg signals as normal or abnormal. our results show improved accuracy and efficiency in diagnosing valvular heart disease, demonstrating the potential of this integrated approach for point-of-care diagnostics.
got: this study integrates advanced signal processing techniques from the field of biomedical engineering with innovative user interface technologies to develop a novel, interactive system for diagnosing valvular heart disease. our system utilizes a tablet terminal with a touch panel to display the phonocardiogram (pcg) signal, allowing medical professionals to easily segment and analyze the signal in real-time. the proposed approach employs a feature selection method, specifically information gain (ig), to reduce the dimensionality of the signal in the frequency domain, facilitating efficient classification using support vector machines (svms). to further enhance the user experience, the system incorporates a touch sensor and controller, enabling intuitive control of the analysis process. our preliminary results demonstrate improved accuracy in diagnosing abnormal pcg signals compared to existing methods, highlighting the potential of this integrated system to improve the efficiency and accuracy of valvular heart disease diagnosis.
