abstract1: purpose: we propose to apply a probabilistic framework, namely chanceconstrained optimization, in the intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning subject to range and patient setup uncertainties. the purpose is to hedge against the influence of uncertainties and improve robustness of treatment plans. methods: impt plans were generated for a typical prostate patient. nine dose distributions are computed — the nominal one and one each for ±5mm setup uncertainties along three cardinal axes and for ±3.5% range uncertainty. these nine dose distributions are supplied to the solver cplex as chance constraints to explicitly control plan robustness under these representative uncertainty scenarios with certain probability. this probability is determined by the tolerance level. we make the chance-constrained model tractable by converting it to a mixed integer optimization problem. the quality of plans derived from this method is evaluated using dose-volume histogram (dvh) indices such as tumor dose homogeneity (d5% – d95%) and coverage (d95%) and normal tissue sparing like v70 of rectum, v65, and v40 of bladder. we also compare the results from this novel method with the conventional ptv-based method to further demonstrate its effectiveness results: our model can yield clinically acceptable plans within 50 seconds. the chance-constrained optimization produces impt plansmore » with comparable target coverage, better target dose homogeneity, and better normal tissue sparing compared to the ptv-based optimization [d95% ctv: 67.9 vs 68.7 (gy), d5% – d95% ctv: 11.9 vs 18 (gy), v70 rectum: 0.0 % vs 0.33%, v65 bladder: 2.17% vs 9.33%, v40 bladder: 8.83% vs 21.83%]. it also simultaneously makes the plan more robust [width of dvh band at d50%: 2.0 vs 10.0 (gy)]. the tolerance level may be varied to control the tradeoff between plan robustness and quality. conclusion: the chance-constrained optimization generates superior impt plan compared to the ptv-based optimization with explicit control of plan robustness. nih/nci k25ca168984, eagles cancer research career development, the lawrence w. and marilyn w. matteson fund for cancer research, mayo asu seed grant, and the kemper marley foundation.« less
abstract2: search engine advertising sea has attracted the attention of academic researchers from marketing, information systems, computer science, mathematics and other related fields. aiming to provide a picture of the state of the art in this field, the present research systematically searches for and reviews the literature on sea, analyses and structures them, and provides directions for future research. we searched extensively and collected 97 journal papers on sea published in 64 journals from different disciplines and analysed them to answer our research questions. using systematic mapping approach, this research has identified research topics ten categories of topics were identified and visualised in a graphical representation, research approaches, nature of research and the interdependencies between them. the research also has identified directions for future research.
gen_abs: this study proposes a novel probabilistic framework for optimizing search engine advertising (sea) campaigns under uncertainty, leveraging insights from intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning. inspired by the application of chance-constrained optimization in impt to mitigate range and patient setup uncertainties, we develop a similar approach to address uncertainty in sea campaign optimization. our framework, dubbed "robust sea," incorporates probabilistic constraints to ensure the robustness of campaign objectives, such as click-through rate (ctr) and conversion rate, under uncertainty. we demonstrate the efficacy of this approach using a case study on a hypothetical campaign, and compare its performance to a conventional optimization approach. our results demonstrate that robust sea can yield more robust and effective campaign plans, while also reducing the risk of over- or under-optimization. furthermore, this research provides a systematic review of the literature on sea, identifying key research topics and directions for future study. this work has significant implications for the development of more resilient and effective sea strategies, and highlights the potential for interdisciplinary research in the application of chance-constrained optimization to real-world problems.
cot: in radiation oncology, the treatment planning process for intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) is inherently uncertain due to range and patient setup uncertainties. to address this challenge, we propose an innovative approach that integrates chance-constrained optimization from computer science with the systematic mapping of search engine advertising from marketing research. by applying the probabilistic framework of chance-constrained optimization to impt planning, we can generate robust treatment plans that explicitly control the tradeoff between plan robustness and quality. however, the evaluation of these plans requires a clear understanding of the target and normal tissue doses, which is often missing in current studies. to address this limitation, we developed a novel systematic mapping approach that categorizes and visualizes the literature on search engine advertising to identify research topics, methods, and interdependencies. this comprehensive framework will enable us to analyze the impact of different uncertainty scenarios on impt plans and identify the most effective optimization strategies. our research aims to improve the robustness and efficacy of impt treatment plans while providing a new perspective on search engine advertising research.
tot: in this interdisciplinary study, we propose a novel approach to optimizing search engine advertising (sea) campaigns using a probabilistic framework inspired by intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning. we leverage the concept of chance-constrained optimization, which has been successfully applied in impt to account for range and patient setup uncertainties, to develop a robust sea framework. by casting sea as a stochastic optimization problem, we can explicitly control the trade-off between campaign quality and robustness to uncertainty in user behavior, ad placement, and bid pricing. our approach allows for the simultaneous optimization of multiple metrics, including click-through rates, conversion rates, and return on ad spend, while incorporating uncertainty estimates of user behavior and market fluctuations. using a systematic review of 97 papers on sea, we identified key research gaps and opportunities for future investigation. we demonstrate the efficacy of our approach through computational experiments, showcasing improved campaign performance and robustness compared to traditional optimization methods. our framework has the potential to revolutionize the field of sea by providing a data-driven, uncertainty-aware approach to campaign optimization, ultimately enhancing the effectiveness and efficiency of online advertising.
raw: we propose an innovative framework that leverages the principles of chance-constrained optimization and machine learning to develop robust and adaptive search engine advertising (sea) strategies. by integrating the probabilistic framework used in intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning with the insights from systematic reviews of sea, our approach enables the optimization of advertising campaigns under uncertainty, incorporating factors such as user behavior, market fluctuations, and platform algorithms. we develop a novel model that explicitly accounts for the uncertainties in sea, analogous to the range and patient setup uncertainties in impt. our method uses a mixed integer optimization problem to minimize the risk of poor advertising outcomes while maximizing expected returns. we evaluate the performance of our framework using dose-volume histogram (dvh) indices adapted for sea, including metrics such as click-through-rate (ctr), conversion rate, and return on ad spend (roas). our results demonstrate the potential for improved robustness and adaptability of sea campaigns, outperforming conventional methods and enabling data-driven decision-making under uncertainty. this research has far-reaching implications for the development of more effective and resilient online advertising strategies.
one_shot: we propose an interdisciplinary framework to optimize search engine advertising (sea) campaigns by leveraging insights from intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning. inspired by the probabilistic approach of chance-constrained optimization in impt, we develop a novel optimization method to manage uncertainty in sea, incorporating factors such as user behavior, bid prices, and platform limitations. this framework, dubbed "proton-sea," utilizes mixed-integer linear programming to generate robust and adaptable sea plans that optimize returns while minimizing the risk of uncertainty. we demonstrate the effectiveness of proton-sea through case studies and comparative analyses with traditional sea optimization methods, highlighting improved campaign performance and reduced uncertainty. our approach has far-reaching implications for the development of more resilient and efficient sea strategies.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach to optimize intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) planning under uncertainty, leveraging insights from search engine advertising and systematic mapping to structure and analyze the problem space. by casting impt planning as a chance-constrained optimization problem, we can explicitly control plan robustness under range and patient setup uncertainties. we develop a mixed-integer optimization framework that integrates probabilistic constraints and tractable reformulations, enabling efficient computation of robust treatment plans. our approach is grounded in a systematic review of the literature on search engine advertising, which informs the development of a structured framework for identifying and addressing uncertainty in impt planning. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our method through computational experiments and dose-volume histogram analysis, showing improved target coverage, dose homogeneity, and normal tissue sparing compared to conventional methods. this work contributes to the development of more robust and effective impt treatment plans, with potential applications in precision medicine and cancer therapy.
got: this paper proposes an innovative approach to personalizing intensity-modulated proton therapy (impt) treatment plans using a probabilistic framework inspired by search engine advertising (sea) strategies. by applying chance-constrained optimization to impt planning, we can explicitly control plan robustness against range and patient setup uncertainties. we draw on the concepts of sea to develop a novel method for identifying optimal tolerance levels, which balances plan quality and robustness. our systematic review of sea literature reveals insights into the optimization of advertising campaigns that can be adapted to impt planning. we demonstrate the feasibility of our approach using a prostate cancer case study, comparing the results of chance-constrained optimization with conventional ptv-based methods. our findings show that this approach can yield clinically acceptable plans with improved target dose homogeneity and normal tissue sparing. this research has significant implications for the development of more personalized and robust impt treatment plans, and provides a new direction for future research in medical optimization and sea.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: with the help of some techniques based on certain inverse pairs of symbolic operators, the authors investigated several decomposition formulas associated with srivastava's hypergeometric functions of three variables. some operator identities have been constructed in this matter. with the help these operator forms 15 decompositions are found which are expressed through product of hypergeometric gauss and appell's functions.
abstract1: it is critical to confirm the optimum dose and the active duration of the sterilization by quinestrol on rodents for some studies and practices. we fed female and male brandt's voles 5 doses of 0. 5 mg / kg,1. 0 mg / kg,2. 0 mg / kg,3. 0 mg / kg,or 6. 0 mg / kg quiestrol for 7 consecutive days. we then assessed their anti-fertility effects on the voles by analyzing reproduction indices including birth date,reproductive rate and reproductive capacity. results showed that quinestrol delayed the breeding of brandt's voles in the lab,but the effect was not significant due to the small sample size. in 90 days,some doses of quinestrol decreased the reproductive rate and average litter size of female and male,however,the inhibitory effect still was not significant,again possibly due to small sample size; some doses of quinestrol decreased markedly the reproductive capacity of brandt's voles. the suppression effects were not significantly correlated with the doses of quinestrol. the optimum doses of quinestrol causing sterility were below 2. 0 mg / kg for male and 1. 0 mg / kg for female. the dose applyed for brandt's vole should be below 2. 0 mg / kg,which can reduce total litter size of brandt's vole obviously within 90 days.
abstract2: problem to be solved: to provide an inorganic granular material that is significantly economical than conventional products and may be used as a seismic isolation material that offers excellent seismic isolation effect and also as a filling material for an anchoring structure having high durability against tension.solution: the present invention provides a seismic isolation material or a filling material for an anchoring structure made of an inorganic granular material containing 70% or more of inorganic particles of particle diameters up to 75 μm, having a void ratio between 0.3 and 0.6 inclusive, and containing a chemical admixture at percentages between 0.08% and 10% inclusive.
gen_abs: this study investigates the application of quinestrol as a novel, eco-friendly, and economical seismic isolation material in anchoring structures. by leveraging the anti-fertility effects of quinestrol on brandt's voles, we develop an innovative approach to create a quinestrol-based inorganic granular material with enhanced seismic isolation properties. we use srivastava's hypergeometric functions of three variables and operator identities to mathematically model the effect of quinestrol on the material's void ratio and chemical admixture content. our results demonstrate that quinestrol concentrations below 2.0 mg/kg can significantly reduce the total litter size of brandt's voles, and this effect is correlated with the material's void ratio. we find that the optimal quinestrol content for seismic isolation lies between 0.08% and 10%, leading to a novel quinestrol-based material with improved durability against tension. this hybrid approach paves the way for the development of more sustainable and cost-effective seismic isolation materials, while also providing a new perspective on the application of quinestrol in environmental science.
cot: this study proposes a novel approach to seismic isolation and anchoring structure reinforcement by integrating a quinestrol-based sterilization method with an inorganic granular material. building on the findings of paper 1, which identified the optimum doses of quinestrol for sterilizing brandt's voles, we explore the potential of incorporating quinestrol into the inorganic granular material to enhance its seismic isolation properties while maintaining its sterilization efficacy. by synthesizing the quinestrol-based granular material, we observe a significant improvement in its seismic isolation performance, as well as a notable reduction in the reproductive capacity of brandt's voles exposed to the material. our results suggest that this novel material can be used as an effective seismic isolation device while also serving as a sterilization agent, offering a unique solution for both applications. the study's findings have implications for the development of sustainable and efficient seismic isolation systems, as well as for the advancement of reproductive studies in rodents.
tot: a novel seismic isolation material has been developed by incorporating quinestrol-treated inorganic granular particles into a conventional seismic isolation material. the quinestrol treatment, previously shown to induce sterility in rodents at concentrations below 2.0 mg/kg, is hypothesized to enhance the material's durability against tension and seismic activity by reducing the reproductive rate of microorganisms that may compromise the material's integrity. the inorganic granular particles, with diameters up to 75 μm and a void ratio between 0.3 and 0.6, are combined with quinestrol at concentrations between 0.08% and 10%. this novel material is expected to offer excellent seismic isolation performance while maintaining a high level of durability against tension. the quinestrol treatment is also predicted to reduce the material's long-term degradation rate and enhance its resistance to environmental stressors, thereby extending its lifespan and reducing maintenance costs. preliminary results from laboratory tests suggest that the quinestrol-treated material exhibits improved performance compared to its conventional counterpart, paving the way for its application in seismic isolation and anchoring structures.
raw: this study presents a novel approach to developing a quinestrol-based seismic isolation material for anchoring structures, leveraging the sterilization effects observed in rodents. by integrating the chemical admixture of quinestrol with an inorganic granular material, we aim to create a material with exceptional seismic isolation properties and durability against tension. the admixture composition is designed to mimic the optimal quinestrol doses (below 2.0 mg/kg) identified in previous studies, which were shown to significantly reduce litter size in brandt's voles. our material formulation includes 70% or more of inorganic particles with diameters up to 75 μm, a void ratio between 0.3 and 0.6, and a quinestrol concentration between 0.08% and 10%. the resulting material is expected to exhibit enhanced stability and resistance to seismic activity, while also serving as a cost-effective alternative to conventional products. through a combination of experimental and computational modeling, we evaluate the mechanical properties and seismic isolation performance of the developed material, providing insights into its potential applications in anchoring structures and mitigating seismic hazards. our study bridges the gap between material science and life sciences, demonstrating a novel approach to integrating biomimicry and materials engineering for disaster resilience.
one_shot: the integration of sterilization techniques with seismic isolation materials has the potential to revolutionize the management of invasive species in seismically active regions. we propose a novel approach that combines the anti-fertility effects of quinestrol, a synthetic estrogen, with the seismic isolation properties of inorganic granular materials. by developing a quinestrol-infused seismic isolation material, we aim to create a sustainable and cost-effective solution for controlling invasive species populations in areas prone to earthquakes. our preliminary study suggests that quinestrol doses below 2.0 mg/kg can significantly reduce reproductive rates and litter sizes in brandt's voles, while also demonstrating the feasibility of incorporating quinestrol into inorganic granular materials. this interdisciplinary research has the potential to mitigate the ecological and economic impacts of invasive species in seismically active regions, while also advancing our understanding of the interactions between seismic activity and species populations.
few_shot: this study integrates the concepts of sterilization and seismic isolation to develop a novel seismic isolation material that incorporates a quinestrol-based chemical admixture. quinestrol, a known anti-fertility agent, is added to an inorganic granular material to inhibit the growth of microorganisms within the material. this approach enables the creation of a durable and cost-effective seismic isolation material that can withstand tension and provide excellent seismic isolation effects. the admixture is designed to be incorporated at percentages between 0.08% and 10%, with a target of 70% or more inorganic particles of particle diameters up to 75 μm, and a void ratio between 0.3 and 0.6 inclusive. our results demonstrate the feasibility of this approach, with improved durability and seismic isolation performance compared to conventional products. this novel material has the potential to be used in a wide range of applications, including structural reinforcement and protection of critical infrastructure during earthquakes.
got: this study integrates innovative materials science with reproductive biology to develop a novel, cost-effective and durable seismic isolation material that can also be used to study the effects of quinestrol on rodent reproduction. by modifying the inorganic granular material described in paper 2, we created a quinestrol-loaded seismic isolation material with improved durability against tension and excellent seismic isolation effects. our experimental design involved feeding brandt's voles with quinestrol-loaded granular material at varying doses (0.5-6.0 mg/kg) for 7 consecutive days, and assessing their reproductive indices, including birth date, reproductive rate, and reproductive capacity. we found that the quinestrol-loaded material at doses below 2.0 mg/kg significantly reduced the reproductive rate and average litter size of female and male brandt's voles, while also exhibiting excellent seismic isolation effects. our results suggest that this material can be used as a novel tool to study the effects of quinestrol on rodent reproduction, while also providing a cost-effective and durable seismic isolation solution for anchoring structures. this interdisciplinary approach opens up new avenues for studying the effects of quinestrol on rodent reproduction and developing novel materials for seismic isolation.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the article deals with the research of external interference signal effect on the information flow of the loaded communication line. the impact degree is determined by the component of metric tensor cosine of the angle between contravariant vectors computed using simulation of the segment of orthogonal tensor model of communication network. we determined the option for finding the normalization coefficients for interacting flows.
abstract1: social curation is the process through which the general public, rather than web publishers themselves, tag, organize, and share content with others. in this paper, i focus on the case of pinterest, an image-based social curation site. i specifically highlight how the individual acts of collection on the site contribute to a new kind of public construction of knowledge online. this is specific to pinterest, i argue, because the way that the site is set up—both in terms of its technological and business structure. my goals for the paper are as follows. first, i question how pinterest’s notion of curation compares with traditional or already existing notions of professional curation and personal collection. then, i offer an analysis of the activity on pinterest; whereas users believe they are participating in personal acts of collection and reflexive identity building, i counteract this with an examination of the entire business “ecosystem” of the site, showing how these personal activities can also be unde...
abstract2: the hi/lo bilayer resist system exposure in e-beam lithography (ebl) process, intended for mushroom-like profile fabrication, was studied. different exposure parameters and theirs influence on the resist layers were simulated in casino software and the obtained results were compared with the experimental data. the afm technique was used for the estimation of the e-beam penetration depth in the resist stack. performed numerical and experimental results allow us to establish the useful ranges of the exposure parameters.
gen_abs: this study explores the intersection of social curation and nanoscale lithography, leveraging the insights from both pinterest's social curation ecosystem and the hi/lo bilayer resist system exposure in e-beam lithography. we propose a novel framework for analyzing the effects of external interference on information flow in complex communication networks, drawing on the intermediate idea of metric tensor cosine and contravariant vectors. this framework, termed "curated lithography," integrates the social construction of knowledge on pinterest with the parameters of e-beam lithography. by modeling the behavior of users on pinterest as interacting flows in a communication network, we can simulate and predict the optimal exposure parameters for fabricating mushroom-like profiles. our numerical simulations and experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of this approach, enabling the fabrication of complex nanostructures with high precision. the findings have implications for both social media research and nanotechnology, highlighting the potential for interdisciplinary collaboration in the development of new materials and fabrication techniques.
cot: this paper proposes a novel framework for understanding the collective construction of knowledge in the context of emerging technologies. by integrating insights from social curation and e-beam lithography, we investigate how the activities of users on social platforms like pinterest intersect with the physical properties of materials in the nanoscale fabrication process. we argue that the process of social curation can be seen as a form of "collective sensing," where individual acts of collection and sharing contribute to a shared understanding of the world, and that this process can be made more effective through the use of simulation and optimization tools like those used in the e-beam lithography process. by examining the interplay between social and physical systems, we develop a new perspective on the role of collective intelligence in the creation of knowledge and innovation. our results have implications for the design of social platforms, the development of advanced materials, and the future of collaborative innovation.
tot: the intersection of social media and nanotechnology has given rise to a new paradigm for knowledge construction and fabrication. this paper explores the synergy between social curation platforms, such as pinterest, and advanced lithography techniques, such as e-beam lithography (ebl). we investigate how the collective acts of collection and sharing on pinterest can inform the design of novel nanoscale structures, specifically mushroom-like profiles, through the optimization of ebl process parameters. by simulating different exposure scenarios using casino software and comparing the results with experimental data, we demonstrate the potential for social curation to influence the fabrication of complex nanostructures. furthermore, we examine the role of individual users in contributing to the public construction of knowledge online, highlighting the unintended consequences of their actions on the ebl process. our findings suggest that the collaboration between social media and nanotechnology can lead to novel fabrication techniques and new insights into the nature of collective knowledge construction.
raw: this paper presents an interdisciplinary investigation into the intersection of social curation and nanotechnology fabrication. specifically, we explore the potential applications of social media platforms, such as pinterest, in the design and optimization of bilayer resist systems for electron-beam lithography (ebl). by leveraging the collective curation efforts of users on pinterest, we can generate and analyze vast amounts of data on image-based collections and collaborative filtering patterns. these insights can be used to inform the development of more efficient and effective exposure parameters for ebl processes. we demonstrate the feasibility of this approach by simulating and comparing the effects of different exposure parameters on the resist layers using a combination of computational modeling and experimental data. our results show that the crowdsourced data from pinterest can be effectively used to predict the optimal exposure ranges for ebl, leading to improved fabrication of mushroom-like profiles. this work highlights the potential for interdisciplinary collaborations between social media analytics and nanotechnology research.
one_shot: we investigate how social curation platforms, such as pinterest, influence the construction of knowledge in online environments by integrating user-generated content. specifically, we explore the interplay between individual acts of collection and the technological and business structures of platforms like pinterest. to this end, we develop a new framework for understanding the relationships between user activity, platform design, and knowledge representation. we then apply this framework to a novel problem in e-beam lithography, where the fabrication of mushroom-like profiles requires the optimization of resist layers and exposure parameters. by simulating the e-beam lithography process using casino software and analyzing the resulting exposure parameters, we demonstrate how the principles of social curation can inform the design of more efficient and effective fabrication processes. our results show that the curated knowledge structures generated on platforms like pinterest can be leveraged to predict optimal exposure parameters and improve the accuracy of fabrication processes, leading to breakthroughs in fields such as nanotechnology and materials science.
few_shot: we investigate how social curation platforms like pinterest influence the diffusion of scientific knowledge and expertise through crowdsourced, user-driven knowledge construction. by analyzing the collective behavior on pinterest, we identify patterns of knowledge dissemination and discovery among users, and reveal how these processes intersect with the platform's technological and business structure. we develop a novel framework that incorporates insights from both sociology and materials science to study the dynamics of knowledge sharing and expertise formation in online environments. specifically, we examine how the mechanisms of social curation can be leveraged to facilitate the accelerated development of new materials and technologies, such as those fabricated using e-beam lithography, by simulating the effects of user-driven knowledge sharing on material properties and performance. our findings demonstrate the potential for social curation to accelerate technological innovation and knowledge transfer, and highlight the need for interdisciplinary approaches to understanding the complex interplay between human behavior, technology, and scientific discovery.
got: we propose a novel interdisciplinary framework for understanding the intersection of material fabrication and social construction of knowledge. by integrating the principles of e-beam lithography (ebl) with the concept of social curation, we explore how the creation of micro- and nano-scale structures can be influenced by the collective actions of online communities. specifically, we examine how the process of social curation on platforms like pinterest, where users engage in image-based collection and sharing, can inform the design and optimization of ebl processes. through a combination of computational simulations and atomic force microscopy (afm) experiments, we demonstrate how the exposure parameters of ebl can be fine-tuned to create "mushroom-like" profiles, inspired by the collective curation patterns observed on pinterest. this work highlights the potential for social curation to serve as a source of inspiration for materials science and fabrication, and vice versa, shedding new light on the relationship between human creativity and technological innovation.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: environmental monitoring project teaching curriculum design was based on the concept of modern vocational education to develop students' ability to apply technology to build the core curriculum system,teaching content to ‘necessary'and‘good enough'for the degree,the relevance and practicality were high lightened. teaching process to the project as a carrier task- driven,effective teaching docking target and environmental monitoring job of professional competence can be realized.
abstract1: the present invention discloses a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment, whose active components are complexed by lucid ganoderma polysaccharide, lycium barbarum polysaccharide and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide, the mass proportion ratio of each component is lucid ganoderma polysaccharide 10-250, lycium barbarum polysaccharide 1-50, polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide 2-10. the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide is high in purity and activity, has an efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment. the present invention also discloses the preparation method of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, said method is simple, stable and efficient in the process, suitable for industrial production and economic. in addition, the present invention further discloses the use of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide in the preparation of health-care food having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment.
abstract2: the subject matter relates to beverages prepared by mixing spelt flour or spelt meal hydrolysate, said hydrolysate being prepared by boiling spelt flour or meal suspension at 90 110 oc for 30 180 minutes in the presence of an acid catalyst at ph 2.8 4.8, so as the consistency index kvalue is under 500 and flow index value m is over 0.75, with a water extract prepared atboiling point from coffee, cocoa bens, coconut and other suitable aromatic crops, powder extracts of these crops and/or natural or nature-identical flavourings obtained from these crops. the prepared beverage is preserved by pasteurization or by addition of preserving agents.
gen_abs: title: integrating polyphenol-rich polysaccharides and sustainable beverages for cancer prevention and environmental conservation this interdisciplinary study combines the efficacy of polyphenol-rich polysaccharides from a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex with the development of sustainable beverages. building on the environmental monitoring project-based curriculum design, we designed an educational program to develop vocational students' ability to integrate technology and environmental monitoring to create innovative products. we utilized this concept to develop a novel beverage recipe, incorporating the polysaccharide complex with a spelt flour hydrolysate-based beverage. the polysaccharide complex was produced using a simplified and efficient method, with a mass proportion ratio of 10-250:1-50:2-10. the polysaccharide complex was then added to a spelt flour hydrolysate-based beverage, prepared by boiling spelt flour or meal suspension at 90-110°c for 30-180 minutes. the resulting beverage exhibited enhanced antioxidant and anti-tumor activities, with improved nutritional value and environmental sustainability. this integrated approach not only promotes cancer prevention but also contributes to environmental conservation through the use of sustainable ingredients and production methods. the study demonstrates the potential of interdisciplinary collaboration and vocational education in developing innovative solutions for public health and environmental issues.
cot: a novel beverage formulation is proposed, integrating the synergistic effects of a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide with the nutritional benefits of spelt flour hydrolysate, to create a functional food product that regulates tumor microenvironment. the complex polysaccharide, comprised of a specific mass ratio of lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharides, exhibits high purity and activity in modulating tumor microenvironment. the spelt flour hydrolysate, prepared through an acid-catalyzed process, is optimized to achieve a consistency index below 500 and a flow index above 0.75, ensuring a stable and flowable product. by combining these two components, the proposed beverage not only leverages the immunomodulatory effects of the complex polysaccharide but also incorporates the nutritional benefits of spelt flour hydrolysate, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. this novel formulation presents a promising approach for the development of a functional food product that supports health and wellness, particularly in cancer prevention and treatment.
tot: we propose a novel approach to develop functional beverages that target tumor microenvironment regulation. by incorporating the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, previously shown to have efficacy in regulating tumor microenvironment, into a spelt flour or spelt meal hydrolysate-based beverage, we aim to create a synergistic effect that enhances the bioavailability and stability of the polysaccharide complex. the beverage preparation method involves boiling spelt flour or meal suspension in the presence of an acid catalyst to produce a hydrolysate with optimal consistency and flow indices, followed by the addition of the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide. we anticipate that this novel beverage will not only retain the polysaccharide complex's efficacy in regulating tumor microenvironment but also provide a convenient and palatable delivery method for cancer prevention and treatment. our research will investigate the effects of this functional beverage on tumor growth, angiogenesis, and immune response, providing valuable insights into its potential as a therapeutic agent.
raw: this study investigates the functional properties of two newly developed beverages, namely, "polysac" and "speltcraft," which incorporate complex polysaccharides from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum (pgp) in combination with spelt flour or spelt meal hydrolysate. the pgp complex is prepared via a novel method, which ensures high purity and activity, and is used to regulate tumor microenvironment. the spelt-based beverages are designed to enhance the bioavailability and efficacy of pgp through spelt's unique nutritional profile and hydrolysate characteristics. by integrating these two components, we aim to create a synergistic effect that not only regulates tumor microenvironment but also provides a range of health benefits associated with spelt consumption. our results demonstrate the potential of polysac and speltcraft as novel food products that can be used to support cancer prevention and treatment. the combined effects of pgp and spelt on gut health, immune response, and tumor growth inhibition are explored in this study, providing a comprehensive understanding of the functional properties of these two beverages.
one_shot: we propose the development of a novel, functional beverage targeting cancer prevention and intervention. by integrating the bioactive complex polysaccharide from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum (as disclosed in paper 1) into a spelt flour-based hydrolysate (as described in paper 2), our approach leverages the synergistic effects of polysaccharide and hydrolysate to modulate tumor microenvironment and enhance immune response. the spelt hydrolysate, with its optimized consistency and flow indices, serves as a stable and efficient delivery system for the bioactive polysaccharide. this integrated formulation, further enriched with aromatic extracts and flavorings, creates a bioactive beverage with enhanced efficacy, stability, and consumer acceptability, thereby offering a novel platform for cancer prevention and intervention.
few_shot: we propose a novel health-promoting beverage formulation that integrates a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide with spelt flour or spelt meal hydrolysate. this synergy leverages the polysaccharide's efficacy in regulating tumor microenvironments and the hydrolysate's ability to modulate gut health and immune function. the beverage's bioactive compounds are prepared through a simple, stable, and efficient process, suitable for industrial production. we demonstrate the improved health benefits of this integrated formulation in preclinical and human trials, highlighting its potential as a novel functional food for cancer prevention and management.
got: we propose a novel approach to develop a functional beverage that leverages the synergistic effects of a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide and spelt flour hydrolysate. by combining the bioactive properties of the complex polysaccharide, which has been shown to regulate tumor microenvironment, with the functional properties of spelt flour hydrolysate, which provides a unique texture and flavor profile, we aim to create a beverage that not only tastes great but also offers potential health benefits. our method involves the preparation of the complex polysaccharide using a simple, stable, and efficient process, followed by the mixing of the resulting polysaccharide with spelt flour hydrolysate, which is prepared by boiling spelt flour suspension in the presence of an acid catalyst. the resulting beverage is then preserved by pasteurization or addition of preserving agents. our research aims to investigate the feasibility and efficacy of this novel beverage in regulating tumor microenvironment and improving overall health outcomes.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: field: chemistry.substance: invention relates to fodder production, particularly to an antibacterial and antimould composition for treating cereal crops, fodder, basal feed and drinking water and a method for production thereof. the composition contains 10-90% monoglycerides of c-corganic acids and 10-90 wt % glycerine for total weight of the composition.effect: obtaining an antibacterial and antimould composition for treating cereal crops.11 cl, 9 tbl, 3 dwg, 6 ex
abstract1: we present a method to predict collisions with objects thrown at a quadrotor using a pair of dynamic vision sensors (dvs). due to the micro-second temporal resolution of these sensors and the sparsity of their output, the object's trajectory can be estimated with minimal latency. unlike standard cameras that send frames at a fixed frame rate, a dvs only transmits pixel-level brightness changes (“events”) at the time they occur. our method tracks spherical objects on the image plane using probabilistic trackers that are updated with each incoming event. the object's trajectory is estimated using an extended kalman filter with a mixed state space that allows incorporation of both the object's dynamics and the measurement noise in the image plane. using error-propagation techniques, we predict a collision if the 3σ-ellipsoid along the predicted trajectory intersects with a safety sphere around the quadrotor. we experimentally demonstrate that our method allows initiating evasive maneuvers early enough to avoid collisions.
abstract2: state immunity is an important public international law issue to china which places more emphases on state sovereignty in its international law practice. fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo & ors therefore is exceptionally important to affirm china's stance on the state immunity doctrine. once again, as confirmed in this case, the absolute state immunity doctrine is firmly applied in such cases that involve china (including hong kong and macau) unless there is an explicit waiver.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel method for ensuring the safety of quadrotor-based aerial application systems in agricultural environments, specifically for the treatment of cereal crops with antibacterial and antimould compositions. building on the event-based vision sensing technology from paper 1, we integrate a dynamic vision sensor (dvs) with a quadrotor platform to track the trajectory of spherical objects, such as crop-protecting drones or applicators, in real-time. we enhance the existing probabilistic trackers with knowledge of the chemical composition and physical properties of the antibacterial and antimould compounds, as described in the intermediate idea. this integrated system predicts potential collisions with the quadrotor and the treated crops, utilizing an extended kalman filter to estimate the 3σ-ellipsoid along the predicted trajectory. to address the legal and regulatory implications of this technology, we apply the principles of state immunity doctrine, as discussed in paper 2, to ensure that the quadrotor's operation complies with international law. our experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of this integrated system in preventing collisions and ensuring the safe application of crop-protection agents, while respecting the sovereignty of agricultural operations in international environments.
cot: this paper presents an interdisciplinary approach to ensure the sovereignty and safety of chinese quadrotor-based aerial vehicles (uavs) operating in international airspace. we develop a collision prediction system using dynamic vision sensors (dvs) to estimate the trajectory of spherical objects in real-time. inspired by the absolute state immunity doctrine applied in international law, particularly in china, we propose a novel framework to integrate the dvs-based collision prediction system with a state sovereignty-aware decision-making framework. this framework enables the uav to initiate evasive maneuvers while respecting the principles of state sovereignty and immunity, as mandated by international law. we demonstrate the feasibility of our approach through simulations and experiments, showcasing its potential to safeguard the safety and sovereignty of chinese uavs in international operations. our work contributes to the development of autonomous systems that balance technological advancements with international law and diplomacy.
tot: we propose a novel, interdisciplinary approach to establishing safe operation zones for quadrotor-based systems in international airspace, particularly in regions where state sovereignty is emphasized. by integrating dynamic vision sensors (dvs) with probabilistic tracking and extended kalman filters, as described in paper1, we can enhance the quadrotor's real-time collision avoidance capabilities. however, the efficacy of such systems is contingent upon the regulatory frameworks governing international airspace use. leveraging the legal precedents outlined in paper2, specifically the absolute state immunity doctrine, we demonstrate how our dvs-based collision avoidance system can be deployed in regions with high state sovereignty, such as china, hong kong, and macau. by navigating the intersection of technological innovation and international law, this research facilitates the development of quadrotor-based systems that can operate safely and efficiently in a variety of global contexts, subject to explicit waivers and regulatory approvals.
raw: this paper presents a novel approach to ensuring quadrotor safety in international jurisdictional waters, where state immunity laws may complicate liability in the event of a collision. we integrate a dynamic vision sensing (dvs) system with probabilistic object tracking and extended kalman filtering to predict collisions with spherical objects. by leveraging the high temporal resolution of dvs sensors, our method estimates object trajectories with minimal latency, allowing for early initiation of evasive maneuvers. in a novel application of this technology, we explore the use of state immunity laws as a mechanism for liability transfer in cases of quadrotor collisions. specifically, we investigate the implications of china's stance on absolute state immunity in fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo and its potential impact on quadrotor operators in international waters. our results demonstrate the feasibility of using dvs-based collision prediction to inform quadrotor operators of potential liability risks, and highlight the importance of considering state immunity laws in the development of safe and responsible autonomous aerial vehicle systems.
one_shot: the integration of dynamic vision sensors (dvs) and probabilistic tracking methods for quadrotor navigation can be extended to address legal and regulatory complexities in unmanned aerial vehicle (uav) operations. in regions with strict state immunity laws, such as china, quadrotors equipped with dvs-based collision avoidance systems may be subject to jurisdictional restrictions. we propose a novel approach to ensure compliance with international laws and regulations, leveraging the quadrotor's real-time collision prediction capabilities to dynamically adapt to changing environmental conditions and jurisdictions. our method utilizes the quadrotor's dvs-based collision prediction to inform the selection of safe flight corridors and altitudes, thereby reducing the risk of collisions and potential liability under state immunity laws.
few_shot: we propose an integrated framework for ensuring state sovereignty in unmanned aerial vehicle (uav) navigation. by leveraging dynamic vision sensors (dvs) to track objects in real-time, our method predicts potential collisions with minimal latency. building on probabilistic tracking and extended kalman filter techniques, we estimate the object's trajectory and anticipate collisions. in international law contexts, state immunity principles, such as those upheld in fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo & ors, emphasize the importance of respecting state sovereignty. our approach can be adapted for uav operation in regions where state immunity is a concern, such as china, hong kong, and macau. by integrating dvs-based collision prediction with state immunity guidelines, we provide a novel solution for responsible uav navigation in sensitive international environments.
got: ensuring the safety of unmanned aerial vehicles (uavs) in international airspace requires not only advanced navigation systems but also a deeper understanding of their legal implications. this paper integrates computer vision and public international law to develop a novel framework for predicting and mitigating collisions between uavs and objects in international airspace. building on recent advances in dynamic vision sensors (dvs), we propose a collision prediction method that leverages the high temporal resolution of dvs to estimate object trajectories with minimal latency. to address the legal implications of such collisions, we incorporate the principles of state immunity into our framework, ensuring compliance with international law when operating uavs in foreign jurisdictions. specifically, we develop a probabilistic model that accounts for both the object's dynamics and the measurement noise in the image plane, while also considering the absolute state immunity doctrine in cases where explicit waivers are not provided. our approach enables uav operators to initiate evasive maneuvers in a timely and legally compliant manner, paving the way for the safe and efficient operation of uavs in international airspace.
