abstract1: with the upcoming transition from petascale to exascale computers radically new methods for scalable and robust computing are required. computing at the speed of exascale, that is, more than 1018 floating point operations per second, will only be possible on systems with millions of processing units. unfortunately, the large number of functional components like computing cores, memory chips and network interfaces will greatly increase the probability of failures, and it can thus not be expected that an exascale application will complete its execution on exactly the same resources it was started. in this paper, we investigate the impact of unfavorable process placement and oversubscription of compute resources on the performance and scalability of typical application workloads like cp2k, mom5 and bqcd. we provide results on two hpc architectures, a cray xc40 with proprietary aries network routers and dragonfly topology, and an infiniband cluster.
abstract2: analyzing and characterizing the phonocardiogram (pcg) signal is important for diagnosing the valvular heart disease. the pcg can record heart sounds, noise and the additional sounds. since pcg is recording the heart sound, it is important to analyze the clear pcg input signal only. the analyzation of the pcg signal will be consisting of segmenting the signal into s1 and s2 and then compare, whether the pcg is normal or abnormal. in the existing system the wavelet decomposition approach is used to analyze the pcg signal. features are extracted from a pcg signal in frequency domain to classify signals. in the proposed approach the feature selection reduces features provided for classification. coiflet is used for feature extraction, and different feature selection statistical methods are used. information gain (ig), mutual information (mi) etc. feature selection methods are compared using classifiers like knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms. in this paper, two methods are used to analyze the pcg and the accuracy of the information gain (ig) is improved when compared to mutual information (mi).
gen_abs: the advent of exascale computing poses significant challenges for robust and scalable computing systems, where hardware failures and resource oversubscription may occur frequently. however, leveraging the increasingly complex task of analyzing biomedical signals, such as phonocardiograms (pcgs), could provide a unique opportunity to develop novel fault-tolerant and adaptive methods for exascale computing. inspired by the usa hire testing platform, which ensures the integrity and security of hiring processes by limiting access to authorized personnel, we propose an adaptive, secure, and scalable framework for pcg signal analysis on exascale systems. our approach employs a multi-agent system, where each agent represents a compute unit on the exascale platform, and a feature selection method based on information gain (ig) to reduce the dimensionality of pcg signals. to ensure robustness against hardware failures, we develop a distributed coiflet-based wavelet decomposition approach, which enables real-time feature extraction and classification of pcg signals. our framework demonstrates improved accuracy and scalability on various pcg datasets, showcasing the potential of leveraging biomedical signal analysis to develop novel fault-tolerant and adaptive methods for exascale computing.
cot: high-performance computing (hpc) will be crucial for analyzing and characterizing large datasets from various domains, including biomedical signals like the phonocardiogram (pcg). however, the upcoming transition to exascale computing poses challenges due to increased component failures and resource variability. this paper integrates research from scalable hpc and signal processing to develop a novel framework for robust analysis of pcg signals. we investigate the impact of process placement and resource oversubscription on the performance and scalability of pcg signal analysis using wavelet decomposition and feature extraction methods like coiflet. we employ a range of feature selection methods, including information gain and mutual information, to reduce dimensionality and improve classification accuracy using machine learning algorithms like knn, naive bayes, and svms. our results show that the proposed framework can achieve higher accuracy and robustness in pcg signal analysis on exascale systems, paving the way for large-scale biomedical signal processing applications on next-generation hpc architectures.
tot: as high-performance computing (hpc) systems transition to exascale, ensuring robust and scalable operations amidst increasing hardware failures becomes paramount. this paper addresses a novel challenge at the intersection of hpc and biomedical signal processing: developing a fault-tolerant framework for analyzing phonocardiogram (pcg) signals in the presence of hardware failures. building on recent advances in hpc and pcg signal analysis, we propose a hybrid approach that leverages feature extraction techniques, such as coiflet wavelets, to identify robust biomarkers from pcg signals. to mitigate the impact of hardware failures, we integrate techniques from the hpc community, including process placement and oversubscription management, to ensure reliable signal analysis. our experimental results on two hpc architectures demonstrate the efficacy of this approach, achieving improved accuracy in pcg signal classification compared to traditional methods. this work bridges the gap between hpc and biomedical signal processing, opening avenues for scalable and fault-tolerant analysis of time-series biomedical data.
raw: as the exascale computing landscape emerges, applications will be subject to unprecedented hardware variability, where millions of processing units and interconnects will contribute to an increasingly complex and dynamic system. this paper investigates the relationship between robust computing and signal processing in a high-performance computing (hpc) context. we propose a novel approach to mitigate the impact of process placement and oversubscription on hpc applications, leveraging insights from signal processing and machine learning. specifically, we explore the use of feature selection and extraction techniques, commonly employed in bio-signal analysis, to identify and prioritize relevant application-level metrics and signals. by analyzing and classifying performance anomalies in real-time, our approach enables adaptive and self-aware computing, where the system can dynamically adjust to changing hardware conditions. we demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach on a set of hpc architectures and applications, including cp2k and mom5, and discuss the potential for broader applications in fields such as medicine and finance.
one_shot: as computing systems transition to exascale, the likelihood of failures and resource oversubscription increases, necessitating more robust and scalable methods for hpc applications. in the field of medical diagnostics, signal analysis plays a crucial role in diagnosing conditions like valvular heart disease, where phonocardiogram (pcg) signals must be accurately processed to distinguish between normal and abnormal readings. this paper proposes a novel approach to analyzing pcg signals using coiflet features and statistical feature selection methods, which can improve classification accuracy. we apply this approach to hpc systems with millions of processing units, exploring how it can facilitate efficient and reliable signal processing under conditions of resource variability and unpredictability. our results demonstrate the feasibility of integrating signal processing and hpc techniques to advance the diagnosis of heart conditions, providing a scalable and robust solution for medical diagnostics in the exascale era.
few_shot: as high-performance computing (hpc) systems transition to exascale, ensuring the robustness and scalability of applications becomes increasingly challenging due to the likelihood of hardware failures. meanwhile, analyzing medical signals, such as the phonocardiogram (pcg), is crucial for diagnosing heart diseases. this paper integrates the two domains by developing a novel approach for robust feature extraction and classification of pcg signals on exascale systems. by utilizing a distributed computing framework, we adapt and optimize wavelet-based feature extraction for exascale architectures, incorporating techniques such as feature selection and dimensionality reduction. experiments on a pcg dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in improving classification accuracy and robustness to hardware failures, showcasing the potential for scalable and fault-tolerant medical signal processing on exascale systems.
got: the advent of exascale computing poses significant challenges in ensuring the reliability and robustness of complex scientific simulations. this paper presents a novel approach to addressing these challenges by integrating machine learning techniques with error-resilient computing methods. specifically, we investigate the use of feature selection and extraction methods, inspired by the analysis of phonocardiogram signals, to identify and mitigate the impact of process placement and oversubscription on application performance. by applying wavelet decomposition and feature selection techniques, such as coiflet and information gain, to the analysis of application workloads like cp2k, mom5, and bqcd, we demonstrate improved robustness and scalability on hpc architectures. our results show that this integrated approach can significantly reduce the effects of process placement and oversubscription, leading to improved application performance and increased reliability on exascale systems. this work has important implications for the development of robust and efficient scientific simulations on emerging exascale architectures.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: a hospital big data-based innovative diagnosis and treatment decision-making support model( info button) was proposed for grass-root medical institutions in sichuan province in view of uneven distribution of its medical resources and of being " difficult and expensive to see a doctor" according to an analysis of the major health information projects and health information management. how to construct the model was elaborated with its problems pointed out.
abstract1: abstract besides having positive impact on sustainable development, natural building materials also have technical properties comparable with standard materials and important additional qualities. this article focuses on thermal, moisture and biological properties of thermal insulating materials based on vegetal and animal fibres. there is a lack of data regarding natural building materials and furthermore the properties of these types of materials can be site-dependent. therefore the natural insulating materials have been tested for thermal, moisture and biological properties. in the research array, there were following materials: treated sheep wool, raw sheep wool, wood fibre, hemp, flax, straw bale and compressed straw panel. the main examined parameters are the thermal capacity, thermal conductivity, volume density and sorption isotherm, which are stated and compared. in addition to that, the microbiological identification tests were made together with the mould growth observation. the results of all tests and measurements are stated and discussed in the article with the emphasis on the situation in the real building stock where the test bordering conditions possibly occur
abstract2: a camera configuration tool that facilitates a method for configuring and managing internet protocol (ip) cameras across a network. a software program scans a network to discover a plurality of ip cameras, wherein camera data for each of the plurality of ip cameras is retrieved and added to a camera table as a camera item that is selectable. through a graphical user interface, a user can then manage and configure the plurality of ip cameras. a web interface access request, restart request, live feed request, and reset request can be sent to a specific camera from the plurality of ip cameras through the graphical user interface in addition to adjusting image settings and transmission settings, and uploading a firmware file to the specific camera. the camera data for the plurality of ip cameras can also be exported to a video management software, spreadsheet file, or text file.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel approach to monitoring and managing hospital environments using a big data-driven framework that integrates thermal comfort analysis and ip camera configuration. building on the thermal properties of natural building materials, we developed a data-driven model to optimize hospital interior environments, addressing the challenges of uneven medical resource distribution in rural areas. the proposed framework employs a camera configuration tool to monitor and manage hospital spaces, leveraging ip cameras to collect real-time data on temperature, humidity, and air quality. this data is then fed into a machine learning algorithm that utilizes thermal comfort analysis to identify areas of improvement and provide personalized recommendations for patients and staff. our approach not only enhances patient comfort and experience but also contributes to the reduction of healthcare-associated infections by identifying and mitigating environmental factors that contribute to microbial growth. this study demonstrates the potential of interdisciplinary research to drive innovation in healthcare infrastructure and management.
cot: the integration of sustainable building practices with advanced monitoring and management systems is crucial for optimizing energy efficiency and indoor environmental quality in buildings. this study explores the potential of natural building materials, such as thermally insulating materials based on vegetal and animal fibres, as alternatives to standard building materials. a comprehensive evaluation of the thermal, moisture, and biological properties of these materials, including treated sheep wool, raw sheep wool, wood fibre, hemp, flax, straw bale, and compressed straw panel, was conducted. the results of this study were then used to develop a camera configuration tool that enables the monitoring and management of these natural building materials in real-time. the tool allows for the remote control and configuration of ip cameras installed in buildings, providing real-time data on temperature, humidity, and mould growth, which can be used to adaptively manage the building's environment and optimize the performance of the natural building materials. this integrated approach enables the creation of a more sustainable and resilient built environment, where the performance of natural building materials can be closely monitored and optimized.
tot: this interdisciplinary research study explores the application of natural building materials as a sustainable solution for thermal insulation in building construction, while leveraging internet-of-things (iot) technologies to monitor and manage their performance. by investigating the thermal, moisture, and biological properties of various natural materials, including treated sheep wool, raw sheep wool, wood fibre, hemp, flax, straw bale, and compressed straw panel, this study provides a comprehensive understanding of their potential in building insulation. a web-based camera configuration tool was developed to enable remote monitoring and management of these materials in real-time, facilitating the identification of any potential issues related to moisture, mould growth, or other biological concerns. the tool's capabilities include live feed access, image and transmission setting adjustments, and firmware updates. the results of this research aim to provide a novel framework for the integration of natural building materials with iot technologies, enabling the development of smart, sustainable, and resilient buildings that prioritize environmental well-being and occupant comfort.
raw: real-time monitoring of natural building materials' performance in real-world conditions is crucial for their widespread adoption in sustainable construction. this paper presents a novel approach to integrating thermal, moisture, and biological property analysis with camera-based monitoring of natural insulating materials in real building stock. by leveraging a camera configuration tool, we enable remote and automatic data collection from ip cameras installed in buildings containing natural materials. this setup allows for real-time monitoring of temperature, humidity, and mold growth, as well as the collection of data on material degradation and performance. we demonstrate the feasibility of this approach using a case study of various vegetal and animal fibre insulating materials, including treated sheep wool, wood fibre, hemp, flax, straw bale, and compressed straw panel. our results provide valuable insights into the performance and durability of natural building materials in real-world conditions, paving the way for their more effective integration into sustainable construction practices.
one_shot: this paper proposes an integrated framework for monitoring and analyzing the performance of natural building materials in real-world building stock using networked camera systems. by leveraging a camera configuration tool to collect and visualize thermal and moisture data from ip cameras installed in buildings, we enable the detection of early signs of material degradation and biological growth, such as mold and mildew. the system facilitates real-time monitoring of building conditions, facilitating data-driven decision-making for building maintenance and retrofitting. we demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach using a case study of a building equipped with a network of ip cameras and sensors, analyzing the thermal and moisture performance of natural insulation materials such as sheep wool and flax.
few_shot: we investigate the feasibility of using novel, natural building materials as a sustainable solution for monitoring and optimizing building performance and energy efficiency. our research integrates thermal, moisture, and biological properties of vegetal and animal fibres with a camera configuration tool for ip cameras. by leveraging the thermal capacity, thermal conductivity, and sorption isotherm of natural insulating materials, such as treated sheep wool, wood fibre, hemp, flax, straw bale, and compressed straw panel, we create a predictive model for monitoring building energy usage and indoor climate conditions. the camera configuration tool is used to collect and analyze environmental data from ip cameras, enabling real-time monitoring and adaptive adjustments to improve energy efficiency and reduce indoor air quality issues. this integrated system enables building managers to optimize energy consumption, minimize waste, and promote sustainable development in the built environment.
got: this study integrates the fields of sustainable building materials and computer vision to develop a novel monitoring system for natural building materials in real-world conditions. by leveraging a camera configuration tool to manage and configure ip cameras, we can collect high-resolution images and thermal data of natural insulating materials in various environments. our research focuses on seven natural materials, including treated sheep wool, raw sheep wool, wood fibre, hemp, flax, straw bale, and compressed straw panel. we utilize the camera tool to capture images and thermal data, which are then analyzed using computer vision algorithms to determine the thermal capacity, thermal conductivity, and volume density of each material. the collected data is also used to predict the sorption isotherm and identify potential microbiological issues, such as mould growth. our results provide valuable insights into the performance of natural building materials in real-world conditions, enabling architects, engineers, and builders to make informed decisions about sustainable building design and maintenance. this research has the potential to revolutionize the way we monitor and manage natural building materials, leading to more efficient and sustainable building practices.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: in china, collective disputes on unlawful dismissal compensation resulted from lock-out or workplace relocation are increasing. however a reasonable compensatory standard doesn’t take shape because regulations in labor contract law of prc don’t reflect the formation mechanism of compensation accurately. the paper try to make a case study based on a cooperative game model, and draw the conclusion that it is risk preference of bargaining parities impacts the compensatory amount.
abstract1: social curation is the process through which the general public, rather than web publishers themselves, tag, organize, and share content with others. in this paper, i focus on the case of pinterest, an image-based social curation site. i specifically highlight how the individual acts of collection on the site contribute to a new kind of public construction of knowledge online. this is specific to pinterest, i argue, because the way that the site is set up—both in terms of its technological and business structure. my goals for the paper are as follows. first, i question how pinterest’s notion of curation compares with traditional or already existing notions of professional curation and personal collection. then, i offer an analysis of the activity on pinterest; whereas users believe they are participating in personal acts of collection and reflexive identity building, i counteract this with an examination of the entire business “ecosystem” of the site, showing how these personal activities can also be unde...
abstract2: the present invention discloses a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment, whose active components are complexed by lucid ganoderma polysaccharide, lycium barbarum polysaccharide and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide, the mass proportion ratio of each component is lucid ganoderma polysaccharide 10-250, lycium barbarum polysaccharide 1-50, polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide 2-10. the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide is high in purity and activity, has an efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment. the present invention also discloses the preparation method of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, said method is simple, stable and efficient in the process, suitable for industrial production and economic. in addition, the present invention further discloses the use of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide in the preparation of health-care food having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment.
gen_abs: title: co-curating health: a participatory approach to developing personalized polysaccharide-based therapies for tumor regulation this paper brings together the disciplines of social curation and polysaccharide-based therapeutics to develop a novel approach to personalized medicine. building on the concept of social curation on platforms like pinterest, we propose a co-curation model where patients, healthcare professionals, and researchers collaborate to develop and refine personalized polysaccharide-based therapies for tumor regulation. using a cooperative game model, we demonstrate that the risk preference of bargaining parties can significantly impact the compensatory amount of these therapies, highlighting the need for a participatory approach to address collective action problems in healthcare. we combine the computational power of social curation with the efficacy of lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharides to develop a modular, adaptable system for designing and testing these therapies. our approach has the potential to revolutionize the field of personalized medicine by empowering patients and healthcare professionals to co-create effective, accessible, and affordable treatments for cancer.
cot: this interdisciplinary study explores the intersection of social curation and traditional medicine through the lens of social media platforms and their potential impact on health outcomes. building on the concept of social curation as presented in the case of pinterest, we examine how online communities curate and share health-related content, including traditional remedies and natural products such as the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide. our investigation reveals that social curation on platforms like pinterest can facilitate the discovery and dissemination of traditional medicine, influencing public perceptions of health and wellness. by analyzing the content and user interactions on these platforms, we identify patterns and networks that can inform the development of novel health-care products, such as the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide. this study provides a framework for understanding the role of social media in shaping public health narratives and highlights the potential for social curation to contribute to the development of evidence-based traditional medicine.
tot: this interdisciplinary study bridges the gap between social media and bioactive compounds by examining the intersection of social curation on platforms like pinterest and the efficacy of complex polysaccharides in regulating tumor microenvironments. we propose that the act of social curation, particularly on image-based platforms like pinterest, can influence the construction of public knowledge and shapes individual perceptions of health and wellness. by analyzing the activities on pinterest, we reveal how users' personal acts of collection and sharing of health-related content can be influenced by the site's business ecosystem, leading to the dissemination of information that may not be grounded in scientific evidence. conversely, we explore the potential of polysaccharides from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum to regulate tumor microenvironments, and examine the possibility of harnessing these compounds in the development of health-care foods. our research aims to investigate the convergence of these two domains, shedding light on the complex relationships between public knowledge construction, social media, and the efficacy of bioactive compounds in tumor regulation.
raw: this study explores the intersection of social curation and biotechnology in the development of personalized health-care remedies. building on the pioneering work of social curation on platforms like pinterest, we investigate how user-driven curation of health-related content influences the co-creation of knowledge and decision-making processes. specifically, we examine how the collective act of pinning and sharing health-oriented content on pinterest can shape public perceptions of health-related information and influence individual choices regarding health-care. this study combines a critical discourse analysis of pinterest's user-generated content with an investigation of the biological efficacy of specific health-related compounds, such as the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, which have been shown to regulate tumor microenvironment. by integrating insights from social curation and biotechnology, we identify novel opportunities for the development of personalized health-care remedies that leverage social media platforms and evidence-based biotechnology. our findings have implications for the future of health-care decision-making and the role of user-generated content in shaping health-related knowledge and practices.
one_shot: social curation platforms like pinterest have transformed the way individuals collect, organize, and share knowledge online, challenging traditional notions of curation and personal collection. however, the commercial ecosystem of these platforms often obscures the underlying dynamics of user engagement. we propose a novel approach to understanding the interplay between social curation and health-related information dissemination. by analyzing the sharing and collection behaviors on pinterest, we identify patterns of health-related content organization and dissemination, revealing how users construct and negotiate health information within online communities. this analysis is integrated with a computational modeling framework, which simulates the efficacy of traditional chinese medicine compounds, such as the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, in regulating tumor microenvironments. our results demonstrate that social curation can significantly influence the spread and perceived efficacy of health-related information, with implications for personalized medicine and public health education.
few_shot: we investigate how social curation on image-based platforms like pinterest influences the discovery and dissemination of health-related information, particularly in the context of cancer diagnosis and treatment. by analyzing user-generated content and the pinterest ecosystem, we identify patterns of knowledge construction and sharing that can inform the development of targeted health interventions. we then explore the potential of a novel polysaccharide complex, derived from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum, to regulate tumor microenvironments and enhance treatment outcomes. by integrating insights from social media analysis and natural product chemistry, our research aims to develop evidence-based strategies for harnessing the power of social curation in health communication and improving cancer care.
got: a novel interdisciplinary approach is proposed to design and develop personalized health-care food products that regulate tumor microenvironments. by integrating the principles of social curation from online platforms like pinterest with the efficacy of polysaccharide complexes from natural sources, this research aims to create a crowdsourced, community-driven framework for identifying and producing health-promoting food products. the study builds on the concept of social curation, where individuals collect and share content online, and applies it to the selection and combination of natural compounds with proven anti-tumor properties. using the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide as a case study, the research will investigate how online communities can contribute to the discovery and development of novel health-care food products. the outcomes of this research will have significant implications for personalized nutrition, health-care food industry, and the role of online communities in shaping health-related knowledge and practices.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: this paper summarizes and compares some of the techniques that have been used over the past 50 years to detect and track slow moving surface targets in dense clutter environments from a high speed airborne radar platform. starting from non-coherent single port gmti radar implementations, airborne gmti radars have undergone an evolutionary development to arrive at the modern coherent multiple port gmti configurations that have now become the standard throughout the industry.
abstract1: the frequency with which melioidosis results from inhalation rather than percutaneous inoculation or ingestion is unknown. we recovered burkholderia pseudomallei from air samples at the residence of a patient with presumptive inhalational melioidosis and used whole-genome sequencing to link the environmental bacteria to b. pseudomallei recovered from the patient.
abstract2: the invention provides a sleep deprivation model system with simulation narrow and small spaces for pilots. the sleep deprivation model system comprises the closed spaces and a tested screening system. the tested screening system is arranged in the closed spaces and comprises a cognitive function assessment subsystem, a sleep quality assessment subsystem, an emotion assessment subsystem, a social function assessment subsystem and a sleep detection device; the cognitive function assessment subsystem is used for assessing cognitive behavior and cognitive functions of testees; the sleep quality assessment subsystem is used for assessing sleep behavior of the testees; the emotion assessment subsystem is used for assessing emotion indexes of the testees; the social function assessment subsystem is used for assessing social parameters of the testees; the sleep detection device is used for detecting sleep parameters of the testees. according to the technical scheme, the sleep deprivation model system has the advantage of accurate detection data.
gen_abs: this study integrates the disciplines of microbiology and sleep science to develop a novel sleep deprivation model system for pilots exposed to airborne pathogens. building on the discovery that melioidosis can be contracted through inhalation, we adapted airborne ground moving target indication (gmti) radar technology to detect and track airborne pathogens, such as burkholderia pseudomallei. we designed a sleep deprivation model system with simulated narrow and small spaces for pilots, incorporating a cognitive function assessment subsystem, sleep quality assessment subsystem, emotion assessment subsystem, social function assessment subsystem, and a sleep detection device. the system utilizes a coherent multiple port gmti configuration to track airborne pathogens in real-time, providing accurate detection data for both pathogens and pilot physiological responses. this integrated system enables the assessment of sleep quality and cognitive function in pilots exposed to airborne pathogens, with implications for occupational health, infectious disease control, and aviation safety.
cot: pilots working in high-stress, confined environments are at risk of sleep deprivation, which can lead to impaired cognitive function, decreased reaction time, and increased error rates. we propose an interdisciplinary research project to investigate the relationship between melioidosis, a disease caused by burkholderia pseudomallei, and sleep deprivation in pilots. we aim to design a controlled study where pilots are exposed to a sleep deprivation model system, similar to the one described in paper 2, while being monitored for melioidosis exposure through whole-genome sequencing of environmental air samples, as demonstrated in paper 1. by examining the effects of sleep deprivation on the immune system and the subsequent risk of melioidosis infection, we can gain insights into the biological mechanisms underlying the relationship between sleep, stress, and disease. this research has the potential to improve pilot health and safety, as well as provide new knowledge on the interactions between sleep, stress, and disease susceptibility.
tot: this study investigates the effects of sleep deprivation on the susceptibility and exposure to burkholderia pseudomallei, the bacterium responsible for melioidosis, in a simulated environment. we adapted a sleep deprivation model system, typically used to assess pilots' cognitive and emotional states, to a controlled setting that mimics the living quarters of individuals in endemic regions. participants underwent sleep deprivation in a small, enclosed space with air sampling, and their cognitive and emotional states were monitored using a comprehensive assessment system. meanwhile, burkholderia pseudomallei was introduced into the environment, and air samples were collected to assess the presence of the bacteria. our results show that sleep-deprived individuals exhibit increased susceptibility to melioidosis, as evidenced by a higher frequency of b. pseudomallei isolation from air samples. moreover, the sleep deprivation model system provided accurate detection of individual cognitive and emotional states, which correlated with the risk of melioidosis exposure. these findings have significant implications for the prevention and management of melioidosis in high-risk populations, and suggest that sleep deprivation is a critical factor in the transmission dynamics of this disease.
raw: melioidosis, a disease caused by burkholderia pseudomallei, can result from inhalation, but the incidence of this route of infection is poorly understood. to investigate this, we integrated whole-genome sequencing with an innovative sleep deprivation model system to investigate the link between environmental exposure and inhalational melioidosis. the sleep deprivation model system, designed for pilots, consists of a controlled environment with a tested screening system that assesses cognitive, sleep, emotion, social, and sleep quality parameters. we used this system to simulate the sleep deprivation experienced by pilots and assessed the impact on immune function and respiratory susceptibility to b. pseudomallei. we found that sleep deprivation significantly impaired immune response and increased respiratory susceptibility to b. pseudomallei, suggesting a novel mechanism by which melioidosis may be acquired through inhalation. our findings indicate that sleep deprivation could be a significant risk factor for melioidosis in high-risk individuals, such as pilots, and highlight the need for further research on the relationship between sleep, immune function, and infection risk. this study provides new insights into the transmission of melioidosis and has implications for occupational health and disease prevention in high-risk populations.
one_shot: melioidosis, a bacterial disease caused by burkholderia pseudomallei, poses a significant threat to human health. inhalation of the bacteria is a critical route of transmission, but the frequency and circumstances of such events are poorly understood. to elucidate the relationship between environmental exposure and inhalational melioidosis, we investigate the potential role of indoor environments in spreading the disease. using a sleep deprivation model system, specifically designed to simulate conditions encountered by pilots in small, enclosed spaces, we explore the impact of sleep deprivation on cognitive and behavioral responses to environmental exposure. our innovative approach combines whole-genome sequencing of environmental air samples with a sophisticated sleep and cognitive assessment system to identify correlations between sleep quality, cognitive performance, and the presence of b. pseudomallei in indoor air. this interdisciplinary study aims to inform strategies for mitigating the risk of inhalational melioidosis in high-risk populations.
few_shot: we investigate the impact of sleep deprivation on immune function and disease susceptibility, specifically melioidosis, an infectious disease caused by burkholderia pseudomallei. using a novel sleep deprivation model system, we simulate the effects of prolonged wakefulness on cognitive function, sleep quality, and social behavior in a controlled environment. by exposing participants to b. pseudomallei aerosol in a controlled setting, we demonstrate that sleep deprivation significantly increases the risk of inhalational melioidosis, highlighting the importance of adequate sleep in immune system regulation. our findings have implications for the development of targeted interventions to mitigate the effects of sleep deprivation on immune function and disease susceptibility.
got: melioidosis, a disease caused by the bacterium burkholderia pseudomallei, can be transmitted through inhalation, percutaneous inoculation, or ingestion, but the relative frequency of these routes is unknown. this study aimed to investigate the environmental sources of inhalational melioidosis and the effects of environmental exposure on human behavior. we combined whole-genome sequencing of environmental bacteria recovered from the residence of a patient with presumptive inhalational melioidosis with a sleep deprivation model system designed to mimic the effects of working in confined spaces, such as military aircraft cabins. the sleep deprivation model system, comprising a closed space and a multi-modal assessment system, allowed us to evaluate cognitive, emotional, social, and sleep quality parameters of test subjects in a simulated sleep-deprived state. we found that exposure to b. pseudomallei in the environmental samples was linked to the patient's exposure, and that the sleep deprivation model system detected significant impairments in cognitive function and sleep quality among test subjects. our findings suggest that environmental exposure to b. pseudomallei may exacerbate the effects of sleep deprivation in individuals, highlighting the need for improved protective measures and early detection of melioidosis in high-risk populations.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: to oretical calculation and finite element analysis in view of the spline-shaft for cutting part of sun wheel of shearer, so as to find out the causes of the failure and deformation,and put forward the way of increasing the spline shaft diameter and the spline tooth number and changing the heat treatment proess. the practice proved that: the improved strength of spline shaft of the sun wheel can meet the requirements of working conditions.
abstract1: the present invention discloses a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment, whose active components are complexed by lucid ganoderma polysaccharide, lycium barbarum polysaccharide and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide, the mass proportion ratio of each component is lucid ganoderma polysaccharide 10-250, lycium barbarum polysaccharide 1-50, polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide 2-10. the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide is high in purity and activity, has an efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment. the present invention also discloses the preparation method of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, said method is simple, stable and efficient in the process, suitable for industrial production and economic. in addition, the present invention further discloses the use of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide in the preparation of health-care food having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment.
abstract2: receiving, passing and dribbling the ball in football are the main components of the most popular sports game in the world. these elements are allowing the football players, or teams, to meaningfully and practically organize their own action in attacking or in defensive part of their game. today there is an extremely large number of exercises by which these elements can be improved, and their learning, and after upgrading, are closely related, and learning one element automatically requires learning a second and after a third element. the aim of this study was to describe the training and exercises in which football players can adopt, improve and automate the handover and dribbling.
gen_abs: we propose a novel approach to develop intelligent sports nutrition products that enhance athletic performance by regulating the tumor microenvironment. building on the complex polysaccharide from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum, we integrate finite element analysis and spline-shaft design principles from the intermediate idea to create a customized, high-performance sports nutrition supplement. this supplement is designed to optimize the strength and durability of the spleen, a critical organ for immune function and adaptation to exercise. by combining the polysaccharide complex with a personalized, data-driven approach to sports training, as inspired by the research on football players' learning and improvement of dribbling and passing skills, we aim to create a synergistic effect that enhances athletes' immune function, reduces inflammation, and improves overall performance. our interdisciplinary research project has the potential to revolutionize the field of sports nutrition and provide a new paradigm for optimizing athletic performance and overall health.
cot: this study explores the synergistic relationship between polysaccharide complexes and motor skill learning in the context of sports medicine. we investigate the efficacy of a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide (lgpl) in enhancing football players' ability to master ball-control skills, such as passing and dribbling. by analyzing the molecular structure and activity of lgpl, we identify key components that may facilitate motor learning and plasticity. our results demonstrate that lgpl enhances neuronal activity and synaptic transmission in brain areas responsible for motor control, leading to improved ball-control skills. furthermore, we develop a novel training protocol that combines lgpl supplementation with specific exercises to optimize motor skill acquisition and retention. our findings have significant implications for sports medicine and rehabilitation, as well as for the development of novel therapeutic approaches to enhance motor function in individuals with neurological disorders.
tot: the integration of cutting-edge polysaccharide research and sports science has led to a groundbreaking discovery. a novel approach to enhancing athletic performance in high-intensity sports, such as football, involves the use of a natural complex polysaccharide derived from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum. this complex polysaccharide, when administered to football players, has been shown to modify the tumor microenvironment in the brain, promoting neuroplasticity and optimizing neural connections. specifically, the complex polysaccharide enhances the efficiency of receiving, passing, and dribbling skills, allowing for improved coordination, reaction time, and decision-making. the polysaccharide's efficacy is attributed to its unique composition, comprising a mass proportion ratio of lucid ganoderma polysaccharide (10-250), lycium barbarum polysaccharide (1-50), and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide (2-10). this breakthrough research suggests that the administration of this complex polysaccharide can be a key factor in the development of elite athletic performance, and its implications for sports medicine and neurology are vast.
raw: this study integrates the fields of polysaccharide research and sports science to develop a novel, evidence-based approach to enhancing athletic performance and promoting overall health. building on the efficacy of the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide (lgplps) in regulating tumor microenvironments, we investigated its potential application in optimizing football players' handover and dribbling skills. our hypothesis is that the immunomodulatory and antioxidant properties of lgplps can enhance muscle function, reduce inflammation, and improve motor coordination, thereby facilitating more efficient ball control and movement on the field. we designed a randomized controlled trial to examine the effects of lgplps supplementation on football players' handover and dribbling performance. our results show significant improvements in ball control, speed, and agility among the supplemented group, accompanied by enhanced antioxidant status and reduced muscle damage markers. the lgplps complex appears to promote a favorable microenvironment for motor skill acquisition and refinement, offering a new paradigm for optimizing athletic performance and potentially translating to other sports and physical activities.
one_shot: we propose an innovative interdisciplinary approach to optimize football training through the use of polysaccharide complexes derived from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum. these polysaccharides, known for their efficacy in regulating tumor microenvironments, can be employed as a novel, evidence-based tool to enhance athlete recovery and performance. by integrating the complex's anti-inflammatory properties with football-specific exercises, such as passing, dribbling, and ball control, we aim to develop a tailored training program that mitigates muscle damage, accelerates rehabilitation, and improves overall physical fitness. our hypothesis is that this approach will enable football players to adopt and automate complex motor skills more efficiently, leading to enhanced team performance and reduced injury risk.
few_shot: we design a novel, interdisciplinary approach to optimize the performance of football players through a polysaccharide-based, lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum (glp) complex. by harnessing the bioactive properties of this complex, we develop a personalized training program that integrates football skills with nutritional interventions. our method leverages machine learning algorithms to analyze player data, simulating the complex interactions between physical performance and nutritional response. the glp complex is used as a bioactive agent to enhance muscle recovery, reduce inflammation, and improve cognitive function, thus enabling players to adapt and automate their skills in receiving, passing, and dribbling. through a combination of in vitro experiments and football training simulations, our approach demonstrates improved player performance, reduced injury rates, and enhanced team cohesion. this integrated framework has the potential to revolutionize the way football training is conducted, providing a data-driven, holistic approach to athlete development.
got: this study investigates the development of a novel, polysaccharide-based dietary intervention to enhance football performance and its potential anti-tumor effects. we synthesized a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide (lgplps) with optimized mass proportion ratios. our results show that lgplps significantly improves handover and dribbling skills in football players by enhancing their ability to meaningfully and practically organize their action in the game. moreover, our in vitro studies demonstrate that lgplps has efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment, making it a promising candidate for anti-tumor therapy. we propose a new model of sports performance enhancement, where dietary interventions can be used to improve motor skills and potentially reduce the risk of cancer. this study provides the first evidence for the effectiveness of lgplps in enhancing football performance and its anti-tumor effects, opening up new avenues for interdisciplinary research in sports nutrition and cancer therapy.

abstract1: in this paper we present a channel measurement campaign in indoor environment aiming the characterization of the angular properties of the channel in the presence of the human body. measurements were performed by using isolated uwb antenna and body-mounted antenna on different positions. multipath components are estimated by means of sage algorithm. from these estimations, clusters are detected and characterized in the delay and angular domains.
abstract2: the present invention relates to a natural vagina cleanser for women, more specifically, which is produced by mixing 55-70 wt% of bamboo salt, 10-15 wt% of allium fistulosum l. powder, 10-15 wt% of plantaginis semen powder, and 10-1 wt%of portulaca oleracea l. powder, thereby providing excellent cleanliness and causing less skin irritation cleanser due to a cytostatic action (an action killing only harmful bacteria excluding normal bacteria necessary for body) of natural ingredients, compared to an existing. moreover, the present invention effectively prevents vaginal diseases including vaginosis, metritis, leukorrhea, itchiness, and odor.
gen_abs: wearable wireless sensing for personal hygiene and health monitoring: a novel approach combining angular channel characterization and natural cleanser development. we propose an innovative wearable device that integrates wireless sensing and natural cleanser technology to monitor and maintain personal hygiene in real-time. by leveraging the angular channel characterization techniques presented in paper 1, we develop a wearable device that estimates blood transit time (ptt) using photoplethysmography (ppg) signals. the estimated ptt values are compared to reference values obtained from a more invasive central line. meanwhile, our device incorporates a natural cleanser, inspired by paper 2, which utilizes a blend of bamboo salt, allium fistulosum, plantaginis semen, and portulaca oleracea powders to maintain vaginal cleanliness and prevent infections. our research combines the benefits of wireless sensing and natural cleanser technology to provide a non-invasive, user-friendly solution for personal hygiene and health monitoring. this wearable device has the potential to revolutionize the way we approach personal care, particularly for individuals with compromised immune systems or those at risk of infection. our results demonstrate the feasibility and efficacy of this integrated approach, paving the way for future commercial applications.
tot: this study investigates the impact of a novel vaginal cleanser, comprising a mixture of bamboo salt, allium fistulosum l., plantaginis semen, and portulaca oleracea l. powders, on the indoor wireless channel characteristics in the presence of the human body. building upon previous works in channel modeling and natural ingredient-based cleansers, we hypothesize that the unique antimicrobial properties of the vaginal cleanser may influence the multipath components and angular properties of the indoor wireless channel. to test this hypothesis, we conduct a comprehensive measurement campaign using body-mounted and isolated uwb antennas in various positions, and employ the sage algorithm to estimate multipath components and detect clusters in the delay and angular domains. our results provide valuable insights into the interplay between the vaginal cleanser's antimicrobial properties and the indoor wireless channel characteristics, with potential implications for the development of more effective and safe wireless communication systems in indoor environments.
raw: this paper presents a novel approach to developing wearable technologies for vaginal health monitoring and maintenance. by integrating advances in ultra-wideband (uwb) channel measurement and multipath estimation, we design and prototype a body-mounted antenna system for characterizing the angular properties of the vaginal channel. our system uses the spectral angle grouping (sage) algorithm to identify and track multipath components and clusters in the delay and angular domains. by analyzing the spatial and temporal characteristics of the vaginal channel, we can better understand the impact of wearable devices on vaginal health. to this end, we develop a natural vaginal cleanser based on a synergistic blend of bamboo salt, allium fistulosum, plantaginis semen, and portulaca oleracea powders, which has been shown to have cytostatic properties against harmful bacteria while preserving beneficial flora. our wearable device and natural cleanser are designed to work in concert to provide a comprehensive solution for vaginal health monitoring and maintenance, enabling early detection of vaginal diseases and prevention of infections. our research has the potential to improve the quality of life for women worldwide.
one_shot: the presence of the human body significantly affects wireless channel characteristics, particularly in indoor environments. by analyzing the angular properties of the channel in the presence of the human body, we can better understand the propagation mechanisms and multipath components that influence wireless communication. this knowledge is crucial for developing effective wireless communication systems that can adapt to the dynamic and complex channel conditions caused by human presence. inspired by the principles of natural body cleansing and the importance of maintaining a healthy vaginal microbiome, we propose a novel approach to develop a wireless communication system that can detect and adapt to the presence of the human body, reducing interference and improving communication quality. our system, based on a combination of channel measurement and machine learning techniques, can identify and mitigate the effects of human-induced multipath components, leading to improved wireless communication in indoor environments.
few_shot: we explore the effects of human presence on wireless channel characteristics in indoor environments, leveraging measurements of multipath components and angular properties. building on this understanding, we develop a novel approach to create a natural, human-friendly cleanser for the vaginal area, utilizing plant-based ingredients that exhibit cytostatic properties against harmful bacteria. by integrating insights from wireless channel characterization and natural product development, our method ensures excellent cleanliness and minimizes skin irritation during vaginal cleanser application. we demonstrate the efficacy of our approach through simulations and experiments, showcasing the potential for improved hygiene and reduced risk of vaginal diseases.
got: this paper presents a novel approach to developing wearable, non-invasive, and bio-inspired vaginal health monitoring and treatment systems that leverage the unique properties of the human body as a channel. by combining ultra-wideband (uwb) sensor technology with plant-based ingredients, we aim to create a wearable, portable, and non-invasive device that can monitor and analyze the angular properties of the vaginal canal in real-time, detecting changes in the environment and identifying potential risks of vaginal diseases. our approach utilizes a sage-based algorithm to estimate multipath components and characterize clusters in the delay and angular domains, allowing for the detection of early biomarkers of vaginal health. the device incorporates a non-toxic, biocompatible, and cytostatic formula, derived from natural ingredients (bamboo salt, allium fistulosum, plantaginis semen, and portulaca oleracea), that effectively prevents bacterial overgrowth and promotes a healthy vaginal microbiome, thereby reducing the risk of vaginal diseases such as vaginosis, metritis, leukorrhea, itchiness, and odor. this integrated system has the potential to revolutionize the field of gynecological health monitoring and treatment, providing a personalized, non-invasive, and effective solution for women's health.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: select adder (csla) is one of the speedest adder utilized as a part of numerous computational frameworks to perform quick number-crunching operations. the carry select adder utilizes an effective plan by imparting the common boolean logic (clb) term. the modified csla architecture building design has created utilizing binary to excess-1 converter (bec). this paper introduces an unique method that replaces the bec using common boolean logic. experimental analysis illustrates that the proposed architecture achieves advantages in terms of speed, area consumption and power.
abstract1: the present invention discloses a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment, whose active components are complexed by lucid ganoderma polysaccharide, lycium barbarum polysaccharide and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide, the mass proportion ratio of each component is lucid ganoderma polysaccharide 10-250, lycium barbarum polysaccharide 1-50, polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide 2-10. the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide is high in purity and activity, has an efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment. the present invention also discloses the preparation method of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, said method is simple, stable and efficient in the process, suitable for industrial production and economic. in addition, the present invention further discloses the use of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide in the preparation of health-care food having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment.
abstract2: the invention relates to an anti-pilling color-changing fabric which comprises a fabric body. the fabric is characterized in that a color-changing coating (2) is coated on the surface of the fabric body, the color-changing coating (2) is a thermo-sensitive compound, the fabric body comprises a surface layer, a core layer and an inner layer, the surface layer is made of real silk, the core layer is made of acron, the inner layer is made of real silk, and the core layer is made of stretch yarn obtained after warp interlacing processing. the fabric is in different colors at different temperatures, so that the color changing effect is achieved, and requirements of young people who pursue uniqueness and unconventionality are met. the fabric is anti-pilling.
gen_abs: an innovative textile-based platform is proposed for the targeted delivery of antitumor polysaccharides, exploiting the thermosensitive properties of color-changing fabrics. inspired by the anti-pilling color-changing fabric described in paper 2, we integrated a layer of lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, as disclosed in paper 1, into the fabric's color-changing coating. this polysaccharide composite exhibits optimal bioactivity and stability, allowing for controlled release at specific temperatures. by leveraging the modified carry select adder (csla) architecture introduced in the intermediate idea, we developed a novel binary-to-excess-1 converter (bec) using common boolean logic (clb), enabling efficient and accurate temperature-controlled release of the polysaccharide complex. the resulting textile platform exhibits unique color-changing properties while delivering therapeutic agents directly to tumor microenvironments. this innovative approach has significant potential for the development of smart textiles for personalized health care and cancer treatment. experimental results demonstrate the feasibility and efficacy of this concept, paving the way for further research in the field of theranostics and tissue engineering.
cot: we propose a novel approach to develop intelligent textiles for cancer therapy and diagnosis by integrating polysaccharides with thermochromic properties. by combining the tumor-regulating efficacy of lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharides with the unique color-changing properties of thermo-sensitive compounds, we aim to create smart fabrics that can detect and respond to changes in the body's microenvironment. specifically, our fabric will incorporate a color-changing coating that responds to temperature fluctuations, allowing for real-time monitoring of the body's temperature and potentially indicating the presence of cancer. the fabric's surface will be made of real silk, with a core layer of acron and an inner layer of stretch yarn, ensuring both comfort and durability. this innovative textile has the potential to revolutionize cancer diagnosis and therapy, providing a non-invasive, real-time monitoring system that can aid in early detection and treatment of the disease.
tot: we propose an innovative approach to develop a novel, fabric-based platform for targeted cancer therapy and tissue engineering, leveraging the synergistic effects of a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide (lg-lb-ps) and thermosensitive color-changing fabrics. by integrating the lg-lb-ps complex with a specially designed, temperature-responsive textile, we create a smart fabric that not only exhibits unique color-changing properties but also releases the polysaccharide complex in response to thermal stimuli. this fabric-based system, dubbed "thermopolysaccharide fabric" (tpf), exhibits a precise and tunable release of lg-lb-ps at specific temperatures, enabling localized and controlled delivery of the complex to the tumor microenvironment. the tpf system demonstrates exceptional stability, biocompatibility, and biodegradability, making it an attractive platform for targeted cancer therapy and tissue engineering applications. furthermore, the fabric's anti-pilling property ensures durable and long-term performance, while its color-changing feature allows for real-time monitoring of the release process. this pioneering approach represents a significant advancement in the development of smart biomaterials for cancer treatment and regenerative medicine.
raw: this study presents a novel, temperature-sensitive, and fabric-based delivery system for cancer therapy, leveraging the synergistic effects of a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide (lg-lb-ps) and an anti-pilling color-changing fabric. by incorporating the lg-lb-ps into the core layer of the fabric, which is comprised of acron and stretch yarn, we create a thermally responsive system that releases the polysaccharide complex in response to body temperature. the color-changing coating, a thermo-sensitive compound, is applied to the fabric surface, allowing for real-time monitoring of polysaccharide release and subsequent effects on tumor microenvironment regulation. our results demonstrate that the lg-lb-ps delivery system, integrated with the fabric platform, exhibits enhanced efficacy in regulating tumor microenvironment and promoting anti-tumor activities. the fabric's unique properties, including anti-pilling and thermochromic effects, ensure a comfortable and user-friendly experience for patients undergoing cancer treatment. this innovative approach has the potential to revolutionize cancer therapy by providing a non-invasive, real-time, and personalized treatment platform.
one_shot: we propose a novel textile-based biomaterial that combines the therapeutic properties of a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide complex with the unique properties of a color-changing fabric. by integrating the bioactive complex into a thermo-sensitive coating on a fabric body comprising a silk surface layer, an acron core layer, and a silk inner layer, we create a fabric that not only responds to temperature changes with a dynamic color shift but also exhibits anti-pilling properties. this innovative material has potential applications in health-care textiles, such as wound dressings or orthotics, where temperature regulation and comfort are crucial for patient recovery and well-being. our method enables the industrial production of this material, making it suitable for mass production and commercial use.
few_shot: this paper introduces a novel approach to regulating tumor microenvironment using a thermoresponsive, anti-pilling fabric embedded with lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharides. the fabric, designed with a silk-acron-silk core, utilizes a thermo-sensitive coating that changes color in response to temperature fluctuations, providing a unique indicator of the tumor microenvironment's response to the polysaccharide treatment. by integrating four key components - lucid ganoderma polysaccharide, lycium barbarum polysaccharide, polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide, and the thermoresponsive fabric - our system enables real-time monitoring of the tumor microenvironment's response to treatment, facilitating more effective and personalized cancer therapy.
got: we present a novel, biocompatible, and thermostabilized biomedical textile that integrates the regenerative properties of a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide with the unique temperature-sensitive properties of a color-changing fabric. this innovative material, dubbed "thermo-regulate," is designed to dynamically respond to temperature fluctuations, thereby modulating the tumor microenvironment and promoting a conducive environment for tissue regeneration. the fabric's surface layer is crafted from real silk, providing a luxurious texture and biocompatibility, while the core layer, made from acron, offers durability and thermal stability. the inner layer, composed of real silk, enables the integration of the complex polysaccharide, which is evenly distributed throughout the fabric via a novel, cost-effective coating process. the resulting fabric exhibits anti-pilling properties, making it suitable for a range of biomedical applications, including wound dressings, implantable devices, and tissue engineering scaffolds. our research demonstrates the feasibility of this technology, paving the way for the development of next-generation biomaterials that harmonize temperature responsiveness with regenerative potential.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: this work aims to realize identity recognition using images from one database [1]. this facial database is composed of 3d dynamical images or 4d considering the time component as the fourth dimension, where the head movements are removed and only the facial dynamism is considered. the identity recognition is done by an artificial intelligence method based on artificial immune system of negative selection. the performance of the immune system is satisfactory and encouraging. the obtained results are efficient, robust, precise and reliable to recognize the facial identity.
abstract1: intro: computer network defense has models for attacks and incidents comprised of multiple attacks after the fact. however, we lack an evidence-based model the likelihood and intensity of attacks and incidents. purpose: we propose a model of global capability advancement, the adversarial capability chain (acc), to fit this need. the model enables cyber risk analysis to better understand the costs for an adversary to attack a system, which directly influences the cost to defend it. method: the model is based on four historical studies of adversarial capabilities: capability to exploit windows xp, to exploit the android api, to exploit apache, and to administer compromised industrial control systems. result: we propose the acc with five phases: discovery, validation, escalation, democratization, and ubiquity. we use the four case studies as examples as to how the acc can be applied and used to predict attack likelihood and intensity.
abstract2: the invention discloses a motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence comprise s1: performing normalization processing of any given motion vector field; s2: calculating the curl and the divergence of each point in the normalized motion vector field to obtain a curl view and a divergence view; and constructing a dual vector field of the normalized motion vector field; s3: performing motion trial sampling in the normalized motion vector field and the dual vector field to obtain a tangential motion trail and a radial motion trail respectively; and s4: performing path integration of the curl and the divergence on the motion trails so as to obtain a cd (curl and divergence) motion feature with invariable scale and curl. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence can depict the curl and the divergence of motion integrally, and is high in robustness. as a motion descriptor with universality, the cd feature can be widely applied in motion analysis and behavior identification under different scenes.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel framework for predicting and analyzing the likelihood and intensity of cyber attacks on industrial control systems (ics). building on the adversarial capability chain (acc) model, we integrate a motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence to analyze the physical world interactions of attackers. the proposed framework, called motion-acc, combines the acc's five phases (discovery, validation, escalation, democratization, and ubiquity) with the motion feature extraction method's ability to extract features from motion trails. the motion features are used to predict attack likelihood and intensity, while the acc phases provide a structured approach to understanding the adversary's capabilities. we demonstrate the effectiveness of motion-acc using a case study of an ics system and show that it can accurately predict attack likelihood and intensity, providing valuable insights for cybersecurity and physical security measures. this research contributes to the growing need for integrated approaches to cyber-physical security, enabling a more comprehensive understanding of the threats faced by ics systems.
cot: advances in computing and networking have created an increasingly complex landscape of cybersecurity threats, with attacks often consisting of multiple steps and phases. however, current models of cyber risk analysis struggle to accurately predict the likelihood and intensity of these attacks. to address this, we propose a novel framework that bridges the gap between cybersecurity and computer vision. building on the adversarial capability chain (acc) model, which describes the phases of adversary capabilities, we incorporate motion feature extraction techniques from computer vision to quantify the dynamics of real-world attacks. specifically, we adapt the curl and divergence (cd) motion feature extraction method to analyze the temporal and spatial patterns of attack escalation. by integrating the acc and cd features, we introduce a novel framework for predicting the likelihood and intensity of cyber attacks by capturing the temporal evolution of adversary capabilities. this framework enables more informed decision-making in cybersecurity by providing a quantitative understanding of the costs of attacking and defending against complex cyber threats. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through case studies and simulations.
tot: we propose a novel framework for predicting and analyzing the likelihood and intensity of cyber attacks on complex systems by integrating adversarial capability chain analysis with motion feature extraction. the adversarial motion extraction and analysis (amea) framework leverages the adversarial capability chain (acc) model from paper 1 to quantify the cost for an adversary to attack a system, and combines it with the motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence from paper 2 to analyze the motion patterns of potential attacks. by applying the acc model to identify the phases of an attack (discovery, validation, escalation, democratization, and ubiquity) and the curl and divergence-based motion feature extraction to analyze the motion trails of attackers, the amea framework provides a more comprehensive understanding of the attack dynamics. this integrated approach enables the identification of high-risk attack paths and the detection of anomalies in motion patterns, thereby enhancing the defense capabilities of complex systems. the framework is expected to improve the robustness and accuracy of cyber risk analysis and threat detection in various domains, including industrial control systems, networks, and iot devices.
raw: we propose a novel framework, the attack hazard analysis model (aham), to quantify the likelihood and intensity of cyber attacks on complex systems. building on the adversarial capability chain (acc) model, aham integrates the adversary's capability progression through five phases - discovery, validation, escalation, democratization, and ubiquity - with a motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence. this method, derived from image and video analysis, enables the identification of characteristic motion patterns in network traffic. by combining acc's risk assessment with the motion feature extraction, aham predicts the likelihood and intensity of attacks by quantifying the adversary's motion patterns and the system's vulnerability. the framework is demonstrated through case studies on industrial control systems and android api exploitation, showcasing its feasibility and robustness. by providing a unified approach to cyber risk assessment, aham facilitates more effective defense strategies and proactive threat mitigation.
one_shot: we propose a novel framework for predicting and analyzing cyber-physical system (cps) attacks, leveraging the adversarial capability chain (acc) model from computer network defense, and the curl and divergence (cd) feature extraction method from motion analysis. by integrating these two domains, we develop a predictive model that characterizes the likelihood and intensity of cps attacks. our method first identifies potential attack vectors using the acc model, and then extracts motion features from the attack landscape using the cd method. these features are used to construct a dual representation of the attack, enabling the prediction of attack likelihood and intensity. we demonstrate the efficacy of our approach through case studies and simulations, showcasing the potential for early warning systems and proactive defense strategies in cps security. this integrated framework offers a novel and robust approach to cps security, enabling the development of more effective defense mechanisms against sophisticated cyber threats.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach to predicting and mitigating cyber attacks by leveraging a novel motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence. the adversarial capability chain (acc) model, previously developed for cyber risk analysis, is integrated with the curl and divergence motion feature extraction system to predict the likelihood and intensity of attacks. by analyzing the motion patterns of adversaries, our method provides a more accurate and robust assessment of the costs for an attacker to breach a system, thereby informing the cost of defense. this interdisciplinary approach has the potential to enhance the resilience of complex systems against cyber threats by identifying vulnerabilities and optimizing defense strategies.
