got: predicting the likelihood and intensity of cyber attacks on industrial control systems (ics) requires a comprehensive understanding of the capabilities of adversaries. building on the adversarial capability chain (acc) model, we propose a novel approach to integrating motion feature analysis with cyber risk assessment. by applying the curl and divergence-based motion feature extraction method to ics-related motion data, we can identify potential attack vectors and predict the likelihood of exploitation. our framework, dubbed "motion-enabled adversarial capability assessment" (meaca), combines the acc's five-phase model with the curl and divergence-based motion feature extraction method to assess the attack likelihood and intensity of ics systems. meaca enables real-time monitoring of ics motion data, allowing for proactive defense strategies against potential threats. our research demonstrates the feasibility of meaca in identifying vulnerable ics systems and prioritizing defensive efforts, ultimately enhancing the security of critical infrastructure.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the utility model relates to a fertilizer screening plant is through setting up 3 the first connections of sieve that have different apertures sieve mesh in traditional hopper to through the slope and the action of gravity of slope, organic fertilizer can do exercises on the sieve inclined plane from the tendency, can't just stay through the fertilizer of sieve mesh in the fertilizer storehouse that sieve and hopper junction were seted up. what final screening finished is exactly sharp fertilizer. the utility model provides a fertilizer screening plant screening efficiency is high, sieves effectually.
abstract1: this chapter extends probabilistic analytical methods introduced in chap. 2. the principle of transformation of medical information into data visualization is introduced in this chapter. this enables transforming a diverse array of clinical problems into simple, standard forms which lend themselves more easily to diagnostic solutions. data distillation followed by data visualization allows clinical problems to be formulated into decision making “nodes” which lend themselves to graphical methods of problem solving. this method is exploited widely in computer science and forms the basis for many optimization algorithms used ubiquitously. candidate diagnosis, differential diagnosis can be selected and evaluated probabilistically. like fault tree analysis, these methods can be qualitative and quantitative. illustrative case examples are provided following introduction of the theoretical fundamentals.
abstract2: there has been much recent interest in the response analysis and optimisation of the linear energy harvester under ambient vibrations. to transfer maximum power to an electrical load in a resonant system, the load resistance should be equal to the sum of the electrical analogue of mechanical damping and internal resistance. however, principally because of the limited bandwidth offered by the linear energy harvester, the potential benefit of nonlinearity has recently been applied to improve the effectiveness of energy harvesting devices. for example, a duffing-type oscillator can provide a wider bandwidth and greater effectiveness when subject to periodic excitations. the motivating hypothesis has been that the nonlinear duffing energy harvester can also be optimised to maximise the available electrical power. this paper presents theoretical optimisation and numerical studies under three different conditions with the designed duffing-type devices. first, the simplest model without any transmission mechanism and optimisation constraints is considered. second, a device operated under low frequency and large force excitations using a ball screw to convert low-speed linear motion to high-speed rotation is analysed, where the optimum lead and load resistance are derived. finally, considering the limitation of some dimensions in practical implementation, the constrained optimisation subjected to the maximum displacement of the seismic mass is also shown in this paper.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel approach to optimizing energy harvesting from ambient vibrations using nonlinear systems, while leveraging probabilistic analytical methods for decision-making and fault diagnosis. building on the principles of data distillation and visualization introduced in probabilistic analytical methods, we apply this framework to optimize the performance of a duffing-type energy harvester. the proposed method enables the formulation of complex energy harvesting problems into standard forms, facilitating the identification of optimal operating conditions and fault diagnosis. theoretical optimisation and numerical studies under various conditions are presented, including the consideration of transmission mechanisms and practical implementation constraints. furthermore, we demonstrate the application of this approach to a fertilizer screening plant, where the probabilistic analytical method is used to optimize sieve mesh apertures and improve screening efficiency. this work showcases the potential of interdisciplinarity in energy harvesting and decision-making, opening up new avenues for the development of efficient and fault-tolerant energy harvesting systems.
cot: this paper introduces a novel approach to optimize the performance of energy harvesting devices in medical diagnostic applications. building on the concept of probabilistic analytical methods for clinical problems and data visualization, we propose a framework that integrates linear and nonlinear energy harvesting principles with decision-making protocols for diagnostic solutions. specifically, we develop a data-driven model that employs the duffing-type nonlinear energy harvester to maximize electrical power output, while simultaneously exploiting the transformation of medical information into data visualization. this enables the formulation of clinical problems into decision-making nodes, which can be solved using graphical methods, such as fault tree analysis. we demonstrate the feasibility of this approach through theoretical optimization and numerical studies of the duffing-type energy harvester under various operating conditions. our results show that this interdisciplinary framework can lead to more effective and efficient diagnostic solutions, thereby improving the overall performance of medical diagnostic devices.
tot: this paper presents a novel approach to optimizing the performance of linear energy harvesters (lehs) in real-world applications, leveraging the principles of probabilistic data analysis and visualization. by transforming clinical problems into standard forms, we can apply data-driven methods to optimize the design and operation of lehs, specifically nonlinear duffing-type oscillators. our approach enables the identification of optimal operating conditions, such as load resistance and frequency excitations, to maximize electrical power output. we develop a framework that integrates data visualization and optimization algorithms to analyze and optimize leh performance under different conditions, including transmission mechanisms and practical implementation constraints. our results demonstrate the potential for significant improvements in energy harvesting efficiency, with implications for a wide range of applications, from wearable devices to infrastructure monitoring systems. this work bridges the gap between data-driven problem-solving and energy harvesting optimization, offering a new paradigm for the design and operation of lehs.
raw: this paper presents a novel approach to optimizing energy harvesting from linear energy harvesters under ambient vibrations using probabilistic and nonlinear methods. by employing data visualization and decision-making nodes from probabilistic analytical methods, we transform clinical problems into simple, standard forms that lend themselves to diagnostic solutions, enabling the optimization of nonlinear energy harvesting devices. our theoretical optimization studies demonstrate the effectiveness of a duffing-type oscillator in providing a wider bandwidth and greater effectiveness under periodic excitations. we then apply these principles to optimize the nonlinear energy harvester by formulating decision-making nodes for optimization parameters, such as load resistance and lead, under different operating conditions. our numerical studies show significant improvements in available electrical power under various scenarios, including low-frequency and large-force excitations. this integrated approach enables the design of more efficient energy harvesting devices and opens up new avenues for the application of probabilistic and nonlinear methods in interdisciplinary research.
one_shot: we propose a novel approach to optimize energy harvesting in medical devices by integrating nonlinear resonant systems with data-driven diagnostic tools. by leveraging the principle of data distillation and visualization, we transform clinical problems into standard forms that can be addressed using probabilistic methods and graphical optimization algorithms. we apply this framework to a linear energy harvester with a duffing-type oscillator, where we theoretically optimize the device under different conditions, including low-frequency excitations and constrained displacement limits. by maximizing the available electrical power, we demonstrate the potential for improved diagnostic accuracy and energy efficiency in medical devices, such as implantable sensors and pacemakers. our results showcase the feasibility of this interdisciplinary approach, paving the way for the development of more effective and autonomous medical devices.
few_shot: we present an interdisciplinary approach to optimize energy harvesting in distributed sensor networks using nonlinear energy harvesters and probabilistic analytical methods. by transforming medical information into data visualization, we identify critical nodes for energy harvesting optimization, such as fault-prone devices or data-intensive applications. our theoretical framework combines data distillation and visualization with nonlinear energy harvester dynamics to maximize available electrical power. we analyze three scenarios: a simple model without transmission mechanism, a device operated under low frequency and large force excitations, and a constrained optimization subjected to maximum displacement of the seismic mass. our results demonstrate improved energy harvesting efficiency and reduced transmission peaks, enabling resilient and efficient operation of distributed sensor networks in harsh environments.
got: this paper presents an innovative approach to optimizing energy harvesting from ambient vibrations using nonlinear resonators, enabled by probabilistic analytical methods for data-driven design. we extend the principle of data distillation and visualization introduced in probabilistic analytical methods to transform the complex dynamics of nonlinear energy harvesters into simple, standard forms for decision-making. this approach allows for the formulation of energy harvesting problems into "nodes" that can be graphically solved, leveraging optimization algorithms from computer science. we optimize the nonlinear duffing-type oscillator under different conditions, including transmission mechanisms and practical implementation constraints, to maximize available electrical power. by integrating probabilistic analytical methods with nonlinear energy harvesting, we demonstrate the feasibility of data-driven design for energy harvesting applications. this research has the potential to revolutionize the field of energy harvesting, enabling more efficient and effective energy harvesting systems that can be easily designed and optimized using data visualization and probabilistic methods.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: h. 264 / svc realizes the basic level rate control,but does not detect scene change in video sequences effectively and consider the influence of the scene change on the coding quality. this article combines hierarchical b frames forecasting pyramid coding structure of h. 264 / svc,presents an improved real- time algorithm of scene change detection based on image complexity,and modify quantization parameters of different temporal b frames when gop encoding unit exists scene change. experimental results show that does not produce mutation of channel bit rate and significantly improves video quality at the scene change.
abstract1: the ergodicity-nonergodicity (ene) transition of the self-consistent mode-coupling theory (mct) is marked by the point at which the time correlation of collective density fluctuations is not zero in the long-time limit. the nonergodic state, reaching beyond the ene transition of simple mct, is characterized by a finite shear modulus. the mct, formulated in the current set of papers, predicts that the single-particle density correlation, unlike the collective density correlation, decays to zero at long times on either side of the ene transition. the self-diffusion coefficient remains finite. this differs from the existing mct results in which both collective and single-particle correlations are simultaniously frozen at the ene transition. we discuss in this paper mechanisms by which a sharp fall in self-diffusion coefficient may occur within the present model. this overdamping or the so-called adiabatic approximation for the supercooled state does not maintain microscopic momentum conservation. within this approximation, the self-diffusion constant approaches zero at the ene transition point. this approximate result, which is similar to the prediction of the existing mct models, further illustrates the process of cage formation with increase of density. at a qualitative level, our analysis shows that the self-diffusion process depends on the structure as well as short-time transport properties of the supercooled liquid. we solve the integral equations for the nonergodicity parameters to analyze the full implications of the adiabatic approximation.
abstract2: the present invention relates to a natural vagina cleanser for women, more specifically, which is produced by mixing 55-70 wt% of bamboo salt, 10-15 wt% of allium fistulosum l. powder, 10-15 wt% of plantaginis semen powder, and 10-1 wt%of portulaca oleracea l. powder, thereby providing excellent cleanliness and causing less skin irritation cleanser due to a cytostatic action (an action killing only harmful bacteria excluding normal bacteria necessary for body) of natural ingredients, compared to an existing. moreover, the present invention effectively prevents vaginal diseases including vaginosis, metritis, leukorrhea, itchiness, and odor.
gen_abs: this study integrates the self-consistent mode-coupling theory (mct) with the principles of vaginal microbiome maintenance to develop a novel, dynamic system for vaginal health monitoring and intervention. building on the hierarchical b-frames forecasting pyramid coding structure of h.264/svc, we propose a real-time algorithm to detect scene changes in vaginal microbiome dynamics, thereby optimizing the coding quality of natural ingredients for vaginal health. by modifying quantization parameters of different temporal b-frames when a gop encoding unit detects scene change, we can effectively prevent vaginal diseases such as vaginosis, metritis, and leukorrhea, while minimizing skin irritation. the proposed system, which combines the adiabatic approximation of the mct with the cytostatic action of natural ingredients, is validated through experiments using a natural vagina cleanser composed of bamboo salt, allium fistulosum l. powder, plantaginis semen powder, and portulaca oleracea l. powder. our results demonstrate that the proposed system can significantly improve video quality and vaginal health at scene changes, providing a new framework for the development of personalized, dynamic vaginal health interventions.
cot: the transition from an ergodic to a nonergodic state in supercooled liquids, characterized by a finite shear modulus and non-zero time correlation of collective density fluctuations, has significant implications for the self-diffusion process. recent studies have shown that the self-diffusion coefficient approaches zero at the ergodicity-nonergodicity (ene) transition point, indicating a sharp fall in mobility. however, this result is based on an adiabatic approximation that neglects microscopic momentum conservation. in this study, we investigate the effects of incorporating natural ingredients, inspired by traditional vaginal cleansers, into the supercooled liquid to enhance self-diffusion and mitigate the adiabatic approximation's limitations. by mixing bamboo salt, allium fistulosum l. powder, plantaginis semen powder, and portulaca oleracea l. powder, we create a novel, cytostatic vaginal cleanser that not only prevents vaginal diseases but also promotes self-diffusion in supercooled liquids. our results show that the addition of these natural ingredients leads to a significant increase in self-diffusion coefficient, indicating a potential solution to the adiabatic approximation's limitations. this study provides new insights into the interplay between self-diffusion and the structure of supercooled liquids, with potential applications in the development of novel, non-invasive treatments for vaginal diseases.
tot: the collective dynamics of supercooled liquids exhibit a transition from ergodic to nonergodic behavior, characterized by a finite shear modulus and non-zero time correlation of collective density fluctuations. however, the implications of this transition on the self-diffusion process remain poorly understood. we propose a novel approach to investigate the self-diffusion coefficient in the supercooled regime using a combination of theoretical modeling and experimental verification. our approach leverages the self-consistent mode-coupling theory and the adiabatic approximation to analyze the cage formation and its impact on the self-diffusion process. we demonstrate that the adiabatic approximation, although not conserving microscopic momentum, provides an efficient means to solve the integral equations for the nonergodicity parameters. furthermore, we experimentally validate our theoretical predictions using a novel natural vagina cleanser formulation based on bamboo salt, allium fistulosum l., plantago semen l., and portulaca oleracea l. the cytostatic properties of this formulation allow us to control the bacterial flora in the vagina, thereby creating a controlled environment for experimentation. our results show that the self-diffusion coefficient decreases sharply at the ergodic-nonergodic transition, indicating a strong impact of cage formation on the diffusion process. this work contributes to a deeper understanding of the complex dynamics of supercooled liquids and opens new avenues for the development of novel, effective, and biocompatible materials.
raw: we report a novel approach to understanding the dynamics of supercooled liquids in confined geometries, inspired by the self-consistent mode-coupling theory (mct). our study reveals that the ergodicity-nonergodicity transition (ene) of mct can be harnessed to design a vaginal cleanser with improved biocompatibility and efficacy. by incorporating natural ingredients, such as bamboo salt, allium fistulosum l. powder, plantaginis semen powder, and portulaca oleracea l. powder, into a mct-driven framework, we demonstrate that the cytostatic action of these compounds can be optimized to selectively target harmful bacteria while preserving the natural vaginal microbiome. our results show that the adiabatic approximation of the supercooled state, which facilitates the cage formation process, can be exploited to create a cleanser with reduced skin irritation and improved therapeutic outcomes. this interdisciplinary approach has the potential to revolutionize the treatment of vaginal diseases, including vaginosis, metritis, and leukorrhea, and provides a new paradigm for understanding the complex dynamics of supercooled liquids in confined geometries.
one_shot: the collective behavior of supercooled liquids is closely linked to the dynamics of microorganisms living in dense environments, such as the vagina. by extending the self-consistent mode-coupling theory (mct) to incorporate the effects of natural antimicrobial agents, we investigate how these agents modulate the ergodicity-nonergodicity (ene) transition and cage formation in supercooled liquids. our analysis reveals that certain plant extracts, similar to those used in natural vagina cleansers, can enhance the self-diffusion coefficient and prevent the freezing of collective density correlations at the ene transition. this suggests that the structure and short-time transport properties of the supercooled liquid can be manipulated by incorporating specific antimicrobial agents, with implications for the development of novel, targeted treatments for vaginal diseases.
few_shot: we investigate the effects of rheological changes on the self-diffusion process in supercooled liquids using a self-consistent mode-coupling theory (mct) framework. by analyzing the ergodicity-nonergodicity (ene) transition, we reveal a sharp fall in self-diffusion coefficient due to the formation of a finite shear modulus, indicative of a nonergodic state. this phenomenon is linked to the adiabatic approximation, which neglects microscopic momentum conservation. to bridge the gap between liquid dynamics and practical applications, we propose a novel, biocompatible cleanser for vaginal health, formulated with bamboo salt, allium fistulosum, plantaginis semen, and portulaca oleracea, which effectively prevents vaginal diseases while causing minimal skin irritation. we demonstrate that the nonergodic state of supercooled liquids shares similarities with the viscoelastic properties of the proposed cleanser, where a finite shear modulus enables a cytostatic action against harmful bacteria. our findings suggest a novel approach to designing biocompatible materials with tailored rheological properties for targeted applications in medicine and beyond.
got: we explore the novel concept of utilizing the nonergodic state of supercooled liquids as a natural inspiration for designing novel, cytostatic-based vaginal cleansers. by integrating insights from the ergodicity-nonergodicity (ene) transition of the self-consistent mode-coupling theory (mct) and the antimicrobial properties of plant extracts, we propose a bio-inspired approach to prevent vaginal diseases. our model leverages the finite shear modulus of the nonergodic state to mimic the structure and dynamics of supercooled liquids. this is achieved by formulating a vaginal cleanser using a combination of bamboo salt, allium fistulosum l. powder, plantaginis semen powder, and portulaca oleracea l. powder, which exhibit cytostatic actions against harmful bacteria while preserving beneficial flora. our simulations and experiments demonstrate that this bio-inspired approach effectively prevents vaginal diseases, including vaginosis, metritis, leukorrhea, itchiness, and odor, by leveraging the nonergodic state's unique properties. this interdisciplinary research contributes to the development of novel, natural, and effective vaginal cleansers, offering a promising solution for women's health.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the basic theory of ecological ethics is about the the value and right of nature and man's attitude of nature.chinese and western eco-ethic culture involves the relation between man and nature and man's attitude about nature with different stress. it is important to construct localized eco-ethics to match with eco-civilization construction in sino-western ecoethic cultural complementation. the essay writers attempt in macro-view to make a brief comb through and sort out cultures in western and chinese ecological ethic to offer a theoretic backup for ecological ethic view and solution to modernization issue and construction on ecological civilization.
abstract1: search engine advertising sea has attracted the attention of academic researchers from marketing, information systems, computer science, mathematics and other related fields. aiming to provide a picture of the state of the art in this field, the present research systematically searches for and reviews the literature on sea, analyses and structures them, and provides directions for future research. we searched extensively and collected 97 journal papers on sea published in 64 journals from different disciplines and analysed them to answer our research questions. using systematic mapping approach, this research has identified research topics ten categories of topics were identified and visualised in a graphical representation, research approaches, nature of research and the interdependencies between them. the research also has identified directions for future research.
abstract2: the emission of electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range has been observed in many experiments of laserplasma interaction. these fields can have very high intensity and estimated frequency band up to several gigahertz. the radiation normally affects the behaviour of most of the detectors, often up to hundreds of nanoseconds from the laser pulse, and can represent a serious limitation for the time-of-ight detection of fast particles, and in general for the safe operation of the electronic equipment. in this work we describe the measurements of this electromagnetic pulse, under different conditions of laser-plasma interaction. c
gen_abs: this interdisciplinary research project investigates the intersection of search engine advertising and laser-plasma interaction, exploring the potential applications of this intersection in the development of eco-friendly technologies. by incorporating the principles of ecological ethics and the concept of eco-civilization construction, we aim to identify areas where search engine advertising can be leveraged to mitigate the environmental impacts of laser-plasma interactions. our systematic review of 97 journal papers on search engine advertising and analysis of electromagnetic pulse measurements in laser-plasma experiments reveal promising avenues for research. we propose the development of a novel approach to search engine advertising, incorporating localized eco-ethics and eco-friendly communication strategies to promote responsible technology use and support the construction of eco-civilization. this research has implications for the development of sustainable technologies and the promotion of environmentally responsible practices in the tech industry.
cot: we propose an innovative interdisciplinary research project that bridges the gap between search engine advertising and laser-plasma interaction. building upon the systematic mapping of search engine advertising research topics (paper 1), we aim to investigate the impact of electromagnetic pulses generated during laser-plasma interactions on search engine advertising systems. these high-intensity electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range (paper 2) can significantly affect detector behavior and electronic equipment operation, potentially compromising search engine advertising performance and user experience. our research will explore the interdependencies between laser-plasma interactions and search engine advertising systems, analyzing the effects of electromagnetic pulses on ad delivery, targeting, and user behavior. we will develop novel methods to mitigate these effects and design more robust search engine advertising systems that can operate safely and reliably in the presence of electromagnetic interference. this research has far-reaching implications for the safe and efficient operation of search engine advertising systems, particularly in applications where high-energy lasers are used.
tot: search engine advertising (sea) has become a significant area of study in various disciplines, including marketing, information systems, and computer science. however, the widespread adoption of sea has introduced a new challenge: the potential for electromagnetic interference (emi) from high-intensity radiofrequency-microwave radiation generated during laser-plasma interactions. this emi can affect the performance of search engine platforms, compromising the accuracy of search results and user experience. to address this issue, we propose a novel approach to mitigate emi in sea environments. our research combines the systematic mapping of sea literature with experimental measurements of emi in laser-plasma interactions. we investigate the interdependencies between sea research topics and emi effects, and identify key factors influencing the impact of emi on search engine performance. our findings provide a foundation for developing emi-resistant search engine architectures, ensuring the reliability and security of sea platforms. this research has significant implications for the development of future-proof search engines and the safe operation of electronic equipment in sea environments.
raw: this paper proposes a novel approach to mitigate the effects of electromagnetic pulses (emps) on time-of-flight (tof) detectors in high-intensity laser-plasma interaction experiments. by leveraging the insights from search engine advertising (sea) research, we develop a systematic framework to analyze and structure the literature on emps and their impact on detector behavior. our interdisciplinary research combines the expertise from marketing, information systems, computer science, and mathematics to identify key research topics and approaches for mitigating emps. we demonstrate the feasibility of our approach by applying it to a case study of laser-plasma interaction experiments, where we measure and model the emps under different conditions. our results show that by optimizing sea strategies, we can reduce the impact of emps on tof detectors by up to 70%, enabling faster and more accurate particle detection. this research has significant implications for the safe operation of electronic equipment in high-energy physics experiments and opens up new avenues for interdisciplinary research at the intersection of marketing, information systems, and high-energy physics.
one_shot: we explore the impact of radiofrequency-microwave electromagnetic pulses on search engine advertising systems. these pulses, generated by high-intensity laser-plasma interactions, can interfere with electronic equipment, potentially compromising the accuracy and safety of search engine advertising. by analyzing the effects of electromagnetic pulses on detector systems, we can better understand how to mitigate these disruptions in search engine advertising, which rely on sensitive detectors to monitor user behavior. our research systematically reviews the literature on search engine advertising and its vulnerabilities to electromagnetic interference, providing insights into the development of more robust and reliable advertising systems. we propose a novel approach to modeling and mitigating the effects of electromagnetic pulses on search engine advertising, leveraging graph neural networks to simulate and predict the behavior of detectors in the presence of electromagnetic interference.
few_shot: this paper explores the intersection of search engine advertising systems and laser-plasma interactions. we investigate how the emission of electromagnetic pulses in laser-plasma experiments affects the operation of seas, highlighting the need for more robust detection and mitigation methods. by applying a systematic mapping approach and integrating graph neural networks with signal processing techniques, we develop a novel framework for predicting and adapting to emp-induced disruptions in seas, ensuring the reliable and efficient operation of online advertising systems under various environmental conditions.
got: we propose an interdisciplinary research project that investigates the impact of high-intensity electromagnetic pulses on the operation of search engine advertising systems (seas). these pulses, generated by laser-plasma interactions, can interfere with the sensitive electronics of seas, compromising their performance and reliability. to better understand the effects of these pulses, we will conduct a comprehensive analysis of the electromagnetic properties of seas, including their susceptibility to radiofrequency-microwave radiation. our research will involve a systematic review of existing literature on seas, including topics such as advertising strategies, search algorithms, and user behavior. we will also design and conduct experiments to measure the effects of electromagnetic pulses on seas, using advanced detectors and data analysis techniques. by integrating insights from marketing, computer science, and physics, our research aims to provide a novel understanding of the vulnerabilities of seas and their potential mitigation strategies, ultimately contributing to the development of more robust and reliable online advertising systems.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the objective of this work was to investigate the lead pollution around the environment of phosphat mine in kanyang area of guizhou to provide theoretical basis for food safety in livestock around the phosphate mine.all samples were determined with method of icp-ms.all samples were polluted except soil by lead pollution in environment.the lead accumulation from high to low is as follows:chiligrassradishricecorncabbagewatersoil 2soil 1.the lead was not checked in the beef;chicken,duck,pig muscle and chicken kidney were severe polluted(pi3),other organs were polluted to some extents.the results indicated that the serious lead pollution is exist in livestock around the phosphate mine.
abstract1: li kai xian (1502-1568) was a famous playwright during jia jing period of the ming dynasty. he started his life as a bureaucrat from eighth year of jia jing (1529) and resigned this position at twentieth year of jia jing (1541). he turned back to his hometown and devoted himself to the composition of chinese traditional opera. there was only a little amount of opera related to ＂shui hwu zhuan＂, except several za jus, during the time from the beginning of ming dynasty to jia jing period. li kai xian’s ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ was highly probable the earliest opera choosing shui hwu zhuan as its subject matter among the ming chuan qis. considering the increasing amount of shui hwu zhuan -subjected opera after wan li period, from the perspective of subject matter, li kai xiao’s opera occupied a pioneering position. ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ based on the novel ＂shui hwu zhuan＂. li kai xian adapted some plots in order to reflect the reality and imply sardonicism toward the present by criticizing the past. this not only made his opera more profound, but also affected the trend of opera composition afterwards. hence, the author chose ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ as title by first portraying li kai xian’s life story and operas, then discussing his usage of the subjects and creating concepts, in addtion, describing some questions about the disharmony of music in this opera aroused by some opera critics at that time. hopefully, this study can convey a fair analyzing of the subject matter features and the motif of this opera.
abstract2: the complexity due to massive growth of power systems has to increased demand of electrical energy giving rise to numerous stability challenges. in these situations, flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers have increasing been applied. this paper discusses and compares the transient stability enhancement by use of sssc and ipfc facts controllers when applied in transient stability enhancement (tse) in a dynamic ieee 14 bus system with fault applied at bus 04. it is achieved by observing the behaviour of damping power oscillations. time domain responses for angular frequencies and voltages responses have been analysed.
gen_abs: this interdisciplinary research project investigates the environmental impact of lead pollution from phosphate mining on the ecosystem and human health, while exploring the potential applications of flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers in mitigating the effects of lead pollution. building on the pioneering work of li kai xian in adapting traditional opera to reflect social commentary, we apply a similar framework of environmental critique to the lead pollution issue. using the intermediate concentration plasma mass spectrometry (icp-ms) method, we analyze the lead accumulation in various soil and plant samples around the phosphate mine, revealing a significant lead pollution gradient. we then simulate the transient stability enhancement of facts controllers in a dynamic ieee 14-bus system, applying the findings to a hypothetical facts controller application that mitigates the effects of lead pollution on the ecosystem. our results highlight the need for sustainable practices in phosphate mining and demonstrate the potential of facts controllers in environmental remediation, offering a novel perspective on the intersection of traditional opera, environmental science, and electrical engineering.
cot: this paper explores the application of creative perspectives in addressing complex problems in power systems. building on the pioneering work of li kai xian, a renowned playwright of the ming dynasty, we investigate the use of "sardonicism" and "critical thinking" in designing flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers for enhancing transient stability in power grids. inspired by li kai xian's opera, "lin choun bao jian ji," which adapted plots to reflect reality and imply sardonicism towards the present, we propose a novel approach to facts controller design. by incorporating elements of "dramatic tension" and "plot adaptation" into the design process, we aim to create more effective and resilient power grids. our case study, applied to a dynamic ieee 14-bus system with a fault at bus 04, demonstrates the potential of this approach in enhancing transient stability and reducing power oscillations. this research contributes to the development of more creative and adaptive strategies for addressing the complexities of modern power systems, and has implications for the design of more resilient and efficient power grids.
tot: this study explores the application of a novel paradigm in the field of power systems, drawing inspiration from the concept of dramatic irony in traditional chinese opera. by analyzing the use of sardonicism and social commentary in li kai xian's "lin choun bao jian ji", we identify a valuable principle for developing more effective flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers. our analysis reveals that by incorporating elements of drama and storytelling into the design of facts controllers, we can enhance their ability to mitigate power oscillations and improve transient stability in dynamic power systems. specifically, we investigate the performance of series series compensator (sssc) and interphase power flow controller (ipfc) facts controllers in a 14-bus system and demonstrate how their design can be optimized to reflect the "dramatic" fluctuations in power demand and supply. our findings suggest that this approach can improve the effectiveness of facts controllers and provide a new direction for the development of more adaptive and responsive power systems.
raw: this interdisciplinary research project explores the intersection of cultural storytelling and technological innovation by examining the impact of power system dynamics on artistic expression. by analyzing the historical context of traditional chinese opera composition, particularly li kai xian's "lin choun bao jian ji," we identify parallels between the use of non-linear storytelling and the challenges of managing power system stability. the opera's use of complex plot structures and sardonic commentary on societal norms reflects a similar adaptive response to the complexities of its time, echoing the need for innovative solutions in power system stability. to address the increasing demand for electrical energy, flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers have emerged as a key solution. however, their effectiveness in enhancing transient stability is dependent on the dynamics of the power system. this project aims to develop a novel approach to power system stability analysis, inspired by the principles of non-linear storytelling and adaptive narrative structure. by integrating insights from opera composition and power system dynamics, we propose a new framework for facts controller design, enabling more effective transient stability enhancement and paving the way for a more resilient and sustainable energy future.
one_shot: this interdisciplinary study investigates the application of flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers in enhancing the resilience of traditional chinese opera performances, specifically "lin chou bao jian ji" by li kai xian, against the backdrop of increasing energy demands and power system complexities. by adapting facts controllers to mitigate stability challenges, we compare the effectiveness of static series compensator (sssc) and interphase power flow controller (ipfc) in stabilizing the performance of opera productions, traditionally susceptible to power oscillations and electrical instability. our analysis reveals that these controllers can reduce voltage fluctuations and angular frequencies, providing a novel approach to preserving the integrity of cultural heritage performances in the face of modern energy demands.
few_shot: we investigate a novel approach to modeling and mitigating complex societal challenges by integrating traditional chinese opera analysis with power grid stability enhancement techniques. by applying a li kai xian-inspired narrative framework to flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controller design, we create a dynamic model that captures the rhythmic patterns and evolving relationships between social and technical systems. this integrated framework enables the prediction and optimization of power grid stability under transient conditions, reflecting the pioneering role of li kai xian's opera in reflecting and influencing societal changes. our simulations demonstrate improved transient stability and reduced oscillations in power grids, offering a novel perspective on the intersection of art and technology in addressing complex system challenges.
got: in this study, we explore the intersection of artistic creativity and technological innovation through the lens of complex power systems. building on the pioneering work of li kai xian, a 16th-century chinese playwright, we examine how the principles of narrative composition and audience engagement can inform the design of flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers. specifically, we investigate the application of facts controllers, such as static synchronous series compensator (sssc) and interline power flow controller (ipfc), in enhancing transient stability in a dynamic ieee 14-bus system. our analysis reveals that the adaptive, non-linear characteristics of these controllers can be likened to the creative, adaptive strategies employed by li kai xian in his masterpiece, "lin choun bao jian ji". by leveraging insights from both fields, we demonstrate the possibility of designing more resilient and responsive power systems that can mitigate power grid instability and promote a more sustainable energy future. this interdisciplinary approach opens new avenues for research and development in the fields of power systems engineering and narrative composition.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: in the present invention, provided are a chinese character learning card and chinese character learning tool of augmented reality using a part of chinese letters finding chinese characters where chinese blocks (11) are combined with each other, thereby making chinese characters and enabling users to insert the combined chinese character in a card board (12), and images of the combined chinese character are put in a terminal of users (20) while matching the chinese character through internet by using servers and receiving the material through the terminal (20), thereby enabling users to learn definition and pronunciation with use of augmented reality.
abstract1: there has been much recent interest in the response analysis and optimisation of the linear energy harvester under ambient vibrations. to transfer maximum power to an electrical load in a resonant system, the load resistance should be equal to the sum of the electrical analogue of mechanical damping and internal resistance. however, principally because of the limited bandwidth offered by the linear energy harvester, the potential benefit of nonlinearity has recently been applied to improve the effectiveness of energy harvesting devices. for example, a duffing-type oscillator can provide a wider bandwidth and greater effectiveness when subject to periodic excitations. the motivating hypothesis has been that the nonlinear duffing energy harvester can also be optimised to maximise the available electrical power. this paper presents theoretical optimisation and numerical studies under three different conditions with the designed duffing-type devices. first, the simplest model without any transmission mechanism and optimisation constraints is considered. second, a device operated under low frequency and large force excitations using a ball screw to convert low-speed linear motion to high-speed rotation is analysed, where the optimum lead and load resistance are derived. finally, considering the limitation of some dimensions in practical implementation, the constrained optimisation subjected to the maximum displacement of the seismic mass is also shown in this paper.
abstract2: li kai xian (1502-1568) was a famous playwright during jia jing period of the ming dynasty. he started his life as a bureaucrat from eighth year of jia jing (1529) and resigned this position at twentieth year of jia jing (1541). he turned back to his hometown and devoted himself to the composition of chinese traditional opera. there was only a little amount of opera related to ＂shui hwu zhuan＂, except several za jus, during the time from the beginning of ming dynasty to jia jing period. li kai xian’s ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ was highly probable the earliest opera choosing shui hwu zhuan as its subject matter among the ming chuan qis. considering the increasing amount of shui hwu zhuan -subjected opera after wan li period, from the perspective of subject matter, li kai xiao’s opera occupied a pioneering position. ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ based on the novel ＂shui hwu zhuan＂. li kai xian adapted some plots in order to reflect the reality and imply sardonicism toward the present by criticizing the past. this not only made his opera more profound, but also affected the trend of opera composition afterwards. hence, the author chose ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ as title by first portraying li kai xian’s life story and operas, then discussing his usage of the subjects and creating concepts, in addtion, describing some questions about the disharmony of music in this opera aroused by some opera critics at that time. hopefully, this study can convey a fair analyzing of the subject matter features and the motif of this opera.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel approach to designing nonlinear energy harvesting devices inspired by the creative process of traditional chinese opera composition. building on the theoretical optimisation and numerical studies of duffing-type oscillators for maximum electrical power output, we introduce a new paradigm that incorporates elements of chinese character learning and augmented reality. by leveraging the innovative use of chinese character blocks and ar technology, as seen in the chinese character learning card and tool, we develop a novel framework for designing nonlinear energy harvesting devices that can be optimised in real-time through user interaction and feedback. specifically, we demonstrate how the concept of "lin choun bao jian ji," a pioneering opera that adapted and critiqued traditional subject matter, can inform the design of nonlinear energy harvesting devices that can adapt and learn from their environment. our results show that this approach can lead to significant improvements in energy harvesting efficiency and provide a new perspective on the intersection of art, technology, and sustainability. this work has implications for the development of autonomous systems and the creation of innovative learning tools.
cot: this paper proposes a novel approach to designing a nonlinear energy harvester inspired by the artistic and literary innovations of li kai xian, a renowned playwright from the ming dynasty. specifically, we explore the idea of applying the concept of "pioneering position" in opera composition to energy harvesting, where the device's ability to occupy a unique position in the frequency response is leveraged to maximize power transfer. our theoretical analysis and numerical studies demonstrate that a duffing-type oscillator can be optimized to achieve a wider bandwidth and greater effectiveness when subjected to periodic excitations, analogous to li kai xian's use of novel subject matter and creative plot adaptations in his opera "lin chou bao jian ji". we investigate three different conditions: a simple model without transmission mechanism or optimisation constraints, a device operated under low frequency and large force excitations, and a constrained optimisation subjected to maximum displacement of the seismic mass. our results show that the nonlinear energy harvester can outperform its linear counterpart, paving the way for novel applications in energy harvesting and inspiring new approaches to creative problem-solving.
tot: the integration of nonlinear energy harvesting and traditional chinese opera composition has led to a novel approach for optimizing resonant systems. by leveraging the pioneering work of li kai xian in adapting the novel "shui hu zhuan" to create profound and sardonic operas, we propose a methodology for designing nonlinear energy harvesters that resonate at multiple frequencies. our theoretical framework combines the principles of duffing-type oscillators with the concept of "harmony and disharmony" in traditional chinese music. we demonstrate that the optimization of load resistance and internal damping in nonlinear energy harvesters can be achieved by mimicking the creative process of li kai xian's opera composition. specifically, we show that the "lin chou bao jian ji" can be reinterpreted as a metaphor for the optimal relationship between mechanical damping and internal resistance in nonlinear energy harvesters. our results have implications for the design of efficient and robust energy harvesting devices, as well as the creative application of traditional cultural heritage in modern engineering contexts.
raw: this interdisciplinary research project integrates concepts from nonlinear energy harvesting and chinese traditional opera to design a novel, adaptive energy harvesting system inspired by the artistic innovations of li kai xian. building on the theoretical optimisation of nonlinear duffing energy harvesters, we propose a system that utilizes the dynamic, adaptive properties of traditional chinese opera to optimize energy harvesting performance. by analyzing the complex, nonlinear dynamics of "lin choun bao jian ji," a pioneering opera by li kai xian, we identify key principles of adaptability and resilience that can inform the design of a nonlinear energy harvester. specifically, we explore how the opera's use of multiple rhythms, dynamic character development, and complex plot structures can be translated into a novel, adaptive energy harvesting strategy. our research aims to develop a system that can harness the full potential of nonlinear energy harvesting by incorporating the adaptive, dynamic properties of traditional chinese opera, thereby increasing the efficiency and effectiveness of energy harvesting devices.
one_shot: this paper presents an interdisciplinary approach to designing and optimizing nonlinear energy harvesting systems inspired by the innovative adaptations of traditional chinese opera composition. by analyzing the works of li kai xian, a renowned playwright from the ming dynasty, we identify similar patterns of creative problem-solving and optimization in his operas. specifically, his pioneering use of the shui hu zhuan subject matter and adaptive storytelling techniques to engage audiences can be seen as analogous to the pursuit of optimal performance in nonlinear energy harvesting systems. we propose a novel framework for designing nonlinear energy harvesters that incorporates principles of adaptive resonance and frequency modulation, drawing upon the harmonic and rhythmic structures found in traditional chinese opera. this framework is demonstrated through numerical simulations and experiments, showcasing improved power output and efficiency in energy harvesting systems. by integrating insights from art and science, our work offers a new perspective on the development of sustainable energy solutions.
few_shot: this paper presents an interdisciplinary study that integrates mechanical and performance analysis of nonlinear energy harvesters with dramaturgical and narrative aspects of traditional chinese opera. by applying the theoretical framework of duffing-type oscillators to optimize energy harvesting performance, we reveal parallels with the innovative storytelling techniques employed by li kai xian in his operas, particularly in "lin choun bao jian ji". our results show that the optimized nonlinear energy harvester can be used to model the dynamic relationships between li kai xian's creative process, audience engagement, and narrative impact. this study demonstrates the potential of interdisciplinary research in understanding the intersection of performance, creativity, and technological innovation. by exploring the resonance between nonlinear energy harvesting and dramatic narrative, we provide new insights into the optimization of complex systems and the creative process.
got: this interdisciplinary study explores the intersection of cultural resonance and energy harvesting, leveraging the pioneering work of li kai xian, a 16th-century chinese playwright, and modern linear energy harvesting technology. by analyzing the subject matter and motifs in li kai xian's opera "lin chou bao jian ji," which was one of the earliest to adapt the novel "shui hu zhuan," we identify a unique framework for understanding the interplay between cultural narratives and technical optimization. we then apply this framework to the design of nonlinear duffing energy harvesters, demonstrating how the resonance and frequency response of these devices can be optimized to match the cultural resonance of traditional chinese opera. our results show that the use of nonlinear energy harvesters can enhance the effectiveness of energy harvesting systems, while also providing a new perspective on the cultural significance of traditional chinese opera. this study has implications for the development of sustainable energy technologies and the preservation of cultural heritage.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: catechesis is a education that tells the christians what they should believe. many christians know the need, but learning is tedious and teaching is difficult. serious game in the field of education have both the intention of learning and an element of fun. in this study, i designed a educational serious game for catechesis and implemented the game. the heidelberg and westminster catechism were content. the key game play was to choose the correct answer by the touch. the effect was confirmed by the test users. the content of later study was planned. i hope this study can be well utilized to church educational system.
abstract1: analyzing and characterizing the phonocardiogram (pcg) signal is important for diagnosing the valvular heart disease. the pcg can record heart sounds, noise and the additional sounds. since pcg is recording the heart sound, it is important to analyze the clear pcg input signal only. the analyzation of the pcg signal will be consisting of segmenting the signal into s1 and s2 and then compare, whether the pcg is normal or abnormal. in the existing system the wavelet decomposition approach is used to analyze the pcg signal. features are extracted from a pcg signal in frequency domain to classify signals. in the proposed approach the feature selection reduces features provided for classification. coiflet is used for feature extraction, and different feature selection statistical methods are used. information gain (ig), mutual information (mi) etc. feature selection methods are compared using classifiers like knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms. in this paper, two methods are used to analyze the pcg and the accuracy of the information gain (ig) is improved when compared to mutual information (mi).
abstract2: an efficient and improved traffic information system provided by the present invention comprises a total processing device, and the total processing device is in circuit connection with a monitoring server, an alarm device, a remote monitoring terminal, a networks management device, an environmental information acquisition device and an energy consumption management module, and is in signal connection with a gps navigation module, an etc module, a wireless communication module and a wind speed sensor.
gen_abs: this study proposes an innovative traffic information system that utilizes a serious game-like approach to analyze and classify heart sounds, ultimately improving traffic flow and reducing congestion. building on the wavelet decomposition approach used in phonocardiogram (pcg) signal analysis, we integrate a machine learning-based feature selection method, such as information gain, to reduce noise and optimize traffic signal timing. the system, comprising a total processing device, monitoring server, alarm device, and environmental information acquisition device, is connected to a gps navigation module and wireless communication module. by leveraging the educational aspects of catechesis, we design a game-like interface for pcg signal analysis, where users can interactively learn to identify abnormal heart sounds, thereby improving traffic flow. the proposed system is tested in a real-world scenario, demonstrating a significant reduction in congestion and improved traffic efficiency. this interdisciplinary approach has the potential to revolutionize traffic management and heart disease diagnosis, while also providing a novel platform for education and skill-building.
cot: this paper presents an innovative telemedicine system that integrates phonocardiogram (pcg) analysis with traffic monitoring to enhance disease diagnosis and traffic management. by leveraging the same machine learning techniques used in pcg signal analysis, we propose a novel approach to traffic surveillance. our system employs a wavelet decomposition approach, similar to that used in pcg signal analysis, to extract features from traffic data, such as speed and flow. we apply feature selection methods, including information gain and mutual information, to reduce the dimensionality of the data and improve classification accuracy. to classify traffic conditions, we utilize classifiers like knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms, which have been shown to be effective in pcg signal analysis. the proposed system is connected to a monitoring server, alarm device, and remote monitoring terminal, enabling real-time traffic monitoring and alerts. by integrating pcg analysis and traffic monitoring, our system has the potential to improve disease diagnosis and traffic management, while also providing a framework for future telemedicine applications.
tot: this paper presents a novel approach to developing an intelligent healthcare system for remote monitoring of cardiovascular diseases, leveraging the integration of phonocardiogram (pcg) signal analysis and traffic information systems. building upon the existing wavelet decomposition approach for pcg signal analysis, we propose a hybrid system that incorporates machine learning techniques for feature selection and classification. the system utilizes a coiflet transform for feature extraction and evaluates the performance of various feature selection methods, including information gain (ig) and mutual information (mi), in conjunction with classifiers such as knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms. the system is further enhanced by integrating it with a traffic information system, which provides real-time traffic updates and environmental data. this integration enables the system to provide personalized health recommendations and alerts based on the patient's location, traffic conditions, and environmental factors. the proposed system demonstrates improved accuracy in diagnosing valvular heart disease and offers a novel solution for remote healthcare monitoring, particularly in areas with limited medical resources.
raw: this paper presents a novel approach to developing an intelligent traffic management system that incorporates machine learning techniques for real-time traffic monitoring and predictive analytics. by leveraging the phonocardiogram (pcg) signal analysis framework from medical research, we adapt the wavelet decomposition approach and feature extraction methods to analyze traffic noise patterns and anomalies. the proposed system utilizes a total processing device connected to various sensors and modules, including gps navigation, environmental information acquisition, and energy consumption management. we investigate the application of feature selection methods, such as information gain and mutual information, to reduce noise and improve classification accuracy using machine learning algorithms like knn, naive bayes, and svms. our results demonstrate the feasibility of using pcg-inspired signal processing techniques to enhance traffic information systems, enabling more efficient traffic management and reduced congestion. this interdisciplinary approach has the potential to revolutionize intelligent transportation systems by providing real-time insights into traffic patterns and anomalies.
one_shot: this paper proposes a novel approach to developing a smart traffic management system for optimizing urban mobility and road safety. building on the phonocardiogram (pcg) signal analysis technique from medical diagnostics, we integrate machine learning algorithms with iot sensor data from traffic infrastructure to create a real-time monitoring system. the system uses wavelet decomposition and feature extraction methods to analyze traffic patterns and identify anomalies, leveraging techniques such as coiflet and information gain (ig) for efficient feature selection. this integration enables the system to predict traffic congestion, detect potential accidents, and provide proactive alerts to drivers and traffic management authorities. our results show improved accuracy in traffic prediction and anomaly detection, demonstrating the feasibility of this interdisciplinary approach in enhancing urban transportation systems.
few_shot: we present a novel approach to developing an intelligent traffic management system that integrates cardiovascular health monitoring with urban traffic management. by leveraging the phonocardiogram (pcg) signal analysis, we develop a real-time monitoring system that can detect abnormal heart valve conditions, such as valvular heart disease, and adapt traffic signal control to minimize congestion caused by sudden medical emergencies. our system combines wavelet decomposition, feature extraction using coiflet, and statistical feature selection methods to classify pcg signals and predict traffic congestion hotspots. in real-time, the system adjusts traffic signal control using a decentralized multi-agent reinforcement learning framework to minimize congestion and reduce the risk of accidents. experimental results demonstrate the improved accuracy of pcg signal classification and the reduced traffic congestion under various scenarios, showcasing the potential of this integrated approach to improve urban mobility and cardiovascular health.
got: the integration of phonocardiogram (pcg) analysis and intelligent transportation systems (its) has the potential to revolutionize the diagnosis and monitoring of cardiovascular health in real-time, especially for individuals with heart conditions exacerbated by traffic-related stress. this study proposes a novel, hybrid approach that combines signal processing techniques from pcg analysis with real-time traffic information systems. the method involves segmenting pcg signals into s1 and s2 heart sounds using wavelet decomposition, followed by feature extraction using coiflet and feature selection using information gain (ig) and mutual information (mi). the extracted features are then classified using machine learning algorithms, such as knn, naive bayes, c4.5, and svms. the system is integrated with a smart traffic management platform, which provides real-time traffic updates and environmental data, including air quality, noise pollution, and weather conditions. this integrated system enables predictive analytics to identify traffic-related stressors that may affect heart health, allowing for early interventions and personalized health recommendations. the proposed system has the potential to improve the accuracy of pcg analysis, enhance patient outcomes, and reduce healthcare costs.

abstract1: poster presentation background asthma is one of the leading chronic medical problems exacerbated during pregnancy, and this condition affects approximately 3% to 8% of the pregnant population. the health care team needs to be educated on factors that may trigger an exacerbation of asthma in pregnancy, appropriate management, treatment techniques to share with their patients, and the complications that an exacerbation of maternal asthma may pass on to the newborn. the goal for all asthmatic patients is to remain asymptomatic during pregnancy to assist in improving maternal and newborn outcomes. case a 30‐year‐old, g4p1 woman at 27 weeks gestation was transported by air to our large level‐iii mid atlantic trauma center from a satellite medical emergency department with status asthmaticus for further management. presenting with possible influenza virus, her history included asthma, positive cigarette use, and a recent respiratory infection. her respiratory status worsened upon arrival, and she exhibited severe hypoxemia and required intubation. deterioration in maternal and newborn status prompted obstetricians to make the decision to deliver the infant via cesarean to improve the outcomes for mother and newborn. unfortunately, the mother did not survive the recovery phase. this event indicated a need for the health care team to refresh their knowledge on current triggers, treatment and management techniques, and the neonatal complications related to exacerbation of asthma during pregnancy. conclusion asthma is a chronic medical condition that complicates pregnancy. roughly one third of the symptoms of pregnant women improve, one third remain the same, and one third worsen. health care providers need to diligently monitor their patients for signs and symptoms of exacerbation. management strategies should focus on identifying risk factors, educating the patient and the health care team, and improving communication skills to guarantee successful outcomes for mother and fetus.
abstract2: during geophysical and ecosystem surveys in mediterranean and atlantic shallow waters, research vessels, fishing boats, and passenger ferries were used as acoustic sources of opportunity to characterize bottom sediments of different types including mud, fluid mud, and sand. the ships sailed a straight course at constant engine speed while a compact vertical array of pressure or pressure-gradient sensors, deployed from a small boat, sampled the generated noise field over a broad frequency range. different acoustic observables were investigated that do not depend on the knowledge of ship noise characteristics to mitigate the impact of such uncertainty. for sole pressure measurement, extraction and parameterization of striation structures in range-frequency spectrograms at different receiver depths allow to determine effectively compression wave speed and thickness of a sediment layer as compared to a reference. for additional pressure gradient measurement, vertical impedances are estimated at different depths whose range and frequency dependence are highly sensitive to bottom properties and, in particular, density. global optimization via genetic algorithm and sequential bayesian estimation via particle filtering maximize the degree of similarity between predicted and measured impedance data. the paper will critically review the experimental results and compare them with ground truth data. [work supported by onr, fnrs-cnpq, wbi-capes, preface project ec dg env fp7.]
gen_abs: this study develops a novel method for non-invasive, real-time monitoring of maternal-fetal health during pregnancy, leveraging acoustic sensing technology inspired by oceanography. by adapting pressure and pressure-gradient sensors used in marine sediments characterization, we create a wearable device that estimates maternal and fetal physiological parameters, such as respiratory rate and fetal heart rate, without the need for direct contact or invasive procedures. the device relies on machine learning algorithms, including genetic algorithms and particle filtering, to optimize acoustic signal processing and mitigate the impact of environmental noise. our formulation of adalimumab (mumap) in a novel liquid pharmaceutical composition with trehalose, sodium acetate, and acetic acid as stabilizers enables prolonged storage and reduces unnecessary waste. we demonstrate the efficacy of this approach in a clinical setting, monitoring pregnant women with asthma and providing early warnings for potential complications. our findings have the potential to revolutionize prenatal care, enabling timely interventions and improving maternal and fetal outcomes.
cot: asthma exacerbations during pregnancy pose significant risks to both maternal and fetal health. in this study, we explore the intersection of oceanography and obstetrics to develop a novel approach for monitoring and managing asthma in pregnant women. by leveraging the principles of seismic exploration, we adapt the technique of using ship noise to characterize bottom sediments to create a analogous framework for analyzing respiratory patterns in pregnant women. a compact vertical array of pressure and pressure-gradient sensors is used to sample the respiratory noise field, allowing for the extraction and parameterization of striation structures in range-frequency spectrograms. this information is then used to estimate vertical impedances at different depths, which are highly sensitive to lung properties and density. global optimization via genetic algorithm and sequential bayesian estimation via particle filtering are employed to maximize the degree of similarity between predicted and measured impedance data. this innovative approach has the potential to revolutionize the monitoring and management of asthma in pregnant women, allowing for more effective identification and treatment of exacerbations, and ultimately improving maternal and fetal outcomes.
tot: during pregnancy, maternal asthma exacerbations pose significant risks to both mother and fetus. this paper proposes a novel, interdisciplinary approach to mitigate these risks by leveraging insights from underwater acoustics to develop a non-invasive, real-time monitoring system for maternal asthma. by analyzing the acoustic signals generated by ships' propellers, we can indirectly determine the properties of the lower respiratory tract, enabling early detection of asthma exacerbations. using pressure and pressure-gradient sensors, similar to those employed in underwater sediment analysis, we can extract relevant features from respiratory sounds, such as striation structures and impedance data. these features can be used to train machine learning models that predict asthma exacerbations, allowing for timely interventions. our system has the potential to improve maternal and fetal outcomes by providing healthcare providers with actionable, real-time data on maternal respiratory health. we will present experimental results from a proof-of-concept study and discuss the broader implications for healthcare and environmental monitoring systems.
raw: acoustic sensing of maternal health: a novel approach to monitoring pregnancy-associated asthma and newborn outcomes during pregnancy, asthma can significantly impact maternal and fetal health. however, current monitoring methods are limited by their reliance on invasive and costly technologies. this study proposes an innovative approach to address this challenge by leveraging acoustic sensing technology, inspired by geophysical surveys of ocean sediments. by deploying a compact vertical array of pressure sensors on a small boat, we can measure the noise field generated by a nearby ship, which can be used to infer the properties of the maternal body and placenta. this method can detect subtle changes in maternal health, including asthma exacerbations, allowing for early intervention and improved outcomes for both mother and fetus. our approach combines machine learning algorithms with sequential bayesian estimation to optimize the accuracy of acoustic sensing. we demonstrate the feasibility of this method in a case study of a pregnant woman with asthma, comparing our results with ground truth data from clinical records. this novel application of acoustic sensing has the potential to revolutionize the monitoring and management of pregnancy-associated asthma, reducing healthcare costs and improving maternal and fetal health outcomes.
one_shot: this interdisciplinary research project integrates asthma management in pregnant women with geophysical survey methods to investigate the impact of environmental triggers on respiratory health outcomes in mother and newborn. we aim to develop a novel, data-driven approach to predict asthma exacerbation risks in pregnant women by analyzing geophysical data from coastal areas, where environmental factors such as noise pollution from shipping traffic can exacerbate respiratory symptoms. our method combines machine learning algorithms with acoustic observables from pressure and pressure-gradient sensors to identify correlations between sound wave propagation and asthma symptom severity. this research has the potential to inform evidence-based management strategies for asthma in pregnancy, ultimately improving maternal and newborn health outcomes.
few_shot: asthma is a significant health concern during pregnancy, with approximately 3% to 8% of pregnant women affected, exacerbating chronic medical problems and potentially passing complications to newborns. this study integrates geophysical and ecosystem surveys with asthma research to analyze the complex relationships between environmental factors, maternal health, and fetal outcomes. by leveraging acoustic sensors and ship noise fields, we investigate how sediment properties, like mud, fluid mud, and sand, influence the propagation of compressional waves and subsequently the respiratory health of pregnant women. we develop a novel approach combining machine learning with sequential bayesian estimation to optimize the degree of similarity between predicted and measured impedance data, allowing for more accurate predictions of maternal and newborn outcomes. our results provide a new framework for understanding the connection between environmental and health factors, enabling proactive management strategies for improved maternal and fetal outcomes.
got: exacerbations of asthma during pregnancy pose significant risks to both maternal and fetal health. we propose a novel approach to mitigate these risks by leveraging advanced underwater acoustic sensing technologies, inspired by the methods used to study bottom sediments in marine environments. by adapting compact vertical arrays of pressure or pressure-gradient sensors to monitor respiratory sounds in patients with asthma, we can extract and parameterize striation structures in range-frequency spectrograms to determine airway compression wave speed and thickness of a mucous layer. this non-invasive method can be used to monitor airway changes in real-time, allowing for early detection of exacerbations and timely intervention. we will demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach in a cohort of pregnant women with asthma, comparing the results with traditional clinical assessments and exploring the potential for seamless integration with existing healthcare systems. this interdisciplinary research has the potential to improve maternal and fetal outcomes by providing a more accurate and non-invasive means of monitoring asthma exacerbations during pregnancy.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: we give further insights into the weighted hurwitz product and the weighted tensor product of joyal species. our first group of results relate the hurwitz product to the pointwise product, including the interaction with rota--baxter operators. our second group of results explain the first in terms of convolution with suitable bialgebras, and show that these bialgebras are in fact obtained in a particularly straightforward way by freely generating from pointed coalgebras. our third group of results extend this from linear algebra to two-dimensional linear algebra, deriving the existence of weighted hurwitz monoidal structures on the category of species using convolution with freely generated bimonoidales. our final group of results relate hurwitz monoidal structures with equivalences of of dold--kan type.
abstract1: this paper deals with the notion of justice in my faith journey for korean reunification. this paper is a study of biographical and ideological disclosure. after an analysis of my personal journey in the korean student democratic movement, minjung theology is analyzed in order to construct a new society in which all people will live together in peace and justice. for the understanding of justice, i work with the notion of oppression in iris marion young (2 january 1949 - 1 august 2006), a professor of political science at the university of chicago. in doing this i am claiming that korean reunification is about a going forward to create a new creation of a unified nation of people and to seek the construction of a new society in which all people will live together in a peaceful and just society. the rich understandings that emerge from the relationship between faith and ideology i work with in this paper will contribute, i believe, to a definition of justice that is not limited to a political thing, but aims to protect the dignity of every person and to contribute to the full participation of all koreans in one society.
