abstract1: talking drums have been a unique means of information dissemination in the rural areas of africa. the talking drums have two varieties: bata and dundun. they are employed for different purposes. bata is used by the sango worshippers during their festival. dundun is used for a variety of purposes, ranging from commercial, extra-mundane, cultural, proverbial and information purposes. however, dundun talking drums are employed to pitch two communities against each other. they may also be used to disguise the ‘praisee’ physical defects. if one is bald, the talking drummer has a means of saying one is not. if the praise poem of a short man is being chanted, the talking drummer has a means of saying he is tall. therefore, talking drums, as useful as they are, have their own abuses to which they are put. the study is driven by democratic-participant media theory which posits that the grass roots should have access to the media of communication, and that the media should give priority attention to the disadvantaged in the society. key words : talking drums, delineation, boundary, border
abstract2: the emission of electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range has been observed in many experiments of laserplasma interaction. these fields can have very high intensity and estimated frequency band up to several gigahertz. the radiation normally affects the behaviour of most of the detectors, often up to hundreds of nanoseconds from the laser pulse, and can represent a serious limitation for the time-of-ight detection of fast particles, and in general for the safe operation of the electronic equipment. in this work we describe the measurements of this electromagnetic pulse, under different conditions of laser-plasma interaction. c
gen_abs: this interdisciplinary study explores the phenomenon of electromagnetic pulses generated by laser-plasma interactions and their impact on information dissemination in rural africa. inspired by the democratic-participant media theory, we investigate how the emission of electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range can be leveraged to enhance communication in areas where traditional media are lacking. by applying the mathematical framework of korn's first inequality and poincaré's inequality to the electromagnetic pulse, we establish a novel relationship between the intensity of the pulse and the distortion of information transmitted through talking drums. our research reveals that the electromagnetic pulse can be used to create a "border" between communities, similar to how talking drums are employed to pitch communities against each other. furthermore, we develop a method to "delineate" the distortion of information transmitted through talking drums using the electromagnetic pulse, providing a new perspective on the uses and abuses of talking drums in rural africa. this study has significant implications for the development of participatory and inclusive communication systems in remote areas.
cot: this interdisciplinary study explores the concept of information dissemination in rural african communities, leveraging the unique properties of talking drums and the principles of laser-plasma interaction to develop a novel, community-driven approach to communication. we investigate how the rhythmic patterns and electromagnetic pulses generated by talking drums can be harnessed to enhance the effectiveness of information transmission, particularly in the context of community development and social cohesion. by integrating the democratic-participant media theory with the phenomenon of electromagnetic wave emission, we demonstrate that talking drums can be used as a form of "electromagnetic communication" to convey complex information and promote grassroots participation. our findings have implications for the development of novel, community-based communication systems that balance the needs of the disadvantaged with the demands of technological advancement. this research offers a new perspective on the intersection of traditional and modern communication technologies, with potential applications in fields such as community development, public health, and conflict resolution.
tot: this study investigates the potential of exploiting the properties of electromagnetic waves emitted during laser-plasma interactions to develop novel, low-cost, and community-based communication systems inspired by the traditional african talking drums. building on the principles of democratic-participant media theory, we explore the feasibility of harnessing the high-intensity, radiofrequency-microwave electromagnetic pulses (emps) generated by laser-plasma interactions to create a decentralized, grass-roots communication network. the emps can be used to modulate the acoustic properties of materials, effectively creating a new form of "talking drum" that can convey information over long distances, similar to the traditional african drums. we propose to investigate the use of emps to create a hybrid, acoustic-electromagnetic communication system that leverages the traditional cultural significance of talking drums while utilizing cutting-edge scientific principles. our research aims to develop a sustainable, community-driven communication platform that addresses the information needs of disadvantaged communities in rural areas, while also providing a novel solution for detecting and mitigating the effects of laser-plasma interactions in high-energy applications.
raw: this paper integrates two seemingly disparate fields – traditional african communication systems and laser-plasma interactions – to explore the concept of "information pollution" in the context of complex systems. we investigate the intersection of talking drums and electromagnetic pulses, examining how the latter can be used to disrupt or distort traditional communication systems. specifically, we analyze how the high-intensity electromagnetic waves emitted during laser-plasma interactions can be used to interfere with the transmission and reception of information through talking drums, potentially altering the intended meaning and impact of the communication. this phenomenon is particularly relevant in rural african communities where talking drums are a crucial means of information dissemination. our study employs a mixed-methods approach, combining ethnographic research with experimental measurements of electromagnetic pulses. the findings have implications for the study of complex systems, the design of robust communication systems, and the preservation of traditional cultural practices in the face of modern technological advancements.
one_shot: we propose a novel framework that leverages the unique characteristics of talking drums to develop non-invasive, electromagnetic-based sensors for detecting subtle changes in environmental conditions. by analyzing the sound patterns produced by talking drums, we can infer the presence of electromagnetic pulses (emps) generated by laser-plasma interactions. this integration of traditional communication tools and cutting-edge sensor technology enables real-time monitoring of emps, which can have significant implications for applications such as early warning systems for environmental hazards and improved detection of fast particles in high-energy experiments. our results demonstrate the feasibility of this approach, providing a new perspective on the potential applications of talking drums in modern scientific research.
few_shot: this study integrates insights from african talking drum cultures with the physics of electromagnetic pulse (emp) effects in laser-plasma interactions. by recognizing the parallels between the "border" and "boundary" concepts in talking drum communication and the spatial-temporal impact of emps on electronic equipment, we develop a novel framework for mitigating the effects of high-intensity electromagnetic radiation on sensitive systems. our approach, inspired by the deliberate use of talking drums to "pitch" communal narratives, employs machine learning algorithms to predict and adapt to emp-induced disruptions in real-time. this interdisciplinary research aims to enhance the resilience of critical infrastructure and communication networks against emerging threats.
got: this study explores the intersection of traditional communication systems and electromagnetic phenomena in the context of rural african communities. building on the concept of talking drums as a form of democratic-participant media, we investigate the potential for electromagnetic radiation emitted during laser-plasma interactions to impact the operation of talking drums. specifically, we examine how the high-intensity electromagnetic pulses generated by laser-plasma interactions can interfere with the cultural and social practices surrounding the use of talking drums. our research aims to understand the effects of electromagnetic radiation on the functionality and cultural significance of talking drums, with implications for the preservation and adaptation of traditional communication systems in the face of modern technological advancements. this interdisciplinary study combines insights from social anthropology, physics, and communication studies to shed light on the complex interplay between traditional and modern communication systems.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: a new scorpionfish, ebosia saya sp. nov. (scorpaenidae: pteroinae), is described on the basis of 10 specimens collected from the saya de malha bank in depths of 95–126 m. comparisons with two valid indo-pacific species, ebosiableekeri (doderlein in steindachner and doderlein 1884) (western pacific ocean) and ebosiafalcata eschmeyer and rama-rao 1978 (northern and eastern indian ocean), showed e. saya to be most similar to the latter, with both species sharing 8 (usually) anal-fin soft rays, 17 (usually) pectoral-fin rays, the elongated parietal spine of males relatively narrow and strongly falcate posterodorsally, and the pectoral fin predominantly yellow in males, whereas e. bleekeri has 7 (usually) anal-fin soft rays, 16 (usually) pectoral-fin rays, the elongated parietal spine of males relatively broad and weakly falcate, and the pectoral fins entirely red in males. however, e. saya differs from e. falcata in having 5 scale rows above the lateral line [vs. 4 or 5 (modally 4) in e. falcata], slightly greater total numbers (5–20, mean 12.3) of preocular, supraocular, and postocular spines at 60–90 mm sl (vs. 4–18, 8.4), shorter postorbital length 18.1–19.2 (mean 18.7) % sl [vs. 19.8–23.5 (21.5) % sl], and the blotch above the pectoral-fin base and blotches on the pectoral-fin membrane all relatively smaller. fresh coloration of e. falcata is described for the first time, based on specimens from the andaman sea (easternmost record for the species).
abstract1: the purpose of this paper is to present some reflections address the issue of port security in colombia ; in which some shortcomings and difficulties that have limited their growth as they are defined : the maritime terrorism, human trafficking , drug trafficking , arms trafficking and smuggling; on which it is necessary to present some proposals regarding the model of human resource management to be implemented by port authorities and administrations
abstract2: the invention discloses a motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence comprise s1: performing normalization processing of any given motion vector field; s2: calculating the curl and the divergence of each point in the normalized motion vector field to obtain a curl view and a divergence view; and constructing a dual vector field of the normalized motion vector field; s3: performing motion trial sampling in the normalized motion vector field and the dual vector field to obtain a tangential motion trail and a radial motion trail respectively; and s4: performing path integration of the curl and the divergence on the motion trails so as to obtain a cd (curl and divergence) motion feature with invariable scale and curl. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence can depict the curl and the divergence of motion integrally, and is high in robustness. as a motion descriptor with universality, the cd feature can be widely applied in motion analysis and behavior identification under different scenes.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel, interdisciplinary approach to maritime security and surveillance, leveraging motion analysis and behavioral identification techniques to enhance port security in colombia. building on the concept of motion feature extraction based on curl and divergence, as proposed in [paper 2], we develop a system for detecting and tracking suspicious behaviors in port environments. by applying the cd (curl and divergence) motion feature to analyze motion patterns and flows within ports, we can identify potential security threats, such as human trafficking, drug trafficking, and arms smuggling. our system integrates with a proposed human resource management model for port authorities and administrations, as discussed in [paper 1], to provide a comprehensive framework for monitoring and responding to security incidents. we also draw inspiration from the unique characteristics of the scorpionfish species, ebosia saya, which exhibits remarkable adaptability and variation in its appearance, to inform the development of our system's adaptability and robustness. our system is designed to be scalable and deployable in various port environments, providing a versatile tool for enhancing maritime security and promoting the safety of personnel and cargo.
cot: port security in colombia faces significant challenges due to maritime terrorism, human trafficking, drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and smuggling. effective management of human resources by port authorities and administrations is crucial to address these issues. inspired by advances in computer vision, we propose an innovative approach to enhance port security through the application of a motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence. this method, known as cd-port, utilizes a normalized motion vector field to calculate curl and divergence views, construct a dual vector field, and perform motion trail sampling to obtain tangential and radial motion trails. the cd (curl and divergence) motion feature is then extracted by integrating the curl and divergence on these trails. by leveraging this motion descriptor, cd-port enables robust and efficient identification of suspicious activities, such as smuggling and human trafficking, in port environments. our proposed system has the potential to significantly improve port security in colombia and other regions, while also contributing to the advancement of computer vision and motion analysis research.
tot: this paper proposes an innovative approach to enhance port security in colombia by deploying a motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence in surveillance systems. the method, inspired by the motion descriptor cd feature, enables the detection and identification of suspicious activities, such as maritime terrorism, human trafficking, drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and smuggling. by integrating the cd feature with human resource management strategies, we can improve the effectiveness of port authorities and administrations in monitoring and controlling security threats. the proposed system, dubbed "port-cd," involves four stages: normalization of motion vector fields, calculation of curl and divergence, motion trail sampling, and path integration of curl and divergence. port-cd is capable of depicting the curl and divergence of motion integrally, providing a robust and scalable solution for motion analysis and behavior identification in complex scenes. we demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of port-cd in a simulated port environment, showcasing its potential to improve port security and compliance with international regulations. the proposed framework serves as a novel and realistic approach to addressing the shortcomings and difficulties in port security faced by colombia and other countries.
raw: this paper presents an innovative approach to enhance port security in colombia, leveraging the extraction of motion features to detect and prevent illicit activities. building on the concept of curl and divergence, our method, dubbed "portguard," utilizes a motion feature extraction system to analyze the behavior of individuals and vessels within port premises. by applying the curl and divergence to normalized motion vector fields, our system generates a comprehensive "cd feature" that captures the dynamic patterns of motion, enabling robust anomaly detection. we propose the integration of this cd feature into a human resource management model, implemented by port authorities and administrations, to enhance surveillance and risk assessment capabilities. the cd feature's universality and invariance to scale make it an ideal tool for motion analysis and behavior identification in various scenarios, including maritime terrorism, human trafficking, and arms smuggling. our approach addresses the shortcomings of traditional port security measures and offers a novel, scalable, and effective solution to improve port security in colombia and beyond.
one_shot: secure port operations are increasingly vulnerable to various threats, including maritime terrorism, human trafficking, drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and smuggling. to address these challenges, effective human resource management is essential for port authorities and administrations. inspired by the concept of motion feature extraction, we propose a novel approach to analyze the dynamics of port security risk factors. by leveraging the curl and divergence (cd) motion feature extraction method and system, we can construct a dual vector field representing the spatial and temporal variations of security threats. this framework enables the identification of high-risk areas and vulnerabilities, facilitating targeted interventions. by integrating the cd motion feature extraction with human resource management strategies, we aim to develop a more proactive and responsive approach to port security, ultimately enhancing the resilience of global supply chains and economic stability.
few_shot: the increasing threat of maritime terrorism, human trafficking, and other malicious activities in ports raises concerns about the security and management of human resources in these critical infrastructure nodes. to address this challenge, we propose a novel motion feature extraction method, based on curl and divergence, to analyze and identify suspicious behaviors. by integrating this method with a proposed human resource management model, we can enhance the security and efficiency of port operations. our approach leverages the robust motion feature extraction method to detect anomalies in human movement patterns, which is then used to inform the optimization of staffing, training, and resource allocation for port authorities and administrations. this interdisciplinary approach aims to improve the resilience and response of ports to security threats, while reducing the risk of human trafficking and other malicious activities.
got: this paper proposes a novel approach to enhancing port security through the integration of motion analysis and human resource management. building on the motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence, which effectively captures the intricacies of motion patterns, we develop a motion analysis system to detect and track suspicious activities in port areas. the system uses a dual-vector field approach to identify potential security risks, such as human trafficking, drug trafficking, and arms smuggling, by extracting features from motion trails and performing path integration of curl and divergence. to address the human resource management shortcomings in port security, we implement an adaptive personnel allocation strategy that leverages machine learning algorithms to optimize the deployment of security personnel based on real-time motion data. this hybrid approach enables more effective and efficient security operations, reducing the likelihood of security breaches and improving overall port security. the proposed system is evaluated through simulations and field tests, demonstrating its potential to significantly enhance port security and mitigate the risks associated with maritime terrorism, human trafficking, and other forms of illicit activities.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: espanolla reapertura de japon y su transformacion durante el periodo meiji tuvieron implicaciones regionales que llevaron al reforzamiento de los vinculos con las colonias asiaticas del imperio espanol durante la segunda mitad del siglo xix. en el marco de las relaciones iniciadas con el proyecto para la firma del tratado hispano-japones de 1868, destacaron tres procesos historicos. el primero fue la incorporacion del gobierno espanol al sistema de "tratados desiguales" vigente en japon. en segundo lugar, el papel de las islas filipinas, marianas y carolinas en la nanshin-ron o "teorias del avance hacia el sur". finalmente, destaco la intensificacion de las relaciones entre los archipielagos japones y filipino durante la ultima decada de este siglo. englishthe reopening of japan and its transformation during the meiji period had regional implications which led to the strengthening of links with the spanish empire's asian colonies during the second half of the nineteenth century. three historic projects stand out within the framework of relations established through the project to sign the hispano- japanese treaty of 1868. the first was the spanish government's inclusion in the "unequal treaties" system in force in japan. the second was the role of the philippine, mariana and caroline lslands in the nanshin-ron or "southern expansion doctrine". finally, the study highlights the intensifying of relations between the japanese and philippine archipelagos during the last decade of this century.
abstract1: the invention discloses an extraction process of chrysanthemum polysaccharide. with commercially available chrysanthemum as a raw material, extraction is carried out by adopting a mode of combining water extraction with alkali extraction, namely water extraction is firstly adopted to obtain one part of polysaccharide extracting solution; filter residues are subjected to alkali extraction to obtain another part of polysaccharide extracting solution; and the two parts of extracting solutions are merged and concentrated to obtain a final product. according to the extraction process of the chrysanthemum polysaccharide disclosed by the invention, compared with an existing conventional extraction process, the obtained chrysanthemum polysaccharide has higher yield, higher anti-oxidation activity and higher anti-inflammatory activity.
abstract2: an efficient and improved traffic information system provided by the present invention comprises a total processing device, and the total processing device is in circuit connection with a monitoring server, an alarm device, a remote monitoring terminal, a networks management device, an environmental information acquisition device and an energy consumption management module, and is in signal connection with a gps navigation module, an etc module, a wireless communication module and a wind speed sensor.
gen_abs: a novel, interdisciplinary approach is proposed to develop a smart, adaptive traffic information system (sats) for mitigating the impacts of natural disasters on urban traffic infrastructure. this system integrates advanced technologies from the fields of polysaccharide-based biomaterials and traffic management. inspired by the biodegradable and self-healing properties of chrysanthemum polysaccharides, we design and fabricate a network of self-healing sensors and materials for early warning systems and infrastructure repair. by leveraging the high yield and anti-oxidation activity of chrysanthemum polysaccharides, we develop a robust and efficient approach to create biodegradable, asphalt-based road materials that can withstand extreme weather conditions. the sats integrates with a big-data analytics platform, incorporating real-time data from gps navigation, environmental monitoring, and energy management systems to provide proactive traffic management and infrastructure maintenance strategies. this innovation is rooted in historical precedents, such as the 19th-century spanish colonial relations with japan and its asian colonies, which facilitated the exchange of knowledge and technologies. by combining cutting-edge biomaterials and traffic management, the sats offers a paradigm shift in urban infrastructure resilience and sustainability.
cot: this study integrates plant biology and transportation systems to develop a novel approach for improving traffic flow and reducing traffic congestion. by utilizing the high anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidation properties of chrysanthemum polysaccharide, we propose a bio-inspired solution to mitigate the effects of traffic-related stress on drivers and passengers. our research combines the extraction process of chrysanthemum polysaccharide, which yields a high-quality bioactive compound with improved yield and enhanced physiological activity, with an intelligent traffic management system. the latter employs an efficient and improved traffic information system, comprising a network of sensors, cameras, and gps navigation, to monitor and optimize traffic flow. our hybrid system aims to reduce driver stress and anxiety, thereby improving traffic safety and reducing the environmental impact of traffic congestion. by leveraging the synergy between plant-based bioactive compounds and intelligent transportation systems, this study offers a promising solution for creating a more sustainable and stress-free urban transportation environment.
tot: an integrated traffic management and environmental monitoring system is proposed to enhance traffic safety and reduce environmental pollution. the system combines a chrysanthemum polysaccharide-based air purification module with an advanced traffic information system. inspired by the efficient extraction method of chrysanthemum polysaccharide, a novel air purification technology is developed to capture pollutants and particulate matter from traffic emissions. the system utilizes a network of sensors, including wind speed sensors and gps navigation modules, to monitor and optimize traffic flow, while minimizing air pollution. the chrysanthemum polysaccharide-based air purification module is integrated with an efficient traffic information system, comprising a total processing device and a monitoring server, to provide real-time traffic updates and air quality alerts. this innovative system aims to improve traffic safety, reduce environmental pollution, and promote sustainable urban development. preliminary results show promising improvements in air quality and traffic efficiency.
raw: we propose a novel, integrated approach to urban traffic management enabled by a hybrid system that combines advanced biotechnology and iot (internet of things) technologies. our system, dubbed "smart traffic harmony," leverages the anti-oxidation and anti-inflammatory properties of chrysanthemum polysaccharide to develop an innovative, eco-friendly traffic calming agent. by extracting chrysanthemum polysaccharide using a novel, two-stage extraction process that enhances yield and activity, we create a bio-based additive that reduces air pollution and mitigates traffic-related stress. this polysaccharide is then integrated into a smart traffic management system, which utilizes a comprehensive network of sensors, gps navigation, and real-time monitoring to optimize traffic flow and reduce congestion. our system includes an energy-efficient management module that minimizes power consumption and an environmental information acquisition device that provides real-time data on air quality and traffic conditions. by merging biotechnology and iot, we demonstrate a sustainable and intelligent approach to urban traffic management, enhancing the quality of life for citizens while promoting environmental stewardship. this integrated system has the potential to transform urban mobility and mitigate the negative impacts of traffic on public health.
one_shot: the urban environment's impact on citizens' health is increasingly evident, with air pollution and poor living conditions contributing to chronic diseases. to address this, we propose an integrated approach that leverages the extraction of chrysanthemum polysaccharides, known for their anti-oxidation and anti-inflammatory properties, in combination with real-time traffic information systems. our system, dubbed "smart green corridors," utilizes environmentally responsive monitoring stations and sensors to detect and mitigate pollution hotspots. by implementing a water-alkali extraction process to yield high-quality chrysanthemum polysaccharides, we can create urban "green corridors" where vegetation and bioactive compounds can be strategically deployed to reduce air pollution and improve public health. this synergistic approach has the potential to transform urban planning and mitigate the effects of climate change on urban ecosystems.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach to optimizing traffic flow in urban environments by integrating a decentralized traffic information system with a chrysanthemum polysaccharide-based extraction process. the traffic system, comprising a gps navigation module, environmental information acquisition device, and energy consumption management module, is linked to a monitoring server and alarm device. the extraction process, combining water and alkali extraction, yields a high-quality polysaccharide with enhanced anti-oxidation and anti-inflammatory activities. by monitoring and adjusting traffic flow in real-time, our system reduces congestion and travel time while utilizing the bioactive properties of chrysanthemum polysaccharide to mitigate the effects of pollution and air quality degradation. simulations demonstrate improved traffic efficiency and reduced environmental impact.
got: this study introduces a novel, sustainable, and intelligent traffic management system that leverages chrysanthemum polysaccharide extraction to enhance road safety and reduce traffic congestion. the system, dubbed "greenflow," integrates a hybrid extraction process of chrysanthemum polysaccharide, which combines water and alkali extraction methods to achieve higher yield, anti-oxidation activity, and anti-inflammatory activity. the extracted polysaccharide is then integrated with a smart traffic management system, comprising a total processing device, monitoring server, alarm device, and environmental information acquisition device. the system is equipped with a gps navigation module, wireless communication module, and wind speed sensor to optimize traffic signal timing and reduce congestion. the chrysanthemum polysaccharide is employed as a natural, eco-friendly, and non-toxic additive in road surface coatings, reducing the risk of accidents caused by slippery roads. the system's efficiency is demonstrated through simulations and real-world tests, showing a significant reduction in traffic congestion and accident rates. this interdisciplinary approach offers a paradigm shift in intelligent transportation systems, providing a safer, more sustainable, and efficient traffic management solution.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: this paper investigates the impact of extreme shocks on stock and bond markets on listed european banks. the originality of our approach consists in dealing jointly with stock and bond markets and taking into account their interdependencies in case of extreme events by using a specific cvrf (cvine risk factor) model which combines copulas and a factorial structure. moreover, contrary to what is generally done in the literature, we do not focus only on the responses of the stock returns but we also examine the response of the balance sheets of the banks and particularly of their short term assets in order to assess their fragility in terms of liquidity. our main findings are the following: 1) the nature of the banks' fragility has changed: today, the interest rate risk should be the first concern before the equity risk, as the banks have extensively increased their exposition to bond market due to flight-to-quality reactions and to large investments in governments bonds after the rescue operations the banks have benefited; 2) in case of a surge in the interest rate and in the links between stock and bond returns, the portfolios of the biggest banks in europe would experience very severe shortfalls for both equity and liquidity buffers. accordingly regulators should monitor the evolution of dependencies between assets and should pay utmost attention to the positive links between stock and bond returns.
abstract1: in this paper we present a channel measurement campaign in indoor environment aiming the characterization of the angular properties of the channel in the presence of the human body. measurements were performed by using isolated uwb antenna and body-mounted antenna on different positions. multipath components are estimated by means of sage algorithm. from these estimations, clusters are detected and characterized in the delay and angular domains.
abstract2: a camera configuration tool that facilitates a method for configuring and managing internet protocol (ip) cameras across a network. a software program scans a network to discover a plurality of ip cameras, wherein camera data for each of the plurality of ip cameras is retrieved and added to a camera table as a camera item that is selectable. through a graphical user interface, a user can then manage and configure the plurality of ip cameras. a web interface access request, restart request, live feed request, and reset request can be sent to a specific camera from the plurality of ip cameras through the graphical user interface in addition to adjusting image settings and transmission settings, and uploading a firmware file to the specific camera. the camera data for the plurality of ip cameras can also be exported to a video management software, spreadsheet file, or text file.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel approach to monitoring and managing wireless sensor networks (wsns) in indoor environments, where devices are prone to multipath interference and human body occlusions. building on the angular characterization of indoor channels and the estimation of multipath components using the sage algorithm, we integrate a camera configuration tool to facilitate the deployment and management of ip cameras for real-time monitoring and anomaly detection. our method leverages the insights from the intermediate idea on extreme shocks, recognizing that wsns are vulnerable to disruptions in event of extreme events, such as natural disasters or cyber-attacks. by taking into account the interdependencies between stock and bond markets, we develop a novel risk assessment framework to evaluate the fragility of wsns in terms of their ability to maintain connectivity and data integrity. our tool allows for real-time monitoring of network performance, camera settings, and firmware updates, enabling proactive measures to mitigate the effects of disruptions and ensure reliable operation of wsns in critical environments.
cot: this paper presents a novel approach to monitoring and optimizing the performance of indoor wireless sensor networks, which are increasingly used for applications such as smart buildings and iot. by integrating channel measurement techniques with camera configuration tools, we enable real-time monitoring of multipath propagation effects caused by the human body. the proposed system uses body-mounted and isolated uwb antennas to estimate multipath components, which are then used to optimize camera placement and configuration. the camera configuration tool, introduced in paper 2, is adapted to retrieve and display camera feeds from strategically positioned ip cameras, providing a visual representation of the indoor environment. this visual feedback is integrated with the channel measurement data to create a comprehensive monitoring system. the system is demonstrated in a smart office setting, where it reduces multipath-induced errors and improves overall network performance. our approach has the potential to enhance the reliability and efficiency of iot applications in indoor environments.
tot: this paper presents a novel approach to optimizing indoor wireless sensor networks using a combination of channel measurement and camera configuration techniques. by leveraging the angular properties of the channel in the presence of the human body, as characterized through a uwb antenna and body-mounted antenna measurement campaign, we can improve the accuracy of camera placement and orientation in indoor environments. our method integrates a camera configuration tool with the channel measurement data to identify optimal camera positions and angles for capturing robust video feeds. the tool, which scans a network to discover and configure ip cameras, allows users to manage camera settings and transmission parameters, including image settings and firmware updates. through experimental results, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in enhancing video quality and reducing the impact of multipath components, paving the way for more reliable and efficient indoor surveillance systems. this work has potential applications in smart spaces, surveillance systems, and internet of things (iot) networks.
raw: we present a novel approach to optimizing the placement and configuration of internet protocol (ip) cameras in indoor environments. building upon the angular properties of the channel characterization presented in paper 1, we propose a camera configuration tool that leverages channel measurement data to predict and mitigate multipath effects. by analyzing the spatial distribution of multipath components, our tool provides recommendations for optimal camera placement and orientation to minimize interference and ensure clear video feeds. furthermore, we integrate the camera configuration tool with ip camera management software, enabling network administrators to remotely access and configure cameras, monitor live feeds, and export video data for further analysis. our proposed system has the potential to improve the accuracy and reliability of video surveillance systems in indoor environments, with applications in smart buildings, public safety, and security monitoring.
one_shot: we propose a novel framework for monitoring and managing the performance of internet protocol (ip) cameras in indoor environments, where human body presence significantly affects the wireless channel. by leveraging the angular properties of the channel estimated through a channel measurement campaign, our framework integrates camera configuration and management tools to optimize ip camera placement and settings for robust video transmission. a software program scans the network to discover ip cameras, retrieves their metadata, and facilitates management through a graphical user interface. the integrated system enables users to configure cameras to adapt to changing wireless channel conditions, ensuring reliable video feed and high-quality images in the presence of the human body. this interdisciplinary approach has the potential to enhance surveillance and monitoring applications in various indoor settings, such as smart buildings and retail spaces.
few_shot: we present a novel approach to optimize camera placement and configuration for wireless sensor networks in indoor environments with human presence. by leveraging the angular properties of the channel characterized through ultra-wideband (uwb) measurements, we develop a camera configuration tool that integrates with ip camera management software. our method scans the network to discover available cameras, retrieves device information, and facilitates remote configuration and management through a graphical user interface. by accounting for multipath components and human body effects on signal propagation, we enable accurate camera calibration and optimal positioning for enhanced video quality and network performance. experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in real-world indoor settings, showcasing improved signal strength, reduced interference, and enhanced video transmission quality.
got: we propose an innovative approach to optimizing the placement and configuration of internet protocol (ip) cameras in indoor environments, where human presence significantly affects the propagation of electromagnetic waves. by leveraging the angular properties of the communication channel in the presence of the human body, as characterized through ultra-wideband (uwb) antenna measurements, we develop a data-driven method to predict and mitigate multipath effects on camera feeds. our camera configuration tool integrates with the uwb measurement data to provide a graphical user interface for configuring ip cameras, adjusting their field of view, and optimizing their placement to minimize the impact of multipath components. the tool also enables live feed requests and firmware updates for each camera. this integrated approach enables more accurate and robust surveillance and monitoring in complex indoor environments, with applications in various fields, including public safety, security, and smart buildings. our method demonstrates significant improvements in video quality and reduces the complexity of camera placement, leading to more efficient and effective surveillance systems.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the invention discloses an aloe and xylooligosaccharide soft capsule capable of loosening bowel to relieve constipation. the aloe and xylooligosaccharide soft capsule is refined through the following components in parts by weight: 30-80 parts of freeze drying powder of full leaves of aloe vera, 220-260 parts of xylooligosaccharide, 450-500 parts of corn oil, 10-20 parts of beeswax, 130-135 parts of gelatin, 50-55 parts of glycerine, 120-135 parts of pure water, 0.10-0.15 part of titanium dioxide and 0.2-0.5 part of sodium copper chlorophyll. the preparation method comprises the following steps: preparing capsule core feed liquid, preparing capsule shell glue liquid, preparing capsules, shaping the obtained capsules under the conditions that the temperature is 20-25 deg c and the relative humidity is 40-50%, drying the shaped capsules under the conditions that the temperature is 20-25 deg c and the relative humidity is 30-40%, cleaning the dried capsules with ethanol of 95%, drying the cleaned capsules, choosing the dried capsules, inspecting the chosen capsules, packaging the qualified capsules, and warehousing the packaged capsules. the aloe and xylooligosaccharide soft capsule disclosed by the invention can promote large intestines to squirm and loosen bowel to relieve constipation, has the function of obviously improving functions of gastrointestinal tracts, does not have any toxic and side effects, and contributes to improving the symptoms of people suffering from constipation and disorderly intestinal tract functions after being eaten for a long term.
abstract1: poster presentation background asthma is one of the leading chronic medical problems exacerbated during pregnancy, and this condition affects approximately 3% to 8% of the pregnant population. the health care team needs to be educated on factors that may trigger an exacerbation of asthma in pregnancy, appropriate management, treatment techniques to share with their patients, and the complications that an exacerbation of maternal asthma may pass on to the newborn. the goal for all asthmatic patients is to remain asymptomatic during pregnancy to assist in improving maternal and newborn outcomes. case a 30‐year‐old, g4p1 woman at 27 weeks gestation was transported by air to our large level‐iii mid atlantic trauma center from a satellite medical emergency department with status asthmaticus for further management. presenting with possible influenza virus, her history included asthma, positive cigarette use, and a recent respiratory infection. her respiratory status worsened upon arrival, and she exhibited severe hypoxemia and required intubation. deterioration in maternal and newborn status prompted obstetricians to make the decision to deliver the infant via cesarean to improve the outcomes for mother and newborn. unfortunately, the mother did not survive the recovery phase. this event indicated a need for the health care team to refresh their knowledge on current triggers, treatment and management techniques, and the neonatal complications related to exacerbation of asthma during pregnancy. conclusion asthma is a chronic medical condition that complicates pregnancy. roughly one third of the symptoms of pregnant women improve, one third remain the same, and one third worsen. health care providers need to diligently monitor their patients for signs and symptoms of exacerbation. management strategies should focus on identifying risk factors, educating the patient and the health care team, and improving communication skills to guarantee successful outcomes for mother and fetus.
abstract2: the utility model discloses a novel accuse cigarette stretch out with automatic cigarette case of igniteing, including shell, inner shell, pin, the shell on the lower cover through the pin swing joint, the shell internal fixation is provided with the inner shell, be provided with the guide slot on the inner shell, guide slot one side is provided with and pushes away the cigarette slider, push away cigarette slider bottom and be provided with and push away the cigarette tray, simultaneously, push away cigarette slider and extension spring one end is connected move about be fixed in the guide slot on, the extension spring other end is fixed in on the inner shell through the extension spring tie point, the beneficial effect of the utility model: this device simple structure, low in production cost, this device is provided with the automatic cigarette control function of play, simultaneously, still can be with in cigarette push back to the casing, the person does not greatly facilitate the use, in addition, this device is provided with the ignition function, possesses to realize the ignition function when going out the cigarette function voluntarily, realizes that the function is diversified.
gen_abs: this study investigates the efficacy of a novel, non-pharmacological approach to managing asthma exacerbations in pregnancy using a personalized, automated cigarette device that incorporates aloe and xylooligosaccharide soft capsules. the device, inspired by the utility model disclosed in paper 2, utilizes a spring-loaded mechanism to dispense the soft capsules, which contain aloe vera and xylooligosaccharide, a prebiotic that promotes gut health and relieves constipation. by promoting gut health, the soft capsules may help mitigate the inflammatory response associated with asthma exacerbations, as described in paper 1. a prospective, randomized controlled trial was conducted to evaluate the safety and efficacy of this approach in pregnant women with asthma. the results show that the intervention group experienced significant reductions in asthma symptoms and improved lung function compared to the control group. furthermore, the soft capsules were found to be safe and well-tolerated. this study provides evidence for the potential of personalized, automated cigarette devices that incorporate aloe and xylooligosaccharide soft capsules as a novel, non-pharmacological approach to managing asthma exacerbations in pregnancy.
cot: title: integrating technology and healthcare to improve asthma management during pregnancy asthma is a leading chronic medical condition complicating pregnancy, affecting approximately 3-8% of pregnant women. current management strategies focus on identifying risk factors, educating patients and healthcare teams, and improving communication skills to guarantee successful outcomes for mother and fetus. however, existing devices for asthma management, such as automatic cigarette lighters, can inadvertently exacerbate respiratory symptoms by promoting smoking behavior. this study proposes a novel, affordable, and user-friendly device that integrates asthma management and smoking cessation techniques. the device, designed in collaboration with healthcare professionals and engineers, combines a smart inhaler with a smoking cessation system, providing real-time monitoring and feedback to patients. the device's automatic cigarette control function and ignition system can be programmed to discourage smoking behavior while promoting adherence to asthma treatment plans. preliminary results suggest that this integrated device can improve asthma control, reduce smoking rates, and enhance maternal and fetal outcomes. keywords: asthma management, smoking cessation, pregnancy, healthcare technology.
tot: asthma is a significant health concern during pregnancy, affecting approximately 3-8% of pregnant women, and exacerbations can lead to severe maternal and neonatal complications. current management strategies focus on identifying risk factors, educating patients, and improving communication skills to guarantee successful outcomes. however, a critical knowledge gap exists among healthcare providers regarding the triggers, treatment, and management techniques for asthma exacerbations during pregnancy. this study proposes a novel approach to address this gap by integrating a user-centered design for a portable, low-cost, and user-friendly device that assists asthma patients in managing their condition during pregnancy. the device, inspired by the utility model of an automatic cigarette lighter, incorporates a smartphone app that tracks patient symptoms, monitors medication adherence, and provides real-time alerts for potential exacerbations. by leveraging the device's automatic ignition function, patients can more easily use their asthma inhalers and receive timely reminders to take their medication. our research aims to evaluate the feasibility and effectiveness of this device in improving asthma management during pregnancy, leading to better maternal and neonatal outcomes.
raw: asthma exacerbation during pregnancy poses a significant risk to both mother and fetus, with approximately 3-8% of pregnant women affected. despite advances in medical knowledge, healthcare providers often lack up-to-date information on triggering factors, treatment techniques, and neonatal complications. this study aims to integrate innovative smoking cessation technology with current asthma management practices to improve maternal and fetal outcomes. a novel, low-cost, and user-friendly smoking cessation device, equipped with automatic cigarette ignition and control, was designed to facilitate smoking cessation. in a pilot study, 20 pregnant women with asthma were provided with the device and received education on asthma management and smoking cessation strategies. results showed a significant reduction in asthma symptoms and improved smoking cessation rates compared to traditional methods. the study demonstrates the potential of integrating innovative technology with evidence-based practices to improve health outcomes for pregnant women with asthma. these findings have significant implications for healthcare providers and policymakers seeking to address the complex needs of this vulnerable population.
one_shot: asthma exacerbation during pregnancy poses significant risks to both maternal and newborn health. current literature highlights the importance of effective management and education for healthcare providers to optimize outcomes. drawing from recent advancements in device design, we propose an innovative approach to asthma management during pregnancy: an automated, portable, and user-friendly inhaler device that incorporates a novel cigarette-free ignition system. this device, inspired by the utility model described in paper 2, simplifies asthma treatment and reduces environmental triggers. by integrating this device into existing asthma management strategies, as discussed in paper 1, healthcare providers can improve patient education and adherence to treatment plans. our interdisciplinary research aims to evaluate the efficacy of this device in reducing asthma exacerbations during pregnancy and improving maternal-newborn outcomes, ultimately enhancing the quality of care for this vulnerable population.
few_shot: asthma exacerbations during pregnancy pose significant health risks to both mother and fetus. current management strategies focus on identifying risk factors, educating patients and healthcare teams, and improving communication skills. however, existing devices for managing asthma symptoms, such as inhalers and spacers, often lack user-friendly designs and automatic control functions. we propose a novel, low-cost, and easy-to-use automatic cigarette case with ignition function, designed to help patients manage asthma symptoms while smoking. by integrating this device into asthma management protocols, healthcare teams can improve patient adherence to treatment plans and reduce the risk of exacerbations. our device's automatic control function and user-friendly design facilitate smoking cessation and symptom management, ultimately improving maternal and fetal outcomes.
got: asthma exacerbations during pregnancy pose significant risks to both maternal and fetal health. this study integrates insights from two seemingly disparate fields: asthma management in pregnancy and cigarette smoking. a novel approach to addressing asthma exacerbations in pregnancy is proposed, leveraging a low-cost, automatic cigarette ignition device designed to reduce smoking initiation and cessation difficulties. this device, previously developed for smokers, is repurposed to support pregnant smokers attempting to quit. by providing a convenient, user-friendly mechanism for smoking cessation, this technology can help mitigate the risks associated with smoking during pregnancy. our research aims to explore the efficacy of this device in reducing asthma exacerbations in pregnant smokers. we hypothesize that the automatic cigarette ignition device will decrease the frequency and severity of asthma attacks in pregnant smokers, ultimately improving maternal and fetal outcomes. this interdisciplinary study combines expertise in asthma management, smoking cessation, and device design to provide a novel solution to a critical public health issue.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: objective management of meals and mealtime behavior is often challenging for parents of young children with type 1 diabetes. parent functioning related to diabetes care may directly affect mealtime behaviors and glycemic control. this study evaluated associations among diabetes-specific parent functioning, parent and child mealtime behaviors, and glycemic control. method parents of young children with type 1 diabetes (n = 134) completed self-report measures assessing diabetes-specific functioning (hypoglycemia fear, diabetes self-efficacy, diabetes-related quality of life) and child and parent mealtime behaviors. hemoglobin a1c and percentage of blood glucose values out of range ( 200 mg/dl) over a 30-day period were abstracted from medical charts as indicators of glycemic control. structural equation modeling was utilized to evaluate predictors and related outcomes of child and parent mealtime behavior. results the proposed model fit the data very well. more frequent problematic child mealtime behaviors were associated with poorer glycemic control; however, more frequent problematic parent mealtime behaviors were marginally associated with better glycemic control. poorer diabetes-specific parent functioning was associated with more frequent problematic child and parent mealtime behaviors. conclusions problematic child mealtime behaviors, such as disruptive behavior, present a significant risk for poorer glycemic control. parents may engage in ineffective mealtime management strategies in an effort to meet glycemic recommendations and avoid hyperglycemia and hypoglycemia. future research will help to determine whether parents may benefit from specific, developmentally appropriate behavioral strategies to manage meals and snacks and promote optimal diabetes management.
abstract1: the business oriented technological system analysis (botsa) program is a new teaching and learning concept developed by eindhoven university of technology (the netherlands) with participation from innovative companies in renewable energy. it is designed to stimulate sustainable entrepreneurship among engineering students in this field. the program combines the placement of students in companies to study and contribute to the development and incubation of sustainable energy innovations, with a curriculum at the university designed to support these internships from a scientific perspective. the teaching method assists students in developing a broad system view that enables them to analyze the potential of, and bottlenecks to promising innovations from a realistic business perspective. this empowers students to identify those techno-economic aspects that are critical to innovation success, and advise the entrepreneurs about these aspects. experience indicates that teachers, students, and entrepreneurs find botsa a valuable way of coaching, learning and working. theoretical support for this method is found in system analysis originating in evolutionary innovation theory in combination with concepts of entrepreneurship, business model generation and sustainable/green innovation.
abstract2: it is critical to confirm the optimum dose and the active duration of the sterilization by quinestrol on rodents for some studies and practices. we fed female and male brandt's voles 5 doses of 0. 5 mg / kg,1. 0 mg / kg,2. 0 mg / kg,3. 0 mg / kg,or 6. 0 mg / kg quiestrol for 7 consecutive days. we then assessed their anti-fertility effects on the voles by analyzing reproduction indices including birth date,reproductive rate and reproductive capacity. results showed that quinestrol delayed the breeding of brandt's voles in the lab,but the effect was not significant due to the small sample size. in 90 days,some doses of quinestrol decreased the reproductive rate and average litter size of female and male,however,the inhibitory effect still was not significant,again possibly due to small sample size; some doses of quinestrol decreased markedly the reproductive capacity of brandt's voles. the suppression effects were not significantly correlated with the doses of quinestrol. the optimum doses of quinestrol causing sterility were below 2. 0 mg / kg for male and 1. 0 mg / kg for female. the dose applyed for brandt's vole should be below 2. 0 mg / kg,which can reduce total litter size of brandt's vole obviously within 90 days.
gen_abs: this study integrates insights from business-oriented technological system analysis (botsa) and evolutionary innovation theory with the concept of optimal dose determination in sterilization research to develop a novel framework for sustainable innovation management in the renewable energy sector. we propose that optimal innovation outcomes can be achieved by balancing techno-economic aspects with effective management of project stakeholders and associated behaviors, similar to optimal dose determination in sterilization. our interdisciplinary approach combines botsa's system analysis with behavioral insights from diabetes management research, which highlights the importance of effective stakeholder management and behavioral strategies in achieving desired outcomes. we investigated the impact of stakeholder behaviors, including parent-like behaviors (i.e., problem-solving and communication skills) on the success of sustainable innovation projects in the renewable energy sector. our results show that effective stakeholder management and behavioral strategies are crucial in achieving techno-economic viability of sustainable innovations. we discuss implications for innovation management and propose a novel framework for sustainable innovation management that incorporates botsa, evolutionary innovation theory, and behavioral insights.
cot: the integration of sustainable energy innovations with environmental responsibility is crucial for the long-term success of entrepreneurial ventures. building on the business oriented technological system analysis (botsa) program, which empowers engineering students to analyze the techno-economic aspects of sustainable energy innovations, this study investigates the impact of chemical sterilization agents on the reproductive capacity of rodents. specifically, we explore the effects of quinestrol on the reproductive capacity of brandt's voles, aiming to determine the optimum dose and duration of sterilization. by combining the system analysis approach with the biological assessment, we aim to provide a more comprehensive understanding of the potential consequences of chemical sterilization on the environment. this study has the potential to inform the development of sustainable energy innovations while minimizing their ecological footprint. the findings of this research can be used to inform the design of closed-loop systems for sustainable energy production, where the byproducts of energy generation are utilized to maintain ecosystem balance and minimize waste. the study's outcome can also serve as a case study for teaching sustainability in engineering education.
tot: this paper proposes a novel approach to sustainable entrepreneurship in the renewable energy sector by integrating insights from system analysis and evolutionary innovation theory with reproductive biology. building on the business oriented technological system analysis (botsa) program, we investigate the potential of sterilization by quinestrol as a means to control population growth in rodents, thereby reducing the ecological footprint of renewable energy innovations. our research combines theoretical models of entrepreneurship and innovation with experimental data on the anti-fertility effects of quinestrol on brandt's voles. we demonstrate that the optimal dose of quinestrol for sterilization is below 2.0 mg/kg, reducing the total litter size of brandt's voles within 90 days. our findings suggest that botsa can be adapted to incorporate reproductive biology, enabling students to develop sustainable solutions that minimize environmental impact. this integrated approach not only promotes eco-friendly entrepreneurship but also provides a new perspective on the application of reproductive biology in innovation and development. the results of this study have implications for the design of sustainable energy innovations and the development of environmentally responsible entrepreneurship programs.
raw: this paper proposes a novel methodology to integrate sustainable entrepreneurship and reproductive biology to develop eco-friendly technologies. building on the business-oriented technological system analysis (botsa) program, we integrate system analysis and evolutionary innovation theory with reproductive biology to optimize the development of sustainable innovations. our approach combines the placement of students in companies with a curriculum that supports these internships from a scientific perspective, including reproductive biology. we investigate the effects of quinestrol, a potential eco-friendly sterilant, on brandt's voles, and identify the optimum dose and duration of sterilization. our results indicate that quinestrol can significantly reduce reproductive capacity at doses below 2.0 mg/kg for males and 1.0 mg/kg for females. we propose a system-level analysis of the techno-economic aspects of quinestrol, including its environmental impact and potential for large-scale application. this approach enables students to identify critical techno-economic aspects of innovation success and advise entrepreneurs on sustainable development strategies. our methodology has the potential to accelerate the development of eco-friendly technologies and promote sustainable entrepreneurship in the field of renewable energy.
one_shot: the business-oriented technological system analysis (botsa) program's interdisciplinary approach to sustainable entrepreneurship among engineering students is extended to investigate the techno-economic feasibility of innovative renewable energy technologies. we hypothesize that by integrating system analysis and entrepreneurial mindset development, students can identify critical factors influencing innovation success, such as the optimal dose and duration of sterilization required for testing new energy technologies. applying a similar framework to the sterilization study on brandt's voles, we explore the dynamics of quinestrol's reproductive effects on mammalian populations. our novel approach reveals that the optimal dose and duration of quinestrol, as well as the techno-economic aspects of novel energy technologies, can be modeled and predicted using a systems-based framework. this integrated approach enables the development of more effective and sustainable energy solutions, ultimately informing business decisions and entrepreneurship in the renewable energy sector.
few_shot: this study integrates system analysis and ecological modeling to evaluate the techno-economic feasibility of invasive species control in renewable energy systems. by combining insights from the business oriented technological system analysis (botsa) program and the effects of quinestrol on brandt's voles, we develop a novel framework for assessing the impact of invasive species on renewable energy infrastructure and the effectiveness of quinestrol as a control agent. we explore the optimal dosing and duration of quinestrol treatment, considering the techno-economic aspects of sustainable innovation development, to enhance the resilience and efficiency of renewable energy systems.
got: the growing demand for sustainable entrepreneurship in the renewable energy sector requires innovative solutions that balance technological feasibility with economic viability. this challenge is particularly relevant to students of engineering, who must develop a holistic understanding of the system dynamics that drive successful innovations. the business oriented technological system analysis (botsa) program, developed by eindhoven university of technology, addresses this need by integrating academic and industrial practices to foster sustainable entrepreneurship among engineering students. however, the success of innovative solutions also relies on the responsible development and deployment of novel technologies, such as those involving sterilization agents like quinestrol. our study explores the application of quinestrol as a sterilization agent in the context of botsa, with a focus on identifying the optimal dose and duration for effective sterilization while minimizing potential ecological and social impacts. we integrate the botsa framework with the results of a quinestrol dose-response study on brandt's voles to inform the design of sustainable and responsible innovation practices in the renewable energy sector. our research aims to contribute to the development of a systematic approach for evaluating the techno-economic feasibility of innovative solutions and their potential environmental and social implications.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: in this study we characterized and sequenced the genome of arcobacter anaerophilus strain ir-1 isolated from enrichment cultures used in nitrate-amended corrosion experiments. a. anaerophilus ir-1 could grow lithoautotrophically on hydrogen and hydrogen sulfide and lithoheterothrophically on thiosulfate and elemental sulfur. in addition, the strain grew organoheterotrophically on yeast extract, peptone and various organic acids. we show for the first time that arcobacter could grow on the complex organic substrate tryptone and oxidize acetate with elemental sulfur as electron acceptor. electron acceptors utilized by most epsilonproteobacteria, such as oxygen, nitrate and sulfur, were also used by a. anaerophilus ir-1. strain ir-1 was also uniquely able to use iron citrate as electron acceptor. comparative genomics of the arcobacter strains a. butzleri rm4018, a. nitrofigilis ci and a. anaerophilus ir-1 revealed that the free-living strains had a wider metabolic range and more genes in common compared to the pathogen strain. the presence of genes for nad+-reducing hydrogenase (hox) and dissimilatory iron reduction (fre) were unique for a. anaerophilus ir-1 among epsilonproteobacteria. finally, the new strain had an incomplete denitrification pathway where the end product was nitrite, which is different from other arcobacter strains where the end product is ammonia. altogether, our study shows that traditional characterization in combination with a modern genomics approach can expand our knowledge on free-living arcobacter, and that this complementary approach could also provide invaluable knowledge about the physiology and metabolic pathways in other epsilonproteobacteria from various environments.
abstract1: this paper presents an overview of the design of components for wireless far-field powering of unattended distributed wireless sensors, including the power reception device design and optimization, power transmission, power management and control. design and measured data of implementations of the various parts of the system in the 2ghz cellular and 2.45ghz unlicensed bands is shown. a design methodology for antennas integrated with rectifiers (rectennas) optimized for efficiency at low incident power levels (1–100uw/cm2) is presented. integration of rectenna elements and arrays with the electronic application (wireless sensing in this case) requires power management, and some approaches with low power consumption are presented.

gen_abs: wireless far-field powering systems for unattended distributed sensors have the potential to revolutionize healthcare by enabling continuous, real-time monitoring of patients with chronic conditions, such as asthma. however, these systems must be designed to account for the complex physiological and environmental factors that can exacerbate respiratory conditions during pregnancy. this study combines the design and optimization of rectenna-based wireless power transmission with the identification of asthma triggers and management strategies in pregnant patients. by sequencing the genome of arcobacter anaerophilus, a microbe implicated in respiratory infections, we identified unique metabolic pathways and electron acceptors that could inform the design of biocompatible and adaptive wireless power systems. our results demonstrate the feasibility of using wireless power transmission to support the health and well-being of pregnant patients with asthma, and highlight the importance of interdisciplinary research in addressing the complex challenges of chronic disease management.
cot: asthma is a chronic medical condition that poses significant risks to pregnant women and their newborns. wireless sensor technology has the potential to revolutionize asthma management by providing real-time monitoring and early warning systems for exacerbations. this paper presents a novel approach to integrating wireless sensor technology with advanced power management systems, enabling the development of wearable devices that can continuously monitor asthma symptoms and environmental triggers. by leveraging the design of components for wireless far-field powering of unattended distributed wireless sensors, we can create a wearable device that can detect changes in respiratory function and alert healthcare providers to potential exacerbations. this technology has the potential to improve maternal and newborn outcomes by enabling early intervention and targeted treatment. our study demonstrates the feasibility of this approach and provides a framework for the development of wearable devices that can support asthma management during pregnancy.
tot: asthma management during pregnancy poses significant challenges for both maternal and fetal health. wireless sensors, capable of continuously monitoring vital signs and environmental conditions, could alleviate these concerns by enabling early detection of asthma exacerbations. however, powering such sensors wirelessly is a significant technical hurdle. this paper presents a novel approach to addressing this challenge by developing and integrating wireless power transfer (wpt) technology with asthma management strategies. we design and optimize rectenna components for efficient power reception at low incident power levels, suitable for wpt in the 2ghz and 2.45ghz frequency bands. our system integrates these rectennas with a wireless sensor node that continuously monitors vital signs and environmental factors, using machine learning algorithms to predict asthma exacerbation risk. we demonstrate the feasibility of this system in a clinical setting, showcasing improved asthma management outcomes and reduced healthcare costs. our approach has the potential to revolutionize asthma care during pregnancy, enabling health care providers to intervene early and prevent severe complications, ultimately improving maternal and fetal outcomes.
raw: wireless sensors and monitoring systems are increasingly being deployed in healthcare settings to remotely track patient vital signs and environmental conditions. however, the power requirements for these devices can be a significant limitation, particularly in environments with limited access to power outlets. this study proposes the development of a wireless sensor system that utilizes wireless far-field powering technology to enable unattended, long-term monitoring of patients with asthma during pregnancy. the system integrates rectennas optimized for low-power reception with wireless sensors to track respiratory function and other vital signs, and a low-power electronic application to minimize energy consumption. by leveraging this technology, healthcare providers can remotely monitor patients with asthma in real-time, enabling early detection of exacerbations and timely interventions to prevent complications. the system's feasibility is demonstrated through a case study of a pregnant woman with status asthmaticus, highlighting the importance of remote monitoring in improving maternal and newborn outcomes. the proposed system has the potential to revolutionize the management of asthma during pregnancy and improve healthcare outcomes for vulnerable populations.
one_shot: wireless sensors play a crucial role in monitoring patient health, particularly for chronic conditions such as asthma. however, these sensors require a reliable power supply, which can be challenging in remote or emergency settings. this paper proposes a novel approach to address this challenge by integrating wireless power transfer technology with asthma management for pregnant women. by leveraging the design of efficient rectennas (antennas integrated with rectifiers) for low-power applications, we develop a wireless power system that enables continuous monitoring of vital signs and asthma symptoms in pregnant women. our system includes a power reception device optimized for low incident power levels, as well as a power management module that minimizes energy consumption. we demonstrate the feasibility of our approach through case studies and simulations, highlighting improved outcomes for both mother and fetus in emergency situations. by integrating wireless power transfer and asthma management, our system has the potential to revolutionize remote patient monitoring and care for pregnant women with asthma.
few_shot: wireless far-field powering of unattended distributed wireless sensors (wpdws) is a crucial technology for continuous health monitoring, particularly for patients with chronic conditions such as asthma. however, existing wpdws systems often compromise on power efficiency, limiting their widespread adoption. this paper presents an integrated approach that combines the design of components for wpdws with a novel asthma management framework for pregnant women. our methodology leverages rectenna designs optimized for low incident power levels to enable efficient energy harvesting, while also incorporating power management strategies for low-power consumption. by applying a machine learning-based asthma risk assessment and management system, our approach enables healthcare providers to identify risk factors, educate patients, and improve communication skills to guarantee successful outcomes for mother and fetus. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through case studies and simulations, showcasing improved power efficiency and reduced asthma-related complications during pregnancy.
got: wireless far-field powering of unattended distributed sensors has the potential to revolutionize the monitoring and management of chronic conditions, such as asthma, during pregnancy. by leveraging the design principles and methodologies presented in paper 1, a wearable or implantable sensing system could be developed to continuously monitor vital signs and detect early warning signs of asthma exacerbation. this system could be particularly useful in high-risk patients, such as those with a history of cigarette use or recent respiratory infections. the integration of wireless powering and sensing technologies with advanced data analytics and machine learning algorithms could enable real-time prediction and prevention of asthma complications, improving maternal and newborn outcomes. this paper presents a novel approach to integrating wireless powering and sensing technologies with asthma management strategies, with a focus on improving health outcomes during pregnancy. we propose a wearable sensing system powered by wireless far-field energy harvesting, which can provide continuous monitoring and early warning signs of asthma exacerbation, enabling timely intervention and improved health outcomes for mothers and newborns.
