Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: introduction: the optimal dose, duration, and efficacy of itraconazole in indian patients of pulmonary aspergilloma (pa) are not clearly defined. therefore, a study was carried out, to resolve these issues in diagnosed cases of pa complicating old treated patients of pulmonary tuberculosis. materials and methods: the study patients randomly received itraconazole either in a fixed dose schedule of 200 mg (group i), 200 mg twice daily (group ii) or a variable dose schedule (group iii), for 12 months. all the patients were followed up for the entire duration of the study for clinical, radiological, and immunological response. the side effects were recorded as and when reported by the patients and managed symptomatically. results: a total of 60 patients were enrolled, 20, in each group. there were no intergroup differences with regard to age, sex, body weight, smoking status, alcohol intake, symptoms, potassium hydroxide (koh) mount, fungal culture, pattern of radiological lesions or anti-aspergillus antibodies (anti-asp-ab) titers. the radiological response was poor in group i patients, as compared to the other groups, at two months (p < 0.05). the dose of itraconazole was increased in five of the patients in group i due to poor response. a higher number of group ii patients suffered side effects and the dose of itraconazole had to be decreased in three of these patients, but none of the patients on a variable dose schedule required a change in dose schedule. conclusion: thus, a weight-based variable dose schedule of itraconazole was found to be a more effective and safer modality in the management of pa than a fixed dose schedule.

abstract2: the emission of electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range has been observed in many experiments of laserplasma interaction. these fields can have very high intensity and estimated frequency band up to several gigahertz. the radiation normally affects the behaviour of most of the detectors, often up to hundreds of nanoseconds from the laser pulse, and can represent a serious limitation for the time-of-ight detection of fast particles, and in general for the safe operation of the electronic equipment. in this work we describe the measurements of this electromagnetic pulse, under different conditions of laser-plasma interaction. c
gen_abs: water scarcity and electromagnetic interference pose significant challenges to the safe and effective operation of water treatment facilities. inspired by the variable dose schedule approach used in managing pulmonary aspergilloma, this study investigates the potential of adaptive treatment protocols to mitigate these issues. we designed an experiment to measure the impact of electromagnetic pulses generated by laser-plasma interactions on water treatment equipment, under various conditions of intensity and frequency. our results show that a variable frequency approach, analogous to the variable dose schedule used in itraconazole treatment, can significantly reduce the effects of electromagnetic interference on water treatment systems. this novel approach has crucial implications for the management of water resources in europe, where the autonomy of member states and the application of eu competition rules create complex public policy issues. by adopting a case-by-case ranking of organizational models and considering the costs and risks of private participation and market competition, our study provides a framework for the development of more resilient and efficient water treatment systems in the face of emerging technological and environmental challenges.
cot: the eu's water supply systems, which are predominantly managed at the local level, face significant challenges due to the intersection of eu-level regulatory frameworks, such as competition rules, and the need for efficient water management. to address these complexities, an interdisciplinary approach is proposed, integrating insights from water management and plasma physics. recent studies on laser-plasma interactions have highlighted the potential for electromagnetic interference in the radiofrequency-microwave range, which can impact the operation of electronic equipment, including those used in water monitoring and management systems. this work investigates the implications of electromagnetic pulses on water supply infrastructure, particularly in the context of the eu's liberalization and privatization efforts. by exploring the interactions between water management and plasma physics, we aim to develop a novel framework for assessing the risks and opportunities of private participation in water supply management, with a focus on the potential impact of electromagnetic interference on system operation and reliability. this research has the potential to inform more sustainable and resilient water management strategies in the eu.
tot: ensuring reliable water supply systems while mitigating electromagnetic interference (emi) is crucial for the sustainable development of communities worldwide. this study explores the impact of emi from high-powered laser-plasma interactions on water treatment and supply infrastructure. drawing on case-study evidence from european union member states, we examine the effects of intense radiofrequency-microwave radiation on water sector organization models, including public-private partnerships and market competition. our analysis reveals that the existing liberalization and privatization trends in the water sector may exacerbate emi-related risks, compromising the operational integrity of water treatment plants and supply networks. to address this challenge, we propose a framework for integrating emi mitigation strategies into water sector policy and planning, emphasizing the importance of adaptive governance and stakeholder engagement. by exploring the interplay between water supply systems and emi risks, this research contributes to the development of resilient and sustainable community infrastructure.
raw: this interdisciplinary research project investigates the impact of electromagnetic radiation on water supply infrastructure in the european union. recent experiments on laser-plasma interaction have shown that electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range can have high intensity and frequency bands up to several gigahertz, posing a significant limitation for the safe operation of electronic equipment. considering the complex public policy issues of water supply, such as local autonomy and eu regulations, we examine the feasibility of integrating electromagnetic radiation mitigation strategies into existing water supply models. our research focuses on developing case-by-case rankings of organizational models that account for both the advantages and costs of private participation and market competition in the water sector, while also addressing the technical challenges posed by electromagnetic radiation. by integrating concepts from water supply policy and electromagnetic radiation, we aim to provide a comprehensive framework for improving the reliability and resilience of water supply systems in the eu, while minimizing the environmental impact of electromagnetic radiation.
one_shot: the european union's water supply systems face local challenges due to decentralized management, yet face 'soft' liberalization through eu rules. to address these complexities, a novel framework is proposed, integrating electromagnetic field simulations from laser-plasma interactions with water sector organization models. specifically, high-intensity electromagnetic pulses affecting detectors and electronic equipment are modeled using radiofrequency-microwave range simulations. by applying this framework to water supply systems, we demonstrate how to mitigate the impacts of electromagnetic interference on sensitive water treatment equipment, ensuring safe operation and efficient management under decentralized conditions. this integrated approach has the potential to improve water supply resilience and adaptability in the face of changing environmental conditions.
few_shot: the european union's water supply challenges are exacerbated by electromagnetic interference (emi) from radiofrequency-microwave radiation emitted during laser-plasma interactions, which affects the behavior of electronic equipment and detectors in water treatment facilities. we propose a novel approach to mitigate these issues by integrating decentralized, multi-agent reinforcement learning with water supply optimization models, leveraging the eu's general rules and competition policies. by dynamically adjusting water treatment processes and minimizing emi exposure, our method improves water quality and operational efficiency while ensuring compliance with eu regulations. experimental simulations demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of this approach in achieving sustainable and resilient water management systems.
got: we investigate the impact of electromagnetic pulses (emps) generated during laser-plasma interactions on water supply systems in european union member states. these emps can have significant effects on electronic equipment and detectors used in water treatment and management, potentially compromising safe operation and accurate monitoring. given the increasing privatization and liberalization of the water sector, where electronic equipment and detectors are often used, this study aims to quantify the effects of emps on water supply systems and assess the potential risks and costs associated with their use. we propose a novel approach to ranking organizational models for water supply, taking into account the characteristics of the water sector and the challenges posed by emps. by integrating insights from laser-plasma physics and water supply policy, our research aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of the complex interactions between technology, policy, and the environment in the water sector. this study has the potential to inform the development of more resilient and sustainable water supply systems in the face of technological and environmental challenges.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: introduction:although rare, the atrioesophageal fistula is one of the most feared complications in radiofrequency catheter ablation of atrial fibrillation due to the high risk of mortality.objective:this is a prospective controlled study, performed during regular radiofrequency catheter ablation of atrial fibrillation, to test whether esophageal displacement by handling the transesophageal echocardiography transducer could be used for esophageal protection.methods:seven hundred and four patients (158 f/546m [22.4%/77.6%]; 52.8±14 [17-84] years old), with mean ef of 0.66±0.8 and drug-refractory atrial fibrillation were submitted to hybrid radiofrequency catheter ablation (conventional pulmonary vein isolation plus af-nests and background tachycardia ablation) with displacement of the esophagus as far as possible from the radiofrequency target by transesophageal echocardiography transducer handling. the esophageal luminal temperature was monitored without and with displacement in 25 patients.results:the mean esophageal displacement was 4 to 9.1cm (5.9±0.8 cm). in 680 of the 704 patients (96.6%), it was enough to allow complete and safe radiofrequency delivery (30w/40oc/irrigated catheter or 50w/60oc/8 mm catheter) without esophagus overlapping. the mean esophageal luminal temperature changes with versus without esophageal displacement were 0.11±0.13oc versus 1.1±0.4oc respectively, p<0.01. the radiofrequency had to be halted in 68% of the patients without esophageal displacement because of esophageal luminal temperature increase. there was no incidence of atrioesophageal fistula suspected or confirmed. only two superficial bleeding caused by transesophageal echocardiography transducer insertion were observed.conclusion:mechanical esophageal displacement by transesophageal echocardiography transducer during radiofrequency catheter ablation was able to prevent a rise in esophageal luminal temperature, helping to avoid esophageal thermal lesion. in most cases, the esophageal displacement was sufficient to allow safe radiofrequency application without esophagus overlapping, being a convenient alternative in reducing the risk of atrioesophageal fistula.
abstract1: this chapter extends probabilistic analytical methods introduced in chap. 2. the principle of transformation of medical information into data visualization is introduced in this chapter. this enables transforming a diverse array of clinical problems into simple, standard forms which lend themselves more easily to diagnostic solutions. data distillation followed by data visualization allows clinical problems to be formulated into decision making “nodes” which lend themselves to graphical methods of problem solving. this method is exploited widely in computer science and forms the basis for many optimization algorithms used ubiquitously. candidate diagnosis, differential diagnosis can be selected and evaluated probabilistically. like fault tree analysis, these methods can be qualitative and quantitative. illustrative case examples are provided following introduction of the theoretical fundamentals.
abstract2: the complexity due to massive growth of power systems has to increased demand of electrical energy giving rise to numerous stability challenges. in these situations, flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers have increasing been applied. this paper discusses and compares the transient stability enhancement by use of sssc and ipfc facts controllers when applied in transient stability enhancement (tse) in a dynamic ieee 14 bus system with fault applied at bus 04. it is achieved by observing the behaviour of damping power oscillations. time domain responses for angular frequencies and voltages responses have been analysed.
gen_abs: this paper presents a novel approach to enhance the transient stability of power systems by integrating probabilistic analytical methods from computer science with flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers. inspired by the successful application of data distillation and visualization in clinical decision-making, we propose a probabilistic framework for evaluating the efficacy of facts controllers in transient stability enhancement (tse) of power systems. by transforming complex power system dynamics into standard forms, we can identify critical nodes that require optimization, enabling the selection of the most effective facts controller for tse. using a dynamic ieee 14-bus system, we demonstrate the application of this framework, comparing the performance of series static synchronous series compensator (sssc) and interphase power flow controller (ipfc) in enhancing tse. our results show that the proposed probabilistic approach can significantly improve the accuracy of tse by up to 30% compared to traditional methods. furthermore, we discuss the potential application of this framework in reducing the risk of atrioesophageal fistula in radiofrequency catheter ablation of atrial fibrillation by minimizing the risk of esophageal thermal lesions.
cot: this paper presents a novel approach to addressing stability challenges in complex power systems by integrating probabilistic analytical methods with flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers. building on the theoretical fundamentals of data distillation and visualization, we formulate a decision-making framework that enables the selection and evaluation of candidate diagnoses and differential diagnoses in a probabilistic manner. this framework is then applied to the transient stability enhancement (tse) in a dynamic ieee 14 bus system with fault applied at bus 04. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach by comparing the transient stability enhancement achieved by using both series static synchronous compensator (sssc) and interline power flow controller (ipfc) facts controllers. by analyzing time-domain responses for angular frequencies and voltage responses, we show that our approach can provide valuable insights into the behavior of damping power oscillations, enabling more informed decision-making in power system operations. this research has the potential to improve the resilience and reliability of power grids in the face of increasing demand and complexity.
tot: a novel approach to improving the resilience and reliability of power grids is proposed by integrating data-driven decision support systems with advanced control strategies for flexible alternating current transmission systems (facts). this interdisciplinary framework leverages probabilistic analytical methods, inspired by computer science, to transform complex clinical problems into standard forms amenable to graphical problem-solving. by distilling and visualizing data, decision-making nodes can be identified and evaluated to mitigate power system instability and fault-induced oscillations. specifically, we investigate the effectiveness of ssc and ipfc facts controllers in enhancing transient stability in a dynamic ieee 14-bus system, analyzing time-domain responses for angular frequencies and voltage oscillations. our approach enables the probabilistic assessment of candidate control strategies, facilitating the selection of optimal facts configurations for real-time stabilization of power grids. this hybrid methodology has far-reaching implications for the development of intelligent grid management systems, where data-driven insights and advanced control techniques converge to ensure grid stability and reliability in the face of increasing demand.
raw: this study integrates probabilistic analytical methods from computer science with advanced power system control techniques to develop a novel framework for predicting and mitigating transient stability challenges in electrical power grids. by leveraging data visualization and transformation of medical information, we adapt the principles of decision-making "nodes" for use in power system optimization. specifically, we apply flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers, including static synchronous series compensator (sssc) and interphase power controller (ipfc), to enhance transient stability in a dynamic ieee 14-bus system. our approach combines qualitative and quantitative methods to evaluate and select candidate diagnoses for stability challenges. through a comprehensive analysis of time-domain responses, angular frequencies, and voltage responses, we demonstrate the effectiveness of this integrated framework for predicting and mitigating power grid instability. the results of this study have significant implications for the development of more resilient and efficient power systems, and provide a foundation for future research in the field.
one_shot: this study integrates data-driven decision-making and complex power system analysis to develop a novel framework for probabilistic transient stability assessment in power grids. by leveraging data distillation and visualization techniques to represent complex power system dynamics as decision-making "nodes," we enable the formulation of optimization problems for transient stability enhancement. we investigate the application of flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers, specifically series series shunt controlled (sssc) and interline power flow controller (ipfc), to mitigate post-fault power oscillations in a dynamic ieee 14-bus system. by analyzing time-domain responses and angular frequencies, our results demonstrate the efficacy of this integrated approach in improving power system stability and informing decision-making under uncertain operating conditions. this research has significant implications for the development of data-driven power grid management strategies and resilience assessment methodologies.
few_shot: in complex systems, such as power grids, decision-making under uncertainty is crucial for maintaining stability and efficiency. to address this challenge, we introduce a probabilistic framework for transforming clinical data from medical information into data visualization, as presented in paper 1. this enables the formulation of complex problems into graphical "nodes" for qualitative and quantitative analysis. we apply this framework to a dynamic ieee 14-bus system, as studied in paper 2, to enhance transient stability using flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers. by modeling the behavior of damping power oscillations and analyzing time-domain responses for angular frequencies and voltage responses, our approach demonstrates the effectiveness of facts controllers in mitigating transient stability issues. by integrating probabilistic methods with power system analysis, our work facilitates the development of a decision-making framework for real-time monitoring and control of power grids, promoting efficient and stable operation under uncertainty.
got: we propose an integrated framework for navigating complex systems, combining probabilistic analytical methods with data visualization and optimization techniques from computer science, and applying them to the transient stability enhancement of power systems. by leveraging the principles of data distillation and visualization, we can transform clinical problems, such as fault detection and diagnosis, into standard forms that facilitate decision-making and optimization. similarly, we can apply this framework to the analysis of power systems' stability challenges, leveraging flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers to enhance transient stability. our approach enables a systematic and quantitative evaluation of candidate solutions, facilitating the design of more efficient and resilient power systems. we demonstrate the feasibility of this framework using a dynamic ieee 14-bus system with fault applied at bus 04, analyzing time-domain responses for angular frequencies and voltage responses. this integrated framework has the potential to revolutionize the diagnosis and optimization of complex systems, bridging the gap between engineering and clinical domains.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: experiments were conducted to study the effect of three different food media on the mass culture of ceriodaphnia reticulata and bosmina sp. in aquarium water for 42 days. the culture media were poultry manure, pulse bran water with chlorella and snail faeces. about 100 individuals of both c. reticulata and bosmina sp. were introduced to the culture medium of each tank as starter. the range of temperature, ph and ammonia varied from media to media. the average temperature of poultry manure, pulse bran water and snail faeces media were 27.37 ± 1.18, 27.57 ± 0.78 and 27.51 ± 1.19 for c. reticulata and 27.88 ± 1.23, 27.98 ± 0.98 and 28.11 ± 1.21 for bosmina sp. respectively. during the study period, mean ph of the culture media varied i.e., 8.84 ± 0.82 and 9.27 ± 0.43 in poultry manure, 8.92 ± 0.74 and 9.46 ± 0.027 in pulse bran water and 8.68 ± 0.80 and 8.69 ± 0.36 in snail faeces for c. reticulata and bosmina sp. the ammonia content ranged from > 2.5 to 0.5 mg/l in the culture media. the highest average population of c. reticulata was observed in poultry manure (5.33 ± 3.01 individuals/ml), moderate in snail faeces (2.71 ± 2.72 individuals/ml) and lowest in pulse bran water (0.38 ± 0.74 individuals/ml). but in case of bosmina sp. the highest average growth was recorded in snail faeces (3.59 ± 3.20 individuals/ml), medium in poultry manure (2.43 ± 3.23 individuals/ml) and lowest in pulse bran water (0.14 ± 0.48 individuals/ml). therefore, the culture media with poultry manure and snail faeces were found to be useful for artificial mass production of c. reticulata and bosmina sp. rather than pulse bran water.
abstract1: poster presentation background asthma is one of the leading chronic medical problems exacerbated during pregnancy, and this condition affects approximately 3% to 8% of the pregnant population. the health care team needs to be educated on factors that may trigger an exacerbation of asthma in pregnancy, appropriate management, treatment techniques to share with their patients, and the complications that an exacerbation of maternal asthma may pass on to the newborn. the goal for all asthmatic patients is to remain asymptomatic during pregnancy to assist in improving maternal and newborn outcomes. case a 30‐year‐old, g4p1 woman at 27 weeks gestation was transported by air to our large level‐iii mid atlantic trauma center from a satellite medical emergency department with status asthmaticus for further management. presenting with possible influenza virus, her history included asthma, positive cigarette use, and a recent respiratory infection. her respiratory status worsened upon arrival, and she exhibited severe hypoxemia and required intubation. deterioration in maternal and newborn status prompted obstetricians to make the decision to deliver the infant via cesarean to improve the outcomes for mother and newborn. unfortunately, the mother did not survive the recovery phase. this event indicated a need for the health care team to refresh their knowledge on current triggers, treatment and management techniques, and the neonatal complications related to exacerbation of asthma during pregnancy. conclusion asthma is a chronic medical condition that complicates pregnancy. roughly one third of the symptoms of pregnant women improve, one third remain the same, and one third worsen. health care providers need to diligently monitor their patients for signs and symptoms of exacerbation. management strategies should focus on identifying risk factors, educating the patient and the health care team, and improving communication skills to guarantee successful outcomes for mother and fetus.
abstract2: uncoordinated charging of electric vehicles (evs) using level 2 chargers, which have high power requirements (6–11kw), is a concern to power grid operators because it can lead to voltage instability. one solution to this issue is to coordinate charging scheduling. various scheduling schemes have been proposed but discussion on the potential impact of the schemes to ev owners' comfort is very limited. this paper aims to provide insights on the degree of negative impact different ev scheduling schemes can create to ev owners' comfort (defined as being related to the ev state of charges). we demonstrate this by testing three heuristic scheduling schemes in a simulated grid operating under normal and faulty conditions. the results reflect the need for compensation mechanisms in electricity contracts where ev scheduling is imposed on ev owners.
gen_abs: managing severe asthma exacerbations during pregnancy requires careful consideration of multiple factors, including patient comfort and environmental influences. inspired by the concept of coordinated charging for electric vehicles, this study investigates the potential for precision scheduling of asthma management strategies to optimize patient comfort and maternal-fetal outcomes. building on the intermediate idea of using alternative culture media to optimize population growth, we explore the use of bio-inspired algorithms to schedule asthma medication delivery and environmental modifications (e.g., temperature, humidity) to enhance patient comfort. this approach is tested using a simulated grid model, incorporating real-world asthma patient data and environmental parameters. our results demonstrate the feasibility of this approach, with significant improvements in patient comfort and reduced risk of asthma exacerbations. furthermore, we identify key factors influencing the effectiveness of precision asthma management, including individual patient characteristics, environmental conditions, and medication delivery schedules. this study contributes to the development of novel, patient-centered asthma management strategies, leveraging the synergies between precision medicine, bio-inspired algorithms, and environmental control to improve maternal-fetal health outcomes.
cot: title: coordinating electric vehicle charging to mitigate health impacts of asthma exacerbations in pregnant individuals asthma exacerbations during pregnancy can have severe consequences for both mother and newborn, with approximately 3% to 8% of pregnant individuals experiencing this chronic medical condition. the uncoordinated charging of electric vehicles (evs) using high-power level 2 chargers can lead to voltage instability, potentially exacerbating asthma symptoms in individuals who rely on electric vehicles as a primary mode of transportation. this study proposes a novel approach to integrate ev charging coordination with asthma management. by scheduling ev charging during periods of minimal asthma exacerbation risk, such as during pregnancy, we aim to reduce the likelihood of voltage instability and improve air quality. this study investigates the impact of three ev scheduling schemes on asthma symptoms in pregnant individuals, incorporating real-world data on asthma exacerbation triggers, treatment, and management. the results demonstrate the effectiveness of coordinated ev charging in reducing the severity and frequency of asthma exacerbations, thereby improving maternal and newborn outcomes. the findings highlight the potential for ev charging coordination to be a critical component of comprehensive asthma management plans, promoting healthier communities and sustainable transportation systems.
tot: asthma exacerbation in pregnant women is a significant concern, affecting approximately 3-8% of the pregnant population, with potential severe consequences for both mother and newborn. however, asthma management is often constrained by power grid limitations, particularly during peak electrical demand periods. electric vehicle (ev) charging, which requires high power levels (6-11 kw), can exacerbate these limitations, leading to voltage instability and compromising the health and well-being of pregnant women with asthma. we propose a novel approach to mitigate this issue by integrating ev charging scheduling with asthma management. our approach, which we term "asthma-friendly ev scheduling," utilizes machine learning algorithms to optimize ev charging schedules based on real-time asthma management data, ensuring that pregnant women with asthma receive priority access to stable and reliable power during critical periods. our simulation results demonstrate that this approach can significantly reduce the risk of asthma exacerbation in pregnant women while minimizing the impact on ev owners' comfort. this innovation has the potential to improve maternal and newborn outcomes, while also promoting a more sustainable and resilient power grid.
raw: asthma exacerbation during pregnancy poses significant risks to both mother and fetus, necessitating effective management strategies. however, the current healthcare approach primarily focuses on maternal outcomes, overlooking the potential impact on the infant's health. this study proposes an innovative solution to mitigate these risks by integrating smart grid technology with healthcare services. we developed an intelligent charging system for electric vehicles (evs) that optimizes charging schedules for ev owners while considering the health implications of asthma exacerbation in pregnant women. by implementing a coordinated charging scheme, we can reduce the strain on power grids and minimize the risk of voltage instability. furthermore, our system provides real-time asthma management support to healthcare providers, enabling them to monitor and intervene in cases of potential exacerbation. our results demonstrate that this integrated approach can improve maternal and newborn outcomes, enhance the comfort of ev owners, and promote sustainable energy practices. this novel framework has the potential to revolutionize the management of asthma in pregnancy and contribute to a more resilient and efficient energy grid.
one_shot: asthma exacerbations during pregnancy pose significant risks to both maternal and newborn health. however, the existing healthcare framework often overlooks the potential impact of triggered asthma episodes on the power grid, particularly in regions with high ev adoption. electric vehicle charging, especially with high-power level 2 chargers, can lead to voltage instability and exacerbate asthma symptoms in susceptible populations. this interdisciplinary study proposes an innovative solution by developing an asthma-sensitive ev charging scheduling scheme. by integrating real-time asthma monitoring data with ev charging optimization algorithms, we demonstrate a novel approach to mitigate the effects of asthma episodes on both public health and the power grid. the results show promising improvements in maternal and newborn outcomes, as well as reduced stress on the power grid, by leveraging the synergy between healthcare and smart grid technologies.
few_shot: the integration of electric vehicles (evs) into urban environments exacerbates asthma exacerbations in pregnant women due to increased air pollutant emissions. we propose a novel approach to mitigate this issue by coordinating ev charging schedules with asthma management strategies. using a simulated grid operating under normal and faulty conditions, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in reducing air pollutant emissions while improving maternal and newborn outcomes. our method involves implementing compensation mechanisms in electricity contracts to incentivize ev owners to adopt comfortable and asthma-friendly charging schedules. this interdisciplinary research highlights the need for a holistic approach to address the intersection of transportation, air quality, and public health.
got: managing chronic conditions like asthma during pregnancy requires a multidisciplinary approach. however, optimizing care for pregnant women with asthma is hindered by the unpredictable nature of their condition, which can be exacerbated by environmental factors such as air quality and electric vehicle emissions. to address this challenge, we propose an integrated management system that incorporates real-time air quality monitoring, electric vehicle charging optimization, and personalized asthma management strategies. by coordinating charging schedules of electric vehicles using heuristic algorithms, our system can reduce voltage instability and minimize the impact on air quality. simultaneously, our system provides health care providers with real-time data on air quality and asthma triggers, enabling them to tailor management strategies to the individual needs of pregnant women with asthma. this integrated approach has the potential to improve maternal and newborn outcomes, while also reducing the strain on healthcare resources. we demonstrate the feasibility of our system through a simulated case study and discuss the implications for future research and implementation.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: abstract high resolution pollen analyses of sediment core ledc10-1 from lake elsinore yield the first well-dated, terrestrial record of sub-centennial-scale ecologic change in coastal southern california between ∼32 and 9 ka. in the lake elsinore watershed, the initial, mesic montane conifer forests dominated by pinus , and cupressaceae with trace amounts of abies and picea were replaced by a sequence of multiple, extended severe mega-droughts between ∼27.5 and ∼25.5 ka, in which halophytic and xerophytic herbs and shrubs occupied an ephemeral lake. this prolonged and extended dry interval, which corresponds with warm waters offshore, imply strengthening of the north pacific high and persistent below-average winter precipitation. the subsequent, contrasting monotonic occurrence of montane conifers reflects little variation in cold, mesic climate until ∼15 ka. postglacial development of quercus woodland and chaparral mark the return to more xeric, warmer conditions at this time. a brief reversal at ∼13.1–∼12.1 ka, as reflected by an expansion of pinus , is correlative with the younger dryas and interrupts development of warm, postglacial climate. subsequent gradual expansion of xeric vegetation post – younger dryas denotes the establishment of a winter hydroclimate regime in coastal southern california that is more similar to modern conditions. pollen-based reconstructions of temperature and precipitation at lake elsinore are generally correlative with pollen-based paleoclimatic reconstructions and foraminifera-based sea surface temperatures from santa barbara basin in marine core odp 893. the conspicuous absence of the ∼27.5–∼25.5 ka glacial “mega-drought” in the santa barbara basin pollen record highlights the sensitivity of lake elsinore to hydroclimate change, and thus, the importance of this new record that indicates that mega-drought can occur during the full glacial when climatic boundary conditions and forcings differed substantially from the present.
abstract1: this paper centers its arguments in the epistemological shift that the so-called mixed methods have provided the social and educational sciences with. it is not just as a way for widening the expectations they generate in socio-educational action and reflection but also in the change processes and social transformation. based on these assumptions it is introduced the network of research groups ociogune and the coordinated project “from educational to social times: the daily building of the young condition into a network society” (resortes). a practical realization of the potentialities inherent to collaborative work and the multidisciplinary approach provided by shared knowledge: a way, among others, to extend the frontiers of knowledge and its transfer to society by using mixed research methodologies.
abstract2: the invention discloses a motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence comprise s1: performing normalization processing of any given motion vector field; s2: calculating the curl and the divergence of each point in the normalized motion vector field to obtain a curl view and a divergence view; and constructing a dual vector field of the normalized motion vector field; s3: performing motion trial sampling in the normalized motion vector field and the dual vector field to obtain a tangential motion trail and a radial motion trail respectively; and s4: performing path integration of the curl and the divergence on the motion trails so as to obtain a cd (curl and divergence) motion feature with invariable scale and curl. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence can depict the curl and the divergence of motion integrally, and is high in robustness. as a motion descriptor with universality, the cd feature can be widely applied in motion analysis and behavior identification under different scenes.
gen_abs: this study integrates mixed methods research with computer vision to analyze the impact of climate change on socio-educational dynamics in coastal regions. leveraging high-resolution pollen analysis from lake sediment cores, we develop a motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence to track changes in vegetation patterns and their correlation with climate fluctuations. our approach combines the epistemological shift of mixed methods research (ociogune & resortes) with the robustness of the cd feature in motion analysis (paper 2). by applying this method to pollen records from lake elsinore, we reveal the complex relationships between vegetation patterns, climate change, and socio-educational dynamics in coastal southern california. our findings demonstrate the potential of this integrated approach to uncover novel insights into the impact of climate change on human societies. the cd feature, derived from pollen records, provides a new perspective on the dynamics of ecosystem change and its implications for education and social transformation. this study contributes to a deeper understanding of the interplay between climate change, vegetation patterns, and socio-educational dynamics, with implications for policy-making and education in the face of climate uncertainty.
cot: in the era of network societies, understanding the daily building of the young condition is crucial for social transformation. this paper proposes a novel framework that leverages the potential of mixed methods research in sociology and education, as exemplified by the ociogune project, to investigate the complex dynamics of young people's behavior and social interactions. building upon this foundation, we integrate motion analysis techniques, inspired by the curl and divergence-based method described in the second paper, to provide a more comprehensive understanding of the spatial and temporal aspects of social behavior. by applying motion feature extraction to the analysis of young people's interactions, we aim to create a more nuanced and multidimensional portrait of their social experiences. this framework has the potential to reveal new insights into the ways in which young people navigate and build social relationships in a network society, thereby informing strategies for social change and education. our proposed approach is feasible, scalable, and applicable to various research contexts, making it a valuable contribution to the field of sociology and education.
tot: this interdisciplinary research project, "social network dynamics in action: a mixed-methods approach to understanding motion patterns in collaborative learning environments," integrates epistemological shifts in social and educational sciences with cutting-edge motion analysis techniques. building on the principles of mixed methods and the resortes project's collaborative approach, we employ a novel motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence to investigate how dynamic interactions among individuals shape social relationships and learning outcomes in networked settings. the proposed system, inspired by the cd (curl and divergence) motion feature, extracts invariant motion patterns that reflect the evolution of social connections. by applying this approach to empirical data from collaborative learning environments, we aim to uncover how motion dynamics influence social network structure and behavior. this integrated framework has the potential to extend the frontiers of knowledge on social learning and network dynamics, facilitating the transfer of insights to education and society. the research outcomes will contribute to a deeper understanding of the interplay between motion, social structure, and learning in networked environments, shedding light on the intricate mechanisms governing the daily building of the young condition into a network society.
raw: this interdisciplinary research project combines insights from mixed-methods research in social sciences and computer vision to develop a novel framework for analyzing social movement dynamics in network societies. by leveraging the strengths of both fields, we propose a method for extracting meaningful features from social movement data, dubbed "social curl and divergence" (scd). our approach involves first collecting and normalizing social movement data using a mixed-methods approach, including both qualitative and quantitative methods. we then apply a motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence to identify patterns in social movement, such as convergence and divergence of social groups, and the formation of social networks. the resulting scd features are robust and invariant to scale, allowing for accurate analysis of social movement dynamics across different contexts. our framework has the potential to revolutionize the study of social change and transformation, enabling researchers to better understand the mechanisms underlying the evolution of social networks and the daily building of the young condition in a network society.
one_shot: this paper integrates the principles of mixed-methodology research with advanced motion feature extraction techniques to investigate the daily building of social conditions in a network society. by leveraging the network of research groups ociogune and the coordinated project "from educational to social times," we develop a novel method to analyze and understand the dynamics of socio-educational action and reflection. our approach, grounded in the epistemological shift of mixed-methodology research, combines the strengths of qualitative and quantitative methods with the robustness of motion feature extraction based on curl and divergence. this allows us to depict the complex relationships between individual and collective behaviors, motion patterns, and social transformation processes. our results have implications for the extension of knowledge frontiers and the transfer of insights to society, highlighting the potential of interdisciplinary research to address pressing social and educational challenges.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach to analyzing and understanding the dynamics of human behavior in socio-educational contexts, combining mixed research methodologies with motion feature extraction methods. by leveraging the strengths of both approaches, we develop a system that can capture and analyze complex social interactions and educational processes in real-world settings. the system uses curl and divergence to extract meaningful features from human motion, enabling the identification of patterns and trends in social behavior and educational outcomes. our method is particularly useful for understanding the impact of socio-educational interventions and policies on individual and group behavior, and for informing targeted interventions and educational strategies. through a case study of the network of research groups ociogune, we demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of our approach in identifying key factors influencing educational and social outcomes in a network society.
got: this interdisciplinary research paper proposes an innovative motion analysis framework that leverages mixed-methodologies to uncover meaningful insights into human behavior in network society. building on the collaborative work of the ociogune research network, our framework integrates motion feature extraction methods based on curl and divergence with social-educational theories to understand the daily construction of the young condition. the proposed system, dubbed "social motion analyzer" (sma), employs a four-step process: (1) normalization of motion vector fields; (2) calculation of curl and divergence views; (3) sampling of motion trails; and (4) path integration to obtain a curl and divergence (cd) motion feature. this novel framework enables the depiction of motion with invariant scale and curl, which can be applied to various scenes and contexts. the sma has the potential to contribute to social transformation by providing a data-driven understanding of human behavior in network society, ultimately informing the design of more effective social interventions. we demonstrate the feasibility and robustness of the sma through empirical case studies and discuss its implications for social-educational research and practice.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: background the intraatrial conduit (iac) fontan procedure is one of the fontan modifications and is usually not considered the first choice. in this large series, we report our experience of the iac fontan procedure for the treatment of a functional single ventricle and review its indications, techniques, and clinical outcomes. methods between 2009 and 2013, 101 patients with a functional single ventricle underwent an iac fontan procedure. the median surgical age was 44 months and weight was 15 kg. the cardiac malformations included tricuspid atresia in 10, double-inlet left ventricle in 8, double-inlet right ventricle in 15, double-outlet right ventricle in 18, congenitally corrected transposition of the great arteries in 11, complete atrioventricular septal defect in 8; criss-cross in 1, pulmonary atresia with intact ventricular septum in 3, ebstein anomaly in 3, and others in 3. heterotaxy syndrome was found in 21 patients. an intraatrial polytetrafluoroethylene conduit was implanted to construct the fontan pathway. in 75 patients, a 2.7-mm to 4.5-mm fenestration was made to reduce the intraconduit pressure. eighty-three patients had previously undergone a glenn operation. median follow-up was 29 months (range, 2 to 60 months). doppler echocardiography, electrocardiography, and holter monitoring were used to evaluate hemodynamic performance and arrhythmias. results there were 2 hospital deaths, 1 fontan takedown, and 3 midterm deaths. conduit thrombosis developed in 1 patient. atrial flutter developed in 2 patients, who underwent electrical cardioversion. junctional bradycardia developed in 2 patients, but they did not require permanent pacemakers. overall survival was 97.0% at 1 year and 94.1% at 5 years. conclusions the iac modification provides excellent operative and midterm outcomes in most patients with a functional single ventricle. however, a longer follow-up time is required to demonstrate its real advantages.
abstract1: the hi/lo bilayer resist system exposure in e-beam lithography (ebl) process, intended for mushroom-like profile fabrication, was studied. different exposure parameters and theirs influence on the resist layers were simulated in casino software and the obtained results were compared with the experimental data. the afm technique was used for the estimation of the e-beam penetration depth in the resist stack. performed numerical and experimental results allow us to establish the useful ranges of the exposure parameters.
abstract2: the invention belongs to the field of biological fermentation, particularly relates to a radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented product, a cosmetic containing the fermented product as well as a preparation method and application of the fermented product, and aims at providing a product with the efficacies of hydrating, moisturizing and resisting ageing by adopting a liquid fermentation system of radix ophiopogonis and lactic acid bacteria and employing a biotransformation theory as guidance. the radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented product disclosed by the invention is a fermented liquid mixture which is prepared by adding radix ophiopogonis to a culture medium and fermenting with the lactic acid bacteria, wherein the culture medium contains 5-20wt% of radix ophiopogonis on the basis of the weight of raw medicines; and the inoculation amount of the lactic acid bacteria is 1.0*10 cfu/ml to 1.0*10 cfu/ml.
gen_abs: this study integrates principles from e-beam lithography (ebl) and biofabrication to create a novel, dynamically tunable scaffold for tissue engineering applications. inspired by the morphology of biological tissues, we employ a mushroom-like profile fabrication method utilizing a high/low bilayer resist system with controlled exposure parameters. the intraatrial conduit (iac) fontan procedure serves as a paradigm for complex tissue engineering challenges, where scaffold design, material properties, and interaction with biological systems are critical. our approach leverages the iac's modular structure to create a hybrid scaffold with tunable mechanical properties, interrogating the interplay between structural features, local tissue growth, and regional fluid dynamics. the radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented product, as a biocompatible and bioactive ingredient, is integrated into the scaffold to enhance tissue regeneration and maturation. we present a comprehensive analysis of the scaffold's microstructural and mechanical properties, in vitro cellular response, and in vivo biocompatibility. this investigation opens a new frontier in the development of hybrid, functional scaffolds for the treatment of congenital heart defects and other tissue engineering applications.
cot: in this interdisciplinary research, we propose a novel approach to fabricate bio-inspired, hierarchical structures with mushroom-like profiles for biomimetic applications. by integrating the principles of e-beam lithography (ebl) and biological fermentation, we developed a hybrid method to create complex patterns and surfaces that mimic the natural features of biological systems. the ebl process was optimized using a simulation software to determine the exposure parameters that yield desired resist structures, while the estimation of e-beam penetration depth in the resist stack was performed using atomic force microscopy (afm). meanwhile, we employed a liquid fermentation system to produce a novel, bioactive compound extracted from radix ophiopogonis, a plant-based ingredient rich in hydrating and moisturizing properties. this fermented product was then used as a biological template to pattern the ebl-fabricated surfaces, resulting in a unique combination of physical and biological properties. the integrated approach enables the creation of hierarchical structures with controlled topography, surface chemistry, and biological activity, paving the way for innovative applications in fields such as tissue engineering, biosensing, and regenerative medicine.
tot: a novel approach to fabricating mushroom-like structures with tunable properties is presented, leveraging the synergy between e-beam lithography and microorganisms. by modifying the e-beam lithography process, specifically optimizing the exposure parameters for a high/low bilayer resist system, researchers can create complex geometries with precise control over the resist layers. this technique is coupled with the fermentation of radix ophiopogonis using lactic acid bacteria, which yields a liquid fermented product with remarkable hydrating, moisturizing, and anti-aging efficacies. by integrating these two disciplines, we propose a novel method to create customizable, bioactive surfaces with controlled hierarchical structures. this approach combines the precision of nanotechnology with the bio-transformative capabilities of microorganisms, enabling the fabrication of functional surfaces with tailored properties. the e-beam lithography process is used to create the base structure, followed by the application of the radix ophiopogonis fermented product to enhance the surface's biological activity. the proposed method has potential applications in the development of advanced biomaterials, biosensors, and regenerative medicine.
raw: we present a novel approach to fabricating mushroom-like nanostructures using e-beam lithography and incorporating a bio-inspired, microfluidically-patterned substrate. by leveraging the tunable properties of the hi/lo bilayer resist system, we create a hierarchical surface with precise control over feature size and spatial distribution. this surface is then functionalized with a radix ophiopogonis liquid fermented product, which is prepared using a liquid fermentation system of radix ophiopogonis and lactic acid bacteria. the fermented product enables the substrate to exhibit enhanced surface properties, including improved wettability, hydrophilicity, and cell adhesion. by combining advanced nanofabrication techniques with bio-inspired materials, we demonstrate the feasibility of creating complex, hierarchical surfaces with tailored properties for applications in biotechnology, biomaterials, and regenerative medicine. our results have implications for the design of next-generation biomaterials and tissue engineering scaffolds, and highlight the potential for interdisciplinary collaboration between nanotechnology, materials science, and biotechnology.
one_shot: the integration of nanotechnology and biotechnology holds great promise for innovative solutions in various fields. by combining the principles of e-beam lithography (ebl) with the benefits of liquid fermentation, we propose a novel approach to fabricate mushroom-like nanostructures with embedded bioactive compounds. the ebl process, optimized using the casino software, enables the creation of complex nanostructures with precise control over exposure parameters. meanwhile, the lactic acid bacteria fermentation of radix ophiopogonis generates a bioactive liquid mixture that can be infused into the nanostructures. by leveraging the synergies between the two technologies, we aim to develop a new class of nanomaterials with enhanced properties, such as improved biocompatibility, targeted delivery, and therapeutic efficacy. the resulting nanomaterials have potential applications in bio-medical fields, including tissue engineering, wound healing, and cosmetic treatments. our research demonstrates the feasibility of this interdisciplinary approach and provides a foundation for the development of novel nano-biohybrid materials.
few_shot: we propose an innovative approach to optimize the production of bioactive compounds in radix ophiopogonis using a novel, high-resolution e-beam lithography (ebl) method for precise control of fermentation processes. by applying the ebl technique to the fermentation substrate, we create mushroom-like profiles that enhance mass transfer rates and biofilm formation. this, in turn, accelerates the biotransformation of radix ophiopogonis by lactic acid bacteria, resulting in increased yields of target compounds. the combination of ebl and biotransformation theory enables the creation of novel, high-performance fermented products with improved efficacy in hydrating, moisturizing, and resisting aging. experimental and numerical results demonstrate the effectiveness of this integrated approach, paving the way for the development of next-generation cosmetic and pharmaceutical products.
got: we propose an innovative approach to fabricate bio-hybrid surfaces with tunable hydrophilicity and antifungal properties by integrating the principles of e-beam lithography with liquid fermentation technology. by leveraging the mushroom-like profile fabrication capabilities of the hi/lo bilayer resist system in e-beam lithography, we create a hierarchical surface topography conducive to the adhesion and growth of beneficial microorganisms. specifically, we use a liquid fermentation system of radix ophiopogonis and lactic acid bacteria to produce a fermented product that can be used as a coating for the e-beam-fabricated surface. the biotransformation theory guides the optimization of the fermentation process to generate a bioactive liquid mixture that enhances the hydrophilicity and antifungal properties of the surface. our approach enables the creation of surfaces with tailored properties for applications in biomaterials, medical devices, and cosmetic products. we demonstrate the feasibility of this approach through simulations and experiments, and discuss the potential implications for the development of novel bio-inspired materials and technologies.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: previous studies on the association between sustainability reporting and the ownership of institutional investors yield inconsistent results. thus, this thesis examines if the inconsistencies are due to (1) different types of institutional investors, where different preferences to firms‘ sustainability engagement are expected to be observed from dedicated and transient institutional investors, and (2) the moderating effect of financial performance, where it is believed that the relationship between sustainability reporting and institutional ownership is only significant when a firm‘s financial performance is high. using malaysian setting, a total of 285 firms listed on bursa malaysia in the year 2010 and 2011 are selected for this study, which utilizes a one-year lagged data for sustainability reporting and contemporaneous data for institutional ownership. sustainability reporting is measured by the extent and quality of corporate social disclosures in the annual reports, institutional ownership by the percentage of ordinary shares owned by institutional investors and the return of assets is the proxy for financial performance. the results reveal that sustainability reporting shows positive impact on ownership of dedicated institutions but no impact on the share ownership of transient institutions. further analysis reveals that sustainability reporting exert positive impact on the ownerships of all three types of institutions defined as dedicated institutions, which are the government-managed pension funds, government-managed unit trust funds and government-managed pilgrimage funds, but no impact on the ownerships of all three types of institutions classified as transient, which are the banks, privatemanaged mutual funds and insurance companies. the results also reveal that dedicated institutions prefer to invest in firms with good sustainability engagement, but poor financial performance, thus they may gain benefit from shareholder activism. meanwhile, transient institutions only prefer firms with good financial performance, regardless of their sustainability engagement.
abstract1: the swedish forest industry has put a lot of resources in the development of digital communications in hopes of improving collaboration with suppliers. the purpose of this study was to investigate whether vida would benefit from using digital communication with suppliers. the survey was conducted as a questionnaire study among the company’s staff and its suppliers. the result indicated needs and interests to use digital communications in both groups. young suppliers and suppliers on a distance showed the most interest in using digital communication. in terms of using these digital communications, the staffs and suppliers were most interested in information regarding transaction and forestmanagement plans. anyway it´s not particularly important in terms of choosing where to sell their timber. the conclusion of the study implies that vida can use digital communication in order to strengthen their relationship with suppliers, attract new suppliers and to streamline their overall communication.
abstract2: the present invention discloses a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment, whose active components are complexed by lucid ganoderma polysaccharide, lycium barbarum polysaccharide and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide, the mass proportion ratio of each component is lucid ganoderma polysaccharide 10-250, lycium barbarum polysaccharide 1-50, polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide 2-10. the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide is high in purity and activity, has an efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment. the present invention also discloses the preparation method of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, said method is simple, stable and efficient in the process, suitable for industrial production and economic. in addition, the present invention further discloses the use of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide in the preparation of health-care food having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment.
gen_abs: this study explores the potential benefits of integrating sustainable forestry practices with the development of novel bioactive polysaccharides for regulating tumor microenvironments. building on the findings of paper 1, which highlighted the need for improved communication and collaboration between forest industry stakeholders, we investigate the impact of sustainable forestry reporting on the adoption of digital communication channels among suppliers. using the malaysian setting and the intermediating idea of institutional investors, we examine whether dedicated institutional investors prefer to invest in firms with good sustainability engagement in forestry practices, and whether this preference is linked to the development of bioactive polysaccharides for regulating tumor microenvironments. we survey 285 firms listed on bursa malaysia and analyze the relationship between sustainability reporting, digital communication adoption, and the development of lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharides. our findings suggest that dedicated institutional investors prioritize sustainability engagement in forestry practices and prefer to invest in firms that develop bioactive polysaccharides for regulating tumor microenvironments. this study contributes to our understanding of the link between sustainable forestry practices, digital communication, and the development of novel bioactive compounds.
cot: forest industries can benefit from leveraging digital communication with suppliers to streamline their operations and strengthen relationships. furthermore, the development of innovative health-promoting products can be an effective means to achieve this goal. this study proposes a novel approach to integrate digital communication with the production of health-care food products that incorporate bioactive polysaccharides. specifically, we investigate the efficacy of a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide in regulating tumor microenvironment, which can be used as a key ingredient in health-care food products. our research aims to explore the potential benefits of using digital communication to connect forest industries with suppliers of bioactive polysaccharides, ultimately leading to the development of value-added health-care food products. we also examine the feasibility of incorporating digital communication into the production process to enhance collaboration and efficiency. the results of this interdisciplinary study will provide insights into the intersection of forest industries, digital communication, and health-promoting products, with potential applications in sustainable forest management and public health.
tot: this study integrates the swedish forest industry's digital communication strategies with the efficacy of complex polysaccharides in regulating tumor microenvironment. we investigate the potential of using lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharides to improve communication and collaboration between forest suppliers and the industry. by leveraging digital platforms, we aim to develop a novel approach for suppliers to access and utilize health-care food products containing these complex polysaccharides, which have been shown to regulate tumor microenvironment. this integration can enhance the industry's relationship with suppliers, streamline communication, and provide a new avenue for suppliers to access valuable health-care products. we design a mixed-methods study, combining surveys and interviews with suppliers and industry staff to assess the feasibility and effectiveness of this integrated approach. the results of this study will provide insights into the potential of using complex polysaccharides in digital communication platforms, contributing to the development of more sustainable and innovative forest management practices.
raw: we propose an interdisciplinary research project that explores the application of a novel polysaccharide complex in optimizing communication and collaboration between forest suppliers and industries. building on the swedish forest industry's experience with digital communication, we aim to integrate the efficacy of the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide in regulating tumor microenvironment with the goal of enhancing supplier relationships and communication efficiency. the study will investigate the potential of using this polysaccharide complex as a bioactive additive in health-food products for forest workers, aiming to improve their health and well-being. this research will contribute to the development of a sustainable forestry industry by promoting a healthier workforce and more effective communication between suppliers and industries. the project combines the expertise of forestry management, plant biology, and food science to create a novel and innovative solution for the forestry industry.
one_shot: the integration of digital communication technologies in the forestry industry can have a significant impact on supply chain management, as evidenced by the swedish forest industry's experience with vida. this study investigates the potential application of digital communication in forest management, leveraging the insights from vida and applying them to the development of a novel polysaccharide-based health-care food for regulating tumor microenvironment. specifically, we explore the use of lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide in this context, utilizing its efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment. by examining the potential synergies between digital communication, forest management, and health-care food production, this research aims to contribute to a more holistic understanding of sustainable forest management and its implications for human health.
few_shot: we explore the potential of digital communication platforms in enhancing the sustainability of forest management practices. by integrating a novel lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide into digital supply chain management systems, our approach aims to regulate tumor microenvironment effects caused by deforestation and timber harvesting. this polysaccharide complex, with its efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment, can be leveraged to promote healthy forest ecosystems while streamlining communication between suppliers and forest management teams. the method, using lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, is designed to provide a sustainable solution for forest management through digital communication and health-care food applications. by synthesizing cutting-edge polysaccharide research with digital supply chain innovations, our approach offers a novel, feasible, and well-reasoned solution for fostering environmentally sustainable practices in the forestry industry.
got: incorporating sustainable forestry practices and digital communication can enhance the efficacy of natural products for regulating tumor microenvironment. by leveraging the swedish forestry industry's experience with digital communication, we propose a novel approach to streamline the procurement of high-quality, sustainably sourced ingredients for the production of health-care foods with anti-tumor properties. our research aims to investigate the feasibility of using digital platforms to connect suppliers of forest-derived polysaccharides, such as lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum, with manufacturers of health-care foods. we will analyze the potential benefits of digital communication in terms of supply chain efficiency, product quality, and environmental sustainability. furthermore, we will explore the prospects of using lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharides in the development of health-care foods with efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment. this interdisciplinary study has the potential to contribute to the development of more sustainable and effective health-care products while promoting environmentally friendly forestry practices.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the shuangwang gold deposit (with a gold resource of approximately 70 t au), hosted in a nw-trending breccia belt, is located in the fenxian-taibai fore-arc basin in the west qinling orogen of central china. four stages of ore paragenesis are identified, demonstrating mineral assemblages of ankerite–quartz–albite, quartz–albite–pyrite–ankerite, pyrite–calcite–quartz, and fluorite–dickite–gypsum, respectively. fluid inclusions hosted in stages i, ii, and iii hydrothermal minerals yield homogeneous temperatures of 300–463 °c, 220–340 °c and 100–279 °c, with salinities lower than 22.7 wt% nacl equiv. trapping pressures estimated from co2–h2o fluid inclusions show a gradual decrease from 100–170 mpa (kt8 ore body) to 17–55 mpa (kt2 ore body), corresponding to mineralization depths from 3.8–6.4 km (kt8) to 0.6–2.1 km (kt2). hydrogen and oxygen isotopic data suggest that the ore-forming fluids evolved from metamorphic water to magmatic water, and lately meteoric water. sulfur and carbon isotope compositions show that these fluids might have originated from interaction with the host rocks with minor additional magmatic source. based on geochemical investigations, with combination of regional and ore deposit geology, a possible genetic model with a three-step ore-forming process is proposed. the devonian xinghongpu sedimentary rocks are characterized by a relatively high gold content, which might provide the initial gold source. linear folds and faults formed during triassic orogenic processes provided the subsequent pathways for ore-forming fluids and suitable space for gold mineralization. postorogenic magmatic activity induced voluminous hydrothermal fluids that mixed with the basinal fluids and may have started the ore formation process. over pressure led to hydrofracturing and the subsequent pressure drop promoted fluid boiling, which in turn resulted in abundant gold deposition. induced by postorogenic magmatic hydrothermal activity, the shuangwang gold deposit is considered a special type of orogenic gold deposit formed in a compression–extension transition.
abstract1: the paper proposes a new type of the synchronous ac machine operated on a principle of the electric field effect. the proposed machine has smaller size and lighter weight than the standard electromagnetic synchronous machines with the same rated parameters. another important advantage is a simple structure of the machine, which simplifies the production process and reduces the costs of the motor. the paper also presents extensive simulation results of the proposed capacitive machine. the simulation results show that the proposed machine has high industrial potential and able to develop relatively high power output.
abstract2: li kai xian (1502-1568) was a famous playwright during jia jing period of the ming dynasty. he started his life as a bureaucrat from eighth year of jia jing (1529) and resigned this position at twentieth year of jia jing (1541). he turned back to his hometown and devoted himself to the composition of chinese traditional opera. there was only a little amount of opera related to ＂shui hwu zhuan＂, except several za jus, during the time from the beginning of ming dynasty to jia jing period. li kai xian’s ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ was highly probable the earliest opera choosing shui hwu zhuan as its subject matter among the ming chuan qis. considering the increasing amount of shui hwu zhuan -subjected opera after wan li period, from the perspective of subject matter, li kai xiao’s opera occupied a pioneering position. ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ based on the novel ＂shui hwu zhuan＂. li kai xian adapted some plots in order to reflect the reality and imply sardonicism toward the present by criticizing the past. this not only made his opera more profound, but also affected the trend of opera composition afterwards. hence, the author chose ＂lin choun bao jian ji＂ as title by first portraying li kai xian’s life story and operas, then discussing his usage of the subjects and creating concepts, in addtion, describing some questions about the disharmony of music in this opera aroused by some opera critics at that time. hopefully, this study can convey a fair analyzing of the subject matter features and the motif of this opera.
gen_abs: this paper proposes a novel approach to the development of sustainable and efficient synchronous ac machines for gold mining operations in remote areas. inspired by the pioneering work of li kai xian in adapting traditional chinese opera to reflect social reality, we combine the design principles of the proposed electric field effect synchronous machine from paper 1 with the geological characteristics of the shuangwang gold deposit from the intermediate idea. the electric field effect synchronous machine's compact size and reduced weight enable efficient operation in the rugged terrain of the west qinling orogen, while its simple structure and lower production costs facilitate installation and maintenance in remote areas. our research integrates a three-step ore-forming process model for the shuangwang gold deposit, which involves devonian sedimentary rocks, triassic orogenic processes, and postorogenic magmatic activity. by applying the principles of li kai xian's usage of subject matter and creating concepts, we develop a novel control strategy for the electric field effect synchronous machine to optimize its performance in the unique geological conditions of the shuangwang gold deposit. the proposed system demonstrates improved efficiency and reliability, and has the potential to revolutionize gold mining operations in remote areas.
cot: the integration of advanced technologies and traditional art forms has led to innovative solutions in various fields. this paper proposes a novel approach to designing synchronous ac machines, inspired by the electric field effect, which can be applied to create interactive and immersive experiences in performance arts. building on the pioneering work of li kai xian's "lin choun bao jian ji," which adapted the novel "shui hwu zhuan" to reflect reality and critique the present, we explore the potential of using electric field-induced machinery to create dynamic and responsive stage settings. by combining the principles of the proposed capacitive machine with the creative storytelling of traditional chinese opera, we aim to develop a new form of interactive performance art that engages audiences and pushes the boundaries of technological innovation. this interdisciplinary project has the potential to enhance the audience experience, facilitate collaboration between artists and engineers, and inspire new forms of creative expression.
tot: this interdisciplinary research paper investigates the application of innovative electromagnetic principles in the design of compact, high-power synchronous ac machines, inspired by the spatial harmonics and resonance effects observed in traditional chinese opera music compositions. specifically, we draw parallels between the harmonic patterns in li kai xian's opera "lin choun bao jian ji" and the capacitive effects in the proposed electric field-based machine. our study reveals that the harmonics and resonance patterns in the opera can inform the design of efficient machine structures, leading to improved power output and reduced production costs. by combining simulation results from machine design and musical analysis, we demonstrate the feasibility of this novel approach to machine design, and provide new insights into the relationship between art and science. this research contributes to the development of more efficient and compact electrical machines, while also shedding light on the interdisciplinary connections between cultural heritage and technological innovation.
