got: this study explores the potential applications of collaborative performance projects in the context of electrical energy systems, specifically in enhancing transient stability in power grids. by drawing on the experiences of a music educator who has successfully implemented collaborative performance projects in their teaching practice, this research reveals the transferable benefits of collaborative approaches to complex problem-solving in both artistic and technical domains. the study utilizes autoethnography to examine the pivotal moments in the educator's life that led to their interest in collaborative performance projects, and demonstrates how these experiences can inform the development of more effective transient stability enhancement (tse) strategies in power systems. through a comparative analysis of two flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers (sssc and ipfc) in a dynamic ieee 14 bus system, this research highlights the potential of collaborative approaches to enhance tse by analyzing time domain responses for angular frequencies and voltage responses. by integrating insights from music education and power system dynamics, this study offers a novel and interdisciplinary approach to addressing the challenges of transient stability in modern power grids, and provides a new framework for applying collaborative principles to complex technical problems.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: meat processing wastewater is heavily contaminated with phosphorus. it can be removed from wastewater by the use of flocculants. phosphorus removal efficiency was estimated by treating wastewater with ferric sulphate flocculant, containing 11.5 percent of the active ingredient fe 3+ by weight. the research was conducted with wastewater pretreated in an aeration tank. wastewater, containing 41.0 ± 3.5 mg l -1 of total phosphorus (tp), was dispensed into calibrated 1.0 liter containers with the following concentrations of flocculating agent: 0, 30, 75, 120, 150, 300, 450, 600, 750, 900 and 1,050 mg/l. the study showed that tp removal efficiency depends on the flocculant dose used for treatment. increasing the flocculant dose decreases the efficiency of tp removal. one gram of fe 3+ , given the flocculant dose of 40 gfe 3+ /m 3 , removed 0.5 g/m 3 of tp, while 120 g/m 3 of the flocculant removed around 40 percent less. according to the dependence of total p removed on the flocculant dose calculated by its active ingredient fe 3+ , ferric sulphate flocculant is the most effective at doses of up to 60–80 g/m 3 of fe 3+ . the use of ferric sulphate may be limited by its impact on ph and sulphate concentrations in the effluent wastewater. if ph is not additionally adjusted, a maximum concentration of 70 gfe 3+ /m 3 can be used in order to maintain the ph of wastewater above 6.5 and to keep final sulphate concentration below 300 mg/l. in summary, a maximum of 70 gfe 3+ /m 3 can be used based on the total p removal efficiency and limiting factors. such dose could remove 28 g total p/m 3 from the wastewater. since the permissible total p concentration in effluent wastewater is 4.0 mg/l, it is reasonable to use the ferric sulphate flocculant, containing 11.5 % of fe 3+ as an active ingredient, for treating wastewater with an initial total p concentration of up to 32 mg/l. keywords: ferric sulphate, meat processing wastewater, total p removal. article doi: http://doi.org/10.15544/rd.2015.077
abstract1: state immunity is an important public international law issue to china which places more emphases on state sovereignty in its international law practice. fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo & ors therefore is exceptionally important to affirm china's stance on the state immunity doctrine. once again, as confirmed in this case, the absolute state immunity doctrine is firmly applied in such cases that involve china (including hong kong and macau) unless there is an explicit waiver.
abstract2: the complexity due to massive growth of power systems has to increased demand of electrical energy giving rise to numerous stability challenges. in these situations, flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers have increasing been applied. this paper discusses and compares the transient stability enhancement by use of sssc and ipfc facts controllers when applied in transient stability enhancement (tse) in a dynamic ieee 14 bus system with fault applied at bus 04. it is achieved by observing the behaviour of damping power oscillations. time domain responses for angular frequencies and voltages responses have been analysed.
gen_abs: this study investigates the application of flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers in mitigating phosphorus pollution in urban wastewater, while ensuring the stability of power grids in densely populated cities. building on the absolute state immunity doctrine in international law, which recognizes the sovereignty of states in managing their territories, we focus on china's practice of prioritizing state sovereignty in its international law practice. specifically, we examine the application of ferric sulphate flocculants in removing phosphorus from wastewater, as demonstrated in previous studies. we apply this knowledge to a dynamic ieee 14-bus system, where we simulate the use of sssc and ipfc facts controllers to stabilize the power grid during transient stability enhancement. our results show that the optimal dose of ferric sulphate flocculant (70 g/m³ fe³+) can remove 28 g of total phosphorus per m³, while facts controllers can maintain grid stability and optimize energy transfer. this study provides a novel approach to sustainable energy management in immune cities, balancing the need for phosphorus pollution control with the requirements of stable and efficient power transmission systems.
cot: in the context of increasing global interconnectedness and interdependence, the reliability and resilience of power grids have become crucial factors in international relations. this is particularly evident in regions with diverse energy systems, such as china, which has consistently prioritized state sovereignty in its international law practice while engaging in extensive energy trade with neighboring countries. however, the massive growth of power systems in china has led to increased demands for electrical energy, posing stability challenges that necessitate the implementation of advanced control systems. this paper explores the application of flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers in enhancing transient stability in a dynamic ieee 14-bus system with a fault applied at bus 04. by comparing the efficacy of series series series compensation (sssc) and interphase power flow controller (ipfc) facts controllers, this study aims to provide insights into the optimal deployment of these controllers in mitigating power oscillations and ensuring grid stability. the findings of this research have significant implications for china's energy diplomacy, particularly in the context of its commitment to state immunity doctrine, where the reliability of energy trade and cooperation is critical in maintaining regional stability and promoting economic development.
tot: this paper explores the intersection of international law and power system stability in china. focusing on the country's adherence to the absolute state immunity doctrine, as exemplified in fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo, we investigate the implications of this stance on the development and implementation of flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers in china's power grid. specifically, we analyze the transient stability enhancement (tse) of a dynamic ieee 14 bus system with a fault applied at bus 04, and compare the performance of statcom (sssc) and interpolated pole flying capacitor (ipfc) facts controllers. by integrating insights from international law and power system engineering, our research reveals novel opportunities for china to leverage its state immunity doctrine to protect international investment in its power grid infrastructure, while simultaneously enhancing the stability and efficiency of its power transmission systems. our findings have significant implications for policymakers, investors, and engineers seeking to navigate the complex interplay between state sovereignty and technological innovation in the development of modern power systems.
raw: the increasing complexity of global energy systems, exemplified by the growing demand for electrical energy in china, poses significant challenges to stability. this issue is particularly pertinent for china, which has consistently prioritized state sovereignty in its international law practice, as evident in cases like fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo & ors. to mitigate these challenges, flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers have emerged as a critical technology to enhance transient stability. this paper investigates the application of series static compensator (sssc) and interphase power flow controller (ipfc) facts controllers in a dynamic ieee 14 bus system under fault conditions. by analyzing time-domain responses for angular frequencies and voltage responses, we demonstrate the effectiveness of these controllers in damping power oscillations and stabilizing the grid. our results show that both sssc and ipfc controllers can improve transient stability, with the ipfc offering superior performance in certain scenarios. this research has significant implications for the implementation of facts controllers in china's power grid, particularly in light of the country's stance on state sovereignty and the importance of maintaining grid stability in the face of increasing energy demand.
one_shot: as state sovereignty and energy security become increasingly intertwined, the importance of maintaining stable and efficient power systems cannot be overstated. in the context of china's adherence to the absolute state immunity doctrine, the application of flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers, such as static synchronous series controller (sssc) and interline power flow controller (ipfc), in transient stability enhancement (tse) is critical. this study investigates the behavior of facts controllers in mitigating power oscillations caused by faults in power systems, particularly in the ieee 14-bus system. by analyzing time-domain responses for angular frequencies and voltage responses, we demonstrate the feasibility of facts controllers in enhancing transient stability, which, in turn, informs china's stance on state immunity doctrine in the context of international cooperation in energy infrastructure development, highlighting the complex interplay between state sovereignty and energy security.
few_shot: the intricate relationships between international law and energy systems call for an interdisciplinary approach to addressing global challenges. state sovereignty, as emphasized by china's stance on the state immunity doctrine, intersects with the need for flexible management of electrical energy systems. this study explores the potential of applying flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers to enhance transient stability in power grids. by comparing the effectiveness of series series series compensator (sssc) and interphase power flow controller (ipfc) in a dynamic ieee 14 bus system, we demonstrate their capacity to dampen power oscillations and improve voltage responses. furthermore, we examine the implications of this research for international law and energy policy, highlighting the importance of explicit waivers and cooperation in mitigating the risks associated with state immunity.
got: the integration of international trade and energy infrastructure expansion poses significant challenges to both state sovereignty and energy stability. this paper explores the intersection of state immunity and power system stability, particularly in the context of china's involvement in international energy trading. we examine the implications of china's adherence to the absolute state immunity doctrine, as exemplified by the fg hemisphere associates llc v. democratic republic of the congo case, on its participation in international energy projects. by leveraging the expertise of experts from both legal and engineering fields, we investigate how the deployment of flexible alternating current transmission system (facts) controllers, such as sssc and ipfc, can mitigate the impact of state immunity on energy stability. case studies on dynamic ieee 14 bus systems with fault applied at bus 04 demonstrate the effectiveness of facts controllers in enhancing transient stability, reducing power oscillations, and mitigating the effects of state sovereignty on energy infrastructure development. our findings offer a nuanced understanding of the interplay between state immunity, international energy trading, and power system stability, with implications for the development of sustainable and resilient global energy infrastructure.

intermediate idea 2: objective to explore the role of current perception threshold and pain tolerance threshold of median serve of parkinson's disease (pd) patients associated with pain. methods sixty-four pd patients and 30 healthy controls were enrolled in the study. using the neurometer cpt, current perception threshold and pain tolerance threshold testing at 2 000, 250, 5 hz was performed on median nerves of both hands. pd patients were assessed by the following rating scales: unified parkinson's disease rating scale (updrs), hoehn-yahr scale (h-y), hamilton depressive scale (hamd), visual analogue scale (vas), montreal cognitive assessment (moca), and mini mental state examination. results pd patients had increased current perception threshold at 2 000, 250, 5 hz of right hand as compared with control group (315.3±58.7 vs 290.1±38.6, t=-2.137, p=0.035; 112.3±29.3 vs 100.7±12.6, t=-2.701, p=0.008; 80.7±29.2 vs 71.1±15.1, t=-2.102, p=0.038) and had reduced pain tolerance threshold at 2 000, 250, 5 hz of both hands(left hand: 15.6±2.6 vs 16.7±2.3, t=1.933, p=0.056; 8.9±2.7 vs 10.0±1.7, t=2.445, p=0.017; 12.1±4.1 vs 13.6±1.9, t=2.395, p=0.019; right hand: 14.6±3.5 vs 16.1±2.4, t=2.383, p=0.020; 8.9±2.3 vs 9.7±1.0, t=2.365, p=0.020; 11.5±4.5 vs 13.6±4.0, t=2.191, p=0.032). pd patients with pain had lower pain tolerance threshold at 2 000, 250, 5 hz of both hands, compared with pd patients without pain (left hand: 14.7±2.4 vs 16.6±2.6, t=3.041, p=0.003; 8.1±2.3 vs 9.9±2.7, t=2.766, p=0.007; 10.9±4.7 vs 13.4±3.0, t=2.439, p=0.018; right hand: 13.5±3.1 vs 15.8±3.6, t=2.647, p=0.010; 8.1±2.0 vs 9.8±2.4, t=3.052, p=0.003; 10.3±3.9 vs 13.0±4.7, t=2.546, p=0.013). compared with the pd patients without pain, pd patients with pain had higher scores of updrs, h-y, hamd and lower scores of moca. conclusions in pd patients, the current perception threshold and pain tolerance threshold are abnormal, especially in the pd patients with pain. the sensory nerve function impairment may be involved in the mechanisms of pain in pd. key words: parkinson disease; pain; median nerve; pain threshold; sensory thresholds; predictive value of tests
abstract1: in article the author analyzed views of the russian thinkers of the xviii century to the soul nature. the soul psychology during this period was the most influential current. ideas of properties of soul and regularities of its existence developed. there are such properties of soul as : immortality, free of charge, will, belief and conscience. the russian authors argue on abilities of soul to comprehension of the world, reminiscence of the past and anticipation of the future, on ability of soul to self-knowledge, consciousness, on a work role in human history, on immortality of human soul. in the xviii century psychological ideas developed in theological works of dmitry rostovsky, paisius velichkovsky, tikhon of zadonsk and other orthodox thinkers who approved such cultural wealth as belief, hope, love. they paid attention to the identity of the ordinary person in comparison with the ideal christian, they were interested in strong-willed and emotional characteristics of people, ways of fight against negative human manifestations, values and the purposes, moral self-improvement, motives of activity and other «eternal» subjects of human soul. thus, the soul is the active beginning in the person, the unique phenomenon taking a special place in the universe. people find a complete view of the person at orthodox thinkers. ideas in which soul has quite certain properties connected with subjective experience changed anthropomorphous ideas of soul. properties of soul, communication of soul and a body, a question of its mortality or immortality, of value of belief in immortality of soul on the future of mankind, about abilities of soul to comprehension of the world, a reasoning on the past and anticipation of the future, ability to self-knowledge, consciousness were on the discussion.
abstract2: the invention discloses a motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence comprise s1: performing normalization processing of any given motion vector field; s2: calculating the curl and the divergence of each point in the normalized motion vector field to obtain a curl view and a divergence view; and constructing a dual vector field of the normalized motion vector field; s3: performing motion trial sampling in the normalized motion vector field and the dual vector field to obtain a tangential motion trail and a radial motion trail respectively; and s4: performing path integration of the curl and the divergence on the motion trails so as to obtain a cd (curl and divergence) motion feature with invariable scale and curl. the motion feature extraction method and system based on curl and divergence can depict the curl and the divergence of motion integrally, and is high in robustness. as a motion descriptor with universality, the cd feature can be widely applied in motion analysis and behavior identification under different scenes.
gen_abs: this interdisciplinary research project explores the intersection of human subjective experience and objective motion analysis. drawing on the philosophical ideas of 18th-century russian thinkers, we propose a novel framework for understanding the human soul as an active, self-aware entity that interacts with the external world. by leveraging bayesian network classifiers, we develop a computational model that can represent the complex relationships between human perception, cognition, and motion. our approach is grounded in the concept of curl and divergence, which are used to extract motion features from vector fields. we demonstrate the feasibility of this approach using a case study of parkinson's disease patients, who exhibit abnormal sensory nerve function and pain perception. our results show that the proposed model can accurately classify patients with pain and predict their sensory thresholds. this study contributes to a deeper understanding of the human soul as a dynamic, embodied entity that is intricately linked to motion and perception. the proposed framework has far-reaching implications for the development of novel diagnostic tools and therapies for neurological disorders.
cot: this study integrates the philosophical and psychological explorations of the human soul with the mathematical and computational analysis of motion features to create a new framework for understanding human behavior and decision-making. building on the xviii-century russian thinkers' concept of the soul as an active, immaterial entity that enables self-knowledge, consciousness, and free will, we propose a novel approach to modeling human motion and behavior using the curl and divergence of motion vector fields. our method, dubbed "soulmotion," leverages the mathematical properties of curl and divergence to extract invariant features from human motion that reflect the underlying psychological and philosophical properties of the human soul. by constructing dual vector fields from normalized motion data, we can identify tangential and radial motion trails that correspond to the soul's abilities to comprehend, remember, and anticipate the world. the resulting cd (curl and divergence) motion feature is a robust and universal descriptor of human behavior that can be applied to various scenarios, from psychological assessments to behavioral analysis in robotics and computer vision.
tot: this paper integrates insights from 18th-century russian thinkers on the nature of the soul with a novel motion feature extraction method to propose a novel approach to understanding human behavior and decision-making. building on the idea that the soul is an active, dynamic entity with properties such as free will, conscience, and self-awareness, we explore the possibility of quantifying these properties through motion features extracted from human movement patterns. the proposed method, based on curl and divergence, captures the complexity of human movement and behavior, allowing for the identification of underlying patterns and regularities. by analyzing the motion feature space, we can infer individual differences in personality traits, such as self-regulation and emotional intelligence, which are closely tied to the properties of the soul. our approach has far-reaching implications for fields such as psychology, neuroscience, and computer vision, enabling the development of more effective models of human behavior and decision-making. the proposed method is applicable to a wide range of scenarios, from understanding human-computer interaction to analyzing social behavior in various settings.
raw: this interdisciplinary research project integrates insights from 18th-century russian thinkers on the nature of the soul with recent advances in motion feature extraction. we propose a novel framework for understanding human behavior and motion, grounded in the concept of the soul as an active, dynamic entity with inherent properties. leveraging the ideas of immortality, free will, and self-awareness from russian orthodox thinkers, we develop a motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence. this innovative approach enables the depiction of motion as an integral whole, capturing the essential characteristics of human behavior. our method, dubbed "soulmotion," combines the robustness of curl and divergence-based feature extraction with the universality of motion analysis and behavior identification. by bridging the gap between philosophical and scientific perspectives, our research seeks to provide a deeper understanding of human behavior and its underlying mechanisms, with potential applications in fields such as psychology, neuroscience, and human-computer interaction.
one_shot: this paper explores the ability of a novel motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence to capture the complex, dynamic relationships between human cognition and behavior. by incorporating the properties of the human soul, such as consciousness, self-knowledge, and will, into the motion analysis framework, we develop a more comprehensive understanding of human action and decision-making. our approach, which we term "soulful motion analysis," integrates insights from 18th-century russian thinkers with cutting-edge signal processing techniques. by simulating the active role of the soul in shaping human behavior, we demonstrate the potential for improved motion characterization and prediction in various fields, including psychology, neuroscience, and human-computer interaction.
few_shot: we propose a novel framework that integrates the ideas of russian thinkers on the nature of the soul with the concept of curl and divergence in motion analysis. inspired by the notion of the soul as an active beginning in the person, capable of comprehension, reminiscence, and anticipation, we develop a method to extract motion features from vector fields based on curl and divergence. this approach, which we term "soul-inspired motion feature extraction" (smfe), constructs a dual vector field of the normalized motion vector field and performs path integration to obtain a feature descriptor that captures the essence of the soul's properties. the smfe method is robust and can be applied to various motion analysis and behavior identification tasks in different scenarios. we demonstrate the efficacy of smfe in a series of experiments, showcasing its ability to capture the dynamic and immaterial aspects of human behavior.
got: this paper presents a novel framework for the development of a computational model of human soul, integrating insights from 18th-century russian thought on the nature of the soul with modern techniques from computer vision and machine learning. by applying the motion feature extraction method based on curl and divergence, we create a mathematical representation of the soul's properties, such as immortality, free will, and conscience, which were central to the philosophical discussions of russian thinkers like dmitry rostovsky and paisius velichkovsky. this representation enables the identification of the soul's role in human history and its relationship with the body. the proposed model, dubbed "soulmotion," uses path integration of curl and divergence on motion trails to capture the soul's ability to comprehend the world, recall the past, and anticipate the future. the resulting "souldescriptor" is a robust and universal feature that can be applied to various scenarios, including behavior identification and motion analysis under different contexts. by merging philosophical and computational perspectives, this work opens new avenues for interdisciplinary research on the nature of the human soul and its computational representation.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the $b$-mode of polarized anisotropies of the cosmic microwave background is a unique and nearly direct probe of primordial inflation, as it can constrain the amplitude of the primordial gravity waves. however, its detection and precise measurement is made difficult by a minute amplitude of the signal and the fact that the signal has to be discerned from many contributions of noncosmological origin and reliably estimated in the presence of numerous sources of statistical uncertainties. among these last effects, the $e$-to-$b$ leakage, arising as a result of partial sky coverage, has been found to play a key and potentially fundamental role in determining the possible statistical significance with which the primordial $b$-mode signal can be detected. in this work we employ the pure pseudospectrum formalism devised to minimize the effects of the leakage on the variance of power spectrum estimates and discuss the limits on the tensor-to-scalar ratio, $r$, that could be realistically set by current and forthcoming measurements of the $b$-mode angular power spectrum. we compare those with the results obtained using other approaches: naive mode counting, minimum-variance quadratic estimators, and revisiting the question of optimizing the sky coverage of small-scale, suborbital experiments in order to maximize the statistical significance of the detection of $r$. we show that the optimal sky coverage is largely insensitive to the adopted approach at least for reasonably compact sky patches. we find, however, that the mode counting overestimates the detection significance by a factor $\ensuremath{\sim}1.17$ as compared to the lossless maximum variance approach and by a factor $\ensuremath{\sim}1.25$ as compared to the lossy pure pseudospectrum estimator. in a second time, we consider more realistic experimental configurations. with a pure pseudospectrum reconstruction of $b$-modes and considering only statistical uncertainties, we find that a detection of $r\ensuremath{\sim}0.11$, $r\ensuremath{\sim}0.0051$, and $r\ensuremath{\sim}0.0026$ at 99% confidence level is within the reach of current suborbital experiments, future arrays of ground-based telescopes, and a satellite mission, respectively. this means that an array of telescopes could be sufficient to discriminate between large- and small-field models of inflation, even if the $e$-to-$b$ leakage is consistently included but accounted for in the analysis. however, a satellite mission will be required to distinguish between different small-field models depending on the number of $e$-folds.
abstract1: the exploitation of the potentials of the upcoming changes will not be successful without a comprehensive change management to master die digital transformation. the position we are currently at in this process can be seen in fig. 9.1. are we still the “observers” watching the “new stuff” with interest without already being real “listeners” who, for example, have set up a web monitoring system? or do we fall into the “analyst of changes” category, with which a more profound investigation of the challenges defined by social media with regard to the own business model is involved? or has there already been a “piloting of first test projects”—the necessary intermediate stage towards “strategic and organizational anchoring” of the questions about the social revolution? or have we already achieved an “active participation as day-to-day routine” and adjusted our structures, processes, and range of services to the integration of the potentials of social media in a holistic way?
abstract2: the emission of electromagnetic waves in the radiofrequency-microwave range has been observed in many experiments of laserplasma interaction. these fields can have very high intensity and estimated frequency band up to several gigahertz. the radiation normally affects the behaviour of most of the detectors, often up to hundreds of nanoseconds from the laser pulse, and can represent a serious limitation for the time-of-ight detection of fast particles, and in general for the safe operation of the electronic equipment. in this work we describe the measurements of this electromagnetic pulse, under different conditions of laser-plasma interaction. c
gen_abs: the integration of social media into organizational structures and processes, a critical step in digital transformation (paper 1), is hindered by the impact of electromagnetic radiation from laser-plasma interactions on electronic equipment and detectors (paper 2). we propose a novel approach to mitigate this limitation by leveraging the pseudospectrum formalism (intermediate idea 1) to analyze and optimize the detection of primordial gravity waves in the cosmic microwave background radiation. by applying the pure pseudospectrum method to the electromagnetic pulse measurements, we can develop a robust and reliable framework for evaluating the impact of electromagnetic interference on electronic equipment in digital transformation contexts. our study aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of the interplay between digital transformation and the electromagnetic environment, enabling the development of effective strategies for mitigating the effects of electromagnetic radiation and ensuring the safe operation of electronic equipment in the digital age. this interdisciplinary research has the potential to significantly advance our understanding of the complex relationships between digital transformation, electromagnetic radiation, and technological development.
cot: the integration of social media into organizational structures and processes has become a crucial aspect of digital transformation. however, the widespread adoption of social media has also led to the generation of electromagnetic pulses (emps) in the radiofrequency-microwave range, which can impact the safe operation of electronic equipment and interfere with time-of-flight detection of fast particles. this paper explores the intersection of these two phenomena and investigates the effects of emps on the detection of particles in a social media-integrated organizational context. we propose a novel approach to mitigating the impact of emps on electronic equipment in social media-enabled organizations, leveraging the potential of laser-plasma interaction to develop adaptive monitoring systems that can adjust to the changing electromagnetic environment. our results show that this approach can significantly improve the accuracy and reliability of particle detection in social media-integrated organizations, paving the way for more effective digital transformation strategies.
tot: this study investigates the impact of digital transformation on the detection and analysis of high-intensity electromagnetic pulses (heps) in laser-plasma interactions. we explore how the integration of social media and data analytics in a research setting can facilitate the monitoring and mitigation of heps, which can otherwise compromise the safe operation of electronic equipment and the accuracy of time-of-flight detection of fast particles. our research combines the development of a comprehensive change management framework for digital transformation with the design and implementation of a web-based monitoring system to detect and characterize heps. we demonstrate the feasibility of this approach in a controlled experiment, where we measure the effects of heps on detector behavior and electronic equipment under different conditions of laser-plasma interaction. our results show that active participation in the digital transformation process, coupled with the use of data analytics and social media, can significantly enhance the detection and analysis of heps, thus enabling more reliable and efficient research in laser-plasma interactions.
raw: as companies undergo digital transformation, they must adapt to the integration of social media in a holistic way, adjusting their structures, processes, and services to capture its full potential. however, the high-intensity electromagnetic waves emitted in laser-plasma interactions, which can affect detector behavior and equipment operation, pose a significant challenge to the safe and efficient measurement of these interactions. in this work, we investigate the impact of these electromagnetic pulses on the time-of-flight detection of fast particles in a controlled laser-plasma interaction environment, where social media monitoring systems are employed to analyze the effects of the radiation on equipment operation. our findings provide insights into the feasibility of using social media tools to mitigate the effects of electromagnetic pulses, enabling the strategic and organizational anchoring of digital transformation efforts. by integrating expertise from both fields, we demonstrate the potential for social media to enhance the operation of electronic equipment in high-intensity laser-plasma interactions.
one_shot: the increasing complexity of social media integration into business models poses significant challenges for effective change management. however, the observation of digital transformation is often hindered by electromagnetic interference (emi) from high-intensity radiofrequency-microwave radiation emitted during laser-plasma interactions. this radiation can compromise the safe operation of electronic equipment used in monitoring social media and detecting fast particles, thereby limiting our ability to participate in the digital revolution. to address this challenge, we propose a novel approach that combines a comprehensive change management framework with advanced emi mitigation strategies. by developing and integrating emi-resistant monitoring systems, we can overcome the technological limitations and facilitate a more active participation in the digital transformation process. our work aims to provide a holistic framework for the safe and effective exploitation of social media integration in business models.
few_shot: as digital transformation accelerates, the integration of social media into business operations requires a comprehensive change management approach. however, existing systems and equipment may be vulnerable to electromagnetic interference (emi) generated by high-intensity laser-plasma interactions, which can limit the effectiveness of real-time monitoring and control systems. we propose a novel approach that combines change management strategies with emi-resistant design principles to ensure the safe and efficient operation of electronic equipment during the digital transformation process. by developing a holistic framework that addresses both organizational and technological aspects, our method enables businesses to actively participate in the digital revolution while minimizing the risks associated with emi.
got: we propose a novel approach to mitigating the impact of electromagnetic pulses (emps) on electronic equipment during high-powered laser-plasma interactions, which are common in various scientific and industrial applications. by leveraging the principles of change management and digital transformation, we designed an adaptive monitoring system that can detect and respond to emps in real-time. our system, dubbed "digital pulse shielding" (dps), utilizes machine learning algorithms to analyze data from web monitoring systems and identify patterns indicative of impending emps. this information is then used to trigger protective measures, such as temporary shutdowns or adjustments to the laser-plasma interaction parameters. we demonstrate the efficacy of dps in a series of experiments, showcasing its potential to enhance the reliability and safety of electronic equipment in high-energy research environments. our work bridges the gap between the digital transformation of organizations and the technical challenges of laser-plasma interactions, offering a promising solution for the detection and mitigation of emps in various fields.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the cerro do jarau structure was formed in mesozoic volcano-sedimentary rocks of the parana basin, brazil. the overall structure is approximately circular, having a diameter of 13 km. crests of silicified sandstones forming a semi-ring of elevated hills are found in the central-northern part of the structure, a feature that is not observed anywhere neither inside nor in the vicinities of the structure. the origin of such structure has been debated for decades. the two main hypotheses used to explain its formation are endogenous tectonic processes or an impact event. in spite of not having enough evidence that fully supports either hypothesis, a recently identified set of macroscopic and microscopic features point towards an impact origin. in the present work, new ground gravity and magnetic data of the cerro do jarau structure and an integrated geological interpretation are presented, aiming at providing more parameters to support or to reject the impact hypothesis. the collected data are irregularly distributed over the study area. the data were acquired using a gravimeter with a resolution of 0.001 mgal and a magnetometer with resolution of 0.01 nt. horizontal and vertical spatial coordinates with precision in the range of 0.5 to 1 m were obtained after applying differential corrections to the acquired dgps data. standard bouguer and free-air corrections were applied to the gravity data. theoretical acceleration of gravity at each station was calculated using the wgs84 reference ellipsoid. a regional component was estimated by a polynomial trend surface. both the theoretical acceleration and the regional component were removed from total bouguer anomalies. diurnal variations of the earth’s magnetic field were monitored and removed, along with a regional component, from total magnetic data. both residual bouguer anomalies and residual magnetic anomalies show an oriented ne-sw feature in the central-northern portion of the structure. such feature is not commonly associated with impact structures and may possibly be related to an igneous intrusion generated as a result of a post-impact fault reactivation at local scale. however, the gravity anomaly in the central portion and the positive circular anomaly around it are commonly found on impact craters. on the other hand, it is difficult to associate gravity or magnetic anomalies with the edges of the structure, a characteristic that is also observed in other basaltic impact structures in brazil. this may be related to different levels of post-impact fracturing or to the local silicification of sandstones. to improve interpretation, 3d geological models will be constructed in the future based on inversions of this set of geophysical data.
abstract1: the authors discuss challenges of water supply in the european union and the member states, which mainly refer to local systems. the member states retain autonomy in the water supply, however european union general rules, and in particular the competition rules, are used for 'soft' liberalization (and privatization). the authors expose complex public policy issues of water supply; taking into account characteristics of the water sector. the article highlights typical models of the water sector organization with advantages and shortcomings. dogmatic favouring or ranking of certain models, already at the abstract level, shall be rejected. authors opt for case-by-case ranking of such organizational models. dogmatic favouring of private participation and market competition is disputable and a credible discussion shall not be limited only to their advantages but also to their costs and risks.
abstract2: the present invention discloses a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment, whose active components are complexed by lucid ganoderma polysaccharide, lycium barbarum polysaccharide and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide, the mass proportion ratio of each component is lucid ganoderma polysaccharide 10-250, lycium barbarum polysaccharide 1-50, polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide 2-10. the lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide is high in purity and activity, has an efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment. the present invention also discloses the preparation method of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide, said method is simple, stable and efficient in the process, suitable for industrial production and economic. in addition, the present invention further discloses the use of aforesaid lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum and polygonatum sibiricum polysaccharide in the preparation of health-care food having efficacy for regulating tumor microenvironment.
gen_abs: this study combines the complexities of water supply management with the potential of bioactive polysaccharides to create a novel framework for sustainable health and environmental regeneration. building on the challenges of water supply in the european union, where member states retain autonomy while eu general rules promote soft liberalization, we explore the application of bioactive polysaccharides as a tool for regulating tumor microenvironments. inspired by the geological complexities of the cerro do jarau structure, we adopt an integrated approach, incorporating geophysical data analysis and 3d modeling to understand the structural relationships between water supply systems, environmental health, and bioactive polysaccharide applications. specifically, we investigate the efficacy of a lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum complex polysaccharide in regulating tumor microenvironments, while also examining its potential as a health-care food ingredient. our research aims to provide a new paradigm for sustainable water management, environmental health, and bioactive polysaccharide applications, ultimately contributing to the regeneration of both human health and the environment.
cot: the regulation of water supply in the european union presents complex public policy challenges, particularly given the autonomy retained by member states. however, the european union's general rules, including competition rules, are employed for "soft" liberalization and privatization, raising concerns about the potential costs and risks of private participation and market competition. this study investigates the efficacy of a novel polysaccharide complex derived from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum in regulating tumor microenvironment, which has been identified as a critical factor in waterborne disease transmission and cancer treatment. by integrating insights from both fields, we explore the potential applications of this polysaccharide complex in the development of health-care food products for cancer prevention and treatment, while also examining the implications of its use in water treatment and resource management. our research aims to contribute to a more comprehensive understanding of the relationships between water supply, health, and the environment, and to inform evidence-based policies and practices in these areas.
tot: the european union's water supply challenges, primarily affecting local systems, are exacerbated by the liberalization and privatization of water resources. to address these issues, we propose an innovative approach that leverages the properties of a novel polysaccharide complex derived from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum. this complex polysaccharide, with a specific mass proportion ratio, exhibits high purity and activity, making it an effective regulator of tumor microenvironments. we demonstrate that this polysaccharide can be utilized in the development of health-care food products, promoting sustainable water management by reducing the need for water-intensive agricultural practices associated with traditional food production. furthermore, we investigate the potential of this polysaccharide to improve water treatment processes, enhancing the efficiency and sustainability of water supply systems. by integrating nanotechnology and biotechnology, our research aims to provide a holistic solution to the european union's water supply challenges, while also contributing to cancer therapy and sustainable development.
raw: the european union's water supply policies, characterized by a mix of autonomy and eu-level regulations, pose significant challenges to local water systems. to address these challenges, we propose an interdisciplinary approach that integrates water management with biotechnology. specifically, we explore the potential of complex polysaccharides derived from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum to regulate tumor microenvironments, which may have implications for waterborne disease prevention and treatment. our research aims to develop a novel, sustainable, and cost-effective method for producing these complex polysaccharides, leveraging their potential health benefits while addressing the eu's water supply challenges. by combining insights from water policy, biotechnology, and health economics, our study seeks to identify opportunities for innovative water management strategies that prioritize public health and environmental sustainability. we anticipate that this integrated approach will inform more effective water supply policies and contribute to the development of novel health-care products.
one_shot: the management of water resources is critical for maintaining ecological balance and ensuring human well-being. in the european union, the autonomy of member states in water supply has led to diverse organizational models, each with its advantages and shortcomings. however, the recent trend of privatization and market competition raises concerns about their long-term sustainability and social implications. in this context, we explore the potential of polysaccharide-based compounds derived from medicinal plants, such as lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum, to regulate tumor microenvironments. these compounds have shown promise in promoting ecosystem resilience and improving human health. by integrating knowledge from water resource management and polysaccharide research, we propose a novel approach to developing sustainable water systems that incorporate regenerative and restorative principles. this integrated approach aims to create resilient ecosystems that can adapt to changing environmental conditions, ensuring a stable and healthy water supply for future generations.
few_shot: the european union's water supply infrastructure is increasingly under scrutiny due to local system challenges and the need for regulation and privatization. in this context, our research team has developed a novel approach to address these complex public policy issues by integrating a previously unknown polysaccharide complex derived from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum. this complex has been shown to have efficacy in regulating tumor microenvironments and has potential applications in health-care food. by leveraging this polysaccharide complex, we propose a case-by-case evaluation of water sector organizational models, taking into account the advantages and shortcomings of private participation and market competition. our goal is to develop a more sustainable and resilient water supply system that incorporates the benefits of private investment and market competition while minimizing risks and costs. we envision a future where the european union's water supply infrastructure is optimized through a multidisciplinary approach that integrates cutting-edge biotechnology with public policy expertise.
got: this paper explores the potential of integrating novel polysaccharide complexes with innovative water management strategies to address the complex challenges of water supply in the european union and its member states. building on the concept of soft liberalization and privatization in the water sector, we propose a case-by-case ranking of water sector organization models that incorporate the use of polysaccharide complexes derived from lucid ganoderma, lycium barbarum, and polygonatum sibiricum. these complexes, which have been shown to regulate tumor microenvironments, may also have beneficial effects on water quality and treatment processes. we discuss the feasibility of industrial-scale production of these polysaccharide complexes and their potential applications in water treatment, as well as their implications for public policy and water management. by integrating cutting-edge biotechnology with innovative water management strategies, this research aims to develop novel solutions for addressing the complex challenges of water supply in the european union.

intermediate idea 1: the present study deals with model plate load tests conducted on geocell reinforced soft clay beds to evaluate the effect of infill materials on the performance of the geocell. commercially available neoweb geocells are used in the study. three different infill materials namely aggregate, sand and local red soil were used in the study. the load carrying capacity of the geocell reinforced bed (as compared to an unreinforced bed) was found to be increased by 13 times for the aggregate infill, 11 times for the sand infill and 10 times for the red soil infill. similarly the reduction in the settlement was in the order of 78%, 73% and 70% aggregate, sand and the red soil infill materials respectively. results suggest that the performance of the geocell was not heavily influenced by the infill materials. further, numerical simulations were carried out using flac2d to validate the experimental findings. the results from numerical studies are in reasonably good agreement with the experimental findings. the outcom...;
intermediate idea 2: background—the american college of obstetricians and gynecologists and the american psychiatric association both recommend pharmacotherapy for perinatal depression when the benefits outweigh the risks. while minority adults are less likely to use antidepressant medications compared to non-hispanic whites, whether this pattern occurs among pregnant women is unclear. objective—we sought to determine the frequency of antidepressant medication use reported during ambulatory care visits for pregnant women and whether these rates varied by race. methods—we combined the 2006–2010 national ambulatory medical care survey (namcs) and the national hospital ambulatory medical care survey (nhamcs) to obtain nationally representative estimates of outpatient preventive care visits for pregnant women. we then obtained estimates of the prevalence of reported depression and antidepressant use during outpatient visits for pregnant women. to determine whether these estimates varied by race, we used multivariable logistic regression analyses accounting for survey design using sas 9.2 (proc surveylogistic) to estimate odds ratios of reported antidepressant use after adjustment for age, insurance status and region of the country. results—antidepressant use was reported during 2.2% of all outpatient visits for pregnant women. providers indicated a depression diagnosis in 4.5% of visits. among visits for depressed pregnant women, providers reported antidepressant use 25.4% of the time for all visits. antidepressant use during pregnancy varied significantly by race/ethnicity. among visits for nonhispanic white women, 3.1% included a code for antidepressant use vs. just 1.0% for non-white women (p<0.0001). after adjustment for age, insurance status, and region of the country, this association persisted with non-hispanic white (vs. non-white) pregnant women having higher odds of antidepressant use (adjusted or 3.3, 95% ci 2.1, 5.3).
abstract1: social curation is the process through which the general public, rather than web publishers themselves, tag, organize, and share content with others. in this paper, i focus on the case of pinterest, an image-based social curation site. i specifically highlight how the individual acts of collection on the site contribute to a new kind of public construction of knowledge online. this is specific to pinterest, i argue, because the way that the site is set up—both in terms of its technological and business structure. my goals for the paper are as follows. first, i question how pinterest’s notion of curation compares with traditional or already existing notions of professional curation and personal collection. then, i offer an analysis of the activity on pinterest; whereas users believe they are participating in personal acts of collection and reflexive identity building, i counteract this with an examination of the entire business “ecosystem” of the site, showing how these personal activities can also be unde...
abstract2: complex indoor and outdoor missions for autonomous micro aerial vehicles (mav) constitute a demand for fast generation of collision-free paths in 3d space. often not all obstacles in an environment are known prior to the mission execution. consequently, the ability for replanning during a flight is key for success. our approach utilizes coarse grid-based path planning with an approximate model of flight dynamics to determine collision-free trajectories. to account for the flight dynamics and to mitigate discretization effects, these trajectories are further optimized with a gradient-based motion optimization method. we evaluate our method on an outdoor map with buildings and report trajectory costs and runtime results.
gen_abs: this study combines the concept of social curation on image-based platforms, such as pinterest, with autonomous navigation systems for micro aerial vehicles (mavs). by leveraging the collective knowledge construction on pinterest, we develop a novel framework for autonomous systems to generate collision-free trajectories in dynamic environments. our approach, termed "visual knowledge graph" (vkg), utilizes pinterest's collective curation to create a knowledge graph that represents the spatial layout and obstacles in a given environment. the vkg is then integrated with a gradient-based motion optimization method to optimize trajectories for mavs. we conduct experiments on outdoor maps with buildings, demonstrating that our approach can significantly reduce trajectory costs and improve flight efficiency. furthermore, we explore the implications of vkg on enhancing public curation, suggesting that collective knowledge construction can be a valuable resource for informing autonomous systems and improving their performance. our findings contribute to the development of more efficient and autonomous navigation systems, while also shedding light on the potential applications of social curation in autonomous systems.
cot: in this interdisciplinary study, we explore the intersection of social curation and autonomous navigation in complex environments. by leveraging the concept of social curation, we develop a novel approach to autonomous micro aerial vehicle (mav) navigation. our method utilizes social curation principles to dynamically plan and replan trajectories around obstacles in real-time, leveraging user-generated content and community-driven knowledge. specifically, we utilize pinterest's image-based collection and organization mechanisms to create a virtual "map" of the environment, where users' collective actions provide valuable information about obstacle locations and navigation pathways. we then integrate this virtual map with a coarse grid-based path planning and gradient-based motion optimization method to generate collision-free trajectories for the mav. our approach enables fast and efficient replanning during flight, while also providing a unique lens through which to analyze the social construction of knowledge in complex environments. through this integration, we demonstrate the potential for social curation to enhance autonomous navigation and shed light on the emergent properties of collective knowledge in dynamic systems.
tot: we propose a novel framework for crowdsourced mapping of complex environments, leveraging the collective curation efforts on social media platforms like pinterest to enrich the navigation capabilities of autonomous micro aerial vehicles (mavs). by analyzing the curation patterns and collective knowledge construction on pinterest, we identified a rich source of geotagged data that can be used to inform the mapping and path planning process for mavs. our approach integrates the social curation data with a coarse grid-based path planning algorithm, which generates collision-free trajectories in 3d space. to further optimize the trajectories and account for flight dynamics, we employ a gradient-based motion optimization method. we evaluate our framework on a real-world outdoor environment, demonstrating improved trajectory costs and runtime compared to traditional path planning methods. this interdisciplinary approach opens up new avenues for autonomous systems to benefit from human collaboration and collective knowledge, and we discuss the potential for our framework to be applied in various real-world scenarios, such as disaster response and urban exploration.
raw: this paper presents an interdisciplinary investigation into the application of social curation principles to autonomous robotics. specifically, we explore the use of social curation techniques inspired by platforms like pinterest to facilitate the generation of collision-free paths for autonomous micro aerial vehicles (mavs). by leveraging the collective knowledge and creative efforts of the online community, we develop a novel framework for collaborative path planning. our approach involves crowdsourcing the creation of a shared, curated map of the environment, which serves as a coarse grid for path planning. this map is then refined through a gradient-based motion optimization method, ensuring that the generated paths are not only collision-free but also optimized for performance. we demonstrate the efficacy of our approach on a real-world outdoor scenario, showcasing the potential for social curation to enhance the autonomy and situational awareness of mavs. our findings highlight the need for a more nuanced understanding of the interplay between human creativity and robotic decision-making in complex environments.
one_shot: this paper presents a novel approach to social navigation for autonomous micro aerial vehicles (mavs) in complex, dynamic environments. by leveraging the collective intelligence of social curation platforms, such as pinterest, we develop a framework for real-time path planning that incorporates user-generated knowledge and feedback. using pinterest's image-based curation as a case study, we demonstrate how user-collected and shared content can be harnessed to inform mav flight planning and replanning in uncertain environments. our method integrates coarse grid-based path planning with a gradient-based motion optimization method, and evaluates its performance on a real-world outdoor map with buildings. the results highlight the potential of social curation as a valuable source of information for autonomous systems, and demonstrate the feasibility of our approach in generating collision-free trajectories in 3d space.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach to optimize the navigation of autonomous micro aerial vehicles (mavs) in complex environments by leveraging social curation principles from online platforms. by analyzing the behavior of users on image-based social curation sites like pinterest, we identify patterns and strategies for efficient trajectory planning and replanning. our method utilizes coarse grid-based path planning with an approximate model of flight dynamics, similar to paper 2, but incorporates the collective intelligence of social curation to adaptively optimize trajectories in real-time. this integration enables mavs to rapidly respond to changing environments and unknown obstacles, improving their ability to complete complex missions while minimizing collision risks. we evaluate our approach on a variety of scenarios, demonstrating improved trajectory costs and runtime efficiency compared to state-of-the-art methods.
got: this paper presents an innovative framework for integrating social curation and autonomous navigation systems. building on the concept of social curation, where individuals collectively construct knowledge online through image-based platforms like pinterest, we explore its application in generating collision-free paths for autonomous micro aerial vehicles (mavs). our approach leverages the collective intelligence of users to generate and refine trajectories in 3d space, addressing the challenges of dynamic obstacle avoidance and replanning during flight. we develop a novel method that combines coarse grid-based path planning with a gradient-based motion optimization technique, incorporating user-generated content as an approximate model of the environment. this framework enables mavs to adapt to changing environments and optimize their trajectories in real-time, while also providing insights into the role of social curation in autonomous systems. our results demonstrate the feasibility and efficiency of this approach, paving the way for future applications in areas such as search and rescue, environmental monitoring, and urban planning.
