this study aims to develop as well as acquire a learning outcome instrument of badminton basic skill (ithd kdbb). this instrument is one of the performance test forms promoting individual performance assessment learning model which is able to measure students’ and athletes’ badminton skills after they have gone through the processes of learning or training. those skills concerning particularly on basic ones such as high serve, clear lob, dropshot, and smash are developed through subjective rating test and accuracy-based test. the method employed in this study is research and development (r&d) carried out within eight main sessions determining the test domain to measure, the analysis of the test making objective, the limitation of the test developed, the arrangement of specific content, the arrangement of specific format, the plan of test piloting and item selection, the plan of normative data collection and the test of manuals and supporting items. this ithb kdbb is tested both theoretically and empirically. in terms of theoretical side conducted through experiment judgment, it is found that its validity coefficient tested using cvr analysis range between 0.60 and 1.00. whereas, in terms of empirical side tested to students fpok upi of badminton club at the age of 11-13 years old carried out twice (the first one joined by 15 student and the second one joined by 50 of them), here are the results. the reliability and validity results of ithb kdbb using test-retest technique are 0.96 (high serve), 0.91 (clear lob). 0.89 (dropshot), 0.89 (smash), and 0.96 (the whole ithb kdbb). however, the consistency estimation of reliability using inter-rater reliability (objectivity) promoting intraclass correlation coefficient (icc) model consists of the following results: 0.98 (high serve), 0.95 (clear lob), 0.95 (dropshot), 0.96 (smash), and 0.96 (the whole ithb kdbb). in the meantime, the test of ithb kdbb validity using predictive validity test comprising students raking criteria with round robin comes up with the following results 0.70 (high serve), 0.69 (clearlob), 0.71 (dropshot), 0.74 (smash), and 0.75 (the shole ithb kdbb). based on those test results, it can be conclude that ithb kdbb being developed is either reliable or valid to measure students’, particularly 11-13 year-olds, badminton basic skills. kata kunci : ithb kdbb, performance test, performance assessment, reliability, validity.
abstract1: social curation is the process through which the general public, rather than web publishers themselves, tag, organize, and share content with others. in this paper, i focus on the case of pinterest, an image-based social curation site. i specifically highlight how the individual acts of collection on the site contribute to a new kind of public construction of knowledge online. this is specific to pinterest, i argue, because the way that the site is set up—both in terms of its technological and business structure. my goals for the paper are as follows. first, i question how pinterest’s notion of curation compares with traditional or already existing notions of professional curation and personal collection. then, i offer an analysis of the activity on pinterest; whereas users believe they are participating in personal acts of collection and reflexive identity building, i counteract this with an examination of the entire business “ecosystem” of the site, showing how these personal activities can also be unde...
abstract2: the purpose of this paper is to present a performance characteristics of digital current detector in dc-dc converter. the digital peak current mode control dc-dc converter using the voltage-controlled oscillator (vco) has already proposed. the peak current is detected by using vco in the proposed method. the gain of amplifier in the current detector affects to the input voltage range of vco. also, it affects the transient response. they are discussed by analysing and simulation.
gen_abs: this paper proposes a novel framework that integrates social curation and performance assessment to measure the development of basic skills in sports, specifically badminton. building on the concept of social curation as described in the context of pinterest, we design a digital platform for collecting and sharing performance data in badminton. the platform utilizes a digital current detector, similar to the one presented in paper 2, to monitor player performance in real-time. this allows for the creation of individual performance assessments, similar to the instrument developed in paper 1, which can measure students' and athletes' skills in high serve, clear lob, dropshot, and smash. our framework addresses the limitations of existing performance assessment instruments by incorporating social curation mechanisms, enabling users to contribute to the development of the instrument and share their performance data with others. we demonstrate the feasibility of our approach through a pilot study, showing high reliability and validity coefficients. this work has implications for the development of performance assessment instruments in sports and highlights the potential of social curation in enhancing the accuracy and scope of performance data collection.
cot: this paper presents an innovative approach to leveraging social curation as a tool for enhancing the performance of digital systems. building on the concept of social curation on platforms like pinterest, we investigate how the collective actions of users can be harnessed to optimize the design of digital circuits, such as dc-dc converters. specifically, we propose a novel method for adapting the voltage-controlled oscillator (vco) used in digital current mode control dc-dc converters, incorporating insights from the social curation ecosystem. our approach utilizes the dynamics of user-driven content organization and tagging on pinterest as a metaphor for optimizing the gain of the amplifier in the current detector, which affects the input voltage range of the vco and the transient response. by analyzing and simulating the performance of the adapted vco, we demonstrate improved efficiency and stability in the dc-dc converter. this interdisciplinary research has implications for the design of more responsive and adaptable digital systems, and highlights the potential of social curation as a design inspiration for technological innovation.
tot: this paper explores the intersection of social curation and digital systems, with a focus on the performance characteristics of digital current detectors in dc-dc converters. building on the concept of social curation, as exemplified by platforms like pinterest, we investigate how the individual acts of collection on these sites can be understood as a form of public construction of knowledge. we propose a novel approach to analyzing the performance of digital current detectors, which uses social curation principles to inform the design of more efficient and adaptive dc-dc converters. specifically, we demonstrate how the gain of the amplifier in the current detector can be optimized by leveraging the collective behavior of users on social curation platforms, leading to improved transient response and input voltage range. our results show that this integrated approach can lead to significant advancements in the field of power electronics, while also providing new insights into the relationship between social behavior and technological innovation.
raw: the rise of social media platforms like pinterest has led to the proliferation of user-generated content, raising questions about the nature of curation and knowledge construction in the digital age. this paper explores the intersection of social curation and digital electronics, focusing on the performance characteristics of digital current detectors in dc-dc converters used in social media infrastructure. by analyzing the activity on pinterest, we demonstrate how the individual acts of collection and sharing on the platform can be seen as a form of curation, with implications for our understanding of knowledge construction and public engagement. meanwhile, we investigate the performance characteristics of digital current detectors in dc-dc converters, which are crucial for the efficient operation of social media platforms like pinterest. our analysis reveals that the gain of the amplifier in the current detector has a significant impact on the input voltage range of the voltage-controlled oscillator (vco) and the transient response of the system. by integrating insights from social media studies and digital electronics, this paper provides a novel understanding of the complex relationships between user-generated content, curation, and the underlying technical infrastructure of social media platforms.
one_shot: this paper explores the intersection of social curation and energy-efficient digital systems, focusing on the role of user-generated content in optimizing power management in dc-dc converters. by analyzing the social construction of knowledge on image-based curation platforms like pinterest, we reveal how individual acts of collection and sharing can inform the design of more adaptive and responsive energy systems. specifically, we investigate how the collective behavior of users in curating and sharing digital content can be leveraged to detect and regulate peak currents in dc-dc converters, leading to improved energy efficiency and reduced electronic waste. our approach combines qualitative analysis of social curation practices with quantitative simulation and experimentation, demonstrating the potential for user-generated data to inform the development of more sustainable and energy-efficient digital technologies.
few_shot: we explore the intersection of digital curation and cyber-physical systems by investigating how social media platforms, like pinterest, influence the behavior of digital current detectors in dc-dc converters. by analyzing the performance characteristics of these detectors in the presence of user-generated content, we reveal how the collective activities of online communities can impact the efficiency and reliability of power electronics. our study demonstrates the potential for social media-driven fluctuations in power consumption, highlighting the need for adaptive control strategies that account for the interactions between digital curation and cyber-physical systems.
got: this paper explores the application of social curation in the design and performance analysis of digital electronics. building on the concept of social curation introduced in the context of pinterest, we investigate how collective knowledge construction can be leveraged to improve the development of digital control systems. specifically, we focus on the design of digital peak current mode control dc-dc converters, where user-generated content on online platforms can be harnessed to optimize the performance of the converter. by analyzing the interactions and collaborative efforts of online communities, we identify patterns and trends that inform the design of more efficient and effective current detectors. our approach demonstrates how the power of social curation can be applied to the development of digital electronics, leading to improved performance and reduced design time. through the integration of social and technical perspectives, this work contributes to the advancement of both fields and highlights the potential for interdisciplinary collaboration in the design of innovative electronic systems.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: since september 2010, in japan serological screening for the detection of human t-cell leukemia virus type 1 (htlv-1) antibodies can be performed for all women during pregnancy with the japanese public funds for strategies for prevention of htlv-1 vertical transmission, because japan, especially kyushu area, has been reported to be one of the areas of highest prevalence of htlv-1 in the world [1, 2]. in our previous study [3], we examined the prevalence of htlv-1 carrier in japanese pregnant women according to the implement rate and results of htlv-1 screening and confirmation tests of women who gave births in japan in 2011. the total rates of positive htlv-1 screening tests and positive western blot (wb) test in positive screening tests were 0.32% and 49.8%, respectively in 2011. considering the response rate and the rate of implementation of wb test, the number of htlv-1 carrier in japanese pregnant women in 2011 was estimated to be 1,560 (0.15%). in addition, although the number of delivery in kyushu area was only 14% of japanese deliveries, 53% of htlv-1 carrier of japanese pregnant women was present in kyushu area. recently, the migration of japanese people from kyushu area to the metropolitan areas has been thought to contribute to a significant decrease of htlv-1 carriers in kyushu area and an increase in kanto (including tokyo) area in japan [1, 4]. to confirm this migration in japanese pregnant women, on december 2014, we requested again 2,544 obstetrical facilities that are members of japan association of obstetricians and gynecologists (jaog) to provide information of htlv-1 tests in pregnant women who delivered at ≥ 22 weeks’ gestation in 2013. a total of 1,356 (53.3%) of 2,544 obstetrical facilities responded and information on a total of 538,167 women, accounting for approximately 54% of all deliveries that occurred in japan during the study period (approximately 1,001,800 births) was provided. in 2013, the total rates of positive htlv-1 screening tests and positive wb test in positive screening tests were 0.35% and 50.8%, respectively. considering the response rate and the rate of implementation of wb test, the number of htlv-1 carrier in japanese pregnant women in 2013 was estimated to be 1,780 (0.18%). table 1 shows the difference in the estimated number of htlv-1 carrier based on positive wb test by area in japan between 2011 and 2013. the estimated number of htlv-1 carrier in 2013 seemed to be more than that in 2011, especially in the northeast and southwest (kyushu) areas. in addition, 51% of htlv-1 carrier of japanese pregnant women was present in kyushu area, although the number of delivery in kyushu area was only 13% of japanese deliveries in 2013. although the migration of japanese people from kyushu area to the metropolitan areas has been supposed to contribute to a significant decrease of htlv-1 carriers in kyushu area, the estimated number of htlv-1 carrier of pregnant women seemed to be increased in kyushu area. in addition, the estimated rate of htlv-1 carrier in pregnant women in kyushu area was still significantly higher than that in the other areas (p < 0.01 by the chi-square test). therefore, there are still remaining problems concerning the locality for strategies for prevention of htlv-1 vertical transmission in japan.
abstract1: this paper proposes a method for voltage dip fault voltage detection and diagnosis in a grid connected wind turbine generator. the method is data-driven. from the measurements of the currents flowing into the grid, three features related to the trajectory of the current vector in the concordia stationary reference frame are extracted. the evaluation of the features for fault diagnosis is done through principal component analysis. in the subspaces spanned by the principal components (2d or 3d) the faults are detected and isolated. simulation results prove the efficiency of the method.
abstract2: one of the defining characteristics of my career as a secondary music teacher has been my persistence with collaboration; gathering groups of musicians together to create special musical events. i have always enjoyed these projects and have believed they have been positive for my students but hadn't considered scrutiny of the reasons why i am obsessed with pursuing them until now. the more i thought about it, the more i realised that this desire for collaboration came from the social setting of childhood. the literature on collaboration in music tends to focus on formal partnerships and composition using technology. there is very little research available on collaborative performance projects in the way that i have encountered them. the purpose of this study is to examine what has driven me to continue pursuing collaborative performance projects. by understanding my own position, it is hoped that i can then explore how these performance projects impact students, teachers and school communities. therefore, this paper attempts to answer the question; what events in my life have led me to the conviction that collaborative performance projects are beneficial? music teachers could consider collaborative performance projects for their students so that they may experience the same benefits that i enjoyed. music teachers who experience a sense of isolation in their musical lives may look to the events that i have experienced as a potential way of overcoming such feelings. in addition, this study may benefit music educators and ensemble directors through understanding aspects of collaborative performance projects not covered in the literature. the researcher i am a doctoral candidate in music education and lecturer in music and music education at a university in western australia. i completed my undergraduate degree in music education, choosing not to take the performance stream and spent several years teaching in the classroom and conducting school music ensembles. after four years of teaching music in the classroom i spent a year as a professional singer. i realised during this year of performing how much i missed teaching and that my original career path was as much vocational as it was practical. this study explores the pivotal moments in my life that has led to my interest in collaborative performance projects. it is important to understand the reason i am drawn to this process of collaboration in order to examine the impact that it has on those who are drawn into them. why auto-ethnography? in considering methodology, the choice of auto-ethnography was made so that i could examine my own cultural context. ellis, adams, and bochner (2011) explain that, "autoethnography combines characteristics of autobiography and ethnography" (p. 2). the autobiography allows the writer to reference pivotal moments or critical incidents in their own lives that are selected in hindsight. these incidents are usually chosen for being transformative in some way. ethnography focuses on the study of social and relational practices within a group of people so that emic and etic (insider and outsider) understanding of that culture can be enhanced. therefore, this idiographic methodology requires the researcher to reference pivotal personal moments as an insider and then analyse the meaning of the narrative as an outsider. this approach requires the ability to recall events in a personal way and write about them using the first person narrative. ellis and bochner (2002) discuss that, "autoethnography represents a significant expansion in both ethnographic form and relationship potential. in using oneself as an ethnographic exemplar, the researcher is freed from the traditional conventions of writing" (p. 15). by utilizing the autoethnography process, i hope to understand my own preferences about using the collaborative process. for so long, it has provided me with moments of inspiration and i have been using it as a model with which to inspire others. …
gen_abs: this interdisciplinary study investigates the impact of collaborative performance projects on the well-being and creative problem-solving skills of individuals in high-stress environments. leveraging the findings from paper 1, which utilized a data-driven approach to detect and diagnose faults in grid-connected wind turbine generators, we apply a similar methodology to identify key performance indicators (kpis) that measure the effectiveness of collaborative performance projects in music education. by analyzing the pivot points in the researcher's personal narrative (paper 2), we develop an autoethnographic framework that integrates the data-driven approach from paper 1 with the qualitative insights from paper 2. our study focuses on the japanese context, where the prevalence of htlv-1 carriers in pregnant women has been a concern, and explores how collaborative performance projects can mitigate the stress associated with this public health issue. we aim to identify kpis that quantify the benefits of collaborative performance projects in this context, providing a novel contribution to the fields of music education and public health. our research has the potential to inform the development of more effective stress-reduction strategies in high-stress environments.
cot: this interdisciplinary research project investigates the intersection of music education and engineering, leveraging the conceptual frameworks of collaboration and creativity to develop innovative approaches to stem education. by integrating the data-driven methods used in voltage dip fault detection in wind turbines (paper 1) with the collaborative performance practices of music education (paper 2), we explore the potential for a novel approach to teaching stem concepts through music and performance. specifically, we aim to develop a framework for using music performance as a tool for data analysis and interpretation, drawing on the mathematical principles underlying voltage dip fault detection. through autoethnographic analysis, we examine the experiences of music educators who have successfully incorporated collaborative performance projects into their teaching practices, identifying key factors that facilitate successful implementation and student engagement. by fostering a deeper understanding of the intersections between music, math, and collaboration, this research has the potential to inform the development of more effective and engaging stem education programs, ultimately enhancing the learning experiences of students and educators alike.
tot: this interdisciplinary study explores the intersections between collaborative performance projects in music education and fault diagnosis in renewable energy systems. by integrating the data-driven methods of voltage dip fault voltage detection and diagnosis in grid-connected wind turbine generators with the collaborative performance projects of music education, we investigate the application of principal component analysis in identifying patterns of creative collaboration. through an autoethnographic approach, we examine the pivotal moments in a music educator's life that led to their passion for collaborative performance projects and the impact of these experiences on students, teachers, and school communities. the study reveals that the social settings of childhood, which fostered a sense of collaboration and community, are analogous to the subspaces spanned by principal components in fault diagnosis. by applying this analogy, we develop a novel framework for designing and evaluating collaborative performance projects that promote social cohesion and enhance the learning experience. this interdisciplinary approach has the potential to inform the development of new educational programs and fault diagnosis methods, ultimately contributing to more effective and sustainable energy systems and music education practices.
raw: this study explores the intersection of technical and social collaboration in the context of wind turbine generator fault diagnosis and music performance projects. building on a data-driven method for detecting voltage dip faults in grid-connected wind turbines, we investigate how the principles of collaborative performance, as experienced by music teachers, can inform the design of more effective fault diagnosis systems. by applying principles of auto-ethnography, we examine the pivotal moments that have driven music teachers to value collaborative performance projects and consider how these insights can be translated to improve the detection and diagnosis of faults in wind turbine generators. the study proposes a novel framework for integrating technical and social collaboration in fault diagnosis, leveraging the collective knowledge and expertise of musicians and engineers to develop more robust and effective fault diagnosis systems. the implications of this work are twofold: it advances our understanding of the role of collaboration in technical systems and provides a new approach to fault diagnosis that can be applied in a wide range of engineering domains.
one_shot: this paper explores the harmonious convergence of data-driven fault diagnosis in wind turbines and collaborative performance projects in music education. by applying data-driven feature extraction and principal component analysis to voltage dip fault detection, we develop a novel method for grid-connected wind turbines. inspired by the autoethnographic study of a music educator who attributes the benefits of collaborative performance projects to their social setting in childhood, we investigate the analogous potential of interdisciplinary collaboration in stem education. by examining the intersections between human-centered approaches to data analysis and music-based learning, we aim to create a framework for fostering collaborative problem-solving in both domains. this research combines the efficiency of data-driven methods with the transformative power of collaborative learning, demonstrating a new paradigm for interdisciplinary education and innovation.
few_shot: this paper integrates insights from voltage dip fault diagnosis in wind turbine generators with collaborative performance projects in music education. by applying data-driven feature extraction and principal component analysis to voltage dip fault detection, we identify a parallel in the social and relational practices of collaborative performance projects, where musicians work together to create special musical events. we leverage autoethnography to examine the researcher's personal experiences and preferences for collaborative performance projects, revealing a connection between social setting and musical collaboration. our study demonstrates that understanding the underlying mechanisms of social and relational practices in music education can inform the development of more effective collaborative performance projects, and vice versa. this interdisciplinary approach has implications for the design of more resilient and efficient grid-connected wind turbine generators, as well as the development of innovative music education programs that foster social connections and creative expression.
got: this paper explores the intersection of music education and electrical engineering through an interdisciplinary investigation of collaborative performance projects and voltage dip fault detection in grid-connected wind turbines. by examining the personal narratives of music educators who incorporate collaborative performance projects into their teaching practices, we identify the social and emotional benefits of these projects, including increased student engagement, motivation, and social skills. we then apply these insights to the development of a data-driven method for voltage dip fault detection in wind turbines, which uses machine learning algorithms to extract features from current measurements and diagnose faults in a 2d or 3d subspace. our findings suggest that the collaborative performance project model can inform the design of more effective fault detection systems, which prioritize human-centered design principles and social benefits alongside technical performance. this research has implications for both music education and electrical engineering, highlighting the potential for interdisciplinary collaboration to drive innovation and improve outcomes in complex systems.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: the therapist-provided conditions of congruence, unconditional positive regard, and empathy are essential to the therapeutic process in child-centered play therapy (ccpt). this grounded theory study explored how these conditions are actualized in ccpt. participants included 4 therapists and 12 children. a process model of the conditions and implications for practice and research are discussed. keywords: congruence, unconditional positive regard, empathy, child-centered play therapy, grounded theory ********** child-centered play therapy (ccpt) is a developmentally responsive intervention for children based on the philosophy of carl rogers's (1957) person-centered approach (axline, 1974; landreth, 2012; ray, 2011). landreth (2012) defined ccpt as a dynamic interpersonal relationship between a child and a therapist who provides selected play materials and facilitates the development of a safe relationship for the child to fully explore and express self through play. although it is one of the most widely used approaches in the united states and has been shown to be an effective treatment for children with a variety of emotional and behavioral issues, few researchers have explored the therapeutic relationship, relational variables, and therapeutic process in ccpt (bratton, ray, rhine, & jones, 2005; lambert et al., 2007; ray & bratton, 2010; shirk & karver, 2003). child-centered practice is based on the conditions identified by rogers (1951) to facilitate client movement toward personality change. however, little is under stood about how the facilitative conditions are demonstrated and actualized in ccpt. rogers (1951) provided a comprehensive framework for understanding human development, psychological maladjustment, and personality change in his 19 propositions (ray, 2011; wilkins, 2010), and rogers (1957) described the environment necessary for constructive personality change to occur when he outlined six necessary and sufficient conditions for therapy: (a) the therapist and the client are in psychological contact, (b) the client experiences incongruence, (c) the therapist is congruent in the relationship, (d) the therapist experiences unconditional positive regard (upr) toward the client, (e) the therapist experiences and communicates empathic understanding toward the client, and (f) the client perceives the therapist's upr and empathic understanding. the third, fourth, and fifth conditions are often referred to as the attitudinal or therapist-provided conditions because they address the therapist's experience and communication of congruence, upr, and empathic understanding within the therapeutic relationship. essential to effective therapeutic practice, the attitudinal conditions are provided by the therapist with the primary purpose of creating a nonthreatening environment that allows the actualizing tendency of the client to be realized and growth to occur. although the attitudinal conditions are minimally explored in research and therapeutic practice, understanding how these three conditions are defined, experienced, and conveyed in ccpt is central to providing effective interventions for children and understanding the process and outcome of ccpt. defining the therapist-provided conditions in person-centered literature, congruence has often been defined as a therapist's ability to be genuine, open, aware, and integrated within the therapeutic relationship (mearns & thome, 2000; rogers, 1957; wyatt, 2001). congruence includes consistency between the therapist's internal awareness and external expression and facilitates empathy and acceptance toward the client (bozarth, 2001c; greenberg & geller, 2001). in ccpt, congruence is described as "being real" (landreth, 2012, p. 70) and is expressed through the play therapist's openness and genuine empathy toward and acceptance of the child (cochran, nordling, & cochran, 2010; vanfleet, sywulak, & sniscak, 2010). …
abstract1: with the upcoming transition from petascale to exascale computers radically new methods for scalable and robust computing are required. computing at the speed of exascale, that is, more than 1018 floating point operations per second, will only be possible on systems with millions of processing units. unfortunately, the large number of functional components like computing cores, memory chips and network interfaces will greatly increase the probability of failures, and it can thus not be expected that an exascale application will complete its execution on exactly the same resources it was started. in this paper, we investigate the impact of unfavorable process placement and oversubscription of compute resources on the performance and scalability of typical application workloads like cp2k, mom5 and bqcd. we provide results on two hpc architectures, a cray xc40 with proprietary aries network routers and dragonfly topology, and an infiniband cluster.
abstract2: the invention discloses an extraction process of chrysanthemum polysaccharide. with commercially available chrysanthemum as a raw material, extraction is carried out by adopting a mode of combining water extraction with alkali extraction, namely water extraction is firstly adopted to obtain one part of polysaccharide extracting solution; filter residues are subjected to alkali extraction to obtain another part of polysaccharide extracting solution; and the two parts of extracting solutions are merged and concentrated to obtain a final product. according to the extraction process of the chrysanthemum polysaccharide disclosed by the invention, compared with an existing conventional extraction process, the obtained chrysanthemum polysaccharide has higher yield, higher anti-oxidation activity and higher anti-inflammatory activity.
gen_abs: as high-performance computing (hpc) systems approach exascale, ensuring robustness and scalability in the face of component failures and resource oversubscription becomes increasingly crucial. inspired by the facilitative therapeutic conditions of congruence, unconditional positive regard, and empathy in child-centered play therapy (ccpt), we propose a novel approach to designing hpc applications that foster a "therapeutic" relationship between the application and the computing system. by introducing a "conditioning" framework, we can dynamically adjust the application's behavior to accommodate changes in resource availability, promoting a resilient and adaptive interaction between the application and the system. this approach is illustrated through a case study of optimizing the cp2k application on a cray xc40 architecture. in parallel, we explore the application of this framework to the extraction of chrysanthemum polysaccharide, where the "conditioning" framework can be used to optimize the extraction process in the face of variable chrysanthemum quality and processing conditions. our results demonstrate the potential of this approach to improve the robustness and scalability of hpc applications and extractive processes, while also highlighting the need for further research in this area.
cot: the advent of exascale computing poses significant challenges in ensuring the reliability and efficiency of complex simulations. as the number of processing units increases, so does the likelihood of component failures, leading to unpredictable execution environments. in this context, we investigate the impact of process placement and resource oversubscription on the performance of typical application workloads. by leveraging insights from materials science, we develop a novel approach to optimizing resource allocation, inspired by the extraction process of chrysanthemum polysaccharide. this process involves combining multiple extraction methods to achieve a more efficient and robust outcome. similarly, we propose a hybrid approach to exascale computing, combining traditional computing methods with novel resource allocation strategies. our approach, dubbed "exaextract," enables the efficient extraction of meaningful results from exascale simulations, even in the presence of component failures. we demonstrate the effectiveness of exaextract on two hpc architectures and show that it leads to significant improvements in application performance and scalability.
tot: high-performance computing (hpc) and biotechnology are converging in the development of novel extraction methods for bioactive compounds. as hpc architectures transition to exascale computing, robust and scalable methods are required to analyze and optimize complex biological systems. this research integrates hpc and biotechnology to develop a novel extraction process for chrysanthemum polysaccharide using a hybrid water and alkali extraction method. our approach leverages the scalability and performance of exascale computing to simulate and optimize the extraction process, eliminating the need for trial-and-error methods. we utilize two hpc architectures, a cray xc40 with proprietary aries network routers and dragonfly topology, and an infiniband cluster, to model and analyze the impact of process placement and oversubscription on the extraction process. our results demonstrate a 30% increase in polysaccharide yield and a 25% improvement in anti-oxidation activity compared to conventional extraction methods. this work demonstrates the potential of hpc in accelerating the discovery and optimization of biotechnology applications, enabling more efficient and effective extraction of bioactive compounds.
raw: the advent of exascale computing has the potential to revolutionize various fields, but it also introduces new challenges in terms of scalability and resilience. in this paper, we investigate the application of robust and scalable computing methods to the extraction and analysis of complex biological data, such as the chrysanthemum polysaccharide. we develop a novel framework that integrates high-performance computing with advanced bioprocessing techniques to optimize the extraction and characterization of valuable biomolecules. our approach combines water extraction with alkali extraction, as in traditional methods, but uses exascale computing to simulate and optimize the process, reducing the need for trial and error and improving the yield and activity of the extracted polysaccharide. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework on the extraction and analysis of chrysanthemum polysaccharide, and show that it can be applied to a wide range of biological systems and applications, enabling the discovery of new biomolecules and their potential applications in fields such as medicine and biotechnology.
one_shot: as high-performance computing (hpc) systems transition to exascale, ensuring robust and scalable computing is crucial. however, the increased complexity of these systems also leads to higher probabilities of component failures. this paper proposes a novel approach to mitigate the effects of process placement and resource oversubscription in hpc systems by harnessing the principles of bio-inspired extraction processes. specifically, we adapt the combination of water and alkali extraction methods used in the extraction of chrysanthemum polysaccharides to develop a fault-tolerant resource allocation algorithm. this algorithm leverages the synergy between different resource types, analogous to the co-extraction of polysaccharide components, to improve system resilience and scalability. we demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach using simulations on typical hpc workloads and showcase its potential to enhance the overall performance and efficiency of exascale computing systems.
few_shot: as high-performance computing (hpc) architectures reach exascale, failures due to component faults and oversubscription of resources become increasingly prevalent. to mitigate these issues, we propose an adaptive, decentralized approach that integrates machine learning and computational biology to optimize resource allocation and fault tolerance in hpc systems. our method leverages the extraction and analysis of polysaccharide data from chrysanthemum plants to develop a novel, robust algorithm that predicts and adapts to component failures in real-time. by applying this algorithm on a cray xc40 and infiniband cluster, we demonstrate significant improvements in hpc system resilience, scalability, and overall performance. this integrated approach enables the efficient execution of complex scientific simulations on exascale architectures, while also providing new insights into the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of chrysanthemum polysaccharides.
got: high-performance computing systems are expected to play a crucial role in simulating the complex interactions between chrysanthemum polysaccharides and their potential applications in pharmaceutical and biomedical research. however, the increasing complexity and unreliability of exascale computing systems pose significant challenges to achieving robust and scalable performance. this paper presents a novel approach to leveraging high-performance computing for the extraction and characterization of chrysanthemum polysaccharides. by developing a robust and scalable workflow that can adapt to failures and resource fluctuations in an exascale environment, we demonstrate the potential to significantly improve the yield and quality of chrysanthemum polysaccharides. our approach combines a novel extraction process, inspired by the chrysanthemum polysaccharide extraction process disclosed in paper 2, with a scalable and fault-tolerant computing framework, as investigated in paper 1. we evaluate the performance and scalability of our approach on two high-performance computing architectures and demonstrate its ability to achieve higher yields and improved properties of the extracted polysaccharides compared to conventional methods.

Sequence_abs: intermediate idea 1: (proquest: ... denotes formulae omitted)the aesthetic appreciation of a choir performance heavily relies on both the singers' skills and the acoustical characteristics of the venue. choir directors usually know that choral performances are greatly influenced by room acoustics, while the choir singers experience the difference between singing in a small room for practice and performing in a comparatively large space like a concert hall. clearly, it would be beneficial for musicians to understand both the effect of room acoustical features on their performance and how best to adjust tempo, phrasing, dynamics, and other musical parameters with respect to a given venue's acoustical environment.investigations into the interplay of acoustics and architecture in ancient greek and roman theaters reveal that architects from this early period, such as vitruvius, were already aware of the physical aspects of sound wave propagation and general aspects of room acoustics (declercq & dekeyser, 2007; farnetani, prodi, & pompoli, 2008). in later times, scholars like athanasius kircher (1602- 1680) systematically explored the characteristics of acoustic spaces through various experiments on the reflection of sound, as documented in part iv of the ninth book of his musurgia universalis (kircher, 1650).likewise, early composers were knowledgeable about the acoustical features of the locations in which their music was performed (in churches, concert halls, chambers, open-air, etc.). indeed, the room size of a venue was reflected in certain composition rules, the way of instrumentation, and specific styles of performance of that time. for instance, in contrast to traditional (unison) gregorian chants, melodic lines (with separate voices) in renaissance polyphony were often composed in a specific manner to avoid stylistically unsuitable dissonances that could occur due to late early reflections and very long reverberation times in large rooms (e.g., wilson, 1959).additionally, composers of the late renaissance and early baroque period developed the venetian polychoral style by placing groups of singers at different positions in a church in order to adapt to the acoustical conditions of (large) churches in venice (e.g., zarlino, 1558). though this kind of musical conceptualization remains a special case of interaction between music and architec- ture, it shows that composers had a considerable knowledge of the influence of room acoustics. later on, musicians and composers like quantz (1752) and mozart (1756) provided a number of recommendations regarding room size and how to adjust the style of performance (e.g., tempo adjustments) to the acoustical characteristics of a venue. for instance, quantz (1752) recommends playing slower in large rooms compared to playing in small chambers to preserve the intelligibility of the music.taking into account the important role of acoustics for music performances, it is surprising that not much research has been done in this area from the musicians' point of view as compared to the listener's perspective which is a crucial factor in designing concert halls. gade (1989a, 1989b), as one of the rare architectural acousticians who has considered acoustic conditions in concert halls from the musician's perspective, suggested a few objective parameters like "support" and "hearing each other" that are linked to the subjective impressions of musicians regarding room acoustics during concerts.empirical studies on the influence of room acoustics on solo music performance revealed that up to 50% of a performance feature's variance such as, tempo or loudness may be explained by room acoustical parameters (scharer kalkandjiev & weinzierl, 2013). solo musicians seem to intuitively adjust their performance to the room's acoustical situation, with "tempo" being one of the parameters significantly influenced by the specific reverberation time (rt) of the music venue (scharer kalkandjiev & weinzierl, 2015; ueno, kato, & kawai, 2010; kato, ueno, & kawai, 2015). …
abstract1: large-scale applications expressed as scientic workows are often grouped into ensembles of inter-related workows. in this paper, we address a new and important problem concerning the ecient management of such ensembles under budget and deadline constraints on infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds. iaas clouds are characterized by ondemand resource provisioning capabilities and a pay-per-use model. we discuss, develop, and assess novel algorithms based on static and dynamic strategies for both task scheduling and resource provisioning. we perform the evaluation via simulation using a set of scientic workow ensembles with a broad range of budget and deadline parameters, taking into account task granularity, uncertainties in task runtime estimations, provisioning delays, and failures. we nd that the key factor determining the performance of an algorithm is its ability to decide which workows in an ensemble to admit or reject for execution. our results show that an admission procedure based on workow structure and estimates of task runtimes can signicantly improve the quality of solutions.
abstract2: the invention discloses an extraction process of chrysanthemum polysaccharide. with commercially available chrysanthemum as a raw material, extraction is carried out by adopting a mode of combining water extraction with alkali extraction, namely water extraction is firstly adopted to obtain one part of polysaccharide extracting solution; filter residues are subjected to alkali extraction to obtain another part of polysaccharide extracting solution; and the two parts of extracting solutions are merged and concentrated to obtain a final product. according to the extraction process of the chrysanthemum polysaccharide disclosed by the invention, compared with an existing conventional extraction process, the obtained chrysanthemum polysaccharide has higher yield, higher anti-oxidation activity and higher anti-inflammatory activity.
gen_abs: we propose a novel approach to optimize the extraction of bioactive compounds from plant materials in cloud-based, large-scale workflows. inspired by the acoustic and architectural considerations in music performance, we develop a framework that accounts for the interplay between the extrusion process and the computational environment. by leveraging dynamic task scheduling and resource provisioning strategies from cloud computing, we create an adaptive workflow management system that adjusts to the varying acoustic properties of the plant material and the cloud infrastructure. we demonstrate this approach using chrysanthemum polysaccharide extraction, where the acoustic characteristics of the extraction process significantly impact the yield and quality of the final product. our results show that by integrating acoustic modeling and machine learning algorithms, we can improve the extraction efficiency and purity of the polysaccharide, while also reducing the computational resources required. this interdisciplinary approach has far-reaching implications for the development of cloud-based, large-scale extraction workflows in the life sciences, and highlights the potential for cross-disciplinary innovation in the fields of music, architecture, and computational biology.
cot: in the era of cloud computing, the increasing complexity of large-scale scientific workflows on infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds poses significant challenges in managing resources under budget and deadline constraints. this paper presents a novel approach to address these challenges by integrating cloud computing with green chemistry, a field that aims to improve the efficiency and sustainability of chemical processes. our research combines the development of novel algorithms for task scheduling and resource provisioning in iaas clouds with the optimization of a green extraction process for chrysanthemum polysaccharide, a valuable bioactive compound with high anti-oxidation and anti-inflammatory activities. by leveraging the ability of iaas clouds to dynamically adjust resources, we designed a cloud-based platform that integrates the extraction process with real-time monitoring and control. our results show that this integrated approach can significantly improve the yield, efficiency, and quality of the extracted polysaccharide while reducing resource utilization and costs. this work demonstrates the potential of interdisciplinary research to transform the field of green chemistry and cloud computing, enabling the development of sustainable and efficient large-scale scientific workflows.
tot: to optimize the extraction of high-value bioactive compounds from plant-based materials in a scalable and cost-effective manner, we propose an integrated approach combining cloud-based workflow management and bio-extraction process design. leveraging the on-demand resource provisioning capabilities of infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds, we develop a novel workflow ensemble management system that integrates with our proposed bio-extraction process design. specifically, we design a hybrid extraction process that combines water and alkali extraction methods to enhance yield, anti-oxidation activity, and anti-inflammatory activity of chrysanthemum polysaccharides. our system uses machine learning-based algorithms to dynamically schedule workflow tasks and allocate cloud resources, optimizing the extraction process under budget and deadline constraints. we evaluate our approach using a set of scientific workflow ensembles and bio-extraction process simulations, demonstrating significant improvements in extraction efficiency, quality, and cost-effectiveness. this integrated approach has the potential to revolutionize the production of high-value bioactive compounds and contribute to the development of sustainable and scalable bioprocessing technologies.
raw: in this paper, we propose a novel approach to optimizing the extraction and processing of valuable bioactive compounds from herbal materials, such as chrysanthemum, in cloud-based infrastructure as a service (iaas) environments. building on recent advances in workflow ensemble management, we develop a hybrid scheduling and resource provisioning algorithm that integrates static and dynamic strategies to efficiently allocate resources and minimize processing time while ensuring high-quality extraction yields. our approach, dubbed "cloudbioextract," incorporates machine learning-based admission control to prioritize workflows based on the structural properties of the extraction process and task runtime estimates. we evaluate cloudbioextract through simulations of large-scale extraction workflows and demonstrate significant improvements in extraction efficiency, product quality, and anti-oxidation and anti-inflammatory activities of the obtained polysaccharides. furthermore, we investigate the impact of uncertainty in task runtime estimations, provisioning delays, and failures on the performance of cloudbioextract, providing valuable insights for the development of robust and efficient cloud-based bioextraction workflows.
one_shot: we propose a novel approach to managing large-scale applications on infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds by integrating machine learning techniques with bio-inspired algorithms for resource provisioning and task scheduling. inspired by the natural world, we draw from the principles of chrysanthemum polysaccharide extraction, where a combination of water and alkali extraction leads to improved yields and enhanced activity. similarly, our hybrid algorithm leverages both static and dynamic strategies to optimize resource allocation and task execution, resulting in significantly improved solution quality. we evaluate our approach using a set of scientific workflow ensembles with diverse budget and deadline constraints, demonstrating improved performance and robustness compared to conventional methods. our work has the potential to revolutionize the management of complex applications on cloud infrastructure, enabling more efficient and effective resource utilization.
few_shot: we propose a novel approach for optimizing the extraction and processing of bioactive compounds from plants in cloud-based environments. by applying algorithms for task scheduling and resource provisioning to combine water and alkali extraction methods, we enhance the yield and quality of polysaccharides such as chrysanthemum polysaccharide. our method leverages the on-demand provisioning capabilities and pay-per-use model of infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds to dynamically adjust extraction parameters and optimize resource utilization. we demonstrate that this approach can significantly improve the anti-oxidation and anti-inflammatory activities of extracted polysaccharides while reducing extraction costs and environmental impact. the results have significant implications for the large-scale production of bioactive compounds in cloud-based settings, enabling more efficient and sustainable supply chains for the pharmaceutical and nutraceutical industries.
got: this paper presents a novel framework for optimizing the large-scale extraction of bioactive compounds, such as chrysanthemum polysaccharide, from plant materials on infrastructure as a service (iaas) clouds. we address the challenge of managing ensemble workflows for bioactive compound extraction under budget and deadline constraints, where task scheduling and resource provisioning play critical roles. our approach combines novel algorithms for dynamic task scheduling and resource provisioning with a bio-inspired admission procedure that selects the most promising extraction workflows based on their structural properties and estimates of task runtimes. we evaluate our framework using a set of case studies, including the extraction of chrysanthemum polysaccharide from commercially available raw materials. our results demonstrate that the proposed framework can significantly improve the yield, anti-oxidation activity, and anti-inflammatory activity of extracted compounds, while reducing the computational and financial overhead of the extraction process. our work has significant implications for the development of scalable and efficient workflows for bioactive compound extraction in cloud computing environments.

the senior high school students with a different prior knowledge..dissertation, department of learning technology, post graduate, state university of malang. advisor: (i) prof. dr i wayanardhana, m.a., (ii) prof. suhadiibnu, m.a., ph.d., and (iii) dr. wasisdjokodwiyogo, m.pd. keywords: problem-based learning, directinstruction, prior knowledge, problem solving, critical thinking. chemistry learning has two characteristics that is, chemistry as products (chemistry knowledge in the form of facts, concepts, principles, chemistry laws, and chemistry theories found by scientists) and chemistry as a process (science process skills). therefore, chemistry learning and chemistry learning assessment must deal with the characteristic of chemistry process and chemistry product. chemistry is considered as a difficult material, it is because of the learning ability of the students and the abstract nature of the material. however, the chemistry has a complex nature and it has many interesting phenomena. chemistry requires experimental activities, and produces knowledge to understand the natural world and production. chemistry is considered as the most difficult subject in the conceptual understanding of the curriculum in the school because of its complex nature. it must be important to be aware of that difficulty when teaching this subject in the class. learning strategies to improve students’ skill to solve some problems in chemistry learning process have been done by some experts. one of that is the implementation of problem-based learning strategy (pbl). pbl is an instructional model that involves students to solve a problem through the stages of scientific methods. along with this, the students can learn the knowledge related to the issues and also they can master the skill to solve the problem. pbl approach can improve the motivation of students, to improve a deep understanding of the subject, to support students’ autonomy and collaborative learning, to improve thinking skill, problem solving skill, teamwork, critical analysis, and communication. learning with pbl can help students to develop their thinking skill and problem solving skill, authentic learning under teacher’s role and finally to develop students to be an independent learners. this study aim to examine; (1) the effect of the problem-based learning strategy and a direct instruction to the chemistry problem solving skill and critical thinking skill; (2) the effect of the level of prior knowledge to the chemistry problem solving and critical thinking skill, and (3) the effect of interaction of the learning strategy and prior knowledge level to the chemistry problem solving critical thinking skill of students in the senior high school. this study was designed as a quasi-experimental research with 2 x 2 factorial design studies. the subject of the study is 140 students in the grade xi of sman 1 and sman 2 watampone. there are two groups of students were given treatment research, they are (1) a group of students with pbl learning strategy and (2) a group of students with a direct instruction strategy. students are also divided into two different levels of prior knowledge that is high and low prior knowledge. test is used to measure the ability of a chemistry problem solving and critical thinking skill of students. the test was given before and after treatment. the hypothesis was tested by multivariate analysis of variance (manova) using spss 22 for windows at a significance level of 0.50. the results showed that: (1) there is a significant difference in chemistry problem solving skill among students’ group with pbl learning strategy compared with a group of students with direct instruction strategy (f=38.546, p=0.000 (f=76.283, p=0.000 0.05); (4) there is a significant difference in students’ critical thinking skill between a student group which is treated by learning strategy compared by a student group which is treated by direct
